column6: Resolved an issue where changes to user group assignments were not being applied immediately.
column7: Resolved an issue where changes to user group assignments were not being applied immediately, resulting in delayed permission updates or access inconsistencies.

column6: Internet Statement Server will return the expected xml hit list when making a query to hfscustomquery.aspx
column7: Internet Statement Server will return the expected xml hit list when making a query to hfscustomquery.aspx

column6: The time taken to generate packets has been significantly reduced when Contents are added in the Documents Sets
column7: The defect has been fixed and now time taken to generate packets has been significantly reduced when Contents are added in the Documents Sets

column6: Improved zooming behavior in Express Scanning client.
column7: Improved zooming behavior in Express Scanning client.  Previously, if you zoom out and then zoom in, the client could zoom out.  Issue more noticeable on smaller images.

column6: Correction for an extra tilde being inserted into certain outbound messages containing repeating segments
column7: In certain cases an extra tilde would be inserted following a field that was configured to repeat.  This was found to occur when a field was configured to repeat, but only one value was available to be sent in the HL7 message.

column6: Document Keywords Now Update Correctly When 'Always Propagate Patient Information' and 'Update Existing Documents' Options Are Both Enabled
column7: An issue has been resolved that prevented keyword updates from being propagated to documents as expected.  This issue could occur when the 'Always Propagate Patient Information' and 'Update Existing Documents' options are enabled and patient records contained information matching the inbound update message, but attached documents had incorrect keywords that needed to be updated.

column6: Resolved Error on shared class related configuration when performing Studio export in 23.1 and higher
column7: Updated code to correct an error on shared-class-related configuration when performing an export in Studio in OnBase Foundation 23.1 and higher. Currently, we are able to export the package, and no errors are showing.

column6: Resolved Performance Issue When Assigning Release of Information Requests
column7: A performance issue that caused Release of Information to become unresponsive when assigning requests has been resolved. Previously, in environments with large amounts of configured Users, Facilities and Templates, clicking "Assign" could cause the application to load for excessive periods of time, potentially resulting in a timeout that required the user to reconnect.

column6: After navigating to another document, document locks do not stay on the initial document when notes or cross-references are open on the Document Information Panel.
column7: An error has been resolved in the Unity Client for document locks working incorrectly. Previously, if a document is open in a viewer along with the notes and cross references in DIP, if the user navigates to a different document, the first document would be locked.

column6: Addressed a defect where Submitting a Unity Form caused the Unity Client to crash.
column7: A critical issue has been resolved where submitting a Unity Form caused the Unity Client to crash in specific use cases due to an external 3rd party library. This release improves user experience and stability during form submissions.

column6: Fixed a defect to make The Send To feature work with New Outlook for Windows in Unity Client
column7: The Send To feature now works with New Outlook for Windows in Unity Client. No exception error message gets thrown.

column6: Optimized the size of App Server installer
column7: Unnecessary files have been removed from the HyRCS build output, reducing install size by good amount for faster, leaner deployments.

column6: Defect Fix for storing file in original formal during scanning
column7: A defect was fixed where files scanned were saved in a format different than the original.

column6: Fix for Date&Time Field Formatting Issue in Unity Forms.
column7: Resolved an issue where the Date&Time field displayed incorrect formatting after executing a Custom Action Event Script in Unity Forms. This fix ensures that the field values are correctly applied, allowing users to submit forms without formatting errors.

column6: Performance fix for Physicians who have been assigned a large amount of deficiencies
column7: A performance issue has been resolved that prevented Physicians with a large amount of pending/unsigned Deficiencies from being able to access and interact with their assigned deficiencies in a reasonable amount of time.

column6: Bug fix for Image Rendering Issue Across Multiple Template Sections Using Image Fragment Master
column7: This fix resolves an issue where Document Fragments that used the same image—via an Image Fragment Master—in multiple template sections (such as the Header, Footer, and Body) failed to render correctly. In such cases, one of the sections would typically display the message: “The picture can't be displayed.”

column6: Fix for HL7 ACKs not processing correctly
column7: A defect has been resolved that causes OnBase to not read the last character in the HL7 ACK received from the customer.  Messages are showing as unsent/failed with "Error, no bytes received for HL7 ACK" in HL7 Event Log.  This defect did not stop HL7 sending, but it did cause an issue with the ACK.

column6: Bug fix for cleaning up extra database entries related to re-submitted completed deficiencies
column7: An issue has been resolved that prevented the cleanup of database entries related to deficiencies that were completed by Physicians and then re-submitted by Analysts. These entries are extra rows used to track the initial completion by the Physician. With this fix, these rows will now be properly cleaned up when the re-submitted deficiency is completed.

column6: Freehand annotations created in the Unity Client print at the correct size in the Web Client.
column7: Addressed an issue that caused Freehand annotations to be printed at the incorrect size in the Web Client. Previously, Freehand annotations created in the Unity Client would print at a smaller size in the Web Client.

column6: Updates to the Context Menu button in the Patient Window
column7:
The issue preventing the context menu graphic from displaying has been resolved.  The ellipses that represent this button will now appear as expected when viewing e-forms from the tabbed view of Patient Window.

The context menu will no longer display above e-forms when viewing corrections forms in the split viewer.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with saving the Workflow Summary portlet charts in Web Client StatusView
column7: Resolved an issue where the charts created in the WorkView Summary portlets were not saved by clicking the Save button in the Chart Viewer window. With this fix, users are now able to save the charts.

column6: Sequence number uniqueness Enforcement in WorkView Item Generation.
column7: This update resolves the issue where duplicate sequence numbers were being generated during concurrent executions of Item Generators in OnBase Studio. The SequenceDataAccess class has been updated to enforce the uniqueness of sequence numbers, ensuring reliable attribute value generation even when multiple Studio instances are run simultaneously. This change enhances data integrity for WorkView applications that rely on WorkView Sequences for auto-generated attribute values.

column6: Resolved an issue with Line, Arrow, Ellipse, and Freehand annotations printing incorrectly in the Web Client.
column7: Line, Arrow, Ellipse, and Freehand annotations now print the same way that they are rendered in the Web Client.

column6: Resolved an issue with the "Always Print" configuration not being respected
column7: The Web Client respects the "Always Print" configuration when printing a Line annotation on an Image Rendered PDF.

column6: If rotate right is changed during document review the changed should carried over both in document separation and Indexing.
column7: If rotate right is changed during document review the changed should carried over both in document separation and Indexing.

column6: CPD Scan Queue Batches should change status when the last unindexed document is deleted
column7: The CPD Scan Queue Batches would get stuck on the same status when the last document to be indexed got deleted, when the status should change according to the current batch state.

column6: All pages of the PCL files are properly displayed.
column7: Only the first page of the PCL files was displayed with no issues. The rest of the pages were rendered with some errors. No page is corrupted when it's displayed on the OnBase Client.

column6: Addressed issue(s) OnBase Client bypass for over-sized images when performing Image Processing: Auto-Detect Orientation
column7: Fixed OnBase Client bypass for over-sized images when performing Image Processing: Auto-Detect Orientation

column6: Document Packaging Performance has been improved
column7: Slow performance in Document Packaging and the turnaround time for Optimizing Document Package was fixed.

column6: This ticket has fixed critical platter management error while trying to process large EDI documents. Now large EDI Files process without error.
column7: This ticket has fixed critical platter management error while trying to process large EDI documents. Now large EDI Files process without error.

column6: Fixed the issue of document not getting opened when the first document within a folder in the Web Client is selected
column7: Resolved the issue of opening the first selected document within a folder in Web Client, with the 'Do Not Automatically Display First Document' enabled.

column6: Fixed an issue with double-clicking forms in Healthcare Form Manager
column7: Fixed an issue where Healthcare Form Manager would display an assigned form in read-only mode if the user double-clicked it from the list. Now users can single-click or double-click to open and edit an assigned form.

column6: OCR is able to process all valid image documents without failing regardless of OCR Format settings
column7: OCR is able to process all valid image documents without failing regardless of OCR Format settings

column6: Bug fixing in import assembly
column7: Once an assembly has been replaced and is set to inactive (assemblystatus=1), it should not be queried when looking for dependencies during the importing of an assembly. Required changes are made to look for the active one so that the error does not occur.

column6: User can draw freehand annotation in the corner of documents without messing up the annotation.
column7: Fix of an issue with freehand annotations in the corner of documents.

column6: Resolution for Unity Forms Invocation Error in Citrix Environment.
column7: A fix has been implemented to address the 'Exception has been thrown by target of invocation' error encountered when multiple users attempt to access Unity Form documents simultaneously in a Citrix environment. The updated logic for CefSharp temporary path resolution ensures that users can now open Unity Form documents without causing the Unity Client to become unresponsive. This enhancement improves the overall user experience by allowing seamless access to Unity Forms regardless of concurrent usage.

column6: Freehand annotation will stay at the same place where it has been drawn.
column7: This is a fix that makes sure the Freehand annotation type stays at the same place where it has been drawn. Previously, the Freehand annotation may have moved to a place other than where it was drawn on a document.

column6: Fixed defects with annotation movement on Image Rendered PDFs
column7: Defects related to the movement of annotations during Image Rendered PDF page rotation was fixed.

column6: A bug is fixed related to "My Owned Items"
column7: An issue that prevented files in "My Owned Items" from loading in the Web Client has been resolved. All files now load correctly, and their associated tasks execute without any problems.

column6: Improvement of Line Annotation behavior
column7: Defect fix for Line annotation in Web Client.

column6: Fix for Keep With Next Setting in Tables
column7: An issue was fixed with “Keep With Next”, where both “Allow Rows to Break” and “Keep With Next” settings now work as intended, ensuring proper content flow and pagination.

column6: A rare, minor error related to memory management with External Access Client has been corrected
The HL7 Auto Sender will now resend entire messages that were interrupted due to a loss of connection
column7: An issue has been resolved that resulted in partial HL7 messages being sent after a connection was restored.  Now, when connectivity resumes the entire HL7 message that was interrupted will be resent.

column6: Correction for an extra tilde being inserted into certain outbound messages containing repeating segments
column7: In certain cases an extra tilde would be inserted following a field that was configured to repeat.  This was found to occur when a field was configured to repeat, but only one value was available to be sent in the HL7 message.

column6: An issue has been resolved that prevented HL7 jobs that are no longer valid from being cleaned up from the database
column7:
The HL7 Backend Processor will clean up jobs that are no longer necessary because a patient merge has occurred.  

This specific situation could occur when a message was received by OnBase that caused patient demographic information to need to be updated using the Patient Record Processor or Medical Records Chart Processor.  While waiting for the job to process, the HL7 Listener would receive a merge message for this same patient that merges the patient identifiers which previously required demographic updates.  The demographic updates related to the original message are no longer required as updates related to the merge have already been processed.

With this fix the HL7 Backend processor now produce an error stating "Unable to find Patient {PatientID} for Job {JobId}."  Following the error the invalid job will be removed from the database.

column6: Handled the error that was coming while selecting from a Cascading Data Set drop-down for limited access users.
column7: Resolved an issue causing users with limited access to be unable to modify keywords they have access to and getting an error while selecting from a Cascading Data Set drop-down.

column6: An issue related to modifying a document's keyword values has been resolved.
column7: A bug which prevented a user from saving modifications to keyword values on a document has been resolved. This issue occurred only when the Keyword panel was accessed before any other option available in the right-click context menu.

column6: Zero-degree and ninety-degree Line annotations are not vanishing after being drawn.
column7: This is a fix that allows for perfectly vertical (zero-degree) and perfectly horizontal (ninety-degree) Line annotations to be drawn. Previously, zero-degree and ninety-degree Line annotations vanished after being drawn.

column6: Fix for First Field Display Issue on Small Devices in Responsive Unity Form.
column7: Resolved an issue where the first field was not displayed on small devices when the Responsive Unity Form had another page with access restrictions. This fix ensures that all fields are visible and accessible, improving user experience across devices.

column6: User Interaction Window Resizing Issue Resolved in Unity Form.
column7: The user interaction window in Unity Forms now automatically adjusts to fit the content size, enhancing user experience. This fix addresses the previously reported issue where the window did not resize appropriately in desktop, ensuring a more seamless interaction.

column6: Remove Expired Session Information task can throw an error in some circumstances
column7: Unity Scheduler Task to Remove Expired Session Information no longer throws an exception when non-Core products are licensed.

column6: Fix bugs related to splitting pdf docs in unity client.
column7: Fix document separation functionality so that users can split PDF docs in Unity Client.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with Document Lock
column7: Resolved an issue where a document remained locked even when it was transitioned out of the queue. With this fix, the document lock is released after a user transitions it out of the queue and is no longer displayed in the document viewer.

column6: Fix for Workview KeywordTypeMappings Not Respected in the Web Client.
column7: This release addresses an issue where Keyword Type Mappings were not respected in the Web Client when a Keyword Type Group was a Multi-Instance Keyword Type Group associated with an Autofill Keyword Set. Users can now expect that the Keyword Type Group auto-fills properly in the Web Client like it does in the Unity Client.

column6: Data Columns Visibility Issue Resolved in WorkView Data Import Configuration.
column7: Addressed a defect where data columns did not appear in the WorkView Data Import Configuration screen. This fix ensures that users can view and utilize all necessary data columns for a seamless import experience.

column6: Fix for Missing Content and Formatting Issues in WorkView Object Printing.
column7: This release addresses a defect where printed content from WorkView Objects in the Unity Client was missing and incorrectly formatted. Users can now expect complete and properly formatted output when printing these objects.

column6: Addressed a loading issue of User Interaction Window in Workflow Classic View.
column7: Resolved an issue where the User Interaction Window did not appear in the Workflow Classic View after users navigated away from the Workflow and executed on the same Ad-hoc task later. This fix ensures that the User Interaction Window is accessible without interruptions.

column6: Resolved errant log messages during application server startup
column7: Resolved errant log messages during application server startup

column6: Corrected an issue processing TIFF images
column7: In specific situations, TIFF images would have an improperly formatted header. This has been accounted for and no longer causes the OnBase Client to close unexpectedly when these images are processed.

column6: Diagnostics Console now correctly installs and displays the icon
column7: Diagnostics Console now correctly installs and displays the icon

column6: Thumbnail loading order in Document Separation is in sync and no performance problem can be seen.
column7: Thumbnail loading order in Document Separation is in sync and no performance problem can be seen.

column6: Document sequence in indexing was corrected.
column7: Document sequence in indexing was corrected. After the fix the document sequence stays same after bringing in the document.

column6: The OnBase Client logic is matched with AppServer/DCS for reading patch codes.
column7: The OnBase Client logic is matched with AppServer/DCS for reading patch codes.

column6: Pages added during QA Rescan should always get added where selected
column7: Pages added during QA Rescan should always get added where selected

column6: Unprocessed documents (Barcode processing) stay in a batch.
Create/Update Physician from Master File HL7 Import Process improperly handling values mapped to the Master File Full Name field.
column7:
An issue has been resolved that was causing the Create/Update Physician from Master File HL7 Import Process to improperly handle values mapped to the Master File Full Name field.

Prior to this fix values passed in fields mapped to the Master File - User Name were being stored as both the account's User Name and Real Name.  This fix ensures that both the Master File - User Name and Master File - Full Name data fields are handled properly and store their values in their respective user account fields.

column6: Resolved an issue with the OAuth Migration dialog box for Mail Box Importer
column7: Resolved an issue with the OAuth Migration dialog box for Mail Box Importer.

column6: Fixed Chart Level Delinquencies Persisting After Chart Deletion
column7: Chart Level Deficiencies that were delinquent at the time their related chart was deleted were not properly being cleaned up.  The software has been updated so that these records are now removed, as expected, upon Chart deletion.

column6: Resubmitted deficiencies on PDFs will fail to burn after they are completed
column7: An issue that prevented re-submitted deficiencies from burning has been resolved.  This issue only affected deficiencies that had been placed on a PDF, completed by a Physician and then re-submitted for completion again by an Reanalyst.

column6: Fix for Orphan Note burn errors related to Missing Information deficiencies being created at the time of burn
column7: An issue has been resolved where Missing Information deficiencies could be marked as failing to burn if the MRMS Analysis Server attempted to burn the document while the deficiency was being created.

column6: On Text documents, Line and Freehand annotation sections are not present in the Notes gallery.
column7: On Text documents, Line and Freehand annotation sections are not present in the Notes gallery. This is true even when Unity Client is configured to allow Line and Freehand annotations. This is because, as of this release, Line and Freehand annotations are not compatible with Text documents.

column6: Switching to any other note type is possible when the user toggles Freehand annotation
column7: This fix allows users to switch between annotations and notes after toggling Freehand annotations. Previously, a user would be prevented from switching to different note/annotation types when toggling a Freehand annotation.

column6: The Freehand Annotation has a showcased section in the Note Gallery.
column7: The Freehand annotation type is now given its own section so that it is showcased in the Note Gallery in the Unity Client. This makes it easier to find and notice the Freehand annotation functionality.

column6: An issue related to the EnableBrowserPDFViewer option has been resolved.
column7: Previously, when the web.config key "EnableBrowserPdfViewer" was set to false and the "Open with Browser PDF Viewer" button in the toolbar was selected, a new window opened and failed to load. This issue has been corrected so that documents successfully load when "EnableBrowserPdfViewer" is set to false and the "Open with Browser PDF Viewer" button is selected.

column6: Fix for attaching documents to an ROI Request that belongs to a Medical Facility that does not have Clinical Document Tabs configured
column7: An issue has been resolved that prevented Release of Information from attaching documents to a request when one or more Patients or Charts associated with the request belonged to a Medical Facility that did not have Clinical Document Tabs configured.  Medical Facilities can enter this state if they are not fully configured or they are created automatically by HL7.

column6: Selecting a Note or Highlight from a list takes the user to the page and automatically select the Note or Highlight on that page.
column7: This fix addresses an error when selecting a Note or Highlight from a list. Prior to this fix, when a user selected a Note or Highlight, the user would be directed to the page, but the selected Note or Highlight would not be selected, forcing the user to manually find the Note or Highlight they selected.

column6: The aspect ratio of Line and Freehand Annotations is maintained properly after zooming in or out of a document.
column7: Zooming in and out of a document with a Line and Freehand annotation now maintains the aspect ratio. Previously, when a Freehand or Line annotation was added to a document that was zoomed out, the aspect ratio would be scaled up, and vice versa for zoomed in documents.

column6: HTML based documents (Workview Objects, Unity Forms, HTML Form, etc) should open without showing any error on the first login of the Click-Once Unity Client in service mode,
column7: Upon the initial login of the Click-Once Unity Client operating in service mode, all HTML-based documents—such as Workview Objects, Unity Forms, and standard HTML Forms—must load seamlessly without triggering any errors or rendering issues. The interface should display each document as intended, ensuring that functionality, formatting, and interactivity are fully intact from the moment the client is launched.

column6: Fixed an issue for Cascading Dataset when unity forms fails to populate child values of a Cascading Dataset.
column7: Resolved an issue where cascading dataset values were failing to display correctly in certain cases on Unity Forms. Users should be able to see the fetched cascading value for the parent and its child dataset when added as a field in the Unity form.

column6: Introduced a new checkbox in Global Settings to enable or disable the display of invalid field errors during form loading.
column7: A new checkbox, labeled “Disable Field Errors on Form Load,” has been introduced in the Global Settings. This setting allows administrators to configure a feature flag that enables or disables the display of invalid field errors when the form loads. By default, the checkbox is unchecked.

column6: Fix for Field Validation Errors Displaying on Form Load.
column7: Resolved an issue where field validation errors were incorrectly displayed upon form load, even when the 'Disable Field Errors on Form Load' checkbox was checked. This fix ensures a smoother user experience by preventing error messages from appearing at the start.

column6: Fix for Navigation Error of Unity Form in Workflow.
column7: We have addressed an issue where users encountered an error message stating "Window cannot be null..." when navigating back to a Unity Form in the Workflow from a different context. This defect caused disruptions in user experience, preventing seamless transitions between forms. The fix ensures that the window context is properly maintained, allowing users to navigate without encountering this error.

column6: Fix user behavior to view primary items in a Workflow inbox in the Web Client.
column7: A user can view the primary item in a Workflow lifecycle inbox within the Web Client without having the Document Type rights to the Document Type the Workflow is assigned to.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with creating revisions of locked documents|Revision of locked document can be created now as part of Workflow Timer Execution as expected.
column7: Resolved an issue where revisions of locked documents were not being created due to an execution error in the Workflow Timer. With this fix, the Workflow Timer is functioning as expected and the revisions of locked documents are created.|Workflow (and really all of OnBase) was not able to create revisions of locked documents and a failure was occurring as part of a Workflow Timer. This issue has been fixed and revision of locked document can be created now as part of Workflow Timer Execution as expected.

column6: Addressed issues with the Workflow inbox column filter value retention in the Unity Client
column7: Resolved an issue where the Workflow inbox column filter value would be removed when switching between Workflow queues or modifying the column width within the inbox. The Workflow inbox column filter now retains its value until the user clears it.

column6: Fix for Document Viewer Right-Click Functionality within WorkView in Web Client.
column7: Addressed a defect in the Document Viewer within WorkView where right-clicking on a document did not display the expected options, including keywords, history, re-index, and more. This fix enhances user experience by restoring full functionality to the right-click context menu, allowing users to efficiently access important document management features.

column6: The Patient Identifier display column has been renamed to "Patient ID"
column7: Prior to this change, the Column Header would be named either "MPI Number" or "Medical Records #".  In some cases, the header name was not updating properly. As you could only have either MPI or MRN selected to display, we have simplified the column header name to be just "Patient ID".  This change will be available immediately for new installs or customers upgrading and leveraging the DBUtil tool to update the database.

column6: The Image Cash Letter X9.37 Process has been added is now available for Check Processing
column7: The Image Cash Letter check processor (X9.37) has been added back to OnBase.

column6: Batches imported through a scheduled sweep should route through the Sweep import step
column7: Batches imported through a scheduled sweep should route through the Sweep import step

column6: Epic Integration: The Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) title bar shows the correct file version.
column7: The Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) will display the version of the EpicSaS.dll file found in the installation directory.

column6: Defect fixed so that Users are able to create FolderPop URLs for child folders
column7: Users are now able to create FolderPop URLs for child folders

column6: Fix for Services running the HL7 Listener and Auto-Sender functionality
column7: An issue was corrected that prevented services that ran HL7 Listener or HL7 Auto-Sender functionality from starting properly.

column6: Fix for HL7 Backend Processor Failing to Run
column7: Resolved an issue where HL7 Backend Processor jobs configured to run via the Unity Scheduler service would unexpectedly fail.

column6: Resolved issue with SD4E resolving missing assemblies
column7: An issue was resolved when SD4E attempts to resolve library dependencies that are not found in GAC.

column6: Resolved intermittent login looping issue
column7: An intermittent login looping issue when using the Esri Web app builder OnBase widget has been resolved. The issue could occur when using Hyland IdS for authentication in load balanced environments.

column6: A bug is fixed related to "Unauthorized Access: Invalid Session Context ..."
column7: A bug is fixed related to "Unauthorized Access: Invalid Session Context ..." while executing a Workflow/wflogin.aspx login via an apps URL action, and performing a page refresh after returning to the original OnBase Apps screen

column6: User should not receive any error when attempting to preview documents via trash can from the Unity client.
column7: User should not receive any error when attempting to preview documents via trash can from the Unity client.

column6: Submitting a Unity Form refreshes the hitlist and viewer, while keeping the same document open and selected.
column7: In the Workflow Queue(with >1000 documents), on submit of a Unity Form, both the hitlist and the viewer are refreshed, and the document remains open in the viewer and selected in the hitlist.

column6: Document locks are released when switching between documents located in different folders.
column7: Document locks are released when switching between documents located in different folders.

column6: Addressed a defect causing print errors in the Web Client when documents have low DPI settings, a problem not present in earlier versions of OnBase (OnBase EP3 (20.3) or earlier)
column7: Fixed a defect causing print errors in the Web Client when documents have low DPI settings

column6: Defect fixed to address error displayed in select folder dialog
column7: No error is displayed in the console

column6: Resolved 'Object Reference' Error When Saving E-Forms in Unity Client.
column7: The issue causing an 'Object Reference...' error when saving E-Forms in the Unity Client with "webBrowserRenderMode=ie" enabled has been addressed. Users can now save E-Forms without encountering the error, ensuring a smoother experience without unnecessary bug report prompts.

column6: Resolved an issue with the SMART on FHIR (SoF) DefPop Integration for Epic affecting "One Epic, Multiple OnBase" customers|Resolved Issue with Smart on FHIR DefPop Integration for Epic and One Epic, Multiple OnBase Customers
column7:
An issue has been resolved in the DeficiencyPop (DefPop) integration where Document IDs were not being correctly prefixed with the facility identifier.

While the One Epic, Multiple OnBase mode requires Document IDs to follow the format HY.<facility_id>.<document_id>, the DefPop workflow was only sending the numeric ID, causing Epic to be unable to locate the document and resulting in failures.

The correct behavior had previously been implemented for FOS and SAS integrations but was missing from DefPop. The integration now correctly applies the facility prefix when configured, ensuring successful document lookup and update within Epic.|An issue has been resolved in the DefPop integration where Document IDs were not being correctly prefixed with the facility identifier when updating deficiency status through the UpdateScanSignatures2 Epic API.

While the One Epic, Multiple OnBase mode requires Document IDs to follow the format HY.<facility_id>.<document_id>, the DefPop workflow was only sending the numeric ID, causing Epic to be unable to locate the document and resulting in failures.

This behavior had previously been implemented for FOS and SAS integrations but was missing from DefPop. The integration now correctly applies the facility prefix when configured, ensuring successful document lookup and update within Epic.

column6: Fixed Display Issues in WorkView Summary Portlet for Finnish and Swedish Browser Language Settings|WorkView Summary portlet displays correctly when the browser language set to Finnish or Swedish.
column7: Resolved an issue where the WorkView Summary portlet displayed a yellow triangle in StatusView when the browser language was set to Finnish or Swedish.|WorkView Summary portlet displays correctly when the browser language set to Finnish or Swedish. It is not displaying yellow triangle in StatusView.

column6: Fix for "A Task Was Canceled" Error in Unity Client when working with Unity Forms.
column7: Resolved an issue causing the Unity Client to throw "A task was canceled." error and crash when working with Unity Forms. This fix enhances stability and improves the user experience during form interactions.

column6: A group of batches are processed in the order they are within the UI.
column7:
If batches are ordered by batchID, newest listed on top of the list, then the newest batches are indexed first.

If batches are ordered by batchID, oldest listed on top of the list, then the oldest batches are indexed first.

If batches are ordered some other way, the batches are processed in the order they are in the list.

column6: Fixed issue with overrideepicdt setting not respected in Epic FOS integration.
column7:
By default, the Front Office Scanning client uses the Document Types sent by Epic to determine which scan buttons to display or hide.  This behavior was previously not functioning correctly in prior versions. 

If you want to override this behavior and always display all scan buttons regardless of the Document Types sent by Epic, then you must add *_overrideepicdt="true"_ to the _<FOSCONFIGURATION>* element in the Epic FOS XML configuration file. For example:

<FOSCONFIGURATION overrideepicdt="true" multiplepages="true" multiplepaths="false">

In this case, all configured scan buttons and their associated Document Types will be available for upload, regardless of whether they match a Document Type sent by Epic.

column6: Epic Integration Scanning: Resolved an issue in OnBase that was uncovered when Epic introduced a change to the FHIRCast process.
column7:
This change resolves an issue where the SAS or FOS application would close with an error message shortly after loading. In FOS, the debuglog.txt file would log an error similar to the following: "Error processing FhirCast event. Error received invalid response when sending heartbeat."

The issue has been been resolved. It was discovered due to a change Epic introduced to the FHIRCast process. FHIRCast is used during integrated scanning to maintain a heartbeat connection between Epic and the OnBase scanning application while there is an active scanning session. While Epic's change aligns closely with FHIR industry standards, it exposed a bug in the OnBase code that broke the FHIRCast connection. The issue was not caused by Epic's change but was uncovered because of it.

column6: Compatibility with Edge/Chromium browsers
column7:
Updated FOS to use Webview2 components for compatibility with current browsers.
Due to the deprecation of IE components by Microsoft it was necessary to update the solution with Webview2 in order to be compatible with Edge/Chromium browsers.

WebView2 Runtime Requirement for FOS

The Microsoft Edge WebView2 Runtime is a prerequisite for Front Office Scanning (FOS) if you are using Hyland IdS to log in. If not already installed, WebView2 should be installed using the Evergreen Runtime distribution mode. The Evergreen Runtime allows Windows Server Update Services or an equivalent update service to apply the latest features and security updates.

The Edge WebView2 Runtime can be obtained from the download section of the Microsoft Edge WebView2 website:
https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/webview2/

For additional information on managing WebView2 applications and the WebView2 Runtime, see the Enterprise management of WebView2 Runtimes article on the Microsoft Documentation site:
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/webview2/concepts/enterprise

column6: This issue addresses a bug where Front Office Scanning (FOS) in the SMART on FHIR environment fails to call the Appointment FHIR endpoint in production after an upgrade.
column7:
Fixed Issue: Keyword Retrieval Failure When Using Epic Document IDs

This issue occurred after a recent enhancement, in OnBase, where Appointment ID, Encounter ID, and Document ID provided by Epic were used directly to query and retrieve keywords.

With the latest update, when Epic sends a Document ID for Appointment or Encounter, the system now queries the DocumentReference resource to extract the appropriate IDs. Modifications have been made to ensure proper ID resolution and prevent query failures.

column6: Payment posting, using Smart on FHIR, was broken in OnBase v24, and the issue has been resolved.  Prior to OnBase v24, the Scan Viewer and Image Document Navigator windows were automatically launched when opening batches linked to images in OnBase.
column7:
When using SMART on FHIR in the payment posting (scan viewer), the integration fails to: 
Automatically launch the Image Document Navigator along side the Scan Viewer. Users must select a different page within the Scan Viewer window, to have the Image Document Navigator pop-up.
Automatically close the Scan Viewer when the associated batch is closed. Only occurs when the connection between the Hyland/Hyperspace servers is not established, which is the case when the Image Document Navigator is not displayed.
Prior to organizations upgrading to an OnBase v24+, the Scan Viewer and Image Document Navigator windows were automatically launched when opening batches linked to images in Hyland/OnBase & automatically closed when the associated payment posting batches were closed.
The resolution is that Hyland/OnBase must respond to all GoToPage action request, even if it is the same page number that the viewer is already on, which they were doing before OnBase v24.

column6: Autofill Expansion Issue Resolved with Custom Action Enhancements when 'Disable Field Errors on Form Load' is enabled.
column7:
We have addressed the defect where autofill was not expanding after custom action sets the primary key on load when 'Disable Field Errors on Form Load' is enabled. 
As part of this fix, there are a few edge cases where this setting may not be respected. Specifically, when a custom action is triggered on form load, validation errors may still appear at the top of the form in the following scenarios:

(1) A custom action on load uses “Invalidate Field” followed by setting a value.
(Note: If the value is set before using “Invalidate Field,” the setting will be respected.)

(2) A custom action on load uses “Invalidate Field” in combination with actions that introduce invalid data, such as:
Non-numeric values in numeric fields
Invalid values in select lists (i.e., values not present in the list)
Partially filled masked fields (e.g., incomplete phone numbers)

These configurations can apply when introduced by form designers during development and are not the result of end-user input. There is no functional harm in using such configurations. However, in these cases, the setting will not suppress the resulting validation errors, and they will still appear at the top of the form. We recommend reviewing and adjusting custom actions accordingly to ensure the desired behavior.

column6:
The Home page, Meeting Search page, and Meeting List page will all now respect the “Tabbed by year” and “Upcoming meetings separated” settings and behave the same way. Due to this change, you may notice that your Agenda Online site may be organized differently than before. You can revisit and adjust the settings according to the behaviors described below.

In prior version, you could customize this behavior in the cshtml pages with the ViewBag.MergePastMeetings = true.  We have removed this sett
column7:
Upcoming, Recent, and Year tabs will display in these scenarios:
Home page/List page/Search page - with only “tabbed by year” setting enabled:
- The Upcoming tab and year tabs will all display at the top of the same table.

Home page/List page/Search page - with both “tabbed by year” and “Upcoming meetings separated” settings enabled:
- The Upcoming tab will be the only tab in the first table.  A second table will display year tabs at the top of the table when meetings over multiple years are returned.

Home page/List page/Search page - with only “Upcoming meetings separated” setting enabled:
- The Upcoming tab will be the only tab in the first table.  A second table will display Recent tab at the top of the table.

Home page/List page/Search page - with neither setting enabled:
- A single table with all meetings in the range is displayed with no tabs.

column6: Resolved an issue where customized Agenda Online pages may not display in certain scenarios.
column7: An error was thrown when Agenda Online was initializing, which caused customized page map files to not be used, and the default web pages would instead be used causing confusion. This has been addressed and if this situation occurs, the user trying to launch the Agenda Online page sees a message in the browser that Agenda Online is not yet initialized and has a link to try again. Trying again will result in a new request which the other load balance appserver should pick up and the proper Agenda Online site pages will display.

column6: Resolved a benign error that could occur when validating HL7 Import Processors in OnBase Configuration.

The error would incorrectly state, "Error - No keywords assigned to the MRG segment", even though the Import Process was not configured for merging. This would occur when an HL7 Import Process was previously configured with a Message Action of "Medical Records Chart" set to "Merge Records", but then was changed to have a Message Action of "Patient Record Processor". OnBase Configuration now correctly clears configuration flags when changing the Message Action from "Medical Records Chart" to another Message Action, so that this error no longer occurs on validation of HL7 Import Processes.

column6: Resolved issue with the EDI HL7 Processor failing to process some HL7 messages in flat files.
