column7: Duplicate name verification for document type groups now includes all configured document type groups. Previously a search restriction exists base if DTG was marked as containing medical documents or not.

column6: Added support for special characters <, >, and | in the Auto-Name string of Document Types that are sent to Adobe Sign.
column7: Previously, if certain special characters existed in the Auto-Name string for a document that was sent to Adobe Sign, an error would be displayed in Workflow when attempting to upload the document and it would fail to send to Adobe Sign. This issue has been resolved and you can now use special characters including <,>, and | in the Auto-Name string of the document.

column6: Resolved issue with Front Office Scanning not cleaning up PDF temporary files.
column7: Front Office Scanning now correctly cleans up PDF files in the temporary file location after import.

column6: Corrected the Integration for Epic installer to properly locate the user folder for non-English versions of Windows.
column7: The Integration for Epic installer now successfully locates the user folder and correctly installs the integration for non-English versions of Windows (e.g., Dutch, German, etc.).

column6: The COM Healthcare Web Viewer configuration file now supports environment variables for WebView2 configuration.
column7: The WebView2 temporary file configuration for the COM Healthcare Web Viewer now supports environment variables. Please see the Integration for Epic documentation for more information.

column6: Resolved an issue with Scan Acquisition Server that could cause the UI not to be visible on launch.
column7: Prior to this fix, the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) UI could be rendered off-screen such that it is not visible to the user on launch. This would only occur if SAS was minimized during a previous scanning session and then not properly closed (e.g., using Task Manager or closing Epic before closing SAS). This fix ensures the UI will be visible on the next launch instead of being hidden.

column6: Corrected an issue with OnBase Feature Layers not loading in ESRI "New" Map Viewer.
column7: OnBase Feature Layers were able to be displayed in maps using the ESRI Map Viewer Classic.  When using the “New” Map Viewer from ESRI,  the OnBase feature Layers would not display on those maps.  We have addressed the issue and OnBase feature layers will now display in the New Map Viewer.

column6: File size increased to 100MB
column7: The file has been increased form 20MB to 100MB.

column6: Corrected an issue with IdP login when multiple service locations are configured in the Office Business Application configuration files.
column7: Login via IdP now works correctly when multiple service locations are configured in the Office Business Application configuration files. The login happens automatically when an Office document is opened from the Unity client and the Office Business Applications are installed. The login also happens automatically when an Office document is opened from the Web client and Desktop Host is configured to open the Office Business Applications.

column6: Fixed an issue where the document title being imported was not updating properly when importing multiple email/attachments in the Outlook integration for OnBase.
column7: When importing multiple emails or attachments in the Outlook integration for OnBase, the Import task pane now correctly updates the document title at the top of the Import task pane.

column6: The document title is now updating when importing multiple emails or attachments in the Outlook integration for OnBase.
column7: When importing multiple emails or attachments in the Outlook integration for OnBase, the Import task pane now correctly updates the document title at the top of the Import task pane.

column6: The User Option "Open Microsoft Office documents using the Office Business Application" is now enabled for users with Microsoft Office 64bit installed.
column7: Users can now select or deselect the option to use the Office Business Applications when viewing Office documents. This option was previously disabled for users with Microsoft Office 64bit installed on their local machine.

column6: Autofill keyword sets are functioning as expected when configured on an auto import folder in Outlook.
column7: An issue was preventing autofill keyword sets from triggering when configured on an auto import folder in Outlook. This has been resolved.

column6: The document preview now works as expected when previewing an Office document during import and the Integrated Office Viewer is enabled.
Keyword Value in Error Message is translated
column7: If a user enters an invalid value on a Keyword that has been translated using Interface Translations, the Keyword Value that is displayed in the error message is also translated.

column6: Rectified the Duplication of  keywords with default Keyword Values when navigating an Import Document queue
column7: When doing an Ad Hoc Import in the Web Client for a Document Type with default Keyword Values, if multiple documents are being imported, navigating to a document in the document queue that previously had the value changed for the keyword with a default Keyword Value caused an additional keyword of that type to be added to the document with the default Keyword Value. This behavior has been rectified, and the duplicate keywords with default values are no longer created on documents that had the values changed.

column6: Addressed an issue where a Cascading Data Set whose primary keyword has masking configured with "Extract Mask Characters" does not display the secondary values in the drop-down in the Web Client
column7: A Cascading Data Set whose primary keyword has masking configured with "Extract Mask Characters" now displays the secondary values in the drop-down in the Web Client, and users no longer have to manually fill out the secondary keywords.

column6: Resolved a performance issue related to Batch Indexing and Release of Information
column7: A performance issue related to Saving and Transitioning batches where the 'Append' action has occurred has been resolved.  This specific issue was experienced by customers who also leveraged Release of Information and the Virtual Print Driver listener to assist in releasing content external to OnBase.

column6: Corrected an issue where the "Auto save un-recorded minutes" feature would not always save the minutes.
column7: Minutes will now be auto saved when closing a meeting, starting a meeting, pausing a meeting, and stopping a meeting.

column6: Fixed an issue with unsaved Minutes Text being cleared out before being saved in some cases.
column7: When writing Minutes Text, the expectation is that unsaved Minutes text will remain until it is saved either manually or by the minutes auto save feature. Previously, there were some cases where that unsaved minutes text would get cleared out prematurely when adding motion and vote information, meaning you would lose the pending minutes text you had just written. This issue has been resolved and the unsaved minutes text is no longer being cleared out prematurely.

column6: Fixed an issue with the recording type selection not being visible when "Use Voting" option was enabled after resetting the media recording type.
column7: The media recording type selection is now visible when restarting a meeting after having reset the Recording type to "None" whether or not the "Use Voting" option is enabled.

column6: Fixed an issue when attempting to launch Minutes or Voting Clients using Hyland Identity Service and you could not log in.
column7: There was an issue when using Hyland IdS to log into Minutes or Voting and the Windows user did not have write privileges to the directory, where the Client was installed, the login screen would be blank, and you could not log in.  This has been resolved and allows systems to maintain no write privileges on the program files directory where the Minutes and Voting Clients are installed if desired, and still be able to log in using Hyland IdS.

column6: Added Support for Non-Latin based Characters
column7: In previous versions, non-Latin characters may not be displayed correctly. This issue has been fixed with this version.

column6: FIXED: Office 365 Viewer not loading
column7: An issue was addressed that prevented documents from being displayed with the integrated Office 365 viewer.

column6: Unsupported 'Reports' can no longer be configured as a source.
column7: Reports' are currently not supported by OnBase Apps, but it was possible to configure them as a source. This configuration should no longer be possible.

column6: FIXED: Configuring Workflow requires WorkView Configuration Rights
column7:
Fixed an issue that required administrators to have WorkView configuration rights n order to configure Workflow. 

Before the change, Administrators configuring Workflows without having WorkView configuring rights, may be able to create a new Workflow actions, but the properties for the action may come up blank. They may also encounter an message 'User does not have the necessary client privileges' when saving.

column6: Corrected an issue with grouping being removed in "My Projects" list.
column7: Applied grouping of projects in the "My Projects" list will now always persist for the user.

column6: Fixed Plan Documents sorting in the Plan Review public portal
column7: Sorting by column in the Plan Documents tab of the Plan Review public portal was recently broken in 23.1.  This issue has now been resolved.

column6: Fixed issue where Removed and Added Content comparison lines were low quality
column7: When entering compare mode in the engineering viewer in Plan Review or CAD Services, the Removed and Added Content lines should now be much sharper and clearer than before.

column6: Updates made to Person and Company contacts in a Project XML
column7:
Added the Street Number field as <Person0> and <Company0> for Person and Company contacts to the Project XML contacts section.
These fields can now be used in generated Comment letters.
We also added descriptive attributes to each person and company field in the Project XML, so you can identify easier what the field is for.

We also updated the Project Contacts UI in the Unity Client. We now display the Street Number field and Labels for all the fields, so you can easily identify what the fields are for.

column6: Retrieving an XML document without a style sheet from the Document Management API no longer throws a FileAccessStream exception.
column7: When attempting to retrieve an XML document without a style sheet applied from the Document Management API, an exception was being thrown in the FileAccessStream Platter Management level. This has now been resolved so that retrieving an XML document without a style sheet from the Document Management API no longer throws this error and the file is retrieved successfully.

column6: Enhanced auto-orientation: Seamlessly print landscape or portrait pages Based on page dimensions for clarity.
column7:
Now, when selecting Auto-Orientation during printing, the system intelligently determines whether each page should be printed in Landscape or Portrait mode based on the page dimensions. This functionality mirrors the behavior of the Unity Client, providing users with a seamless experience across platforms.

The Auto-Orientation feature now dynamically renders each page to fit the entire physical page, ensuring optimal utilization of space and clarity during printing. When printing documents containing a mix of landscape and portrait formats, Auto-Orientation will automatically reorient content pages to match the selected physical page format. For example, in a document containing a page of text and an image, physical page remained portrait (from print format), image will get rotated for landscape images & content(Image, Text) will be rendered on the entire physical page to fit. 

Previously, selecting Auto-Orientation in the web client always resulted in the image being printed in Portrait mode, regardless of the page dimensions. 
This enhancement improves the overall printing experience and minimizes the need for manual adjustments.

column6: An issue that resulted in duplication of packets when printing has been resolved
column7: Prior to this fix, a duplicate ROI Packet could be archived into OnBase when the ROI Packet was printed. This duplicate packet was being generated when the "Archive Complete Packet" option was disabled.

column6: Resolved an issue that could result in the Active Requests list displaying duplicate results.
column7: Prior to this fix, the Active Requests list could include duplicate results. This issue would present when the "Refresh" button was pressed multiple times in quick succession.

column6: A correction has been made to the privileges required to Open or Create ROI Requests
column7: Release of Information was incorrectly checking for the 'Chart Search' privilege when users attempted to Open or Create a ROI Request from the ROI layout.  This requirement has been removed as the 'Chart Search' privilege should only be required for users who are creating ROI Requests from Chart Search.

column6: Navigating between charts in the Packet Preview layout no longer requires clicking a chart multiple times to get it to load
column7: An issue has been resolved that required users to click multiple times to navigate between charts in the Packet Preview layout.  This issue was present when the 'Separate by Chart' functionality was used.  With this fix users will be able to navigate between the charts with a single click as intended.

column6: Resolved a performance issue related to saving a Release of Information request
column7: A performance issue related to saving a Release of Information request has been resolved. This specific issue was more apparent when saving ROI Requests that contained a large amount of documents.

column6: A performance issue that could be encountered when saving a ROI Request has been resolved
column7: The resolved performance issue was identified in solutions leveraging the Virtual Print Driver in conjunction with Release of Information.  The issue would present to end-users as delays or the client becoming unresponsive while saving ROI Requests.

column6: Addressed an issue where the check box "create as revision of original document" is not available when attempting to use the Send To file feature on a previous revision of a document where there are multiple renditions of the document.
column7: The checkbox "Create as revision of original document" did not appear if a different rendition exists as a later revision of the document. This issue has been rectified. Now the checkbox "Create as revision of original document" exists in the "Create New Document" window regardless of what other renditions have been created.

column6: Addressed an issue where an unclear error message was displayed for a user attempting to access a locked document.
column7: Addressed an issue when one user has a document locked but another user attempts to import a revision of the same document, an incorrect error message is thrown, which causes confusion for the users. Now the error message given out to the user is: "The Document is locked and cannot be modified. Please try again later."

column6: .EML attachment handling in outlook emails has been corrected.
column7: When users retrieve and send an .EML document as an attachment, an Outlook window with the SEND button enabled will appear on the right-hand side, with the native format of the document pre-selected and grayed out. Upon sending the email, the document attached will be sent and delivered as a .eml file (not a .tif file), streamlining the email workflow.

column6: User can add a Note to a document within Workflow with a blank Secondary Viewer without facing any issue/error.
column7: User can add a Note to a document within Workflow with a blank Secondary Viewer without facing any issue/error.

column6: COLD import text files should display all pages within Unity Client.
column7: COLD import text files should now display all pages within Unity Client. This software correction solves CSFPR-8202

column6: After navigating to another document, document locks do not stay on the initial document when notes or cross-references are open on the Document Information Panel.
column7: An error has been resolved in the Unity Client for document locks working incorrectly. Previously, if a document is open in a viewer along with the notes and cross references in DIP, if the user navigates to a different document, the first document would be locked.

column6: Resolved the bug in the Unity Forms where the cursor remains hidden behind the border and is not seen within the Multiline Text Box control
column7: Users will now be able to find cursor position within multiline textbox control.

column6: Fixed an issue with non legacy theme required fields alignment issue when Label font is increased
column7: User will now have required field (*) properly aligned for non legacy theme as well.

column6: Resolved an issue when attaching a document to a Unity form with an '&' in the file name, the '&' is converted to '&
column7: Users will now be able to see appropriate symbol contained within the file name while uploading it to an attachment control.

column6: Fixed an issue where users were not able to edit unity forms if they had large document ID, i.e., more than 2B documents in their system
column7: Users will now be able to edit and print Unity Forms even if they have large number of documents in their system.

column6: Fixed an issue with Unity Forms designer when opening form templates, filters were being held after they were cleared, and the Open Form Template window was closed until the window was opened and closed again.
column7: Users will now be able to clear filters from Open Forms Template dialog.

column6: Fixed an issue with the "Attachment Type" column which does not update on a Unity Form when re-indexing an attached document in case of shortable attachments
column7: User will now be able to reindex the attached document which will update attachment type column name in case of shortable attachments.

column6: Fixed an issue with masked value field that gets in a loop when Unity Script runs as part of a Custom Action and modifies that field that triggered the Custom Action and prevents form submission.
column7: Users are now able to save Unity Forms without any issue when modifying masked field which runs Unity Script as Custom Action.

column6: Fixed an issue with barcode which was not able to read 32 bit png barcodes.
column7: Forms will now capable of reading 32 bit png images

column6: Fixed an issue where paragraphs  were not displayed correctly on small screen devices.
column7: Now if the Form has paragraph control, it will wrap text for small screen device.

column6: Fixed an issue with unique keyword value when placed within Unity Forms where users were able to submit form without unique keyword value as well and the value was getting saved as blank.
column7:
Fixed an issue with unique keyword value when placed within unity forms where users were able to submit a form without unique keyword value as well and the value was getting saved as blank.
From now on, if a visible form field is configured as unique in the form template but contains non unique value then user will get a popup with 2 options asking "One or More Unique Keyword fields does not have unique values. Do you want to save the form?"
If the user selects Yes then the form gets saved with duplicate value; whereas if the user selects No, then the user gets option to modify / edit non unique value to make it unique.

Note if the field is hidden and is configured as unique in the form template but contains non unique value, then duplicate value directly gets saved.

column6: Fixed an issue with custom actions "Add repeater row" when trigger from within a repeater section
column7: User should now be able to add repeater row using custom action execution from within repeating section.

column6: Fixed an issue with unity script which failed to delete repeater row or table row.
column7: User should now be able to delete repeater rows or table rows using Unity Script.

column6: Fixed an issue with open form template dialog which failed to display existing list of template
column7: Users will now be able to view list of templates when they try to open form template from Open form template dialog

column6: Fixed an issue with Unity forms buttons label which gets truncated if the label has special characters like ("", @, ^<,*)
column7: Unity Form button label is now displayed completely without being truncated if it contains special characters like ("", @, ^<,*)

column6: Fixed an issue with form creation date inside Form Designer | Open Form Template View | Advanced | Revisions tab when the Unity Client culture is set to es-co
column7: Instead of showing default date 01/01/0001, user is now able to view correct form creation date inside Forms Designer for Spanish culture.

column6: Resolved the bug where Repeater Section with a custom label throws an error
column7:
Resolved an issue where the Repeating Section with a Custom Label throws an error when opening 22.1 form template in a later version.
Users will now be able to open a 22.1 form template in a later version without any exception even if a Repeating Section has a Custom Label.

column6: Resolved an issue where a thin yellow bar appeared instead of the full yellow notification message bar for custom actions that invalidates a field.
column7: Users will now be able to see the full yellow notification bar with complete invalidation message for custom actions that invalidates a field present in a form

column6: Selection of dates from Date Picker to access reports from Distribution service is now fixed.
column7: Changing the region format to English (UK) in the Unity Management Console, will allow users to access Reports from the Distribution Service without encountering the errors present in earlier version. Start Date/End Date date pickers can successfully select dates where at least on of the dates has a day that is more than 12.

column6: Addressed incorrect pop up showing up when a user who   does not have the modify keywords privilege attempts to view the keywords of a document.
column7: If the user doesn’t have the necessary privileges, the keywords will be shown as read-only, and there will not be any message popups.

column6: Thumbnail panel is no longer displayed when thumbnail pages are disabled
column7: Addressed an issue where Thumbnail panel beside Document Viewer in Web Client is still displayed when Thumbnail Page option is unchecked in configuration.

column6: Cross References not working on text documents via double click has been fixed
column7: Double clicking  on text documents now displays the cross-referenced document(s) correctly.

column6: Document Date Label wasnt being respected in the Web Client import dialog
column7: Web Client import dialog now displays the label configured in the "Document Date Label" setting at the doc type level

column6: Rectified incorrect duplication of MIKG fields
column7: In the Web Client, when selecting the first highlighted document on an import hitlist after navigating to a different document on the hitlist, MIKG fields are being duplicated. This behavior has been rectified.

column6: Import non image file format documents with Virtual Print Driver
column7: Users were unable to import documents with Virtual Print Driver for documents that do not have image file format as Default  for the chosen document type  within the Web client. Now this has been rectified.

column6: Addressed an issue with importing large documents
column7: When importing larger documents import button was available prior to file being automatically set. This has been rectified.

column6: Addressed an issue where user is unable to import PDF documents into folder within the Web Client without selecting the Document Type first.
column7: Users are now  able to import a file to a folder, and Import button is no longer greyed out when tries to  without selecting a document type first.

column6: Addressed an issue when in the Batch Indexing Context and viewing a document, when attempting to perform a Send To | Create New Document, the feature is unavailable from the context menu.
column7: Now in Batch Indexing, when a user right-clicks an image or text document from the batch that has a Document Type and selects the Send To option, Create New Document is one of the options presented. When Create New Document is selected, the user is able to create a copy of the page being indexed as a separate document.

column6: Rectified an issue with moving the Notes/Annotations
column7: Addressed an issue where moving the Notes/Annotations around in a document auto selects the documents.

column6: After selecting the redaction button once redactions can be added one after another
column7: Now it is not necessary to re-select the redaction button each time a new redaction is added to a document.

column6: Addressed an issue in Web Client when opened in the Microsoft Edge Mobile browser on Android devices.
Addressed an issue with document separation that caused session loss
column7: Resolved an issue with document separation in Workflow that caused session loss. With this fix, a user's session will not be terminated on performing document separation from Workflow.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with Web Client workflow after executing an ad-hoc task
column7: Fixed an issue within the Web Client workflow where a related document was getting opened on a separate window after executing an ad-hoc task. Now, related item is re-displayed only if it is NOT in a separate window.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with Call REST API configuration
column7: Fixed an error when calling REST API from Workflow. This fix enables users to call REST API from Workflow as before without an error where an invalid request content is created.

column6: Resolved issue when using the 'Create PDF/Tiff' within a system Workflow task on a Word document creating a PDF, some of the bookmarks are not carried over on the PDF.
column7: Update the code to allow 'Create PDF/Tiff' within a system Workflow task on a Word document creating a PDF, all bookmarks are carried over on the PDF.

column6: Workflow action, Set Property to Repository Value, now supports all work items
column7: Workflow action, Set Property to Repository Value, now supports all work items and not just documents. User is now able to configure task workflow action, Set Property to Repository Value, for all work items.

column6: Addressed a performance issue with OnBase Studio when setting up the Send Notification action in Workflow
column7: Fixed a performance issue with OnBase Studio when setting up the Send Notification action in Workflow.  Previously, a database with a large amount of users caused OnBase Studio to freeze when selecting a user from the Type drop-down list in the Recipients tab of the Notifications set up.

column6: Addressed an issue with Workflow logging for security overrides
column7: Fixed an issue with Workflow logging for security overrides that often caused the application server to terminate unexpectedly.

column6: Addressed an issue with User Group Privileges
column7: Fixed an issue where users with no rights to view revisions were still able to see latest revision when viewing from Workflow.

column6: Addressed the Workflow autofill execution slowness issue
column7: Improved Workflow autofill execution with enhanced functionality for "Expand Autofill" and "Check Autofill." Users will now experience improvement in execution time  when expanding selections, and improved validation checks ensure that autofilled data is consistent and reliable.

column6: Addressed an issue with Workflow in Unity Client that involved multiple workflow queues configured "Only use selected filters"
column7: Fixed an issue where Unity Client threw an exception when there were numerous workflow queues that were configured to "Only use selected filters" and the filters assigned exceeded 1000 in number while using Oracle database.

column6: Addressed issues with document focus
column7: Fixed document focus issue after an adhoc task was executed and document list was refreshed within Workflow. Document will remain in focus when the document is not moved to another queue after an adhoc task is executed .

column6: Addressed an issue with scroll bars in the Unity Client
column7: Resolved an issue with scroll bars in the Unity Client. Previously, if there were more Approval Paths than could fit in the Unity Client window, you would be unable to see the additional paths irrespective of how you resized the window. User are now able to scroll through the entire list of approval path within Unity Client.

column6: FIXED: The Full-Text Indexing Service (for WorkView) will not stay started, "Attempting to access an invalid datasource"
column7: Fixed an issue where the full text indexing service stopped after hitting the polling interval with the error message "Attempting to access an invalid datasource"

column6: FIXED: "Unkown Error has Occured" when opening an object from a class with security attributes in Workflow.
column7: If a shared class had security attributes configured, the user may not have been able to open an object in Workflow unless a WorkView Filters for the class was executed first.

column6: FIXED: Headers for filters get out of alignment.
column7: The issue was introduced with the enhancements for accessibility and happens when tabbing through the filter header which may scroll, but the body of the table does not.

column6: FIXED: 'Clear All' button on embedded filter not working.
column7: Fixed an issue where the 'Clear All' button on column headers on an embedded filter didn't do anything.

column6: FIXED: 'First Object' navigation button jumps to 2nd object instead of 1st.
column7: Fixed an issue where when clicking the 'First Object' button on an open object navigated to the 2nd object in the list instead of the first.

column6: FIXED: Delete Document button on folder control throws an exception.
column7: Fixed an issue where clicking on the 'Delete' button on a document in a folder would throw an error.

column6: FIXED: Filter with 6 or more display columns that point to the same local/linked external class via different relationships fails to display certain column data.
column7: The issue would occur when a filter had display columns with 6 or more relationships to the same local/linked external class. In this case, data for some columns may show up as blank. After the implementation of the fix, the data will now show correctly again.

column6: FIXED: Unable to pick an object from a lookup filter for a filter backed dataset.
column7: This issue affected datasets that were backed by a Filter and the dataset was configured to display as filter. In this case, when the user opened the lookup filter and picked an object, instead of setting the attribute to the value, the value would remain blank.

column6: FIXED: Errors is thrown and the Unity Client may crash when creating an association object on an external class.
column7: The issue occured when the embedded filter was set to automatically save the object when adding a new association, but the user did not have rights to modify the ODBC external class.

column6: Fixed: Unity Client becomes unresponsive when modifying a parent/child dataset on a WorkView object in the Workflow Inbox.
column7: The issue occurred when setting a parent child datset on a WorkView object. This only happened when the object was opened in the Workflow inbox.

column6: FIXED: 'One or more items(s) have been deleted' message is shown after the delete was canceled.
column7: When deleting items from an embedded filter, but then choosing to cancel the action, the user would still receive a delete confirmation. No object would be deleted, but the message was confusing.

column6: FIXED: Deletion Issue with Workview Object in Workflow
column7: Users were unable to delete a WorkView object using a Unity Script while in Workflow after upgrading to version 22.1. The error message displayed was 'Failed to execute ad hoc task' and further diagnostics indicated that 'One or more objects were not deleted due to being locked by another user.'

column6: Fixed: Delayed Save Functionality in Object Viewer
column7: The Object Viewer was experiencing delays when saving new objects or modifying existing ones. This issue affected the Save, Save & Close, and Save & New functions. This defect has been resolved. The Object Viewer now saves objects promptly, restoring the performance to match previous versions and ensuring a smooth user experience.

column6: WorkView Attribute Validation Issue with Filter Dataset
column7:
Description: In a View with an attribute set using a WorkView Filter dataset, an "Invalid dataset value" error occurred when more than 50 results shared the same value. This issue arose both when validating by manually typing in the value and when selecting it from a Lookup Filter. In previous versions (EP3), manual typing would fail, but selecting via the Lookup would validate correctly.

Impact: Users were unable to set proper values within the dataset, forcing them to allow values outside the dataset to proceed, thus compromising data integrity.

This defect has been resolved, restoring the functionality to validate values correctly within the dataset, even when there are more than 50 results with the same value.

column6: Fixed: Object Viewer Freezes for a few seconds when executing a workflow action.
column7: Fixed an issue where the Object Viewer would freeze for 3-5 seconds when executing a Workflow task.

column6: FIXED: Purged objects referenced as related objects can cause triggers to fail.
column7: The issue occured when a relationship referenced an object that was deleted and eventually purged. When executing a trigger, the following exception would be thrown: Error processing triggers for class Child, objectid 173943: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.

column6: Fixed Focus Loss in Required
column7: An issue was resolved where required fields displayed via the 'Display Object for Input' action would lose focus after the user initially clicked into the field. This behavior occurred only upon the first click into the field.

column6: FIXED: Screens are missing from Master Security.
column7: Screens were added to the Master Security dialog making it easier to manage access. Before, user groups could only be added individually to each screen.

column6: Reduced file size for CEFSharp
column7: Recent upgrades of the CEFSharp browser control caused additional files to be added making deployment sizes larger. The size has been reduced again.

column6: FIXED: Unity Script backed WorkView Action does not throw an error message when user does not have permissions to update attributes
column7:
When running a Unity script behind a WorkView action (IWorkViewExecuteActionButtonScript), the script fails to populate a relationship attribute properly in versions 21.1 and above. . No error is thrown, misleading users to believe the action succeeded, which could lead to data integrity issues.
The defect has been resolved to ensure that an appropriate error message is displayed in the logs and to the user when the script fails to set an attribute value due to insufficient privileges. This error message will be similar to those provided when other parts of the API access restricted attributes, thus restoring normal behavior and preventing misleading information about the action's success.

column6: FIXED: Unity client may crash when dragging and dropping an email attachment to a WV object.
column7: The issue occurred when dragging an attachment from an email directly a WorkView object to attach it to the static folder. This issue was fixed.

column6: FIXED: Document import from WorkView object shows Document Types user doesn't have rights to
column7: Fixed in issue that allowed users to upload document types that they had no rights to. After the fix, the document type selection will only show documents that users have rights to upload documents to.

column6: FIXED: Workflow configured for WorkView objects is leaving sessions in WAITING status on Oracle database and not cleaning up sessions.
column7: Before the fix, this could cause an an accumulation of sessions that can cause errors "Pooled connection request timed out" and also performance issues for OnBase solutions. This has been fixed.

column6: FIXED: Memory Leak in Web Client.
column7: Fixed an issue where memory was not always released after viewing objects. This could, if the browser was used for very long times without ever restarting, lead to slowness or the Web Client becoming unresponsive.

column6: FIXED: Unable to set the length of an alphanumeric attribute to 256.
column7: Fixed an issue that prevented the length for an alphanumeric attribute to be set to exactly 256.

column6: FIXED: Unable to set a name and associated class for a user defined filter when using Firefox as the browser.
column7: Fixed an issue that prevented user from entering a name and picking a class for the filter. This only occured when using Firefox as the browser.

column6: FIXED: Data for an encrypted attribute may be stored unencrypted in the database.
column7:
Fixed an issue that removed encryption for an encrypted attribute.

This occurred when a new encrypted attribute was created, then modified (i.e. the name was changed), before the configuration was saved. If that corrupt configuration was exported, it could be imported into another system. If that happens, data stored in the database would be stored in clear text.

column6: FIXED: Add to display message displays an error in the Web Client.

After the correction, the in the action specified message will be displayed while other errors (i.e. a misconfigured rule or action) would display an 'Internal server error' in the client, while the full error message is logged to the diagnostics console.

column6: FIXED: CARS rule configuration getting removed from database when deleting a view and not giving error while importing that application to another repository.
column7:
CARS rule/s configuration getting removed from database when deleting a view and not giving error while importing that application to another repository.

Before Change when class having multiple views configured with CARS rules config, and a user deletes at least one view from the class and leaving one or more views that still have CARS rules configured, CARS configuration data for the deleted view(s) used to be orphaned and stays in the database in JSON format (Table - hsi.rmresource.resourcedata) which produce error ("The given key was not present in the dictionary") while exporting the application to other repository.

After Change for deleted View(s) we are modifying the JSON and removing CARS configuration for the deleted view(s) from Database. This ensures no further CARS configuration Orphan records will be created. So during Import to other repository it should not give ("The given key was not present in the dictionary") error. Additionally For customers who have existing orphan records in their Database, Stand alone utility WV component Doctor tool has been enhanced to support the removal of CARS configuration Orphan records from the Database. This should ensure Import will work with out any issues.(As this is stand alone utility, it is not part of the executable generated from the daily build process. The Tech support can reach out to the WorkView Team to create the executable and place it in a common path.)

column6: WorkView CreatedBy & RevisionBy names getting resolved to complete name, instead of getting truncated to first 30 characters
column7:
Prior to the change, WorkView only supported username in CreatedBy & RevisionBy having a length of 30, truncating all the other letters of the name after the first 30 characters. Due to this, users having more than 30 characters of username performing operations in WorkView objects would not see their complete name in Filter Results, Triggers, Calculated Attributes & other macros using CreatedBy & RevisionBy fields.
With this change, going forward across all the features of WorkView showing CreatedBy & RevisionBy will show complete name as supported by OnBase.

column6: Fixed WorkView Client Issue - Filter result shows wrong data when we come back to all filter from a user constraint filter search

Fixed an issue for WorkView client where upon navigating back from user constraint filter search to all filter it continued to show the wrong result (result of previous user constraint).
When using multiple filters, there was an issue where entry constraints were not being cleared when switching to the "All Data" view. For example, if the user initially saw 5 results, applied a filter that showed only 2 results, and then selected "All Data" again, it would still display just 2 results instead of resetting to 5.
When the user switches to a new filter, the previous filter's constraints are cleared to show the correct data. This fix ensures that all data displays properly when changing filters.

column6: Resolved issue with escaped return and new line sequences in Base64 not being handled properly by the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor.
column7: An issue was resolved with the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor not handling escaped return and new line sequences in Base64. Including these characters in Base64 is called line wrapping and would be \r\n (CRLF) characters, which would be escaped as \X0D\\X0A\ in an HL7 message. See the HL7 documentation for best practices regarding line wrapping.

column6: Documents imported via the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor are now stored on multiple disk group mass storage copies.

column6: Resolved an issue with the keep alive configuration for the HL7 Listener and HL7 Auto Sender.
column7: The HL7KeepAliveSeconds keep alive INI configuration setting did not work as intended and has been replaced by the HL7KeepAliveTimeout INI setting. The HL7KeepAliveTimeout INI setting works the same as the KeepAliveTimeout setting in the Hyland Message Engine configuration file. Setting to 0 disables keep alive traffic from being sent. Setting to any positive integer defines the number of minutes between each keep alive packet sent by the HL7 service.

column6: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Advanced Document Import Processor creating empty documents.
column7: Prior to this fix, when a Document Type was configured with a default non-image File Format (e.g., PDF, Text, etc.), or was mapped in the HL7 Advanced Document Import Process to a non-image File Format, it was possible for the HL7 Listener to create 0kb files in the disk groups. This only occurred when using a Path Reference in the field mapped to $$FILEDATA. This could occur when either, A) no file path was specified in the HL7 message for the field mapped to $$FILEDATA, or B) there was no file in the directory specified by the file path in the field mapped to $$FILEDATA.

column6: Enhanced auto-orientation: Seamlessly print landscape or portrait pages Based on page dimensions for clarity.
column7:
Now, when selecting Auto-Orientation during printing, the system intelligently determines whether each page should be printed in Landscape or Portrait mode based on the page dimensions. This functionality mirrors the behavior of the Unity Client, providing users with a seamless experience across platforms.

The Auto-Orientation feature now dynamically renders each page to fit the entire physical page, ensuring optimal utilization of space and clarity during printing. When printing documents containing a mix of landscape and portrait formats, Auto-Orientation will automatically reorient content pages to match the selected physical page format. For example, in a document containing a page of text and an image, physical page remained portrait (from print format), image will get rotated for landscape images & content(Image, Text) will be rendered on the entire physical page to fit. 

Previously, selecting Auto-Orientation in the web client always resulted in the image being printed in Portrait mode, regardless of the page dimensions. 
This enhancement improves the overall printing experience and minimizes the need for manual adjustments.

column6: Resolved Issue with Smart on FHIR DefPop Integration for Epic and One Epic, Multiple OnBase Customers
column7:
An issue has been resolved in the DefPop integration where Document IDs were not being correctly prefixed with the facility identifier when updating deficiency status through the UpdateScanSignatures2 Epic API.

While the One Epic, Multiple OnBase mode requires Document IDs to follow the format HY.<facility_id>.<document_id>, the DefPop workflow was only sending the numeric ID, causing Epic to be unable to locate the document and resulting in failures.

This behavior had previously been implemented for FOS and SAS integrations but was missing from DefPop. The integration now correctly applies the facility prefix when configured, ensuring successful document lookup and update within Epic.

Workarounds include switching to new Esri experience builder apps with the Hyland Content Widget, 
or disabling a logging setting in the GISWebApi web.config file: 
<add name="ScriptExceptionHandler.ashx_*" path="ScriptExceptionHandler.ashx" verb="*" type="Hyland.Applications.Web.Handlers.ScriptExceptionHandler,Hyland.Applications.Web" preCondition="integratedMode,runtimeVersionv4.0" />

column6: Compatibility with Edge/Chromium browsers
column7:
Updated FOS to use Webview2 components for compatibility with current browsers.
Due to the deprecation of IE components by Microsoft it was necessary to update the solution with Webview2 in order to be compatible with Edge/Chromium browsers.

WebView2 Runtime Requirement for FOS

The Microsoft Edge WebView2 Runtime is a prerequisite for Front Office Scanning (FOS) if you are using Hyland IdS to log in. If not already installed, WebView2 should be installed using the Evergreen Runtime distribution mode. The Evergreen Runtime allows Windows Server Update Services or an equivalent update service to apply the latest features and security updates.

The Edge WebView2 Runtime can be obtained from the download section of the Microsoft Edge WebView2 website:
https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/webview2/

For additional information on managing WebView2 applications and the WebView2 Runtime, see the Enterprise management of WebView2 Runtimes article on the Microsoft Documentation site:
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/webview2/concepts/enterprise

column6: Fix for Documents Imported containing unexpected non-base64 encoded characters

column6: Fixed issue with overrideepicdt setting not respected in Epic FOS integration.
column7:
By default, the Front Office Scanning client uses the Document Types sent by Epic to determine which scan buttons to display or hide.  This behavior was previously not functioning correctly in prior versions. 

If you want to override this behavior and always display all scan buttons regardless of the Document Types sent by Epic, then you must add *_overrideepicdt="true"_ to the _<FOSCONFIGURATION>* element in the Epic FOS XML configuration file. For example:

<FOSCONFIGURATION overrideepicdt="true" multiplepages="true" multiplepaths="false">

In this case, all configured scan buttons and their associated Document Types will be available for upload, regardless of whether they match a Document Type sent by Epic.

column6: Integration for Epic: Resolved an issue where payment posting using Smart on FHIR failed to launch the Image Document Navigator alongside the Scan Viewer when users opened batches linked to images in OnBase.

- Automatically launch the Image Document Navigator alongside the Scan Viewer. Users had to select a different page within the Scan Viewer for the Image Document Navigator to be displayed.
- Automatically close the Scan Viewer when the associated batch is closed. This issue occurred only when the connection between the OnBase and Hyperspace servers had not been established, which was the case when the Image Document Navigator was not displayed.
This issue has ben resolved, restoring the functionality in place prior to OnBase Foundation 24.1. The Scan Viewer and Image Document Navigator windows are now automatically launched when users open batches linked to images in OnBase, and they are automatically closed when the associated payment posting batches are closed.

column6: Autofill Expansion Issue Resolved with Custom Action Enhancements when 'Disable Field Errors on Form Load' is enabled.
column7:
We have addressed the defect where autofill was not expanding after custom action sets the primary key on load when 'Disable Field Errors on Form Load' is enabled. 
As part of this fix, there are a few edge cases where this setting may not be respected. Specifically, when a custom action is triggered on form load, validation errors may still appear at the top of the form in the following scenarios:

(1) A custom action on load uses “Invalidate Field” followed by setting a value.
(Note: If the value is set before using “Invalidate Field,” the setting will be respected.)

(2) A custom action on load uses “Invalidate Field” in combination with actions that introduce invalid data, such as:
Non-numeric values in numeric fields
Invalid values in select lists (i.e., values not present in the list)
Partially filled masked fields (e.g., incomplete phone numbers)

These configurations can apply when introduced by form designers during development and are not the result of end-user input. There is no functional harm in using such configurations. However, in these cases, the setting will not suppress the resulting validation errors, and they will still appear at the top of the form. We recommend reviewing and adjusting custom actions accordingly to ensure the desired behavior.

This defect caused disruptions in user experience, preventing seamless transitions between forms. The fix ensures that the window context is properly maintained, allowing users to navigate without encountering this error.

column6: Fix for Field Validation Errors Displaying on Form Load.
column7: Resolved an issue where field validation errors were incorrectly displayed upon form load, even when the 'Disable Field Errors on Form Load' checkbox was checked. This fix ensures a smoother user experience by preventing error messages from appearing at the start.

column6: Fixed user behavior to view primary items in a Workflow inbox in the Web Client.
column7: The user can view the primary item in a Workflow Life Cycle inbox within the Web Client  without having the Document Type rights to the document type the Workflow is assigned to.

column6: Fix for Document Viewer Right-Click Functionality within WorkView in Web Client.
column7: Addressed a defect in the Document Viewer within WorkView where right-clicking on a document did not display the expected options, including keywords, history, re-index, and more. This fix enhances user experience by restoring full functionality to the right-click context menu, allowing users to efficiently access important document management features.

column6: A crash bug occurring on Verification Report generation for Autofill Processor has been fixed.
column7: When presented with keyword data of certain combinations of length and spacing, the Verification Report generator would crash the OnBase Client. The root cause for these crashes has been eradicated.

column6: Rotation made in Document Review is carried over in Document Separation and when it goes to Indexing
column7: Rotation made in Document Review is shown/carried over in Document Separation and when it goes to Indexing. Those changes are still there/respected and applied.

column6: Resolved issue where Unity Client's "Acquire" scan preview feature drops pages using HP ScanJet Pro 2500 f1 scanner in some scenarios.
column7: Resolved issue where Unity Client's "Acquire" scan preview feature drops pages using HP ScanJet Pro 2500 f1 scanner in some scenarios.

column6: Solved issue where Brainware Integration could perform poorly when servicing higher backlogs.
column7: Solved issue where Brainware Integration could perform poorly when servicing higher backlogs.

column6: Users are able to save merged PDF/Image documents in Document Separation
column7: Users are able to save merged PDF/Image documents in Document Separation

column6: Addressed issues with the Workflow inbox column filter value retention in the Unity Client
column7: Resolved an issue where the Workflow inbox column filter value would be removed when switching between Workflow queues or modifying the column width within the inbox. The Workflow inbox column filter now retains its value until the user clears it.

column6: Resolved Extraneous Startup Error Messages
column7: Resolved extraneous log messages that may occur during Application Server start.

column6: Resubmitted deficiencies on PDFs will fail to burn after they are completed
column7: An issue that prevented re-submitted deficiencies from burning has been resolved.  This issue only affected deficiencies that had been placed on a PDF, completed by a Physician and then re-submitted for completion again by an Reanalyst.

column6: Resolved an issue related to Missing Information deficiencies incorrectly generating Orphaned Note Burn Failures
column7: Prior to this change an Orphan Note Burn Failure was being created if the Medical Records Analysis Server attempted to burn the document an Analyst was in the process of adding a Missing Information deficiency to.

column6: Users should be able to login to the Unity Client via the same Citrix Server after the initial successful login
column7:
Fixed RFA - CSFPR-8179. 
Users should be able to login to the Unity Client via the same Citrix Server after the initial successful login

column6: Introduced a new checkbox in Global Settings to enable or disable the display of invalid field errors during form loading.
column7: A new checkbox, labeled “Disable Field Errors on Form Load,” has been introduced in the Global Settings. This setting allows administrators to configure a feature flag that enables or disables the display of invalid field errors when the form loads. By default, the checkbox is unchecked.

column6: Integration Issue with certain .aspx files
column7: During the upgrade to OnBase version 24.1.7.1000, we encountered an issue with the hfsCustomQuery.aspx endpoint, which now returns an HTML-encoded response instead of XML. This change was introduced with JIRA CCT-2849. Additionally, certain .aspx files (e.g., hfsCustomQuery.aspx, hfsNotice.aspx, hfsStmt.aspx) were missing from the new installation, but this issue has been fixed.

column6: Addressed a defect related to special characters when used as static symbols for masking fields in Unity Forms.
column7: The defect has been addressed so that the encoding and decoding of special characters work as expected when used as static symbols for masking fields in Unity Forms.

column6: Fixed an issue for cascading dataset when unity forms fails to populate child values of a cascading dataset.
column7:
Fixed an issue for cascading dataset when unity forms fails to populate child values of a cascading dataset.

User should be able to see the fetched cascading value for parent and its child dataset when added as a field in unity form

column6: Fixed: Customer keywords, and Distribution Options weren't saved while creating new recipient
column7: While creating new recipients, Customer keywords, and Distribution Options are now getting saved (or getting saved as blank/null).

column6: Resolved a condition where creating a rendition of a document does not set the Full-Text status correctly.
column7: When a document is in a committed scan queue, creating a rendition of the document could set the Full Text status incorrectly. This can cause the new rendition to not be processed by Full-Text. This has been resolved and the correct status is now set, allowing renditions to be processed.

column6: Corrected an issue with OnBase Feature Layers not loading in ESRI "New" Map Viewer.
column7: OnBase Feature Layers were able to be displayed in maps using the ESRI Map Viewer Classic.  When using the “New” Map Viewer from ESRI,  the OnBase feature Layers would not display on those maps.  We have addressed the issue and OnBase feature layers will now display in the New Map Viewer.

column6: Corrected an issue running Unity scripts during importing in the Outlook integration
column7: An issue occurred when running Unity scripts during import in the Outlook integration. This has been corrected and import scripts now function as expected.

column6: When opening a shared Form, user is no longer prompted to enter Windows authentication.
column7: Popup for Windows authentication no longer appears appear when opening shared Form URL.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with the scrollbar in Workflow within Unity Client
column7: Resolved an issue where the scrollbar was not displayed when there were more approval paths than what could fit within the Unity Client window. With this fix, users are now be able to scroll through the entire list of approval path within Unity client.

column6: FIXED: Configuring Workflow requires WorkView Configuration Rights
column7:
Fixed an issue that required administrators to have WorkView configuration rights in order to configure Workflow. 

Before the change, Administrators configuring Workflows without having WorkView configuring rights, may be able to create a new Workflow actions, but the properties for the action may come up blank. They may also encounter an message 'User does not have the necessary client privileges' when saving.

column6: Partial purging does not create data integrity in Oracle anymore
column7: Partial purging successfully rolls back all related table data if the purge operation is cancelled.

column6: Resolved a performance issue related to saving a Release of Information request
column7: A performance issue related to saving a Release of Information request has been resolved. This specific issue was more apparent when saving ROI Requests that contained a large amount of documents.

column6: Resolved issue with the Document Corrections form not displaying in some environments.
column7: When clicking Add Corrections in the Healthcare Web Viewer integrated with Epic, the Document Corrections form will be displayed alongside the document being viewed. Prior to this fix, in some environments, the form would not display due to environmental factors which could indicate to the Healthcare Web Viewer it was being viewed on a touch screen device (even if it was not a touch screen device). This would cause the Healthcare Web Viewer to incorrectly hide the Document Corrections form.

column6: Resolved issue with the EDI HL7 Processor failing to process some HL7 messages in flat files.
column7: An issue was discovered which could cause the EDI Processor to fail to process specific HL7 messages due to issues with the buffer. The buffer was improved to better parse HL7 messages and resolve the issues causing the messages to fail.

column6: Resolved an issue against date & time keyword within a workflow
column7: Resolved an issue against date & time keyword within a workflow not inheriting keywords when Import Ad-Hoc is used where server and client had different culture.

column6: Addressed AFKS using Workview Filter issues
column7: AutoFill query is executed twice when expanding an AutoFill against a WorkView Filter, this issue has been addressed and resolved.

column6: The HL7 Medical Records Chart Processor now correctly stores the first instance of a repeating MRN field.
column7: When processing HL7 messages with repeating fields mapped to MRN Chart Data Field, the HL7 Listener Medical Records Chart Processor will always use the first instance of the repeating field. Prior to this fix, the HL7 Listener was using the first instance of the MRN repeating field to link the chart to, wowever, the last instance of the MRN would be imported as metadata on the chart (medrecnumber in the chart table). Therefore, we had conflicting data in the database, with the chart being linked to a the correct MRN in the database but having different MRN metadata listed on the chart. This software change ensures that the chart is correctly linked to the first instance of an MRN repeating field and that the same MRN is added as metadata to the chart. All other chart data fields (e.g., MPI, CSN, etc.) use the last instance of a repeating field, but the MRN is the exception to this behavior, by design.

column6: Resolved race condition which could cause HL7 messages to fail to automatically retry when the HL7 Auto Sender encounters a connection issue.
column7: This fix further improves the resiliency of the HL7 Auto Sender when it encounters a connection issue with the Export Destination. The HL7 Auto Sender can now successfully automatically retry a message if the connection was interrupted or closed on the first attempt.

column6: Addressed an issue where a Cascading Data Set whose primary keyword has masking configured with "Extract Mask Characters" does not display the secondary values in the drop-down in the Web Client
column7: A Cascading Data Set whose primary keyword has masking configured with "Extract Mask Characters" now displays the secondary values in the drop-down in the Web Client, and users no longer have to manually fill out the secondary keywords.

column6: Resolved a performance issue related to Batch Indexing and Release of Information
column7: A performance issue related to Saving and Transitioning batches where the 'Append' action has occurred has been resolved.  This specific issue was experienced by customers who also leveraged Release of Information and the Virtual Print Driver listener to assist in releasing content external to OnBase.

column6: Addressed an issue in the Diagnostic Console when a document is opened from the Revisions/Renditions dialog.
column7: The user can click on a revision in the Revision/Rendition dialog, and the associated document is correctly displayed.

column6: Addressed an issue where an unclear error message was displayed for a user attempting to access a locked document.
column7: Addressed an issue when one user has a document locked but another user attempts to import a revision of the same document, an incorrect error message is thrown, which causes confusion for the users. Now the error message given out to the user is: "The Document is locked and cannot be modified. Please try again later."

column6: .EML attachment handling in outlook emails has been corrected.
column7: When users retrieve and send an .EML document as an attachment, an Outlook window with the SEND button enabled will appear on the right-hand side, with the native format of the document pre-selected and grayed out. Upon sending the email, the document attached will be sent and delivered as a .eml file (not a .tif file), streamlining the email workflow.

column6: Resolved the bug where Repeater Section with a custom label throws an error
column7:
Resolved an issue where the Repeating Section with a Custom Label throws an error when opening 22.1 form template in a later version.
Users will now be able to open a 22.1 form template in a later version without any exception even if a Repeating Section has a Custom Label.

column6: Fixed an issue with form creation date inside form designer -> open form template view -> advance -> revisions tab when unity client culture is set to es-co
column7:
Fixed an issue with form creation date under open form template view -> advance -> revisions tab when unity client culture is set to es-co.
Instead of showing default date 01/01/0001 user will now be able to view correct form creation date inside forms designer for Spanish culture

column6: Addressed the Workflow autofill execution slowness issue
column7: Improved Workflow autofill execution with enhanced functionality for "Expand Autofill" and "Check Autofill." Users will now experience improvement in execution time  when expanding selections, and improved validation checks ensure that autofilled data is consistent and reliable.

column6: Fixed an issue where Unity Client displayed an error when there were numerous workflow queues configured to "Only use selected filters"
column7:
Fixed an issue where Unity Client displayed an error when there were numerous workflow queues configured to "Only use selected filters" and there were filters assigned exceeding 1000 while using the Oracle database. 
Users will not encounter any exception while using Oracle database having workflow queues that are configured to "Only use selected filters" and there are filters count exceeding 1000 .

column6: Resolved an issue where customized Agenda Online pages may not display in certain scenarios.
column7: An error was thrown when Agenda Online was initializing which caused customized page map files to not be used and the default web pages would instead be used causing confusion. The workaround for this was to reset IIS then the custom pages loaded properly.  This was seen at times when scheduled maintenance was being performed on a Hyland Cloud hosted system.  This has been addressed and if this situation occurs, the user trying to launch the Agenda Online page sees a message in the browser that Agenda Online is not yet initialized and has a link to try again. Trying again will result in a new request which the other load balance appserver should pick up and the proper Agenda Online site pages will display.

column6: Addressed an issue with re-indexing documents with Custom File Format.
column7: Users were unable to re-index custom image file format to regular image file format. Now documents can be reconciled to the standard/built-in file format after being imported to a Custom File Format.

column6: Resolved a condition where documents imported by a COLD process would have incorrect hit highlighting.
column7: Documents containing whitespace compression that are imported into OnBase using a COLD process and indexed with OnBase Full-Text no longer have incorrect hit highlighting. For documents exhibiting this problem, re-indexing the document is necessary.

column6: Viewing cross-references and native PDFs via the Healthcare Web Viewer hosted in DeficiencyPop now work as expected.
column7: When the Healthcare Web Viewer is launched from the DeficiencyPop IIS application, there were issues with viewing cross-references, as well as viewing Native PDFs from the toolbar button. These issues have been resolved and now work as expected when launched via the Epic Hyperdrive SMART on FHIR integration.

column6: Resolved issues with the HL7 Back-End Job Processor when using multiple services to process jobs concurrently.
column7: We identified and resolved several issues that could cause the HL7 Back-End Job Processor to stop processing jobs when more than one service was configured to process jobs concurrently.

column6: Corrected an issue with IdP login when multiple service locations are configured in the Office Business Application configuration files.
column7: Login via IdP now works correctly when multiple service locations are configured in the Office Business Application configuration files. The login happens automatically when an Office document is opened from the Unity client and the Office Business Applications are installed. The login also happens automatically when an Office document is opened from the Web client and Desktop Host is configured to open the Office Business Applications.

column6: Autofill keyword sets are functioning as expected when configured on an auto import folder in Outlook.
column7: An issue was preventing autofill keyword sets from triggering when configured on an auto import folder in Outlook. This has been resolved.

column6: When an Outlook auto-import folder is configured with a secondary autofill keyword set value where this is only one record, the prompt to select the value no longer appears
column7: A bug has been corrected when an Outlook auto-import folder is configured with a secondary autofill keyword set value where this is only one record. The prompt to select the single value no longer appears. The value is automatically selected, as expected.

column6: Resolved issue with cache files not being cleaned up by the DeficiencyPop for Epic COM Integration.
column7: An issue was identified and resolved with the DeficiencyPop for Epic (DP4E) COM Integration where the WebView2 would continue to grow with each use. After this fix, the WebView2 cache should remain fairly consistent in size rather than continuing to grow.

column6: Fixed an issue with unity script which failed to delete repeater row or table row.
column7: User are now able to delete repeater rows or table rows using Unity Script.

column6: Fixed an issue with open form template dialog which failed to display existing list of template
column7: Users will now be able to view list of templates when they try to open form template from Open form template dialog.

column6: Rectified an issue with moving the Notes/Annotations
column7: Addressed an issue where moving the Notes/Annotations around in a document auto selects the documents.

column6: Addressed an error when Docpop or Web Client is opened within an iFrame.
column7: The fix ensures that both Classic and Single Window Mode work properly if Docpop or Web Client gets opened within an iFrame (Embedded Application).

column6: fixed an issue where the conditional text wrapper tag was not working when using it with the item summary sheet tag templates
column7: Conditional text wrapper tags can now be used in item summary sheet tag templates

column6: Fixed an exception when viewing or editing the "Create or Update Agenda Item From Document" workflow action after exporting and importing it into a new system.
column7: The exception displayed on the action was: "An error occurred while creating the property pages for the selected action or rule." This made the workflow action un-usable and a whole new action would need to be created or a database update made to the existing action.  This exception has been addressed, and you are now able to view and edit the workflow action and confirm its configuration.

column6: Resolved issue where users could not print bar code sheets from the Application Client Connector
column7: Resolved an issue where users were unable to automatically generate bar code sheets using Application Client Connector contexts. Also added support for generating bar code sheets in Chrome, Firefox, and Edge. This fix allows users to print bar code sheets in modern browsers, as expected.

column6: Resolved a condition where having hundreds of connection strings may cause performance degradation.
Resolved a condition where a benign "invalid value" warning would be logged.
Unity Client tab order updated for External Autofill Expansion
column7: Tabbing after performing an External Autofill Expansion successfully targets the next expected keyword in the Keyword Panel

column6: AutoFill Keyword Sets using Unity Scripts issues addressed.
column7: Fixed an issue where AutoFill Keyword Set using a Unity Script was not populating keywords in the core clients.

column6: Corrected an issue causing Configuration to crash when generating reports.
column7: Configuration will no longer crash if a Configuration Report is assigned to a Workflow lifecycle, but the database does not have a valid Workflow licensee.

column6: Fillable PDFs that are created via PDFSharp are having the fillable data removed when converted from PDF to Image File Format
column7: Code changes were made. Fillable PDFs that are created via PDFSharp contain the fillable data when converted from PDF to Image File Format.

column6: Resolved an issue with rows in 100% width tables that are cut off towards the end of the PDF document converted from HTML
column7: Updated the code to allow HTML documents with tables spanning 100% width and height render PDF document which was converted from HTML .

column6: An issue has been corrected that was preventing Signature Deficiencies placed on the right edge of a document from being automatically rotated 90 degrees.
column7: An issue has been corrected that was preventing Signature Deficiencies placed on the right edge of a document from being automatically rotated 90 degrees.  This affected the Analysis layout in DeficiencyPop.

column6: Certain viewer options will now be disabled by default
column7: Viewer options that are known to be non-functional in DeficiencyPop are now disabled by default. These viewer options appear when a user right-clicks on a document and are: "Keywords," "Re-index," "Send To | File," "Send To | Create New Document," and "Workflow | Execute Workflow."

column6: Resolved issue when a user logs into a core client but into 2 or more domains, one domain will fail to authenticate after an initial account creation where the account in the first domain works every time
column7:
Resolved issue when a user logs into a core client but into 2 or more domains, one domain will fail to authenticate after an initial account creation where the account in the first domain works every time.

Updated the code to allow a user logs into a core client but into 2 or more domains.

column6: Corrected an issue editing a property placeholder within a Document Composition template.
column7: When editing the property placeholder after creation, the placeholder is now selectable.

column6: Corrected an issue with hyperlinks contained in a Document Composition template.
column7: Corrected an issue where hyperlinks contained in a table within a composed document from Document Composition would direct the user to the incorrect destination.

column6: Fixed an issue within Web Client where Workflow property value was not getting populated when using scoped property bag.
column7: Users are now able to select a Workflow property value within the Document Composition when using scoped property bag in the Web Client.

column6: Fixed an issue when attempting to compose document with templates having anchored images or embedded Excel table.
column7: Users are now able to compose documents with anchored images or embedded Excel tables.

column6: Package generation from Workflow now does not display the date range selection overriding the display content selection window to false.
column7: When a package is automatically generated from Workflow in the Unity Client, while from OnBase Studio, the Display Content Selection Window is set to false (unchecked), and the Date range (Display Content Selection) is set to true (checked) in the template from OnBase configuration, then the Date Range dialog does not show up.

column6: FIX: In Document Packaging in the Web Client, when unchecking the Other Documents document set, NOW, it will uncheck all documents that belong to it
column7: Manually (newly) added documents to a template or non-template package in the Web Client, belong to the “Other Documents” document set. When unchecking the box for the Other Documents document set in the dropdown, it continues to uncheck all documents that belong to it. No exception is thrown in the console in the browser any longer while unchecking or checking any document from any document set. This holds true even when the package is generated from Wrokflow action.

column6: Resolved issue when attempting to split jpeg documents within Document Separation (ad hoc or scan queue) in Unity Client. The new document(s) are converted to tiff and the file size on disk is inflated once saved.
column7: Update the code to allow split jpeg documents within Document Separation (ad hoc or scan queue) in Unity Client. The new document(s) retain the original document’s file type and size.

column6: Resolved an innocuous error that would be logged when importing non-medical record documents that were associated with the Medical Records Custom Query.
column7: This change resolved an object reference error that would be logged on the Application Server when uploading documents via Scan Acquistion Server. This only occurred when uploading documents indexed to a document type that was associated with the Medical Records Custom Query. This was an innocuous error - no issues actually occurred with the upload or the indexing of the imported document.

column6: Corrected issue with FHIR Endpoint Resource Operation configuration dialogs in the OnBase Configuration Client.
column7: Resolved an issue with the Mappings button for FHIR Endpoint Resource Operations becoming enabled for Operations that do not support Mappings.

column6: Improved accuracy of Document History logging for FHIR/Web Blob Pass-Through document updates.
column7: When updating a document through FHIR/Web Blob Pass-Through, the Document History will now only log that a document was retrieved if the FHIR update request includes a return preference of representation, which indicates that the system sending the update is requesting the binary data for viewing after the update is complete. Prior to this change, FHIR document update requests always logged a Document Retrieval in the Document History, even if the update request did not include a return preference of representation.

column6: Resolved a condition in Full-Text Search where indexing performance may be degraded for catalogs with many large documents.
Non-UTF8 encoded documents are now properly returned when executing a full-text search whether they are OCR'd or not.
column7: Non-UTF8 not OCR'd encoded documents (i.e. Big5) previously weren't returned in full-text search results. All day-forward indexed documents will now be returned in search results. All previously indexed non-UTF8 encoded documents that were not OCR'd will need to be re-indexed to be properly returned in search results.

column6: Resolved a condition for when performance issues may arise when cleaning up orphaned catalogdocs database entries when tables contain millions of rows.
column7: The task to remove orphaned catalogdocs database entries has been moved to the Unity Scheduler. By default, it is scheduled to run every Sunday at 2:15 A.M. This schedule can be configured when configuring Unity Scheduler tasks.

column6: Removed sessionTimeout configuration from the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7:
This is a breaking change from previous versions of OnBase. The sessionTimeout attribute in the Hyland.Web.HealthcareWebViewer element in the Web Server web.config file is no longer used and has been removed. The timeout attribute in the sessionState element is used instead. If the sessionTimeout attribute is present, i.e., it was copied into the Web Server web.config from a previous session of OnBase, an exception will occur and the Healthcare Web Viewer will fail to load.

This was done to improve stability of the timeout functionality, particularly when Web Server or User Group Timeouts were also configured that were shorter than the Healthcare Web Viewer sessionTimeout. Prior to this fix, a shorter Web Server or User Group Timeout could be configured, and a user's session would be ended according to the timeout configuration, but the user was never notified of their session ending or prompted to extend their session.
