This could result in thumbnails failing to load (red X being displayed instead) and other actions on the document failing, such as attempting to view keywords, save page rotations, etc.

column6: Resolved issues with configuring a blank Destination Network Address for HL7 Export Destinations.
column7: The Export Destinations configuration dialog will no longer accept blank Destination Network Address values. Additionally, in systems that already have a misconfigured Export Destination prior to upgrading, the HL7 Sender will no longer start if a blank Destination Network Address value detected. If this occurs, an error will be written to the HL7 Sender verbose logs: '"Host" does not contain a valid value.'

column6: Corrected issue with the HL7 Sender not logging acknowledgements.
column7: The HL7 Sender now correctly logs received acknowledgements to the export destination log file and/or the configured Document Type for sent messages.

column6: Resolves a possible race condition in the HL7 Back-end Job Processor.
column7: Resolves a possible race condition in the HL7 Back-end Job Processor which can cause the Unity Scheduler Task to end up in an Error status.

column6: Resolved the bug where Signatures were not getting displayed for Image Forms having a PDF Rendition.
column7: Users will now be able to see Signatures for Image Forms with PDF Rendition.

column6: Declined envelopes will now be moved to the Completed envelopes list.
column7: When the status of the envelope is polled and returns with the status of Declined or Deleted, the envelope will be moved to the Completed Envelopes list in the DocuSign Administration portion of the Unity Client.

column6: Resolved an issue that caused a misleading error message to appear in Epic if the Healthcare Web Viewer was closed before the document was fully loaded.
column7: Prior to this fix, if the Healthcare Web Viewer was closed prior to the document being fully loaded, the following error message could appear in Epic Hyperdrive: "An error occurred when attempting to display the viewer: The external system has indicated a document displaying failure".

column6: Fixed a document name refresh issue in the Import task pane when importing multiple emails or attachments.
column7: The document name now updates in the Import task pane when importing multiple emails or attachments.

column6: The end user is no longer able to view keywords on a document restricted by security keywords
column7: When the "Perform security keyword checking during database query" database setting is disabled and the end user retrieves a document restricted by security keywords, the user will no longer be able to view keywords for this document.

column6: License sharing fix
column7: Fixed an issue with license sharing that could result in a shared license being released prematurely, potentially causing downstream licensing errors.

column6: Fixed an error in Minutes when connection to the appserver is lost.
column7: Instead of throwing an error and forcing you to exit the application when connection to the appserver is lost, we now have the option to attempt a reconnect to the appserver in the minutes client. assuming a reconnection is possible the minutes client will pickup right where it left off, minimizing interruption.

column6: Notes word wrap restored
column7: An issue was introduced where Notes were no longer word wrapping text and expanding well beyond the original bounds. This has been rectified.

column6: Fixed an issue with creating multiple Unity Forms via an action on Safari
column7:
When using a Create Unity Form Action and submitting a form, the option “Would you like to complete another form?” appears. If “No” is selected, this closes the window. If “Yes” was selected, on Safari, the user was navigated away from the form back to the first screen with a “Back” arrow above the Toolbar Component.
When prompted to create another form, the action now works as expected.

column6: Improved contrast for the WorkView Client and OnBase Apps when using Dark Mode.
Fixed Defect: Issue with System Name Unique Validation Across Multiple Applications
column7:
The issue was encountered when multiple applications shared the same component System Names, affecting Screens, Sources, Cards, and Actions. While creating applications in the same session, the System Name unique validation worked correctly. However, after closing and reopening the repository, the System Name unique validation failed to work as expected, allowing duplicate System Names to be created across different applications.
This issue was fixed.

column6: Fixed: “Next” button is disabled when filling out System Name before Label in Source Groups, Cards, Actions, and Tiles
column7:
The issue was observed while creating a Source Group, Card, Actions, or Tile. If the System Name field was filled out before the Label field, the “Next” button remained disabled, preventing the user from proceeding. However, if the Label was filled out first, the “Next” button was enabled and remained so even after modifying the System Name.
This issue was fixed.

column6: FIXED: Deleted Unity Forms can be configured for the Create Unity Form action
column7: Deleted Unity Forms can no longer be selected when configuring a Create Unity Form action.

column6: FIXED: Counts for a Workflow source do not respect filters applied to the queue.
column7: Fixed an issue where the counts for a Workflow source would not take into account filters applied to the Workflow queue.

column6: Column width in data grids is now determined based on the visible content.
column7:
Instead of setting the width of all columns to be the same regardless of the data type or content, automatic sizing was enabled on the grid. 
This will determine the width of each column based on the visible content when the results are displayed. The sizing does not take into account all results.
Overall this makes the content of a grid much more read-able as it's not taking up excessive space for columns with short content (dates, numeric values, short alphanumeric content). As a tradeoff however, when the user scrolls and encounters rows with content that is longer that what's in the initial set of results, the column may have to be expanded by the user to view the full content.

column6: FIXED: Reporting Dashboards were cached for the first user logging in.
column7: For dashboards that were configured to show data that is specific to the logged in user, it could potentially show another user incorrect data that doesn't apply to them. This issue was fixed.

column6: FIXED: Closing and opening the same object via Workflow throws an error.
column7: The issue occured when executing a Workflow ad-hoc or system task against a WorkView object. The action would close the currently open object and then use the Display Object action to display it again with a different screen.

column6: Corrected an issue where note and standard not markups created in OnBase 17.0.1 would not display after upgrading to OnBase 21.1+
column7: Old note and standard note markups from OnBase 17.0.a and prior will now display in an OnBase 21.1 and above system.

column6: Imported Platter Management Unity Scheduler Tasks were not visible in Unity Management Console
column7: An issue was found where any imported Platter Management Unity Scheduler tasks were then not showing up in the Unity Management Console. This has now been fixed so that any imported Platter Management scheduler task will then appear in the UMC.

column5: Public Access (Web Controls for Constituent Access)
column6: fixed an issue where you can tab outside of the modal document viewer to improve accessibility and screen reader support.
column7: when tabbing in the document viewer focus will no remain in the controls of document viewer.

column5: Public Access (Web Controls for Constituent Access)
column6: Fixed an issue where the viewer window did not get focus to improve accessibility and screen reader support.
column7: Document viewer will have focus on the document name when a document is opened.

column5: Public Access (Web Controls for Constituent Access)
column6: Fixed an accessibility issue when returning to the document Results list.
column7: Fixed an issue where, when closing the Document Viewer, the user was not returned to the same place where they left off in the Results list. This is resolved and helps improve general accessibility.

column6: Change to web.config file.
column7:
Background: The 21.x version of DevExpress Dashboard, cache the data and do not reload when a refresh is requested. However, they have provided a mechanism to stale the cache after a specific period of time. To address this requirement we have introduced a new configuration dashboardCacheTimeoutSeconds  in Web.Config for Web Client and Reporting Dashboard.

ConfigSection: Hyland.Web.DashboardViewer
Configration Name: dashboardCacheTimeoutSeconds
Expectation: number of seconds after which the dashboard cache is cleared
Default Value: 2

column6: Version Stamp Dialog now displays the right Revision Number
column7: Previously version stamp dialog used to display the revision as one less than the actual value. Now this issue has been rectified.

column6: Corrected an issue with Text Encoding options in the Unity Client's Workflow interface.
column7: Prior to this, the text encoding option could not be ticked.

column6: Resolved a bug when a Unity Forms fails to load when custom action is executed to overwrite value of a keyword field with that of a blank field.
column7: Users will now be able to execute Custom Actions involving overwriting value of a keyword field with that of a blank field without running into exception or failing to load form.

column6: Resolved the bug for keywords those were still shown up in Lookup window for Reverse Autofill Keyword Set despite of Result keyword being configured as hidden.
column7: Users will now be able to hide keywords in Reverse Lookup window by marking Result Keyword as NO while configuring the Reverse Autofill Keyword Set under Configuration option.

column6: Resolved a bug where the number of attachments in the attachment control was not getting updated correctly when the attached documents were deleted or purged
column7: Users will now be able to see the correct updated number of attachments in the attachment control when the form is refreshed (closed and reopened) after deleting or purging the attached documents.

column6: Resolved a bug when the "The Form is being Saved" dialog was missing from UI while saving a Unity/Image Form in the Web Client.
column7:
Fixed an issue with form submission. "The Form is being Saved" dialog was missing from UI while saving a Unity/Image Form in the Web Client.
Users are now be able to see the dialogue message "The Form is being Saved" while saving a Unity/Image Form in the Web Client.

column6: Resolved the bug of signature being accepted as valid when user signs in the drop shadow.
column7:
Resolved an issue where signature was shown as valid even if no evidence of signature was shown when signature was applied in the drop shadow.
Users are now able to see a border beyond which user will not be able to sign.

column6: Resolved an issue where the reverse actions of disabled custom actions get triggered even when the conditions were not met.
column7: Post this fix reverse actions for disabled custom actions are not triggered.

column6: Fixed an issue with Unity Form Field Validation Panel overlapping with Unity Forms causing viewing issues for the users.
column7: Users are now able to view the entire unity form and the Field Validation Panel will not overlap with the form.

column6: Resolved the issue with Unity Form, A Fields in a Repeater not read by Unity Script if it is marked as Invalid.
column7: Unity Form Fields within a Repeater section are now read by the Unity Script even if it is marked as Invalid.

column6: Resolved the bug of Unity Forms calculated fields when using RoundUp() expression upto decimal value '0' on calculated fields.
column7:
Fixed an issue in Unity forms calculated field when using RoundUp() expression.
Users will now be able to get the correct values for RoundUp() expression upto decimal value '0' on calculated fields.

column6: Resolved an issue to allow users to create Unity Form using Unity API when "Form Visibility" is used to control which users can submit forms of that type
column7: Users are now able to use Unity API running under the Unity Scheduler to overrides Form Visibility and are able to create forms.

column6: Fixed an error in Voting Client when connection to the appserver is lost.
column7: Instead of throwing an error and forcing you to exit the application when connection to the appserver is lost, we now have the option to attempt a reconnect to the appserver in the Voting client. assuming a reconnection is possible the Voting client will pickup right where it left off, minimizing interruption.

column6: Fixed a defect that prevented you from using WAMCON to encrypt the Plan Review Portal web config file.
column7: you can now use WAMCON to encrypt the Plan Review Portal web config file.

column6: Fixed a defect that prevented you from using WAMCON to encrypt the Agenda Online web config file.
column7: You can now use WAMCON to encrypt the Agenda Online web config file.

column6: Users passwords are updated successfully in the Web Client when the User Group has the Password Admin Configuration Right.
column7: Previously, passwords were not updated successfully when the User Group had the Password Admin Configuration Right. In the Web Client, after updating the password the Save button was inactive therefore the password change was not initiated.

column6: Various font and UI colors have been updated
column7: Colors within the Web Client have been updated to meet the 4.5 : 1 contrast ratio

column6: Addressed issue(s) with default column filters when navigating between lifecycles
column7: Resolved an issue that caused default column filters to be removed when navigating between lifecycles. Default column filters are now maintained when navigating between lifecycles.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with the availability of some load balancing options in Workflow
column7: Resolved an issue that caused some load balancing options to be unavailable when the Workflow layout was docked in a specific way. The option, "Show Other User's Inbox", is now accessible regardless of custom window docking.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with adding some WorkView objects to Workflow
column7: Corrected an issue with adding certain WorkView objects to Workflow. Extension Class objects can now be added to lifecycles through the right-click menu.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with the Auto Hide feature in Workflow
column7: Resolved an issue where the WorkView object did not load when 'Auto Hide' was selected in the Workflow inbox panel while executing the Display WorkView Object for Input' action.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with image rendering contained in HTML Forms
column7: The Create PDF/TIFF action no longer renders images contained in HTML Forms. Documents created by converting HTML forms to TIFFs render as they should when viewing the HTML form in any client, with the images included.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with inbox grouping in Workflow
column7: Resolved an issue with inbox grouping in Workflow. Previously, inbox grouping reverted to default on refreshing the inbox. Inbox grouping is now maintained after navigating between queues or refreshing the inbox.

column6: Adjusted focus in Unity Client after user interactive task execution
column7: Adjusted focus in Unity Client after user interactive task execution.  This corrects focus placement after a UI task is executed.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with saving a revision when Burn Redaction Notes action was executed on locked document
column7: When creating a revision using Burn Redaction Notes action on locked document, an error message is now logged on the Diagnostic Console stating that the document is locked and hence action has not been executed. When the lock is released, the action executes correctly, which involves burning the note and recreating a revised version of the document.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with error calling REST API from Workflow
column7: Fixed an issue with error calling REST API from Workflow. Users will now be able to call REST API from Workflow without any error.

column6: After migration user is able to import expk with templates when the resource path is not provided. the templates are stored to DB and not at the resource path. All the existing templates move to the DB after the migration.
column7: After migration user is able to import expk with templates when the resource path is not provided. the templates are stored to DB and not at the resource path. All the existing templates move to the DB after the migration.

column6: Fixed an error that threw an exception when trying to retrieve History of an object from the Object Viewer for Oracle database
column7: User is able to retrieve History of an object from the Object Viewer for an Oracle Database in Unity Client and Web Client.

column6: Fixed an issue with toggling the 'Tasks' button between hidden and visible action bar
column7: Clicking the ‘Tasks’ button toggles between the action bar being hidden or visible, and displays list of Task appearing with Proper name & Icon in object viewer.

column6: Fixed an error that did now allow filters to resize properly
column7: Users will have a usable view where embedded filters are showing all the results properly, scroll bar for documents in a Folder component is loading properly.

column6: Fixed Defect: Exception Thrown When Calculated Attribute References Related Attribute on Base Class
column7:
This issue occurred when a calculated attribute on an extension class referenced a related attribute on a base class. The client would throw an exception when attempting to open an object, resulting in the object failing to load and presenting the error message. The exception prevented the object from loading, forcing users to abandon the action and potentially disrupt their workflow.
This issue was fixed.

column6: Fixed: "An unknown error occurred -System.InvalidOperationException" when creating a new script based external class.
column7: The error occurred when creating a new external class based on Unity Script and checking "Configure Unity Script" on the last page of the wizard.

column6: Fixed: Unable to save negative values in Norwegian Locals.
column7: Fixed an issue where negative values in Norwegian locals would be flagged as invalid and prevent the user from saving the object.

column6: Fixed: Security attributes now allow for more than 2000 objects
column7: Resolved an issue affecting users who run filters against classes with identity security attributes. Specifically, when there are more than 2100 objects associated with a user, the filter execution would fail due to a limitation in parameterized queries.

column6: Fixed Defect: Workflow Action Error when Creating WorkView Notification that included the tag <<AttachAllDocumentsInFolder:Specifed Folder>>
column7:
An issue was resolved where executing a Workflow action to create a WorkView Notification using the <<AttachAllDocumentsInFolder:Specified Folder>> function threw the following error: "Collection does not support finding items by name." This issue was present since upgrading to version 22.1 and persisted through to EP5.
The defect prevented users from sending out emails with attached documents via WorkView notifications, impacting daily operations.

column6: FIXED: Unintended Clearing of Relationship Fields in Embedded Filters
column7: An issue has been fixed where saving filter data on an embedded filter could inadvertently clear the relationship between filter items and the base object. This problem was not reflected in the filter results, leading to unexpected behaviors and broken events.

column6: Fixed: Deleting a user defined filter causes an error "Cannot delete a DataObject containing dirty columns, use IModifierQuery instead"
column7:
The error could cause user defined filter to be orphaned in the database and potentially cause issues with dependencies (i.e. during import/export or when deleting related items like attributes).

New tasks were introduced in the WorkView Doctor that allow to clean up these orphans.

column6: Fixed issues with the navigation buttons of the Unity Client
column7: User is able to navigate to the previous or next object using the 4 navigation buttons on the Object Viewer ribbon of Unity Client.

column6: Improved performance for security attributes
column7: Fixed an issue where certain queries could cause a cast to varchar that wasn't necessary. The impact was a result in reduced query performance.

column6: FIXED; When deleting an attribute in Studio, the following exception is thrown "Unable to delete attribute <attributename> The attribute is referenced in one or more user defined filter"
column7:
The issue was caused by orphaned user defined filters that remained in the database when the user defined filter bar was deleted.
In addition, new options have been added to the WorkView Doctor to find and remove previously orphaned configuration items related to deleted filters as well as an option to remove user defined filters for inactive/deleted users.

column6: FIXED: Table header text in a WorkVIew notification is the same color as the background color
column7: Header text RP-2.0 notification table template will no longer show in the client's background color.

column6: FIXED: View Updater unable to convert views created with OnBase 17 or prior.
column7:
In OnBase 18 we introduced a new version of views for WorkVIew. The View Updater can be used to update from older versions of to the new before they can be used in any OnBase Version 18 and newer.

This feature was affected by code changes made in 23.1 which affected the successful conversion of views in OnBase 23.1. The issue has been resolved.
As a workaround in versions before this fix was implemented, and older version of Studio (i.e. from 22.1) can be used for the view conversion. This should only affect customers upgrading from 17 to 23.1 who never converted the views.

column6: Fixed: Permissions for Embedded Filter System Tasks Influenced by First User Login
column7:
An issue was resolved where the first user to log in to client sets the visibility of the embedded Filter System Tasks for all subsequent users connecting to that Application Server.
This defect had the potential to either show more or less system tasks than what the currently logged in user normally has access to which could be confusing. If a less privileged user would log in first and access a view with system tasks on an embedded filters,, users logging in afterwards would see the same system tasks even if they had access to more. The other way around users could potentially see system tasks they normally don't have access to. Execution of the system tasks would still follow the set user rights and would fail unless the user had permission to the system task.

column6: FIXED: InClause collection surpasses the maximum (1000) number of items in Oracle, an error may occur during SQL execution
column7: The error was thrown when some configuration utilizing security attributes or a sub query macro (i.e. ~?filtername) would include more than 1000 items in the queries IN clause. This issue was fixed.

column6: FIXED: Extension class objects are throwing an exception when accessed via the Workflow Inbox.
column7: A defect was introduced in OnBase 23.1 that prevented extension class objects to be access in the Workflow inbox.

column6: Fixed an inconsistency with the usage of 'Reload Item' Action.
column7: When a WorkView item is changed through the UI during the execution of a Workflow task list, the item must be reloaded in order to refresh the values in memory. There were some inconsistencies on how the clients worked under certain circumstances, which now all work the same.

column6: FIXED: The Full-Text Indexing Service (for WorkView) will not stay started, "Attempting to access an invalid datasource"
column7: Fixed an issue where the full text indexing service stopped after hitting the polling interval with the error message "Attempting to access an invalid datasource."

column6: Association class may throw an error if an object from one side of the association is missing from the database.
column7:
An error "System.AgrumentNullException" may be thrown when an object is viewed with an embedded filter for an association class and the object on the other side of the association is missing from the database.

The error can also happen in other areas of the software where associations are used (i.e. Security Attributes). Most likely it can happen with external classes when an external object is removed from the database without removing the orphaned association.

While the error is removed, it is still considered best practice to remove orphaned association classes.

column6: Resolved a condition where column resizing does not work reliably on workstations with multiple monitors.
Addressed issue(s) with queue navigation of Workflow Inbox filters
column7: Workflow Inbox filters are now maintained when navigating between queues. Previously, certain filters were removed after navigating between queues.

column6: The Analysis layout will now display a message when no documents are available.
column7: A message will now display in the document viewer stating "No documents are available for analysis" when the user accesses a chart without documents.  Prior to this change, the user would see a set of ellipses, which made the application look as if it were still loading.

column6: An issue preventing Safari users from accessing the History and Property menus has been resolved.
column7: An issue preventing Safari users from accessing History and Properties has been resolved. Prior to this fix, users attempting to access these menus would find that a new window would open but no information related to the content being accessed would be displayed.

column6: Front Office Scanning will now launch successfully when filtering is applied to a button with multiple Document Types assigned.
column7: Front Office Scanning will no longer throw an error on launch when a Document Type button has both filtering and multiple Document Types configured.

column6: The PDS1839 and PDS1843 installers for Full-Text Search now no longer prompt the user to install the VC++ 2010 redistributable.
column7: Full-Text Search does not use or require the VC++2010 redistributable, but the installer would prompt the user to install it. The installer now no longer prompts the user.

column6: Resolved an issue where the HL7 Listener could send negative acknowledgements (NACKs) with malformed HL7 after receiving HL7 messages with malformed content.
column7: When processing HL7 messages with malformed content, the HL7 Listener will now respond with properly formed HL7 content.

column6: Corrected issue with HL7 Translation Values dialog showing values from all Translation Tables.
column7: When opening the settings for a selected HL7 Translation Table, the HL7 Translation Values dialog would show translation values from all Translation. The HL7 Translation Values dialog now correctly shows only the values for the selected Translation Table.

column6: The HL7 Auto Sender no longer gets stuck in a bad state when the connection is interrupted, or a timeout is reached waiting for an acknowledgement.
column7: Prior to this fix, it was possible for the HL7 Sender to get in a bad state when the connection was interrupted mid-message. This could also happen if the acknowledgement timeout was reached (see HL7 documentation on the -HL7WAITACKTIMEOUT switch). The sender would send invalid HL7 data and had to be restarted to resolve the issue. This issue only impacted OnBase 22.1 and higher.

column6: DeficiencyPop for Epic COM integration now launches successfully from Epic Hyperspace Classic.
column7: Resolved issue with the DeficiencyPop for Epic COM integration not launching from Epic Hyperspace Classic. Note: this issue did not impact Epic Hyperdrive (COM or SMART on FHIR) - only Hyperspace Classic COM integration was impacted.

column6: File size increased to 100MB
column7: The file has been increased form 20MB to 100MB.

column6: Addressed an issue with portlet configuration of StatusView
column7: Addressed an issue with portlet configuration of StatusView when accessed from SharePoint Web. A solution has been provided for all the supported Portlet Types so that the user is able to save a portlet configuration successfully when accessed from SharePoint Web.

column6: Resolved issue when using the 'Create PDF/Tiff' within a system Workflow task on a Word document creating a PDF, some of the hyperlinks are not carried over on the PDF.
column7: Update the code to allow 'Create PDF/Tiff' within a system Workflow task on a Word document creating a PDF, all hyperlinks are carried over on the PDF.

column6: Fixed a defect with font formatting when the Item summary sheet is generated.
column7: Sometimes, the last paragraph in a table lost the proper font format when the Item summary sheet was generated from a tag template, this has been corrected and the font formatting should be respected as expected.

column6: A bug that caused Analysts to be inadvertently logged out of DeficiencyPop has been corrected
column7: An issue has been corrected that caused an Analyst user to be logged out of DeficiencyPop when they hit "Enter" after selecting the responsible Physician field while creating a deficiency.

column6: Fixed an issue when attempting to compose document with templates having anchored images or embedded Excel tables.
column7: Users are now able to compose documents with anchored images or embedded Excel tables.

column6: Fixed an issue within Web Client where Workflow property value was not getting populated when using scoped property bag.
column7: Users are now able to select a Workflow property value within Document Composition when using scoped property bag in the Web Client.

column6: The issue has been fixed where in case of attempting to execute Ad Hoc tasks on E-Forms, intermittent exception: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" is popping-up.
column7: When attempting to execute Ad Hoc tasks on E-Forms, previously intermittent exception: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" was popping-up. It has been fixed and now onwards, executing Ad-Hoc task, there will not be any unwanted error pop-up.

column6: Resolved issue with Physician Acknowledgement stamps not being saved in the location that they were placed in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: Physician Acknowledgement stamps were moving to the upper left corner of the page they were placed upon closing the Healthcare Web Viewer. Stamps will now save correctly and remain in the location on the page they were placed.

column6: Resolves a possible race condition in the HL7 Back-end Job Processor.
column7: Resolves a possible race condition in the HL7 Back-end Job Processor which can cause the Unity Scheduler Task to end up in an Error status.

column6: Corrected issue with the HL7 Patient Record Processor not saving patient confidentiality codes.
column7: An issue was discovered where the HL7 Patient Record Processor was not applying patient confidentiality codes when processing incoming HL7 messages. This issue did not impact the Medical Records Chart import processor.

column6: Resolved incorrect error being logged by the HL7 Sender when connection issues occur.
column7: In some circumstances, it was possible for the HL7 Sender to log the following error when connection issues occurred: "error formatting HL7 message". This error was misleading and has been corrected to only display if there is a problem formatting the outbound HL7 message.

column6: Corrected issue with the HL7 Sender not logging acknowledgements.
column7: The HL7 Sender now correctly logs received acknowledgements to the export destination log file and/or the configured Document Type for sent messages.

column6: PDS1250 prerequisite is available in the installer
column7: PDS1250 prerequisite is available in the installer

column6: Declined envelopes will now be moved to the Completed envelopes list.
column7: When the status of the envelope is polled and returns with the status of Declined or Deleted, the envelope will be moved to the Completed Envelopes list in the DocuSign Administration portion of the Unity Client.

column6: Fixed an issue with error calling REST API from Workflow
column7: Now users will be able to call REST API from Workflow without any error.

column6: Fixed an issue with printing and send to file from hitlist for Unity Forms
column7: Users are now able to print forms from the hitlist without any issue.

column6: FIXED: Unintended Clearing of Relationship Fields in Embedded Filters
column7: An issue has been fixed where saving filter data on an embedded filter could inadvertently clear the relationship between filter items and the base object. This problem was not reflected in the filter results, leading to unexpected behaviors and broken events.

column6: AutoFill Keyword Sets using Unity Scripts issues addressed.
column7: Fixed an issue, AFKS using a Unity Script was not populating keywords in Core clients.

column6: Resolved an innocuous error that would be logged when importing non-medical record documents that were associated with the Medical Records Custom Query.
column7: This change resolved an object reference error that would be logged on the Application Server when uploading documents via Scan Acquistion Server. This only occurred when uploading documents indexed to a document type that was associated with the Medical Records Custom Query. This was an innocuous error - no issues actually occurred with the upload or the indexing of the imported document.

column6: Resolved issue with timeouts in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7:
The sessionTimeout attribute in the Hyland.Web.HealthcareWebViewer element in the Web Server web.config file is no longer used. The timeout attribute in the sessionState element is used instead. 

This was done to improve stability of the timeout functionality, particularly when Web Server or User Group Timeouts were also configured that were shorter than the Healthcare Web Viewer sessionTimeout. Prior to this fix, a shorter Web Server or User Group Timeout could be configured, and a user's session would be ended according to the timeout configuration, but the user was never notified of their session ending or prompted to extend their session. This could result in thumbnails failing to load (red X being displayed instead) and other actions on the document failing, such as attempting to view keywords, save page rotations, etc.

column6: Resolved issue with the HL7 Sender when using the -HL7DEST switch and at least one Export Destination is configured to "Require Secure Connection Policy".
column7:
Prior to this fix, if one of the assigned Export Destinations was configured to "Require Secure Connection Policy", then the HL7 Auto Sender would require all assigned Export Destinations would need to be configured to "Require Secure Connection Policy". Alternatively, "Require Secure Connection Policy" could be disabled for all assigned Export Destinations. This would occur even when using the -HL7DEST switch to configure which Export Destination(s) the HL7 Sender would connect to. 

This fix ensures that secure connection policies are only required for Export Destinations that have "Require Secure Connection Policy" enabled.

column6: The Medical Records Document Attachment Updater will no longer remove documents from closed charts.
column7:
Resolved an issue with the Medical Records Document Attachment Updater Unity Scheduler task. 
The updater initially task removed documents from closed charts if it determined that required patients and/or chart identifiers were not present on the document. Currently, the updater task skips any documents associated with closed charts, as these documents cannot be modified, since they have a lock which cannot be removed.

column6: Resolved high CPU usage issue in the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic.
column7: An issue that impacted some OnBase 23.1.1.1000 through 23.1.4.1000 environments was resolved. In these 23.1 builds, high CPU usage may occur on VM hosts and/or client workstations where the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic is used. The issue does not occur for all customers and cannot be replicated on demand. However, if higher than expected CPU usage is occurring consistently, it is recommended to upgrade to this or a later build of OnBase to resolve the issue.

column6: Reporting Dashboards are no longer cached for the first user logging in.
column7: Reporting Dashboards were cached for the first user accessing the screen with the dashboard. For dashboards that were configured to show data that is specific to the logged in user, it could potentially show another user incorrect data that doesn't apply to them.

column6: The DeficiencyPop integration for Epic now shows all encounters associated with a deficiency episode in Analysis mode.
column7: Prior to this fix, the DeficiencyPop integration for Epic would only show the primary CSN for a deficiency episode when launched in Analysis mode. Now, DeficiencyPop correctly displays all encounters associated with the deficiency episode so that analysis can be performed on the related documents.

column6: Improved performance for security attributes
column7: Fixed an issue where certain queries could cause a cast to varchar that wasn't necessary. The impact was a result in reduced query performance.

column6: Unity Client tab order updated for External Autofill Expansion
column7: Tabbing after performing an External Autofill Expansion successfully targets the next expected keyword in the Keyword Panel

column6: Addressed issue(s) with authentication when logging into multiple domains
column7: Resolved an issue where an Active Directory Enhanced login with multiple domains failed to authenticate. Previously, when a user logged into a core client but into multiple domains, one domain failed to authenticate after an initial account creation.

column6: Corrected an issue with hyperlinks contained in a Document Composition template.
column7: Corrected an issue where hyperlinks contained in a table within a composed document from Document Composition would direct the user to an incorrect destination.

column6: FIX backported to v23.1: In Document Packaging in the Web Client, when unchecking the Other Documents document set, NOW, it will uncheck all documents that belong to it
column7: Backported to v23.1: Manually (newly) added documents to a template or non-template package in the Web Client, belong to the “Other Documents” document set. When unchecking the box for the Other Documents document set in the dropdown, it continues to uncheck all documents that belong to it. No exception is thrown in the console in the browser any longer while unchecking or checking any document from any document set. This holds true even when the package is generated from Wrokflow action.

column6: Resolved issue when attempting to split jpeg documents within Document Separation (ad hoc or scan queue) in Unity Client. The new document(s) are converted to tiff and the file size on disk is inflated once saved.
column7: Update the code to allow split jpeg documents within Document Separation (ad hoc or scan queue) in Unity Client. The new document(s) retain the original document’s file type and size.

column6: The Scan Acquisition Server integration for Epic Hyperdrive now accurately retains the last-selected Document Type during indexing as well as the application window size and position.
column7:
Prior to this fix, the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) did not retain either the last-used Document Type during indexing, nor the application window size and position that the user selected. These settings are now stored in the registry to ensure retention between launches of SAS. This primarily impacts the Hyperdrive integration for Epic (both COM and SMART on FHIR), as the Hyperspace integration managed the scan wrapper differently. So, Windows is now able to retain these settings between Hyperspace Classic SAS launches.

Please note that these settings will only be retained on the workstation/VM where the user is currently logged in.

column6: Copying and Deleting FHIR Mapping Templates now correctly copies or deletes the associated FHIR Paths within the Mapping Template.
column7:
Prior to this fix, FHIR Paths were not copied while copying an FHIR Mapping Template, resulting in an association of FHIR Path with the original and copied Mapping Templates. This implied that if an FHIR Path was modified in the original Mapping Template, it also got modified in the copied Mapping Template, and vice versa. 

Additionally, prior to this change, deleting an FHIR Mapping Template resulted in FHIR paths being orphaned in the database, as they were not deleted upon deletion of the FHIR Mapping Template.

column6: Physician Acknowledgements in the Healthcare Web Viewer are saved after being placed instead of saving when the viewer is closing.
column7:
Hyland identified an issue where a browser can be exiting the Healthcare Web Viewer page so quickly, or in such a way that the save events triggered on close may not have a chance to execute properly. This could result in Physician Acknowledgements not saving as expected after being placed. 

Since Physician Acknowledgements are now saved immediately after being dropped/placed, the acknowledgement note/text will no longer remain red until closing the Healthcare Web Viewer. Newly placed acknowledgements will turn blue, indicating that they have been saved.

column6: SMART on FHIR Hyland IdP Settings no longer require manual configuration for JWKS, Discovery Document, and/or Token endpoints when using the Client Credentials Authorization Grant Type.
column7:
To align with the OAuth2 specification, JWKS, Discovery Document, and/or Token endpoints can automatically be determined based on the Authority URL, which is the base URL for each endpoint. Manual configuration should be required only if the environment does not align with OAuth2 specifications. Prior to this fix, when using the Client Credentials Authorization Grant Type, each endpoint had to be manually configured in the SMART on FHIR Hyland IdP Settings to complete authentication successfully. 

Note: This issue did not affect the Authorization Code Authorization Grant Type, as that Grant Type already had the ability to automatically determine the necessary endpoints based on the Authority URL.

column6: FIXED: Counts for a Workflow source do not respect filters applied to the queue.
column7: Fixed an issue where the counts for a Workflow source would not take into account filters applied to the workflow queue.

column6: Resolved issue with Signature Deficiencies for Epic failing to launch more than once in a single Epic session.
column7: There was an issue that would cause SD4E to fail to launch successfully more than once during a single Epic session. This software change addresses that issue so that SD4E can be launched multiple times during the same session (sequentially, not concurrently).

column6: Addressed issue(s) with Workflow queue count in the Unity Client
column7: Corrected an issue with Workflow queue counts when accessing a queue from a tile in the Unity Client. This fix ensures that the queue count is refreshed when accessing a Workflow queue from a tile while the Workflow interface is still open.

column6: FIXED: Workflow Queues with WorkView filters applied as filters could show incorrect data.
column7:
When a WorkView filter is applied to a Workflow queue as a filter, the results may show incorrect data: When a value is null, it may display the value from the same column but the previous row.
This issue is fixed.

column6: Resolved an issue with the SMART on FHIR (SoF) DefPop Integration for Epic affecting "One Epic, Multiple OnBase" customers
column7:
An issue has been resolved in the DeficiencyPop (DefPop) integration where Document IDs were not being correctly prefixed with the facility identifier.

While the One Epic, Multiple OnBase mode requires Document IDs to follow the format HY.<facility_id>.<document_id>, the DefPop workflow was only sending the numeric ID, causing Epic to be unable to locate the document and resulting in failures.

The correct behavior had previously been implemented for FOS and SAS integrations but was missing from DefPop. The integration now correctly applies the facility prefix when configured, ensuring successful document lookup and update within Epic.

column6: Epic introduced a change to the FHIRCast process.  FHIRCast is used during integrated scanning to maintain a heartbeat connection between Epic and the OnBase scanning window while there is an active scanning session. While Epic's change aligns closely with FHIR industry standards, it exposed a bug in the OnBase code that breaks the FHIRCast connection. The issue was not caused by the change but was uncovered because of it.
column7: After upgrading the Epic Hyperdrive client to version 100.2510, launching integrated scanning initially works. Shortly after loading, an error message appears and the scanning application closes. This affects both SAS and Front Office Scanning (FOS). An error will also appear the the debuglog.txt file similar to "Error processing FhirCast event. Error received invalid response when sending heartbeat"

column6: Corrected Date Format Issue When Sending Data from FOS to Epic via FHIR
column7: An issue was identified where the date format stored by FOS and transmitted to Epic via FHIR did not align with workstation settings for certain image file types, as outlined in the FOS MRG. While PDF documents correctly stored dates in the DD/MM/YYYY format, TIF documents were incorrectly stored as MM/DD/YYYY or MM/DD/YY. This issue has been resolved to ensure consistent date formatting across all supported file types

column6: Defect fixed for error while trying to preview packages with certain documents
column7: A defect which caused an error while trying to preview packages with certain PDF files was fixed. The fix has been tested with a wide range and variety of PDF files and text-based documents

column6: Common keywords are retained in Document Separation when using Enable Restricted Keyword Options Override.
column7: Common keywords are now retained in Document Separation when using Enable Restricted Keyword Options Override.

column6: Addressed a defect where submitting a Unity Form caused the Unity Client to crash.
column7: A critical issue has been resolved where submitting a Unity Form caused the Unity Client to crash in specific use cases due to an external 3rd party library. This release improves user experience and stability during form submissions.

column6: Fixed a defect to make The Send To feature work with New Outlook for Windows in Unity Client
column7: The Send To feature now works with New Outlook for Windows in Unity Client. No exception error message gets thrown.

column6: Performance fix for Physicians who have been assigned a large amount of deficiencies
column7: A performance issue has been resolved that prevented Physicians with a large amount of pending/unsigned Deficiencies from being able to access and interact with their assigned deficiencies in a reasonable amount of time.

column6: The time taken to generate packets has been significantly reduced when Contents are added in the Documents Sets
column7: The defect has been fixed and now time taken to generate packets has been significantly reduced when Contents are added in the Documents Sets

column6: Corrected an issue causing XML Query Placeholders to inadvertently close Document Composition templates.
column7: When using copy/paste/delete/undo and redo on an XML query placeholder, the template may occasionally close without changes being saved. This issue was fixed.

column6: Corrected an issue with property placeholders in Document Composition.
column7: When editing the placeholder, the property is now correctly selected.

column6: Corrected an issue with Document Composition and Topaz Signature Pad fields.
column7: Prior to this change Document Composition templates would not be visible when running Composition Preview.

column6: Corrected an issue with custom masks on placeholders.
column7: Prior to this change, a template would unexpectedly close when configuring the input for custom masks.

column6: Resolved issue with the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) while sweeping Microsoft Office documents.
column7:
While uploading a Microsoft Office document and the document is still open (e.g., in Word, Excel, etc.), Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) will no longer crash.

Workaround for prior builds: Close the document prior to exiting SAS to upload the document.

column6: Resolved issue with documents not linking to Epic Hyperdrive in specific SMART on FHIR integrated scanning workflows.
column7:
Before this fix, if Epic provided a document type in the initial SMART on FHIR integrated scanning workflows that do not require scanning to that document type, any other user-selected document types would not be linked in Epic. The only document type that would be linked in Epic is the document type that was included in the initial SMART on FHIR context. All other document types would not be sent to Epic for linking. 

Note: This issue is unlikely to occur in most solutions as Epic will usually not pass a document type in the SMART on FHIR context when a specific document type is not required by the scanning workflow.

column6: Resolved a condition in Full-Text Search where indexing performance may be degraded for catalogs with many large documents.
Native PDF functionality is now available in the image-rendered PDF viewer in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: The OnBase 23.1 Web Client introduced some native PDF functionality to the image-rendered PDF viewer. The following new native PDF functionality is now working as expected when viewing image-rendered PDFs in the Healthcare Web Viewer: Bookmarks, Hyperlinks, Text Copy, Text Search, and Text Selection. Please see the Web Client and/or Web Server documentation for more information about the native PDF functionality that is now available in the image-rendered PDF viewer.

column6: License sharing fix
column7: Fixed an issue with license sharing that could result in a shared license being released prematurely, potentially causing downstream licensing errors.

column6: Notes word wrap restored
column7: An issue was introduced where Notes were no longer word wrapping text and expanding well beyond the original bounds. This has been rectified.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with creating workflow documentation in OnBase Studio
column7: Corrected an issue with documentation generation in OnBase Studio. Previously, some tags within Workflow documentation caused the process to remain incomplete.

column6: Imported Platter Management Unity Scheduler Tasks were not visible in Unity Management Console
column7: An issue was found where any imported Platter Management Unity Scheduler tasks were then not showing up in the Unity Management Console. This has now been fixed so that any imported Platter Management scheduler task will then appear in the UMC.

column6: Version Stamp Dialog now displays the right Revision Number
column7: Previously version stamp dialog used to display the revision as one less than the actual value. Now this issue has been rectified.

column6: Resolved a bug of signature being accepted as valid when user signs in the drop shadow.
column7: Users now see a border beyond which they are not able to sign.

column6: The Create PDF/TIFF action now renders images contained on HTML Forms
column7: Documents created by converting HTML forms to TIFFs render as they should when viewing the HTML form in Unity client, with the images included.

column6: Corrected an issue with inbox grouping in Workflow
column7: Resolved an issue with inbox grouping in Workflow. Previously, Workflow Inbox Grouping either kept reverting to default on refreshing the inbox or kept moving between queues through an ad hoc task. Inbox grouping is now maintained after navigating between queues or refreshing the inbox.

column6: Corrected an issue where Item Generator would not create WorkView objects.
column7: Item generator will no longer throw an exception when attempting to create WorkView objects.

column6: Fixed Defect: Workflow Action Error when Creating WorkView Notification that included the tag <<AttachAllDocumentsInFolder:Specifed Folder>>
column7: An issue was resolved where executing a Workflow action to create a WorkView Notification using the <<AttachAllDocumentsInFolder:Specified Folder>> function threw the following error: "Collection does not support finding items by name."

column6: FIXED: Unable to set relationship when the lookup attribute is an attribute with a mask.
column7: The issue could occur when the attribute that is used for the lookup would also have a mask configured. In this case, it could happen that after picking a related object from the lookup filter, the value would be cleared after saving.

column6: Position of the Freehand annotation will not change after adding it
column7: This is a fix that ensures the position of a created Freehand annotation does not change after adding it to a document. Previously, after creating a Freehand annotation, that annotation would sometimes move upward compared to the spot where the user intended it to be placed upon creation.

column6: Fix for Unity Form Navigation Error in Workflow Inbox.
column7: Resolved an issue where users encountered an error when selecting a Unity Form in the Workflow inbox and subsequently navigating away from the Workflow interface. This fix ensures a seamless return to the Workflow interface without errors.

column6: No error message gets displayed while trying to create a new Workspace from Template
column7: Fixed a defect to remove an error message while trying to create a new Workspace from Template

column6: Document separation issue while attempting to split PDF document has been fixed and Unity Client is able to do the function without any error.
column7: Document separation issue while attempting to split PDF document has been fixed and Unity Client is able to do the function without any error.

column6: CPD Scan Queue Batches should change status when the last unindexed document is deleted
column7: The CPD Scan Queue Batches would get stuck on the same status when the last document to be indexed got deleted, when the status should change according to the current batch state.

column6: All pages of the PCL files are properly displayed.
column7: Only the first page of the PCL files was displayed with no issues. The rest of the pages were rendered with some errors. No page is corrupted when it's displayed on the OnBase Client.

column6: Users can save merged PDF/Image documents in Document Separation.
column7: Users can save merged PDF/Image documents in Document Separation.

column6: Document Packaging Performance has been improved
column7: Slow performance in document packaging and the turnaround time for Optimizing Document Package was fixed.

column6: Users are able to navigate across different layouts without accidentally locking documents
column7: This fix resolves an issue associated with document locks. Previously, when a user navigated across different layouts, the document lock associated with the first document a user opened would not release the lock on that document even though the lock should have been released.

column6: Resolution for Unity Forms Invocation Error in Citrix Environment.
column7: A fix has been implemented to address the 'Exception has been thrown by target of invocation' error encountered when multiple users attempt to access Unity Form documents simultaneously in a Citrix environment. The updated logic for CefSharp temporary path resolution ensures that users can now open Unity Form documents without causing the Unity Client to become unresponsive. This enhancement improves the overall user experience by allowing seamless access to Unity Forms regardless of concurrent usage.

column6: An issue was fixed with “Keep With Next”
column7: An issue was fixed with “Keep With Next”, where both “Allow Rows to Break” and “Keep With Next” settings now work as intended, ensuring proper content flow and pagination.

column6: A rare, minor memory management issue within External Access Client has been corrected.
The document sequence should stay the same after bringing in the document.
column7: The document sequence should stay the same after bringing in the document.

column6: Pages added during QA Rescan should always go where selected
column7: Pages added during QA Rescan should always go where selected

column6: OnBase Unity Client can now acquire images/photos using the Logitech Brio web camera.
column7: OnBase Unity Client can now acquire images/photos using the Logitech Brio web camera.

column6: Addressed issues with the Workflow inbox column filter value retention in the Unity Client
column7: Resolved an issue where the Workflow inbox column filter value would be removed when switching between Workflow queues or modifying the column width within the inbox. The Workflow inbox column filter now retains its value until the user clears it.

column6: OnBase client no longer crashes when generating a Batch Render Statement
Default Data Source Name No Longer Logs an Error
column7: An error is no longer recorded every 60 seconds when the default data source name exists in configuration. In addition, if there is a data source name entered that has not been configured, that error is no longer recorded every 60 seconds.

column6: An issue related to viewing Renditions and Revisions has been resolved
column7:
An issue has been resolved that was preventing users from accessing Renditions and Revisions related to the currently selected document when the Patient Window was configured to use the Patient Window PDF viewer.

The Patient Window will use the Patient Window PDF viewer when the EnableBrowserPdfViewer web.config key is set to FALSE.

column6: Users should be able to login to the Unity Client via the same Citrix Server after the initial successful login
column7:
Fixed RFA - CSFPR-8179. 
Users should be able to login to the Unity Client via the same Citrix Server after the initial successful login

column6: When using Identity Services Authentication, Pop Integrations Support Configurable Authentication Type
column7: It is now possible to configure Identity Services and a different authentication type configuration for the Pop Integrations, such as DocPop. This allows Identity Services to be configured for the Web Server while using a service user for the Pop Integration, removing the need to configure two instances of a Web Server. This support is identical to when using Active Directory authentication.

column6: Customer keywords, and Distribution Options are now saved while creating new recipient
column7: While creating new recipients, Customer keywords, and Distribution Options were not getting saved (or getting saved as blank/null).

column6: Resolved a condition where creating a rendition of a document does not set the Full-Text status correctly.
column7: When a document is in a committed scan queue, creating a rendition of the document could set the Full Text status incorrectly. This can cause the new rendition to not be processed by Full-Text. This has been resolved and the correct status is now set, allowing renditions to be processed.

column6: Resolved issue with the EDI HL7 Processor failing to process some HL7 messages in flat files.
column7: An issue was discovered which could cause the EDI Processor to fail to process specific HL7 messages due to issues with the buffer. The buffer was improved to better parse HL7 messages and resolve the issues causing the messages to fail.

column6: Fixed an issue with Agenda Item Packets and Documents marked as Excluded from packet
column7: The issue was that if you marked a document to be excluded from the Agenda Item Packet, the previous supporting document would be duplicated in the item packet.  This has been fixed.

column6: fixed an issue where the conditional text wrapper tag was not working when using it with item summary tag templates.
column7: you can now use the conditional text wrapper tag

column6: Corrected an issue causing Configuration to crash when generating reports.
column7: Configuration will no longer crash if a Configuration Report is assigned to a Workflow lifecycle, but the database does not have a valid Workflow licensee.

column6: Resolved Healthcare Web Viewer printing issue for the SMART on FHIR Epic Hyperdrive Integration.
column7:
The Epic Hyperdrive SMART on FHIR scan viewer window does not allow browser PDF controls to be used to render PDF files, including print previews. This impacted the Healthcare Web Viewer - since print previews could not load, documents could not be printed. To resolve this issue, when printing a document, the Healthcare Web Viewer now launches an external window to load the PDF print preview, allowing the document to be printed. This change only impacts the SMART on FHIR integration - the COM integration for Hyperdrive and Hyperspace Classic will continue to successfully launch embedded print previews.

Note: Since the print preview is not contained within the scan viewer window, it is not automatically closed when the scan viewer window is closed. However, the print preview window will only be visible when viewing the patient for which it was launched and will close automatically when that patient context is closed in Epic.

column6: Resolved a memory issue with the Medical Records Orphan Documents Processor task.
column7: Under normal operating conditions, the Attachment Lock Processor that is part of the Orphan Document Processor works without any performance concerns. However, in certain customer environments, the Orphan Document Processor consumed large amounts of memory due to the number of rows in a specific database table. This fix resolves the issue by loading and processing the data from the table in smaller groups so that a large amount of memory is not consumed loading the entire table.

column6: Resolved issue with the HL7 Listener failing to establish secure connections when Local IPv6 addresses are used.
column7:
Prior to this fix, when IPv6 is used, either intentionally or due to the preference configured on the OS, local IPv6 addresses include characters which incorrectly cause connection errors to occur in the HL7 Listener even when configured correctly. This fix resolves that issue and allows connections with local IPv6 addresses to successfully establish when correctly configured.

Note: A workaround for this issue, if an upgrade is not possible, is to configure the OS to prefer IPv4 or disable IPv6.

column6: Resolved issue with the Epic Tapestry Document Linker SMART on FHIR Hyperdrive Integration.
column7: Linking documents to Epic via the "Link Document to Epic Tapestry" Workflow action no longer results in an error response due to a missing parameter.

column6: Resolved a bug when submitting a form. "The Form is being Saved" dialog was missing from UI while saving a Unity/Image Form in Web Clients.
column7:
Fixed an issue for form submission. "The Form is being Saved" dialog was missing from UI while saving a Unity/Image Form in Web Clients.
Users are now able to see the dialogue message "The Form is being Saved" while saving a Unity/Image Form in Web Clients as before.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with default column filters when navigating between life cycles
column7: Resolved an issue that caused default column filters to be removed when navigating between life cycles. Default column filters are now maintained when navigating between life cycles.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with DIP batches incorrectly executing System Work
column7: Resolved an issue with DIP batches incorrectly executing System Work. With this fix, System Work is only performed once when a DIP process has "Perform System Work" enabled.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with adding WorkView objects to Workflow
column7: Addressed an issue related to adding WorkView objects to Workflow. Extension Class objects can now be added to life cycles using the right-click menu.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with tasklists in System Events and System Tasks in OnBase Studio
column7: Addressed issue(s) with tasklists in System Events and System Tasks in OnBase Studio. Previously, tasklists scoped to all system tasks/events were changed to be scoped to their own parent system task/event. On running System Referential Integrity, the tasklists in different system tasks/events were reported as duplicates even though they were scoped to only their parent system task/event.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with updating System Tasks and Events
column7: Corrected an issue with updating System Tasks and Events. System Tasks and Events now converts properly to use the new scoping mechanism.

column6: Resolved issue with the Document Corrections form not displaying in some environments.
column7: When clicking Add Corrections in the Healthcare Web Viewer integrated with Epic, the Document Corrections form will be displayed alongside the document being viewed. Prior to this fix, in some environments, the form would not display due to environmental factors which could indicate to the Healthcare Web Viewer it was being viewed on a touch screen device (even if it was not a touch screen device). This would cause the Healthcare Web Viewer to incorrectly hide the Document Corrections form.

column6: Addressed an issue where a Cascading Data Set whose primary keyword has masking configured with "Extract Mask Characters" does not display the secondary values in the drop-down in the Web Client
column7: A Cascading Data Set whose primary keyword has masking configured with "Extract Mask Characters" now displays the secondary values in the drop-down in the Web Client, and users no longer have to manually fill out the secondary keywords.

column6: Resolved a performance issue related to saving a Release of Information request
column7: A performance issue related to Saving a Release of Information request has been resolved.  This specific issue was more apparent when saving ROI Requests that contained a large amount of documents.

column6: Addressed an issue where an unclear error message was displayed for a user attempting to access a locked document.
column7: Addressed an issue when one user has a document locked but another user attempts to import a revision of the same document, an incorrect error message is thrown, which causes confusion for the users. Now the error message given out to the user is: "The Document is locked and cannot be modified. Please try again later."

column6: Resolved an issue against date & time keyword within a workflow
column7: Resolved an issue against date & time keyword within a workflow not inheriting keywords when Import Ad-Hoc is used where server and client had different culture.

column6: Addressed AFKS using Workview Filter issues
column7: AutoFill query is executed twice when expanding an AutoFill against a WorkView Filter, this issue has been addressed and resolved.

column6: The HL7 Medical Records Chart Processor now correctly stores the first instance of a repeating MRN field.
column7: When processing HL7 messages with repeating fields mapped to MRN Chart Data Field, the HL7 Listener Medical Records Chart Processor will always use the first instance of the repeating field. Prior to this fix, the HL7 Listener was using the first instance of the MRN repeating field to link the chart to, wowever, the last instance of the MRN would be imported as metadata on the chart (medrecnumber in the chart table). Therefore, we had conflicting data in the database, with the chart being linked to a the correct MRN in the database but having different MRN metadata listed on the chart. This software change ensures that the chart is correctly linked to the first instance of an MRN repeating field and that the same MRN is added as metadata to the chart. All other chart data fields (e.g., MPI, CSN, etc.) use the last instance of a repeating field, but the MRN is the exception to this behavior, by design.

column6: Corrected an issue with IdP login when multiple service locations are configured in the Office Business Application configuration files.
column7: Login via IdP now works correctly when multiple service locations are configured in the Office Business Application configuration files. The login happens automatically when an Office document is opened from the Unity client and the Office Business Applications are installed. The login also happens automatically when an Office document is opened from the Web client and Desktop Host is configured to open the Office Business Applications.

column6: Resolved a performance issue related to Batch Indexing and Release of Information
column7: A performance issue related to Saving and Transitioning batches where the 'Append' action has occurred has been resolved.  This specific issue was experienced by customers who also leveraged Release of Information and the Virtual Print Driver listener to assist in releasing content external to OnBase.

column6: .EML attachment handling in outlook emails has been corrected.
column7: When users retrieve and send an .EML document as an attachment, an Outlook window with the SEND button enabled will appear on the right-hand side, with the native format of the document pre-selected and grayed out. Upon sending the email, the document attached will be sent and delivered as a .eml file (not a .tif file), streamlining the email workflow.

column6: Fixed an issue with Form Creation Date inside Forms Designer > Open Form Template View > Advanced > Revisions tab when the Unity Client culture is set to es-co
column7: Instead of showing the default date 01/01/0001, user will now be able to view correct Form Creation Date inside Forms Designer for the Spanish culture.

column6: Rectified an issue with moving the Notes/Annotations
column7: Addressed an issue where moving the Notes/Annotations around in a document auto selects the documents.

column6: Addressed the Workflow autofill execution slowness issue
column7: Improved Workflow autofill execution with enhanced functionality for "Expand Autofill" and "Check Autofill." Users will now experience improvement in execution time  when expanding selections, and improved validation checks ensure that autofilled data is consistent and reliable.

column6: FIXED: Data for an encrypted attribute may be stored unencrypted in the database.
column7:
Fixed an issue that removed encryption for an encrypted attribute.

This occurred when a new encrypted attribute was created, then modified (i.e. the name was changed), before configuration was saved. If that corrupt configuration was exported, it could be imported into another system. If that happens, data stored in the database would be stored in clear text.

column6: Resolved an issue where customized Agenda Online pages may not display in certain scenarios.
column7: An error was thrown when Agenda Online was initializing which caused customized page map files to not be used and the default web pages would instead be used causing confusion. The workaround for this was to reset IIS then the custom pages loaded properly.  This was seen at times when scheduled maintenance was being performed on a Hyland Cloud hosted system.  This has been addressed and if this situation occurs, the user trying to launch the Agenda Online page sees a message in the browser that Agenda Online is not yet initialized and has a link to try again. Trying again will result in a new request which the other load balance appserver should pick up and the proper Agenda Online site pages will display.

column6: Addressed an issue with re-indexing documents with custom file format
column7: Users were unable to re-index custom image file format to regular image file format. Now documents can be reconciled to the standard/built-in file format after being imported to a custom file format.

column6: DeficiencyPop now supports printing when launched from Epic via the SMART on FHIR integration.
column7: Fixed printing OnBase documents from DeficiencyPop when integrated with Epic via SMART on FHIR launches.

column6: Cross-References now correctly launch in a side-by-side view in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: Resolved issue with Cross-References launching in a separate window from Healthcare Web Viewer instead of launching in a side-by-side view.

column6: When an Outlook auto-import folder is configured with a secondary autofill keyword set value where this is only one record, the prompt to select the value no longer appears
column7: A bug has been corrected when an Outlook auto-import folder is configured with a secondary autofill keyword set value where there is only one record. The prompt to select the single value no longer appears. The value is automatically selected, as expected.

column6: Addressed an issue related to sporadic unsupported browser error while accessing a Docpop URL.

Addressed an issue where the check box "create as revision of original document" is not available when attempting to use the Send To file feature on a previous revision of a document where there are multiple renditions of the document.
column7: The checkbox "Create as revision of original document" did not appear if a different rendition exists as a later revision of the document. This issue has been rectified. Now the checkbox "Create as revision of original document" exists in the "Create New Document" window regardless of what other renditions have been created.

column6: Fixed an issue with open form template dialog which failed to display existing list of template
column7: Users will now be able to view list of templates when they try to open a form template from Open Form Template dialog.

column6: Addressed incorrect pop up showing up when a user who does not have the modify keywords privilege attempts to view the keywords of a document.
column7: If the user doesn’t have the necessary privileges, the keywords will be shown as read-only, and there will not be any message popups.

column6: Addressed an issue with importing large documents
column7: When importing larger documents import button was available prior to file being automatically set. This has been rectified.

column6: Addressed an issue where user is unable to import PDF documents into folder within the Web Client without selecting the Document Type first.
column7: Users are now able to import a file to a folder, and Import button is no longer greyed out when tries to without selecting a document type first.

column6: Addressed an issue when in the Batch Indexing Context and viewing a document, when attempting to perform a Send To | Create New Document, the feature is unavailable from the context menu.
column7: Now in Batch Indexing, when a user right-clicks an image/text document from the batch and has a Document Type and goes to the Send To option, Create New Document is one of the options presented. When they select it they are able to create a copy of the page they're indexing as a separate document.

column6: Addressed an issue with Workflow logging for security overrides
column7: Fixed an issue with Workflow logging for security overrides, which sometimes caused the application server to terminate unexpectedly.

column6: Resolved a condition where Client Certificate authentication cannot be used with the Optimized Soap Service Client.
column7: When Client Certificate authentication is used, typically with Unity API Scripts, the Optimized Soap Service Client does not function as expected. The behavior can easily been seen in Unity Scripts, as the Optimized Soap Service Client does not pass Client Credentials to the HTTPWebRequest in the SerializationContext.GetContext method. The result was that Unity Scripts created using the OptimizedSoapServiceClient receive a 403 forbidden. This has now been addressed and the Optimized Soap Service Client functions as expected when using Client Certificate authentication.

column6: Resolved issue when scanning in the registration workflow via the SMART on FHIR integration for Epic Hyperdrive.
column7: The integrated scanning integration now links documents to the correct encounter when launching from one encounter but scanning to a different encounter. An example of this workflow is when the scanning client is launched from a surgical encounter but the document is being scanned to the pre-admission encounter.

column6: Resolved a condition for when performance issues may arise when cleaning up orphaned catalogdocs database entries when tables contain millions of rows.
column7: The task to remove orphaned catalogdocs database entries has been moved to the Unity Scheduler. By default, it is scheduled to run every Sunday at 2:15 A.M. This schedule can be configured when configuring Unity Scheduler tasks.

column6: Resolved issues with the HL7 Back-End Job Processor when using multiple services to process jobs concurrently.
column7: We identified and resolved several issues that could cause the HL7 Back-End Job Processor to stop processing jobs when more than one service was configured to process jobs concurrently.

column6: A correction has been made to the privileges required to Open or Create ROI Requests
column7: Release of Information was incorrectly checking for the 'Chart Search' privilege when users attempted to Open or Create a ROI Request from the ROI layout.  This requirement has been removed as the 'Chart Search' privilege should only be required for users who are creating ROI Requests from Chart Search.

column6: COLD import text files should display all pages within Unity Client.
column7: COLD import text files should now display all pages within Unity Client. This software correction solves CSFPR-8202

column6: Updated jQuery UI to the version 1.13.2.
Cross References not working on text documents via double click has been fixed
column7: Double clicking  on text documents now displays the cross-referenced document(s) correctly.

column6: Import non image file format documents with Virtual Print Driver
column7: Users were unable to import documents with Virtual Print Driver for documents that do not have image file format as Default  for the chosen document type  within the Web client. Now this has been rectified.

column6: Fixed an issue with "Create Form" Action in OnBase Studio which does not allow Form to be selected
column7:
Fixed an issue with "Create Form" Action in OnBase Studio which does not allow Form to be selected.
Admin will now be able to select form which configuring "Create Form" Action within OnBase Studio

column6: Reduced file size for CEFSharp
column7: Recent upgrades of the CEFSharp browser control caused additional files to be added making deployment sizes larger. The size has been reduced again.

column6: FIXED: Unity Client may crash when dragging and dropping an email attachment to a WorkView object.
