column7: The issue occurred when dragging an attachment from an email directly a WorkView object to attach it to the static folder. An error would be displayed and after that, the Client would close.

column6: FIXED: Deletion Issue with Workview Object in Workflow
column7:
Users were unable to delete a WorkView object using a Unity Script while in Workflow after upgrading to version 22.1. The error message displayed was 'Failed to execute ad hoc task' and further diagnostics indicated that 'One or more objects were not deleted due to being locked by another user.'
This defect resulted in a loss of functionality where a previously working business process to delete items via a script and timer no longer functioned. Users were unable to delete objects as intended, disrupting workflow operations.

column6: WorkView Attribute Validation Issue with Filter Dataset
column7:
In a View with an attribute set using a WorkView Filter dataset, an "Invalid dataset value" error occurred when more than 50 results shared the same value. This issue arose both when validating by manually typing in the value and when selecting it from a Lookup Filter. In previous versions (EP3), manual typing would fail, but selecting via the Lookup would validate correctly.
This defect has been resolved, restoring the functionality to validate values correctly within the dataset, even when there are more than 50 results with the same value.

column6: Fixed: Reverse Autofill not populating some or all keyword values
column7: Reverse Autofill was not populating some or all keyword values when a second autofill with a Security Mask is assigned to at the document type level and the users do not have the 'Access Security Masked Keywords'. This has been rectified.

column6: Addressed an issue where only a single secondary keyword is expanded from the "matching instances" of the primary if the secondary values are non-unique
column7: Now for an Autofill even if the secondary values of the "matching instances" are non-unique all matching rows are expanded.

column6: Fixed an issue with WorkView Filter Placeholder style when selecting table style.
column7: WorkView Filter Placeholder property was not functioning correctly when modified using Setup button.

column6: Addressed an issue when selecting a image file format for importing through the Unity Client and selecting 'Browse' a second time, the Windows File Format is locked to that file type
column7: Now Unity Client does not lock the file types when selecting 'Browse' a second time when ad hoc importing through the Unity Client.

column6: Resolved an issue with the keep alive configuration for the HL7 Listener and HL7 Auto Sender.
column7: The HL7KeepAliveSeconds keep alive INI configuration setting did not work as intended and has been replaced by the HL7KeepAliveTimeout INI setting. The HL7KeepAliveTimeout INI setting works the same as the KeepAliveTimeout setting in the Hyland Message Engine configuration file. Setting to 0 disables keep alive traffic from being sent. Setting to any positive integer defines the number of minutes between each keep alive packet sent by the HL7 service.

column6: Fixed a performance issue with OnBase Studio when setting up the Send Notification action within Workflow.
column7: Fixed an issue where a database with a large amount of users caused OnBase Studio to freeze when selecting User from the Type drop-down menu in the Recipients tab of the Notifications setup process.

column6: Backport to 23.1: Enhanced auto-orientation: Seamlessly print landscape or portrait pages Based on page dimensions for clarity.
column7:
Backport to 23.1: 
Now, when selecting Auto-Orientation during printing, the system intelligently determines whether each page should be printed in Landscape or Portrait mode based on the page dimensions. This functionality mirrors the behavior of the Unity Client, providing users with a seamless experience across platforms.

The Auto-Orientation feature now dynamically renders each page to fit the entire physical page, ensuring optimal utilization of space and clarity during printing. When printing documents containing a mix of landscape and portrait formats, Auto-Orientation will automatically reorient content pages to match the selected physical page format. For example, in a document containing a page of text and an image, physical page remained portrait (from print format), image will get rotated for landscape images & content(Image, Text) will be rendered on the entire physical page to fit. 

Previously, selecting Auto-Orientation in the Web Client always resulted in the image being printed in Portrait mode, regardless of the page dimensions. 
This enhancement improves the overall printing experience and minimizes the need for manual adjustments.

column6: Addressed an issue with the validation message when changing a password upon login if it is missing certain required characters.
column7: When changing a password upon logging into the Web Client and entering specific, invalid password criteria, users see a message displayed informing them of what is missing from the new password they created to comply with the password policy.

column6: FIXED: View Updater unable to convert views created with OnBase 17 or prior.
column7:
In OnBase 18 we introduced a new version of views for WorkView. The View updater can be used to update from older versions of to the new before they can be used in any OnBase Version 18 and newer.

This feature was affected by code changes made in 23.1, which in turn affected the successful conversion of views in OnBase 23.1. The issue has been resolved. As a workaround in versions before this fix was implemented, and older version of Studio (i.e. from 22.1) can be used for the view conversion. This should only affect customers upgrading from 17 to 23.1 who never converted the views.

column6: Addressed the issue of being unable to expand Institutional AutoFill Keyword Sets in the Web Client in Institutional Databases
column7: AutoFill Keyword Sets now expand in the Web Client regardless of their and the database types.

column6: Resolved an issue where specific low-quality images render slightly worse in the Web Client
column7: Changed the code and some specific low-quality images render better and text should be readable in the Web Client.

column6: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Advanced Document Import Processor creating empty documents.
column7: Prior to this fix, when a Document Type was configured with a default non-image File Format (e.g., PDF, Text, etc.), or was mapped in the HL7 Advanced Document Import Process to a non-image File Format, it was possible for the HL7 Listener to create 0kb files in the disk groups. This only occurred when using a Path Reference in the field mapped to $$FILEDATA. This could occur when either, A) no file path was specified in the HL7 message for the field mapped to $$FILEDATA, or B) there was no file in the directory specified by the file path in the field mapped to $$FILEDATA.

column6: Resolved issue with HL7 message end bytes not being read properly.
column7: The HL7 Listener now properly checks for the entire end byte sequence in order to ensure the end of a message has been reached. Prior to this fix, it was possible in very rare conditions for an HL7 message end byte sequence to be improperly read, which would cause the HL7 Listener to shut down the connection unexpectedly.

column6: Resolved issue with escaped return and new line sequences in Base64 not being handled properly by the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor.
column7: An issue was resolved with the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor not handling escaped return and new line sequences in Base64. Including these characters in Base64 is called line wrapping and would be \r\n (CRLF) characters, which would be escaped as \X0D\\X0A\ in an HL7 message. See the HL7 documentation for best practices regarding line wrapping.

column6: Documents imported via the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor are now stored on multiple disk group mass storage copies.
column7: When importing documents that are assigned to a disk group that has multiple mass storage copies configured, the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor will now store the documents on all available mass storage copies instead of just Copy 1.

column6: Corrected issue with the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic that could cause an error to appear even though the document loaded successfully.
column7:
Resolved an issue where an Epic error could display, stating that a document failed to load even though the document successfully loaded in the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration. 

Note: This does not address any potential issue with the SMART on FHIR integration viewer. If an issue exists with the SMART on FHIR integration, a separate software change will be completed to address it.

column6: Resolved issue with cache files not being cleaned up by the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic.
column7: An issue was identified and resolved with the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic where the WebView2 cache would continue to grow with each use. After this fix, the WebView2 cache should remain fairly consistent in size rather than continuing to grow.

column6: Fixed an issue with the recording type selection not being visible when "Use Voting" option was enabled after resetting the media recording type.
column7: The media recording type selection is now visible when restarting a meeting after having reset the Recording type to "None" whether or not the "Use Voting" option is enabled.

column6: Resolved issue with cache files not being cleaned up by the DeficiencyPop for Epic COM Integration.
column7: An issue was identified and resolved with the DeficiencyPop for Epic (DP4E) COM Integration where the WebView2 cache would continue to grow with each use. After this fix, the WebView2 cache should remain fairly consistent in size rather than continuing to grow.

column6: Fixed an issue when executing a custom action "Highlight Element"on elements of other pages in a Unity Form then second page was not getting highlighted.
column7: Highlight Element custom actions now works fine with multi page forms.

column6: Association class may throw an error if an object from one side of the association is missing from the database.
column7:
An error "System.AgrumentNullException" may be thrown when an object is viewed with an embedded filter for an association class and the object on the other side of the association is missing from the database.

The error can also happen in other areas of the software where associations are used (i.e. Security Attributes). Most likely it can happen with external classes when an external object is removed from the database without removing the orphaned association.

While the error is removed, it is still considered best practice to remove orphaned association classes.

column6: Resolved race condition in the HL7 Back-end Job Processor when propagating patient demographics.
column7: A race condition was possible which could cause patient demographics not to propagate to all MRNs and Charts related to an MPI. This would, in turn, result in patient demographics not being updated on the related documents as well. A task force bulletin will be released with additional information. Please reach out to your first line of Support and reference [HC-201497] to determine if your solution is impacted.

column6: Path Reference HL7 Advanced Document Import processes no longer leave file in the directory once it is imported.
column7: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Advanced Document Import process leaving document files in the Path Reference directory once the import was complete.

column6: Corrected an issue with document preview during import when using the Integrated Office Viewer.
column7: Document preview now works as expected when previewing an Office document during import and the Integrated Office Viewer is enabled.

column6: Fixed an issue where users were not able to edit unity forms if they had large document ID, i.e., more than 2B documents in their system
column7: Users will now be able to edit and print Unity Forms even if they have a large number of documents in their system

column6: Fixed an issue with Unity Forms designer when opening form templates, filters are being held after they are cleared, and the Open Form Template window is closed until the Window is opened and closed again.
column7:
When opening Form Templates in the Unity Client Form Designer, Filters are being held after they are cleared, and the Open Form Template window is closed until the Window is opened and closed again.
Users will now be able to clear filters from open forms template dialog.

column6: Fixed an issue with the "Attachment Type" column which does not update on a Unity Form when re-indexing an attached document in case of shortable attachments
column7: User will now be able to re-index the attached document which will update attachment type column name in case of shortable attachments.

column6: Fixed an issue with toggling the 'Tasks' button between hidden and visible action bar
column7: Clicking the ‘Tasks’ button toggles between the action bar being hidden or visible, and displays list of Task appearing with Proper name & Icon in object viewer.

column6: Fixed an error that threw an exception when trying to retrieve History of an object from the Object Viewer for Oracle database
column7: Backport to v23.1: User is able to retrieve History of an object from the Object Viewer for an Oracle Database in Unity Client and Web Client.

column6: The OnBase Office Integration installer now functions as expected in Office 2019 environments.
column7: The OnBase Office Integration for 2019 installer now properly installs the integration in both 32 bit and 64 bit environments.

column6: Removed error that blocked the user from importing an application with Templates to a Database which is already migrated and had no dependency on local path
column7: After migration user is able to import expk with templates when the resource path is not provided. the templates are stored to DB and not at the resource path. All the existing templates move to the DB after the migration.

column6: Resolved issue causing Advance Capture to apply an incorrect Document Type Revision when the “Documents require subsequent manual indexing” setting is disabled.
column7: When the “Documents require subsequent manual indexing” setting was disabled, documents recognized by Advanced Capture were assigned an incorrect Document Type Revision. This resulted in application of the wrong overlay when multiple revisions with differing overlays existed. This has been resolved and now the appropriate Document Type Revision number is applied when the document is indexed.

column6: Resolved an issue when attempting to add an external Minutes Packet from file and the document would be added to the meeting with the wrong meeting document type.
column7: You can now successfully add a Minutes Packet meeting document type from file.  In the past if you tried to do this the document would be added to the meeting documents list but it would have the wrong type. It would always be added as an Agenda Packet type and not a Minutes Packet type which would present a problem if you need to publish both documents to Agenda Online, you would only be able to publish one or the other.  We have resolved the defect and now Minutes Packets added from file are added with the correct Minutes Packet type and can publish both the Agenda Packet and Minutes Packet to Agenda Online as needed.  With this change some of the labels in the UI also were updated to clearly reflect the differences between an Agenda Packet and Minutes Packet when adding a Meeting Document from file.

column6: Corrected an issue with field tags showing on generated meeting documents for sections when using Unity Form Agenda items.
column7: If you use Unity Form agenda items and have Sections and Agenda items on the same level of the meeting outline/tag template, then an issue would arise where after the meeting document is generated the tag on the section would remain since it had no value for the section. This could be worked around by adding the field to the legacy item field list in agenda administration which would then tell the document generation process to go and remove any tag with that name that was left over.  We no longer need to do this workaround to clear the tags from the sections on the generated document, meaning if you are using unity forms you should not have to add your unity form fields in the agenda item field list in agenda administration

column6: Addressed an issue where Unity Form Agenda Items that were created via workflow were not entering the Agenda Item review workflow.
column7: After a unity form agenda item is created via workflow, it will enter the appropriate agenda item routing and review workflows.

column6: addressed an incorrect success message on the summary document generation
column7: Summary Document generation was generating a "Minutes document generation" toaster message on success. It now shows the correct message indicating that the "Summary document generation" was successful.

column6: Fixed an issue with missing text overlays in packets for Legacy Agenda items fields.
column7: Legacy agenda item fields will now display in the text overlay of the packet when configured.

column6: Addressed a defect where you would see duplicate meeting document rows when adding a meeting document from file that revised an existing meeting document.
column7:
The Meeting Documents tab in the Meeting window now displays the # of revisions in the name column of each document.  It also shows the published revision # in the Publish column.  A warning icon is displayed in the publish column if the published revision is not the latest. When the document is published the publish column will also display the date and time it was published.  It will display blank if it has not been published. 

Revisions to the meeting document will only occur if the document type is setup to allow revisions. A revision will be made when opening an existing meeting document and making edits to it OR when using the “Add Meeting Document” feature to add a meeting document from file. Standard revision rules as configured on the Doc type will apply.

Using the “Generate Document” feature will not revise existing meeting documents on the meeting, It will add a new meeting document.

column6: Fixed an issue with Location and Organizer field keyword mapping on the meeting type configuration where the keyword value was not being saved if it was too long.
column7: OnBase Agenda will now be truncating the Location and Organizer keyword value if the entered character length is  greater than configured keyword length. Before, in this scenario the keyword value would not be saved.  We've also added a warning icon to the keyword mappings for a meeting type in Agenda Administration if the selected Keyword's length is configured to anything less than 255 for Location (which it always will because you can only set the keyword length to a max of 250) and anything less than 100 for Organizer.  These are the max allowed lengths for each field when creating a meeting.

column6: Fixed an issue with Agenda Online video on demand playback on Apple devices. "This video file cannot be played" Error Code: 224003
column7: On occasion the last media chunk in the chunklist file could end up with a negative duration value. This would cause the Agenda Online player when running on Apple devices to fail playback with error, "This video file cannot be played" Error Code: 224003. Changes were made to remove the invalid media chunk which resolves the error with no ill affect to the video content. MacOS and iOS devices and browsers should now be able to play all meeting videos.

column6: Addressed an issue where the Agenda Online portal would not load custom page maps correctly when using Remoting instead of SOAP for the ServiceClientType
column7: Using Remoting will now properly load custom page maps.

column6: Corrected error message output for Agenda Online errors
column7: Detailed errors for agenda online will now display in the Diagnostics Console and event viewer

column6: Updated Application Enabler's "Retrieve Document" Web Client context to launch in Microsoft Edge
column7: Updated Application Enabler's "Retrieve Document" context in the Web Client to launch automatically in Microsoft Edge. This allows the context to continue to function as expected in Windows 11, which does not support the previous behavior of launching in Internet Explorer.

column6: Optimized Web and Unity Client's External AutoFill Keyword Sets and AutoFill Keyword expansion API calls to increase performance.
column7: Optimized API logic for Web & Unity Client API calls when retrieving External AutoFill Keyword Sets and AutoFill Keyword Expansions to increase performance.

column6: Re-Indexing a document in the Unity Client that utilizes an Autofill Keyword Set will only highlight the changed values
column7: Previously, all fields were highlighted even if they were unchanged.  Highlighting the fields that changed will bring more focus to the keyword panel and visually aid the user.

column6: Resolved performance issue causing the Unity Client to time out when handling documents in the Waiting Step of a Capture Process Designer custom scan queue.
column7: The “Combine Batches,” “Wait for New Imports,” and “Continue When [X] Batches Arrive” options, when enabled, caused longer processing times in the Waiting Step of a CPD custom scan queue. This resulted in the Unity Client timing out and prompting the user to reconnect to the application server. This issue has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue erroneously allowing users to complete indexing when “Prevent Manual Index” was enabled in the Capture Process Designer.
column7: Previously, when the “Prevent Manual Index Selection of Default Document Type” setting was enabled, users were still able to complete indexing on custom capture process batches. This has been resolved.

column6: Combined Viewer now supports searching for Workview Objects when an attribute is mapped to multiple keywords
column7: When an attribute on a Workview Object was mapped to more than one keyword, the Combined Viewer may not have executed the desired search.

column6: The order of the Keyword data is saved correctly when clicking Cclose".
column7: When reordering an alphanumeric mixed case (with or without the case sensitivity searching option enabled) or uppercase keyword data set, upon clicking "close", the new order is appropriately saved.

column6: Resolved issue to not allow orphaned rows in sqbatchcustom table when a scan queue is deleted.
column7: It's possible to have orphaned rows in the sqbatchcustom table even if scan queues are deleted.  This can become an issue when using Export or perhaps other modules where the extra irrelevant rows in sqbatchcustom table could prevent these features from working properly.  This has been resolved.

column6: Using the Send To | Create New Document action in any client will retain the original document’s file type and size.
column7: Using the Send To | Create New Document action in any client will retain the original document’s file type and size.

column6: Resolved an issue with Web/Unity Clients not respecting the Use JPEG compressed TIFF Global Client Setting
column7: When a color image file is exported from OnBase(e.g., via printing, internal email, or saving) or is recreated through processing (e.g., Advanced Capture processing), this option compresses the image first in order to save space and/or reduce network traffic. It can also improve performance when displaying color image files in Core Services applications.

column6: Updated data logics library to newer version to handle conversion of multipage PDF files, signed through DocuSign, to TIFF files
column7: Updated data logics library to newer version to handle conversion of multipage PDF files, signed through DocuSign, to TIFF files. Previously, the Workflow Action 'Create PDF/TIFF File' prompted "Failed to execute ad hoc task" on multi-page PDF files, signed through DocuSign.

column6: Resolved an issue where memory leaks would occur when attempting to render annotations on PDF documents.
column7: The PDFRender functionality now properly disposes of objects created during the render process and releases memory.

column6: Removed duplication of default font store location during Datalogics initialization
column7: If the default font store location is initialized manually, the LST font cache file can grow in size over time, causing performance issues in the application server.

column6: Resolved an error when changing the Encryption Suite from Unrestricted to FIPS 140-2 when the Version Compatibility is Version 23
column7: Resolved an error when changing the Encryption Suite from Unrestricted to FIPS 140-2 when the Version Compatibility is Version 23

column6: Document names no longer display as "null" within the folder tree when executing the Display Folder for Document workflow system task
column7: When repeating the Display Folder for Document workflow system task, the new document name could displayed in the folder tree as "null"

column6: Fixed issue in Custom Queries where focus could be lost from the selected keyword field
column7: Focus on the selected keyword input box in Custom Queries will now be maintained even when a large number of query result tabs are present

column6: The date picker now displays as expected when using a HTML Form Custom Query in the Unity Client
column7: When using a HTML Form Custom Query in the Unity Client, the date picker would not display to the user when selected.

column6: When a search is performed, search results are as expected, if JavaScript is used to populate search criteria on a HTML Form for Custom Query in the Web Client.
column7: While performing a Search, if JavaScript is executed on Submit to populate keywords on a HTML Form Custom Query in the Web Client, the search would not be complete, and no results would be produced as the criteria would not be met.

column6: Checking for duplicate custom query names now works as expected in OnBase Configuration
column7: After renaming a custom query, OnBase Configuration would produce an error if new custom query is renamed with the same name as an existing renamed custom query.

column6: Checking for duplicate Combined View Type names or CV View Tab names now works as expected in OnBase Configuration
column7: After renaming a Combined View Type or a CV View Tab, OnBase Configuration would produce an error if the new CV Type or CV View Tab is created with the same name as an existing renamed CV Type or CV View Tab

column6: OnBase Database - Fixed an issue that could cause a database upgrade to fail if licensed for the Google Services Integration.
OnBase Database - Fixed an issue where were not logging when a database transaction was opened.
column7: OnBase Database - Fixed certain instances coming from Hyland.Data.Legacy that were not logging when a database transaction was opened. This should now be properly logged.

column6: Updates to licenses consumed by Clinicians accessing DeficiencyPop to complete deficiencies
column7: Clinicians signing into DeficiencyPop will now consume a concurrent client license if a solution is licensed for Signature Deficiencies for Epic.

column6: DeficiencyPop Display Column Configuration is now available for solutions licensed for Signature Deficiencies for Epic
column7: DeficiencyPop Display Column Configuration is now available for solutions licensed for Signature Deficiencies for Epic.  Prior to this change, this configuration required the Medical Records Management Solutions license to be present.  This prevented the Display Column configuration from being accessible to customers with solutions that use Signature Deficiencies for Epic and not Medical Records Management Solution.

column6: An issue has been corrected related to using deficiency rejection reasons that are at or near the configurable character limit.
column7: An issue has been corrected that prevented the deficiency rejection dialog box from updating properly when users navigated from a rejection reason that was short in length to a rejection reason that neared the 250 character limit.  Navigating between configured reasons now updates the character limit correctly and ensures that the "Reject" button is enabled appropriately.

column6: Re-Index and Delete menu options are no longer available in DeficiencyPop
column7: Re-Index and Delete menu options are no longer available in DeficiencyPop.  Prior to this change these menu options were available in the right-click context menu however they were not able to be enabled.

column6: Physicians will now be notified when attempting to act on deficiencies that have been reassigned.
column7: An error message has been added to DeficiencyPop to notify Physicians when they attempt to act on a deficiency that is no longer assigned to them, or to the Physician they are attempting to cover for.  The message will state "Unable to complete action.  Deficiency has been reassigned.”

column6: In the Diagnostics Console, the “Service” tab now displays the correct timestamp for both the IN and OUT events.
Thick client shows correct failure message when failing to re-sign a document
column7: The OnBase Client now shows the correct failure message when failing to re-sign a document.

column6: Resolved an issue that stopped the Document Import Process caused by an incorrect currency character in the Index File
column7: Resolved an issue where an incorrect currency character in the Index File caused an exception message, which stopped the Document Import Process. The error will now appear in the verification report which is consistent with other keyword processing errors. Previously, an exception error occurred when attempting to process a Document Import Process with a double byte character.

column6: Resolved an issue where users were unable to utilize Directory Import Processor to import documents if using any of the default document properties within a mapping.
column7: When running Directory Import Processor and using Default Document Properties in the Keyword Mapping Screens "Invalid keyword type number rule -x" errors are presented and the process fails.  This has been resolved.

column6: Addressed an issue with Document Composition Reporting Dashboard Placeholders
column7: Reporting Dashboard Placeholders flagged as "required" will no longer prevent the template from composing.

column6: The Unity Client allows users to add 'Customer Information'
column7: The Fix covered the case when 250 licenses were used for Document Distribution by the customer and another 1 more license were registered, the user were unable to add  'Customer Information' into the Unity Client.

column6: Resolved performance issue when deleting documents from batch
column7: Resolved an issue where deleting documents from a batch in the Unity Client could cause timeouts to occur. This allows users to delete batch documents efficiently and to avoid having to repeat actions due to unexpected timeouts.

column6: Resolved issue where Keyword locks were being cleared between batches without respect to user options
column7: Resolved an issue where Keyword Type locks were being cleared when the user began indexing each batch in a series of multiple batches, even when the "Clear keyword locks between batches" option was deselected. This fix allows users to index multiple batches more efficiently without having to lock the same Keyword Type each time it is encountered in the series of batches.

column6: Resolved issue in the OnBase Client that caused documents to get stuck in the Batch Splitter Custom Processing Step.
column7: When using the OnBase Client, users encountered an issue with documents getting stuck in the Batch Splitter Custom Processing Step. Instead of progressing into Awaiting Comit, these documents were split repeatedly. This misled users as to how many batches actually existed in the scan queue.  The issue has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue causing counts to differ between scan queues and status queues in the Unity Client.
column7: Within the Unity Client, the batch count in custom scan queues included batches which the user did not have access to.  This caused batch counts to differ between scan queues and status queues.  This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue causing an error to appear when attempting to save and transition while multiple users process a backlog of batches via indexing.
column7: Previously, the following error appeared when multiple users were processing a backlog of batches via indexing: "Specified argument was out of range of the valid values.  Parameter name: position". Users are now able to save and transition without receiving the error.

column6: Bug Fix: Include multiple files during ad-hoc import in the Unity Client.
column7: Unity Client users can now upload multiple files to the same document by clicking the Browse button during an ad-hoc import.

column6: Resolved a 'string literal too long' exception encountered when updating scan formats in Oracle Databases
column7: A 'string literal too long' exception was encountered when updating scan formats in Oracle Databases.  Users were unable to switch scan sources for particular scan queues resulting in the creation of a new scan format every time a new scan source was desired.  This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue where User Group Timeouts were not functioning appropriately when in the QA Review Scan Queue.
column7: User Group Timeouts were not functionality appropriately after performing some work in QA Review Scan Queue.  It was possible the timer would pause and cause an "A Task was Cancelled" exception dialog to the end user.  This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved memory leak issues in the Unity Client.
column7:
Unity Client users sometimes encountered the following error:

DocumentViewer Error: 0 : Insufficient memory to continue the execution of the program.
Exception: System.OutOfMemoryException: Insufficient memory to continue the execution of the program.

The memory leaks occurred most frequently while indexing in the Batch Processing layout, especially when handling numerous queues and documents. The issue has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue causing the cursor not to return to the current batch after completing work in the Scan Queue Layout in Batch Processing.
column7: Previously, users sometimes encountered an issue in Batch Processing that occurred when multiple users were working in the same scan queue at the same time. When returning to the Scan Queue Layout after completing a process on a particular batch, the cursor sometimes pointed to a different batch, causing confusion for the end user.  This occurred when another user was creating a batch in the same scan queue. This issue has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue causing inaccurate reporting of document quantity in batches that contain documents with Security Keywords.
column7: Users previously encountered an issue after using Pre-Index or Full-Index Scan Mode to import batches that contain security keywords on the Document Type. When users viewed the imported batches, the interface erroneously reported a document quantity of 0. This has been resolved to display the correct number of documents in the batch.

column6: Resolved error encountered when rotating pages in the Unity Client Document Viewer.
column7: When viewing a multi-page document in the Document Viewer, Unity users sometimes encountered the following error: “Value was either too large or too small for an Int32.” This occurred when selecting the thumbnail of any page other than the first one and attempting to rotate it. After bypassing the error multiple times, the Unity Client sometimes became unresponsive. This issue has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue in Advanced Capture that caused the focus to jump to a different page when adding a new MIKG.
column7: When attempting to index a document after Advance Capture is performed, the focus jumped to a different page if a new MIKG instance was added.  This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue preventing accurate reporting of page counts in Productivity Reports.
column7: In the Reporting Dashboards Productivity Report, the “Number of Pages” column did not accurately reflect page counts (PDF documents were reported as one page each, regardless of the actual number of pages). This has been resolved; page counts for new documents or batches (after the fix) are correctly reported.

column6: Resolved issue resulting in Index button unavailability in the Unity Client during batch processing.
column7: If a Scan Queue contained a document with a Document Type not available to that queue, the Index button became unavailable. This issue, which occurred only in the Unity Client, prevented users from indexing documents. This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issued causing documents to incorrectly transition to “Status Not Found” when exiting the Unity Client.
column7: Previously, when exiting the Unity Client in Service Mode with a new batch in the "Document Review" layout, the batch incorrectly transitioned to “Status Not Found.” This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue preventing the Data Capture Server from properly separating documents using patch code separation.
column7: Previously, when batches were prepared in a particular order, the Data Capture Server separated the documents whenever a barcode was processed, even when the barcode process/scan queue was configured to separate using patch codes only. This behavior is inconsistent with that of the OnBase Client. This issue has been resolved and now the Data Capture Server performs separation in the same manner as the OnBase Client does.

column6: Resolved issue preventing the Unity Client from consistently capturing the Document Date when applied by a script during Bar Code Processing.
column7: Users utilizing the Scan From Disk functionality were unable to consistently capture the Document Date when applied by a script during Bar Code Processing at scan time. This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue causing keyword values to be incorrectly applied to each document when applied by a bar code script with the “Clear Keywords for Folder” option enabled.
column7: Previously, when the “Clear Keywords for Folder” option was enabled, bar code scripts applied non-static keyword values to every other document in a batch (leaving the keyword value blank for the remaining documents). This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue preventing the BarcodeDocumentUpdates.AddKeyword method from expanding an AutoFIll Keyword Set if the value does not match the original barcode value.
column7: When a value other than the original barcode value was passed to the BarcodeDocumentUpdates.AddKeyword method, the AutoFill Keyword Set would not expand even if a matching primary keyword exists.  This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue that prevented the Data Capture Server from releasing memory consumed during Bar Code Processing.
column7: The Data Capture Sever was consuming memory during Bar Code Processing but not releasing it when completed.  This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue preventing the proper functioning of the “New Document” button in Batch Scanning in the Unity Client.
column7: Previously, an issue occurred when users clicked the "New Document" button within the "Scanning complete" dialog and proceeded to scan a document with no barcode. Instead of creating a new document as the user intended, the Unity Client appended it to the original document. This issue has been resolved.

column6: The Web Client can successfully Re-Index a document with the Electronic Form file type assigned.
column7: Previously, the "Electronic Form" file type would need to be whitelisted to allow Re-Indexing.

column6: Fixed issue with generating a Document Package through Application Enabler.
column7: Users can generate a Document Package, using Application Enabler "Generate Document Packet" action in the Unity Client.

column6: Fixed PacketPop display issue with Dark Mode
column7: Users are now able to use PacketPop in Web Client set to Dark Mode with no display issues. Additionally, header was added to the PacketPop page.

column6: Fixed issue for inherited keywords for Currency and Masking for Document Packaging
column7: Users can generate a package in Web Client or Unity Client with inherited keywords for Currency and/or Masking keyword types.

column6: Issue fixed for inherited keyword with Multi Instance Keywords set up for Document Packaging in the Web Client and Unity Client
column7: Issue fixed for inherited keyword with Multi Instance Keywords (MIKGs) set up for Document Packaging in the Web Client and Unity Client.

column6: Fixed an issue with Multi Instance Keywords when creating the final Document Package in the Web Client
column7: When user modifies Multi Instance Keywords (MIKGs) values in the Keyword panel during Document Package creation in the Web Client, MIKGs are now stored in final Package.

column6: Increased security around Hidden Keyword for Document Package creation.
column7: Hidden Keywords behave as expected.

column6: Fixed issue with double-clicking document thumbnails in Document Separation
column7: Resolved an issue where double-clicking an image or PDF thumbnail within Document Separation in the Unity Client would not always open the document in a new viewer. This allows users to view larger versions of documents in a separate viewer window, as expected.

column6: Resolved issue with file format checks flagging Verification Reports
column7: Resolved an issue where the file format check for Document Separation batches in the Unity Client erroneously included Verification Reports and flagged them as unsupported. This allows users to more efficiently and confidently process Document Separation batches by not presenting users with incorrect error messages.

column6: Resolved issue in Document Separation that prevented users with "Delete Uncommitted Only" rights from deleting the last page of a document.
column7: Within document separation, users with "Deleted Uncommitted Only" rights were unable to delete all pages of uncommitted multi-page documents. Additionally, users could not delete uncommitted single-page documents.  This has been resolved.

column6: Fixed an issue with Workflow Ad Hoc task, when viewing a submitted HTML E-Form within Workflow using the "Display HTML Form" Ad Hoc task, the date selection field did not load its existing value.
column7:
It was reported that when a user was trying to view a already submitted HTML E-form within Workflow using the "Display HTML Form" Ad Hoc task, date field returned a blank value.  
This issue is fixed now and date field displays the correct date when the "Display HTML Form" Ad Hoc task has been perform on an already submitted HTML E-form.

column6: Resolved a bug where the OnBase Client crashes on submitting consecutive E-Forms
column7: Users will now be able to submit consecutive E-Forms in the OnBase Client without any crashes.

column6: "Send to Mail Recipient as Attachment" now behaves as expected when selecting multiple file types
column7: Multiple documents of different file types can be attached to an e-mail

column6: SMART on FHIR context keys now treated as case insensitive.
column7: SMART on FHIR context keys are now treated as case insensitive. This aligns with the FHIR specifications and prevents unnecessary errors and configuration overhead.

column6: Resolved issue preventing proper reading of values in the EpicKeywords.txt file for the Scan Acquisition Server integration for Epic.
column7: The EpicKeywords.txt file, which is installed by the Integration for Epic installer for the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) feature, previously contained some values that could not be read because the End-of-Line (EOL) characters were not correct for a Windows environment. The EOL formatting has been corrected to ensure the file is readable by SAS when it is launched.

column6: The Document Viewer within the Folder layout no longer becomes stuck on a certain document when a user creates a Revision or Rendition
column7: Within the Unity Client, when creating a Revision or Rendition from a re-index action within the Folder layout, the Folder Viewer could become frozen on a certain document forcing the user to re-launch the client.

column6: Exception error no longer happens when dragging and dropping a document into a File Cabinet
column7: When the user does not have privileges to the various levels of the File Cabinet structure, an exception error could have been encountered when attempting to drag/drop a file to import into the File Cabinet. This issue is now fixed.

column6: Form Packet Configuration for clinical form packets has been updated to not display non-medical document types.
column7: The clinical form packet configuration was displaying both medical and non-medical forms. This was fixed to only display medical forms since Healthcare Form Manager only supports medical forms. A medical form is defined as a document type assigned to a Medical Facility.

column6: The Full-Text viewer in the Web Client no longer incorrectly displays documents as being locked by an unknown user.
Full-Text documents now query the database less often when they have a large number of revisions.
Full-Text Server will now be able to process documents larger than 2GBs.
Full-Text Search will now correctly process documents that fall under Records Management.
Stored text for OCR'ed documents is now more consistent with the expected results.
Documents entering the Full-Text Catalog at the start of a batch creation will now not be processed until the batch is committed.
Corrected the browser title text when launching the Healthcare Web Viewer directly in an internet browser.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer now correctly respects the dmsOEMProductName and ServerDesignation settings in the appSettings element of the Web Server web.config file. These values are displayed in the browser window/tab title when viewing a document in the Healthcare Web Viewer via an internet browser.

column6: Improved support for displaying long values the HL7 Translation Value dialog window.
column7: Long values in the the OnBase Configuration HL7 Translation Table now display correctly in the dialog window. Additionally, it is now possible to sort based either the HL7 Value or the OnBase Value. No change was made to the supported length of values stored in the HL7 Translation Table.

column6: Corrected an error in an HL7 response when an Assigning Authority is present, but the corresponding identifier is missing.
column7: When an Assigning Authority is populated in an HL7 message, but the corresponding identifier (MPI, MRN, or Chart ID) is blank, the HL7 Listener now correctly states that the identifier is missing. Prior to this change, the error incorrectly stated that the Assigning Authority was missing, even though it was present.

column6: Resolved issue with HL7 Listener and Sender failing to start after incorrectly attempting to whitelist invalid certificate thumbprints.
column7:
When "Any Trusted Certificate" is configured for Certificates or Authorities on an HL7 Connection Security Policy, certificates in the Authorized Certificates list will no longer be verified during startup of the HL7 Listener or Sender. Prior to this change, if any certificate thumbprints were in the list of Authorized Certificates, the HL7 Listener or Sender would attempt to whitelist/validate the configured certificates even when the "Any Trusted Certificate" setting was configured. This could cause the HL7 Listener or Sender to fail to start, if any invalid certificate thumbprints were in the list of Authorized Certificates. Now, the "Any Trusted Certificate" setting properly overrides or ignores the list of certificates in the Authorized Certificates list.

Note: If "Only Authorized Certificates" is configured instead of "Any Trusted Certificate", invalid certificates in the list of Authorized Certificates will still cause the HL7 Listener or Sender to fail to start. This is true for both Certificates and Authorities on an HL7 Connection Security Policy.

column6: HL7 now properly stores the Physician Signature Text when credentials are included as part of the physician's full name.
column7: The "Create/Update physician from Master File" HL7 Import Processor now stores the full physician name, including credentials for the Signature Text in Physician Information. For example, if the following is received in an HL7 message: "DOE^JOHN^^^MD", the Signature Text will be "JOHN DOE MD".

column6: Corrected an issue with column sorting in the Patient Record Administration utility.
column7: The following columns have been updated to sort properly in the Patient Record Administration utility in the OnBase Client: Date of Birth Internal ID, Master Patient Index ID, Medical Record ID and Chart ID.

column6: The Master File HL7 Import Process now correctly stores the physician's phone number.
column7:
Resolved an issue with the "Create/Update physician from Master File" HL7 Import Process, which was correctly placing the phone number in the OnBase user account but was not adding it to the physician's record. MFN HL7 messages now properly add the phone number to the physician information database table as well.

Note: No changes were made to the Physician Information Settings UI. The phone number is not visible there, only the Fax Number.

column6: Ancillary tables are updated when patient merges are performed.
column7: This change improved the updating of patient information across ancillary tables when merging patients. Previously, the source patient may not be updated to the target patient in some, less commonly used, tables.

column6: The address, phone, and specialty fields now populate in the database correctly for the HL7 physician import process.
column7: The "Create/Update physician from Master File" HL7 Import Processor now correctly stores the address, phone, and physician specialty fields when mapped in the HL7 Message Template.

column6: Integrated Office Viewer configuration and logging updates
column7:
The Office Server Path and WOPI Endpoint Path can be entered with or without a trailing slash which helps minimize configuration errors.

Additional Diagnostics trace and error logging was added to show successful and unsuccessful connections to the Office Server Path and WOPI Endpoint Path to assist in troubleshooting during configuration.

column6: Resolved an issue where word wrap was not respected for multi-line text fields when Unity and Image Forms are converted to or displayed as an image.
column7: Users will now be able to view the entire content of the multi-line text fields correctly when Unity and Image Forms are converted to or displayed as an image.

column6: Addressed an issue with Process Notifications sent to deactivated OnBase users
column7: Processor Notifications will not send external mail notifications to users who have been deactivated. Previously, deactivated users continued to receive notifications if they were not removed from the Processor Notification's Recipients.

column6: PDS 1791 VPD listener can be installed without any issue.
column7: PDS 1791 VPD listener can be installed without any issue.

column6: Unity Client launch when installing PDS1791
column7: Unity Client launch when installing PDS1791

column6: PDS1770 Installer - Icon and text name changes to installer
column7: The icon and names were corrected to the OEM requirements.

column6: Director Unity Client  is able to install using command line install parameters
column7: Director Unity Client  is able to install using command line install parameters

column6: Resolved issue with the Integration for Brainware preventing Default Keywords from being applied to a document when it was re-indexed to a different Document Type.
column7: When re-indexing a document to a different Document Type, any Default Keywords configured for that Document Type were not correctly applied. This issue has been resolved; the Default Keywords are now applied when Keywords Values are not extracted during Brainware classification.

column6: Addressed an issue with DocuSign's end of IE 11 browser support
column7: Instead of defaulting to use IE, the DocuSign Integration in the Unity Client will now leverage the browser that is determined by the webBrowserRenderMode setting in the Unity Client .config file.

column6: Addressed an issue where the Name and Email Keyword Values were not being sent to DocuSign in some cases even though the proper role configuration was done.
column7: Name and Email Keyword Values are correctly sent to DocuSign based on the template and role configuration.

column6: Resolved an issue with the integration for Epic Lumens via the Image Retrieval API.
column7: The integration for Epic Lumens requires that the Image Retrieval API provide the height and width of the requested image (in pixels). Prior to this change, if the OnBase database did not have the height and width of the requested image, it would not be provided to Epic Lumens. Now, OnBase will update the height and width of the requested image in the database and correctly provide these values through the Image Retrieval API to Epic.

column6: External System keys and values no longer accept the double-quote character.
column7: External System keys and values no longer accept the double-quote character ("). This is to address an issue with keys/values containing double-quote characters not deleting successfully through the OnBase Configuration Client.

column6: TXT files can now be previewed during importing in the Outlook Integration for OnBase.
column7: When importing a message's attachments, that are of the TXT file type, you can now preview those attachments by clicking the "Click here to preview file" link in the file viewing window.

column6: Office documents now display correctly in the Unity Client, or the Office Business Applications.
OnBase functionality icons are now correctly displaying in the application menu.
An issue saving E-Forms created through the Outlook integration for OnBase has been resolved.
column7: E-Forms can now be saved as expected when using the Outlook integration for OnBase.

column6: Attachments are now correctly importing in the Outlook integration.
column7: When importing an attachment that has MAIL keywords set to Read Only, the import will no longer fail and the attachments will import.

column6: Hidden keywords transfer over successfully in the Web Client.
column7: Whenever multiple instances of a MIKG have hidden keywords, the keywords will transfer over correctly when Re-Indexed by a user who has limited restricted keyword access privileges

column6: The end user is no longer able to view keywords on a document restricted by security keywords
column7: When the "Perform security keyword checking during database query" database setting is disabled and the end user retrieves a document restricted by security keywords, the user will no longer be able to view keywords for this document.

column6: Resolved an issue where a concurrent license was being consumed when a user attempted to log in to a client without being assigned to a user group
column7: When a user attempts to log in to a client without being assigned to a user group, the login attempt should fail without consuming a concurrent license.

column6: Resolved errant System Activation warning message for Pilot Licenses
column7: The System Activation prompt correctly states there is a 14 day grace period to activate the system.

column6: No Release Notes
Addressed issue(s) with Mailbox Importer importing duplicate documents
column7: Resolved an issue where Mailbox Importer imported duplicate documents due to not marking emails as read. Previously, duplicate documents were loaded in OnBase from email through Mailbox Importer that caused numerous duplicate documents to appear in Brainware and OnBase.

column6: An issue related to Signature Deficiencies on Text Documents containing form feeds has been corrected
column7:
An issue has been resolved related to burning signature deficiencies into text documents that used form feeds for page breaks.

Prior to this fix a “Page not found” burning error would appear in the Burning administration layout when signature deficiencies were placed beyond the initial page of the text document.

column6: Correction to Licensing and Privilege requirements for Epic Physician Acknowledgement
column7:
A change has been made to correct the Licensing and Privileges required to use assign Physician Acknowledgement tasks through an OnBase Scan Queue.  

With this change the software will require that:
1. The solution is licensed for the Epic Integration
2. The user has the Indexing privilege
3. The user is accessing a Scan Queue that has been configured for Epic Physician Inbox Linking Support

column6: A defect related to the manual creation of Editable Transcription deficiencies has been resolved.
column7: A defect has been resolved that was preventing Editable Transcription deficiencies from being manually placed on plain-text Transcription documents.  Prior to this fix, Analysts would not be able to manually place the Editable Transcription deficiency after selecting this deficiency type from the ribbon in the Medical Records Management Unity Client.

column6: Delinquency Letters including deficiencies related to visits that have not been discharged will no longer display 01/01/1964 as the discharge date.
column7: Delinquency Letters including deficiencies related to visits that have not been discharged will no longer display 01/01/1964 as the discharge date.  The field on the delinquency letter that displays the discharge date will now appear empty instead.

column6: Addressed an issue where the time stamp for a speaker minute entry is not adding properly.
column7: Using the setting to add a minute when a speaker has spoken will now include the time stamp in the meeting tree in Minutes and also in a minutes document when using the <timestamp> tag.

column6: Fixed an issue in the Minutes client where the Organizer information was not displayed.
column7: You can now view meeting "Organizer" details from meeting details in the Minutes client.

column6: Addressed issue where Unity jumped to page containing a note when opening the Notes List and only one note exists.
column7: Previously, if a user opened the Notes pane and only one note existed, the system would automatically take the user to the page that contained the note. This caused the user to have to scroll back to the page they were on before toggling the notes on. Now the user remains on the page they are currently on and only jump to a different page if they select a specific note they would like to look at.

column6: Corrected an issue In Unity Client where users were getting a document failed to save notfication when attempting to save image markups that are partially not on the page
column7: Corrected an issue in Unity Client relating to placing image markups. Previously, if a user tried to place a checkup markup or freehand markup on an image on the top half of the page in a way that part of the checkbox was on the image and the other part was not, when they would try to save, an error would display alerting the user that the markup failed to save. This was an issue for both click-and-drag and within the Markups tab. Now when applying a checkmark or freehand markup to an image document that goes past the top of the page the markup is still saved.

column6: Fixed issue when Read Only formatted notifications could be changed with hyperlinks.
column7: Read Only formatted notifications cannot be modified with hyperlinks.

column6: Fixed issue with formatting plaintext notifications.
column7: Plaintext notifications cannot be edited to contain bold, italic, or underlined text even when creating a new one and then switching to the original notification.

column6: Fixed issue with "Revert Changes" for Plain Text notification.
column7: "Revert Changes" functionality in OnBase Studio reverts the content of a Plain Text notification properly.

column6: OnBase Patient Window:  Fixed an issue where some right-click menu options caused the menu to close and error
Updated Workflow action "Set Property to Expression" to correct issues with Intelli-sense prompt
column7: Updated Workflow action "Set Property to Expression" to correct issues with Intelli-sense prompt. This prompt has been changed to ensure the closest matching keyword name is selected.

column6: When configuring the Workflow action 'Import Document' in the context of WorkView objects, it is now possible to adjust the 'Class.'
column7: Prior to this change 'Class' was not selectable until 'Application' was changed.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with Workflow's "Compare To" tool in Studio
column7: Resolved an issue with Workflow's "Compare To" tool in OnBase Studio. Previously, the Workflow action, "Set Multiple Property Values from WorkView", returned the attribute value data addresses instead of property names.

column6: Corrected an issue displaying property names in the Workflow action "Display HTML Form."
column7: Property names will now display correctly immediately after saving the action.

column6: Resolved issue in COLD where characters were not being properly stripped when greater than 9 characters were set in the configuration.
column7: When using COLD Input Filters (AFP/PCL/DJDE, PDF), if users set greater than 9 characters to strip, the keywords saved in indexing did not reflect the proper number of characters to strip.  This issue has been resolved.

column6: Resolved an issue that prevented comment edits from being saved in some cases.
column7: I was possible for comment edits to not be saved if another new comment was added after the edit of an existing comment without saving in between. Edits for both comments and standard comments will now be saved properly in this scenario.

column6: Addressed an issue with review comments with no department ID causing an error when creating plan sets
column7: In the past it was possible to create a comment with a user that was not assigned to a department. All users should be assigned to a review department but in case this does not happen, going forward we now do not allow a user that is not assigned to a department to make a comment on the document.  This will prevent the comment data from failing the plan set creation later on.

column6: Corrected an issue where duplicate rows of the same project would display in the workflow queues if the user was part of multiple review departments and the workflow queue was load balanced based on the corresponding departments' user groups.
column7: This issue would cause many rows of duplicated projects for customers with many departments in their review process and could become burdensome to sort through the projects in the queue. Now if a user is part of multiple departments they will only see one instance of the project in a single queue making it much easier to scan through the list of projects in the queue.

column6: Addressed an issue where the dept ID of a review comment doesn't display in the review comment side panel.
column7: Updated the UI of the review comments in the side panel so that the Dept ID will always display. Made comment cards all a standard height. Re-positioned the user and and date values. Updated the page number text color to match the theme color.

column6: Corrected an issue where note and standard not markups created in OnBase 17.0.1 would not display after upgrading to OnBase 21.1+
column7: Old note and standard note markups from OnBase 17.0.1 and prior will now display in an OnBase 21.1 and above system.

column6: Files will no longer be created if a large upload fails
column7: Before this fix, if a large file upload reached the executionTimeout limit, an orphaned file would still be created in the Disk Group. This file was in a partially created state, and not tied to any database records. We are now catching the exception if it is reached, and cleaning up the file at that time so it is not created.

column6: Patient Portal Link now has the ability to view all pages of a Unity Form that has been converted to an image if the default file format of the Document Type is set to an image.
column7:
Patient Portal Link now has the ability to view all pages of a Unity Form that has been converted to an image if the default file format of the Document Type is set to an image.   Now multiple pages are displayed and the user has the ability to scroll through all of the pages.  Previously, the user would receive a message the document could be signed upon arrival to the facility for treatment and this has been resolved. 
The signing of third party forms is no longer supported in Patient Portal Link and can be signed upon arrival to the facility for treatment.  
Internet Explorer is no longer supported for use with Patient Portal Link.

column6: Printing now supports Adobe x64 bit.
column7: When using the OnBase Thick Client, you are now able to print selected PDF files with Adobe x64 bit by using Retrieval, selecting one or more PDF files and then selecting Print from the right-click menu.

column6: Overlays can now be printed from the Unity Client when using an institutional database
column7: When an Overlay was applied to a Document Type within an individual institution, and if there were multiple institutions with different Overlay settings applied to the same Document Type, there was no option to apply that Overlay to the desired document being printed. This issue was fixed.

column5: Public Access (Web Controls for Constituent Access)
column6: The always wildcard setting in the web.config file that existed in the old PAV is also available in NextGen PAV.
column7:
The AlwaysWildcard setting that existed in the old PAV is also available in NextGen PAV in 23.1 version.
The searches without a * or with a* receives the same result, even before this change, the user could manually put a * and do a wildcard search. This setting is turned off by default.

column5: Public Access (Web Controls for Constituent Access)
column6: The document is opened on iPhone without having to modify the browser's settings.
column7: The document is  opened on iPhone without having to modify the browser's settings.

column6: Publishing Export Workstation Configuration installer works properly
column7: Publishing Export Workstation Configuration installer works properly

column6: Corrected an Issue Where Text and PDF Type Documents Were Erroneously Being Converted to Image Type Documents
column7: Corrected an issue pertaining to PDF and Text type documents. The system was allowing Markups to be added to these document types, which it should not have been allowing. This was causing the Text and PDF documents to be converted to a image document upon saving. Markups are now enabled only for image and image rendered file types.

column6: Passwords applied to ROI Packets being saved to file cannot exceed 32 characters in length
column7: A new error dialog has been added to warn users who have entered a password that does not meet the character length requirements.  Prior to this change the field in ROI where passwords were entered would not warn the user when they have exceeded the character limit.  The field would truncate the password pasted by the user to fit the maximum character limit of 31.  The result would be that the user would not know that the password applied to the packet was not the same as the password pasted into the field.

column6: Documents from an external Meditech System were being excluded from the OnBase Release of Information packet
column7: An issue has been resolved that was resulting in documents attached to the OnBase Release of Information packet using the Meditech Report Capture integration not being included in the final release.  These documents appeared to be present on the Attachments screen but were missing from the Release Order screen and the final PDF that was generated as part of the release.

column6: Distributed Disk Services now has improved stability against network traffic.
column7: Various processes, such as antivirus software when scanning, have the ability to send data to Distributed Disk Services. This data sometimes contained null headers, causing DDS to stop until it's recycled. We have resolved this scenario so that null header network traffic to DDS reduces the likelihood that it will take down the service.

column6: Corrected a NotImplementedException in .NET Standard Unity API
column7: Corrected an issue where the .NET Standard Unity API was throwing a NotImplementedException when deserializing a server-side exception to throw on the Client.

column6: Corrected an issue where assembly import trace logs were no longer working
column7:
During the the Import Assembly process, Hyland.Logging TraceLogger was supposed to log the import assembly’s dependencies resolving. However, this stopped working. 

A CurrentDomain_AssemblyResolve event was added to the ImportAssembly AppDomain to leverage preloadedDomainAssemblies to resolve Hyland.Logging and its dependencies.
A CurrentDomain_DomainUnload event was added to the ImportAssembly AppDomain to forcely flush the existing logs to the configured Routes.

Hyland.Logging.Logger.CloseLoggers();

After these corrections, the assembly import trace logs were working again.

column6: Camera option when selecting Acquire in Unity Briefcase now works when using a Microsoft Surface device.
column7: Corrected an issue where when using Unity Briefcase and attempting to Import a doc using the Acquire button, no photo was be saved. Preview displayed a black screen. After the fix was put in place, the capture(s) can be previewed in the acquire dialog and capture(s) are saved.

column6: Unity Client No Longer Gets Stuck Open in Service Mode
column7: Corrected an issue where if you used the option to exit the Unity Client while it was in service mode, the process did not completely close if a window that is currently utilizing CEFsharp was open (such as an E-Form). This issue was first introduced after a CEFsharp upgrade.

column6: Resolved System.OutOfMemoryException messages in Event Viewer relating to Notes Gallery
column7: Resolved an issue where customers experienced overall slowness along with "System.OutOfMemoryException" messages in Event Viewer. Customers would have to restart the Unity Client to resolve the issue but it could come back for some customers several times per day. The 'Force GC Collect' button in the Troubleshooting layout now cleans up memory related to the notes gallery.

column6: Corrected an accessibility issue relating to the Unity Form pop-up screen reader.
column7: The Unity Form pop-up screen reader now reads the pop-up question(s) automatically. The dialog content, such as “Would you like to create a new form”, is now read aloud when the pop-up displays on the screen. Additionally, when the keyboard tab is pressed, "Yes" / "No" buttons become focused, and the screen reader is able to read them appropriately.

column6: Corrected an issue with the sort option for log dates in Unity Client Workflow Transactions
column7: Resolved an issue within Unity Client Document History in which Workflow Transactions were incorrectly sorting the log date column within Unity Client when using a SQL database. Users are now able to sort the column by Log Date ascending or descending.

column6: Users no longer receive the error "Exception has been thrown by the target of an invocation" when viewing a checked out document as another user on the same workstation.
column7: Users no longer receive the error "Exception has been thrown by the target of an invocation" when viewing a checked out document as another user on the same workstation. This behavior was experienced when viewing PDF and Microsoft Word documents in Unity Client.

column6: Cursor automatically focuses in the note box for overlapped text annotations
column7: Resolved an issue where creating an "Overlapped Text" annotation was not focusing the cursor to add text to the note. Users would have to click inside the note to be able to add text. This behavior was fixed for Notes in 20.3.0, but not for the "Overlapped Text" annotation. It is now fixed for both.

column6: Unity Scripts dealing with prearchive events execute correctly
column7: Documents modified on Import via Unity prearchive event scripts refresh correctly

column6: Object "random text.rem" has been disconnected or does not exist at the server' exception error is no longer occurring in the Unity Client
column7: When configured for service mode, the Unity Client would produce an error in Batch Indexing, or while idle after working with Unity Forms or Workview objects or Workflow.

column6: Corrected an issue where selecting a Document Type Group with only 1 Document Type associated was no longer auto-selecting that Document Type in the Batch Indexing Panel.
column7: In the Unity Client, the ‘Document Type’ drop-down menu now populates when a Document Type Group containing only one Document Type is selected when indexing in Batch Processing.

column6: Corrected an issue where accessing the "Customer Information Administration" within the Unity Client within an institutional environment was throwing an error
column7:
The Customer Information Administration window was failing with an error within institutional environments. This administration window is now accessible without error.
Previously, the error(s) seen were as follows:
"Parameter INSTITUIONS_PARAM was not in the correct format."
And Diagnostic Console showed:
"Institution string 410,0,412,413,3,414,4,2,415,411,1,5,99 was not in the expected format System.FormatException: Parameter INSTITUTIONS_PARAM was not in the correct format at Hyland.Core.ServiceHandlers.CustomerInformation.GetRecipientsForProcessClass()"

column6: PDF Documents Open with Render Mode set to Chromium
column7: Fix put 23.1 so that PDF Documents now open as expected when using webBrowswerRenderMode set to Chromium. Previously, if the Web Browser Render Mode was set to "Chromium", and the user tried to open a PDF document, the document viewer would be blank and the PDF document did not render. This was also backported into v22, v21 (EP5), and v20 (EP3).

column6: Corrected an issue where an error message was being thrown in English when the rest of Unity Client was configured for another language.
column7: Corrected an issue where if a user was in Unity Client in a non-English configured version, the 'Create List Report' error was incorrectly being displayed in English. This error now shows in the Unity Client in the language that the system is configured to be in.

column6: Corrected an issue where .CR2 image files were unable to render in OBIMGLIB in Unity Client
column7: An issue was corrected where .CR2 Image files were unable to render in OBIMGLIB in Unity Client. Users would try to view images that were the .CR2 file type and the image would not render correctly. Users can now render these image types.

column6: Excel documents save/update successfully in the Unity Client when the document type has no keywords assigned.
column7: Excel documents with no assigned keywords within the OnBase viewer save/update.

column6: Resolved an issue where a form is submitted successfully after attaching a file that contains no information (0 KB file size)  in a required attachment field. An error occurs indicating the document was not attached to the form after submission.
column7: Users will not be able to submit a form with a 0 KB attachment to it. A error message saying 'the attached file is empty' will appear indicating that a file containing no data cannot be submitted with the form when the attachment field is required.

column6: Resolved an issue where if the 'Automatically Expand Height' setting is checked, the Multiline Text box is not getting expanded and scroll bar is visible.
column7: Users will now not see any scroll bar when 'Automatically Expand Height' setting is checked, and the Multiline Text box will be expanded according to the content size.

column6: Resolved an issue where error was thrown during Test Drive when a Unity Form Attachment control's ID contained a capital letter.
column7: Users will now be able to update the ID of the Attachment controls with capital letters and will not get an error after clicking the Test Drive option.

column6: Resolved an issue where a user with no access to the document type was able to access any autofill keyword set configured in the document type within OnBase if that user had the privilege to use the Test Drive and Forms Designer functions.
column7: When a user with privilege to use Forms Designer and with no access to the document type now uses the Test Drive function, they will see dummy data in the Secondary Keyword fields when they fill any data in the Primary Keyword field.

column6: Notify users with a message when a document is locked by other user.
column7: Resolved an issue with the Display Message When Document Is Locked option. Users will now be informed when a document is locked by another user, and the locked document will become Read-Only.

column6: Fixed an issue with creation of Autofill Keyword Sets when 'Create Autofill Record on create' option was selected.
column7:
Resolved the bug where the AutoFill Keyword Set was not created for a new record when 'Create Autofill Record on create' option was selected for the Primary Keyword and all the autofill fields were becoming blank if the value was not part of an existing autofill set.

Users are now be able to create new Autofill dataset when 'Create Autofill Record on create' option is selected and the autofill fields do not become blank.

column6: Resolved the bug where users cannot navigate to a Read Only Primary AutoFill Keyword Set field using a TAB key action.
column7: Users are now able to navigate to Read Only AutoFill Primary keyword fields or any control fields present on the form using the TAB key action.

column6: Fixed an issue with IME Japanese language suggestion which appeared outside of input control.
column7: Users will now be able to view the IME typed Japanese language suggestion list inside the input box area and not as an external suggestion box.

column6: Resolved an issue where there is no audible accessibility sound to indicate an error when submitting a Unity or Image Form with a required field that is not filled in.
column7: Users will now be able to hear the error messages of all the required fields that are not filled when submitting Unity and Image Forms. Also Pressing TAB will highlight the first error in the Alert message box.

column6: Sporadic 403 error no longer occurs when attempting to display a Unity Form in the Unity Client when using a Load Balancer
column7: When configured to to use a Load Balancer, displaying a Unity Form in the Unity Client would sporadically produce a 403 error.

column6: Fixed the issue when the Select List drop-down arrow on Unity Forms was misaligned with the text box
column7: Users will now be able to view the Select List drop-down arrow aligned with the text box.
