column6: Sporadic 403 error no longer occurs when attempting to edit and publish a Unity Form in the Forms Designer when using a Load Balancer
column7: When configured to to use a Load Balancer, editing and publishing a Unity Form in the Forms Designer in the Unity Client would sporadically produce a 403 error. This is no longer the case.

column6: Resolved a bug with submitting a Unity Form with missing Autofill Keyword Set keywords and the secondary keyword is required
column7:
Resolved the bug, where users faced issue when submitting a Unity Form which had a missing keywords from an Autofill Keyword Set, and the secondary keyword was marked as required. The user was prompted with "There was an error saving the form. Unable to set the Keyword value for one or more form fields".

Users will now be able to submit a Unity Form without any error.

column6: Sporadic 403 error no longer occurs when attempting to preview a Unity Form in a test drive view when using a Load Balancer
column7: When configured to to use a Load Balancer, previewing a Unity Form in the Test Drive view in the Unity Client would sporadically produce a 403 error. This issue is fixed.

column6: Resolved a bug when a Unity Form fails to load when a Custom Action is executed to overwrite the value of a keyword field with that of a blank field.
column7: Users will now be able to execute Custom Actions involving overwriting value of a keyword field with that of a blank field without running into exception or failing to load form.

column6: Corrected an issue with Unity Management Console Task Configuration Report for Workflow timers.
column7: The Timer Name will now consistently be displayed, and when a timer is configured for "All Queues" each queue will be listed.

column6: Right-click functionality within the Unity Web Control Viewer is now enabled.
column7: Right-click functionality within the Unity Web Control Viewer was removed around V18. This has now been added back in for end-users.

column6: Resolved issue preventing the Virtual Print Driver from importing documents into Unity Client.
column7: Users were unable to use the Virtual Print Driver on workstations without the Microsoft Visual C++ 2010/2012 redistributable files installed. This issue has been resolved.

column6: Fixed a defect that prevented you from using WAMCON to encrypt the Plan Review Portal web config file.
column7: You can now use WAMCON to encrypt the Plan Review Portal web config file. This issue was also seen in older versions of OnBase. A workaround does exist to fix this without upgrading.

column6: Fixed an issue that prevents you from using WAMCON to encrypt the Agenda Online web config file.
column7: You can now use WAMCON to encrypt the Agenda Online web config file. This issue was also seen in older versions of OnBase. A workaround does exist to fix this without upgrading.

column6: Delete Page from Thumbnail
column7: Web Client users can delete pages from the thumbnail of a multi-page image document.

column6: Language-specific local culture format settings respect relevant fields
column7: Language-specific local culture format settings respect display date, datetime, floating point, and currency fields appropriately.

column6: User are able to copy text in the Web Client for Keyword Types that are configured with the Auto Display option
column7: Web Client users can highlight and copy the text of a Keyword Value when the Auto Display option is enabled and the document is displayed in the viewer.

column6: Web Client Send to File correctly names saved documents
column7: Documents sent to file from the Web Client are named following their configured Auto-Name string.

column6: Addressed an issue with the Unity Client showing an incorrect message when a document re-index into a revisable Document Type is cancelled.
column7: Updated the Unity Client to not display a message when a document re-index action is cancelled. This will prevent users from seeing an inaccurate message and avoid confusion when a document re-index is cancelled.

column6: Corrected an issue that allows the Web Client to play M4A files without the need to download the file before playing.
column7:
Fixed an issue within the Web Client, where when a user would attempt to open .m4a files, the following error is received:
"Your browser cannot display this encoding type." This error impacted Chrome, Edge, and Firefox. The audio files could only be played after being downloaded first. M4A file now can be played in the Web Client without needing to download them first.

column6: Closing shared Unity Forms no longer logs "A server error has caused your session id to be lost." in the Diagnostics Console.
column7: Closing shared Unity Forms no longer causes "A server error has caused your session id to be lost.  Please close the browser window and log in again." to be logged in the Diagnostics Console.

column6: By default, the PDF Viewer used by the Web Client is now determined by the user
column7: The user can specify whether PDF documents are displayed using the web browser’s configured PDF viewer or the Web Client PDF viewer.  The option is located in the Web Client Preferences: Open PDF Documents using the Web Browser’s configured PDF Viewer.

column6: The DocPop hitlist refreshes after re-indexing a document.
column7: DocPop hitlist with Custom Query results correctly refreshes and loads once a document has been re-indexed within the web client.

column6: Dark mode is available in the Web Client when the dmsVirtualRoot contains a mixed value.
column7: Appearance options are available and usable for the user when the virtual root path for the web server has a mixed case value.

column6: Updated the Web Server LoginCtrl from outputting duplicate IDs which prevented Trace from capturing additional debug information.
column7: The LoginCtrl no longer outputs duplicate ID's which prevented debug information from being captured when using Trace.

column6: Fixed an issue where when a user opened a PDF in the Web Client in a new window with the "Open PDF documents using the web browser's configured PDF viewer" setting disable from preferenecs, the document opened using browser control and not the web client image viewer.
column7: Fixed an issue where when a user opened a PDF in the Web Client in a new window with the "Open PDF documents using the web browser's configured PDF viewer" setting disable from preferenecs, the document opened using browser control and not the web client image viewer. Users could go in and deselect the "Open PDF documents using the web browser's configured PDF viewer" checkbox option in their configuration. When they would then try to open a PDF in a new window, the PDF would still be opened using the browser viewer instead of the build in view in the Web Client. A change was put in place so that the syste resspects the checkbox being selected or deselected so that the user has the option of which viewer to utilize for PDFs.

column6: Reduced the number of flashes when selecting the Custom Queries context menu in the Web Client.
column7: Previously, the right side in the Web Client would flash multiple times when switching to the Custom Queries context screen.  This behavior was observed only in light mode.

column6: Users passwords are updated successfully in the Web Client when the User Group has the Password Admin Configuration Right.
column7: Previously, passwords were not updated successfully when the User Group had the Password Admin Configuration Right. In the Web Client, after updating the password the Save button was inactive therefore the password change was not initiated.

column6: Web Services Publishing will now correctly produce PDF/A-1B documents through Document Composition.
column7: An error was corrected that caused Web Services Publishing to produce a standard-PDF instead of PDF/A-1B.

column6: System Events will now refresh Workflow queues if the item is in a queue.
column7: This change will allow end users to instantly see the result of a System Event within the Workflow interface.

column6: Addressed an issue where documents may not change approval paths in certain circumstances
column7: Corrected an issue in Workflow Approval Management where documents do not change approval paths if an Auto Approval Path is configured, and the ID of that rule is the same as the ID of the next approval path.

column6: Automatically-generated HTML User Forms now provide an empty selection option for keyword datasets
column7: HTML User Forms in Workflow that are automatically generated will now provide an empty selection option for keyword datasets. This change will allow the user to deselect a value from a keyword dataset.

column6: Addressed an issue where the common keywords were not displayed when re-indexing a document
column7: Resolved an issue where the common keywords were not displayed when re-indexing a document with a default document type.  Previously, when the Re-Indexing Document action was executed and the default document type was set, common keywords were not carried over.  The update will now display the common keywords.

column6: Addressed an issue where an invalid exception appeared under certain circumstances when opening a legacy lifecycle Workflow queue through Document Retrieval
column7: Resolved an issue where an invalid exception appeared when a Unity Client user had a legacy Workflow queue with a filter opened in the Workflow interface and attempted to navigate to a different Workflow queue with filter applied through Document Retrieval.

column6: Addressed a document focus issue within Workflow's Web Client interface
column7: Resolved an issue that involved a document losing focus after executing an ad hoc task, where a Workflow Inbox Filter is in use with a column header sort. Focus will now be maintained on the selected document after an ad hoc task is executed while a Workflow filter and column filter are applied.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with System document types not getting added to Workflow
column7: The following System document types can now be added to Workflow after they are generated: SYS Document Purge Report, SYS List Content Reports, SYS Configuration Reports, and SYS Scan Reports.

column6: Email attachments that do not have a file extension are not imported when using the "Create Document from Attachment" Workflow Action
column7: When email attachments without a file extension are selected for import while using the "Create Document from Attachment" Workflow action, those attachments are not imported. A message is displayed in Diagnostics Console when this occurs. The file is not imported as OnBase requires a file extension to determine the file type for imported files.

column6: Updated the Unity Client's Workflow interface to add previously unavailable options
column7: Updated the Unity Client's Workflow interface to add previously unavailable options when using “Match Keyword to User Name” load balancing. In the Unity Client's Workflow interface, two items have been added in relation to “Match Keyword to User Name” load balancing: “Remove All Existing Assignments” and the "<Unassigned>" option.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with Workflow's Related Items pane
column7: Workflow's Related Items pane will no longer show the Portfolio Relations tab when only a single portfolio relation exists. Prior to this, the Portfolio Relations tab would intermittently be displayed and cause confusion among users.

column6: Addressed loading issue of Workflow rules and actions
column7: Adjusted the loading of Workflow rules and actions to handle scenarios more gracefully when an older version of Studio was mistakenly used on an upgraded database. It is never recommended to use an old version of Studio on an upgraded database.

column6: Corrected an incorrect exception when configuring the Workflow action "Call REST API"
column7: Addressed an issue involving display of an incorrect exception when configuring  the Workflow action, "CALL REST API". With this change, an incorrect exception is suppressed when selecting a tree item representing a response from the server and choosing the Modify button or menu item.

column6: Addressed issue(s) related to increased memory consumption
column7: Resolved an issue that led to increased memory consumption in Workflow. Previously, in certain situations, Workflow retained additional memory that was not needed.

column6: Addressed an issue with the "Display Document" Workflow action
column7: Resolved an issue where the user was able to alter and save values when using "Display Document" Workflow action with the "Separate Window" option selected. With this fix, documents are now correctly locked when using the "Display Document" Workflow action with the "Separate Window" option executed on the primary item.

column6: Addressed an issue in the Unity Client that made it unresponsive when executing a Workflow rule
column7: Corrected an issue in the Unity Client that made it unresponsive when executing the Workflow rule, "Prompt User with Question Box". An underlying web component used to render the interactive window was updated to function properly.

column6: Corrected a mapping issue using the Workflow action, Call REST API
column7: Corrected a mapping issue using the Workflow action, Call REST API. This fixes the ability to map a field contained in a nested object when the parent object is an array.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with persistence of Workflow custom layout
column7: Corrected an issue with persistence of Workflow custom layout. Previously, the user needed to reload the layout every time they navigated from Workflow. This fix ensures that Workflow's custom layout will persist even if the user closes the interface.

column6: Improved performance when using the Workflow action "Re-Indexing Document" in the Unity Client
column7: Improved performance when using the Workflow action "Re-Indexing Document" in the Unity Client. For document types that are configured for renditions but not revisions, there is now a noticeable performance improvement.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with display column operators in Workflow's Unity Client interface
column7: Corrected an issue that caused display column operators to be cleared after re-indexing two or more documents within Workflow's Unity Client interface. The filter columns now persists until cleared by a user.

column6: Addressed issue(s) related to the execution of the Workflow action, "Replace Keyword"
column7: The Workflow action, "Replace Keyword" no longer re-triggers Full-Text Listener. Prior to this change, this Workflow action incorrectly caused documents to be re-processed by the Full-Text Listener.

column6: Updated Workflow action Call REST API "Test Results" functionality
column7: Updated Workflow action Call REST API "Test Results" functionality. When updating the Response Schema, the repository no longer needs to be saved prior to "Test Results" picking up the changes.

column6: Addressed display issues with WorkView objects in the Workflow Viewer
column7: The WorkView objects are now successfully displayed in the Workflow Viewer when accessing them from the Web Client. Previously, the WorkView objects were not not getting displayed in the Workflow Viewer.

column6: WorkView attributes with a currency data-type now sorted numerically when used on a Workflow inbox filter
column7: WorkView attributes with a currency data-type are now sorted numerically when used on a Workflow inbox filter. Prior to this change, the sorting was alphanumeric.

column6: Multiline textboxes on Unity Forms now displays properly when used as part of a Workflow filter
column7: Multiline textboxes on Unity Forms now displays properly when used as part of a Workflow filter. Prior to this, word-wrap did not trigger, causing some texts to not be visible.

column6: Create PDF/TIFF File now has additional information in the transaction log
column7: Create PDF/TIFF File action now logs the document handle of the original document and the new document. Previously, this workflow action logged showed only the Document Handle of the existing document.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with cursor position when using the Workflow action, Display HTML Form
column7: Corrected cursor position when using the Workflow action, Display HTML Form. This fix ensures that the cursor position is automatically in an input field when executing the Display HTML Form action.

column6: Improved the performance of "Import CSV" for approval users in Workflow Approval Management
column7: Workflow Approval Management now offers improved performance of "Import CSV" for approval users. Previously, "Import CSV" failed when a Workflow Approval Management solution contained a large number of users.

column6: Corrected an issue causing an incorrect document to display in the Workflow Web Client
column7: Corrected an issue causing an incorrect document to display in the Workflow Web Client. In very rare instances and with only one document left in a user's queue, the Web client displayed a related item for a previously worked document.

column6: Items in the toolbox in the View Designer are now sorted alphabetically.
column7: Listing items in alphabetical order makes it easier to find specific items and should improve workflow.

column6: FIXED: Workview filter can be run multiple times successfully.
column7: Filter and system events can be run multiple times successfully. No pop-up box with an error message is thrown.

column6: CSV files with numeric header do not throw error while importing.
column7:
1) No error is thrown anymore as in previous versions.
2) CSV files with numeric headers can also be imported successfully.

column6: "Error occurred while displaying object" message is displayed when opening a view after adding custom actions to WorkVIew objects.
column7:
Resolved an issue where a view could get corrupted if a component which is referenced in custom actions is moved between containers.

WHen the view is corrupt it would load with an error message 'Error occured while displaying object'. The view still loads in the designer and the view can be fixed by removing custom actions.

column6: Users can now search for more than 2000 items at a time.|Users can now search for more than 2000 items at a time. No error is thrown on the console.
column7: No errors logged into the Diagnostic Console when searching for more than 2000 items.|No errors logged into the Diagnostic Console.

column6: Fixed card layout display.
column7: Multiple cards with styles added and saved are now displayed in the correct layout.

column6: Sorting external class embedded filter using Unity Script is successful now.
column7: Sorting of an external class embedded filter is now allowed by column, when the external class is populated by a Unity Script.

column6: Cache Time Out is disabled with 0 as a valid input.
Error message thrown when Hyphen is entered to cache timeout field.
column7: The Cache Time Out field is added new. Hyphen is an invalid input to this field and throws an error stating the valid value range when entered.

column6: Valid values of the newly implemented default cache time out field in OnBase Studio include 0 and does not include blank.
column7: The newly implemented default cache time out field in OnBase Studio will not accept blank as a valid input, and can have 0 as valid input.

column6: Warning message pop-up box appears with wrong input value to cache time out field in Studio.
column7: Warning message pop-up box appears with wrong input value to cache time out field in Studio, and customer needs to enter valid values.

column6: Date picker functionality for US and Indian time zones works fine in build 23.1.0.153.
column7: Date picker functionality for US and Indian time zones works fine in build 23.1.0.153.

column6: No JavaScript error is thrown for views both with and without components in OnBase Studio v23.1.0.156.
column7: No JavaScript error is thrown for views both with and without components in OnBase Studio v23.1.0.156.

column6: Cut and paste functions work fine in views in OnBase Studio v23.1.0.156
column7: Cut and paste functions work fine in views in OnBase Studio v23.1.0.156

column6: Keyword and attribute masking issues were resolved in WebClient.
column7: Keyword masking is respected in the Attach Document window, attribute masking is respected in the Object Viewer and there is no error when trying to export the application from OnBase Studio.

column6: Fixed an issue with proper loading of Object Viewer with views in Debug mode.
column7: Object Viewer loads properly and all the styles are loaded and applied in the view properly in Debug mode.

column6: Addressed issue with invisible text inserted into the white space
column7: PDF output from template PDF action button on view of the application will no longer result in any invisible text inserted into white space created by "page-break-after: always" CSS styling.

column6: Fixed issue where CultureInfo object provided incorrect data
column7: CultureInfo and information like current, decimal & floating point appear correctly.

column6: In Filter Result of Standard Class with related ODBC external class having composite key, to fetch a large number of records at a time from external table, the number of records per DB Call equals to the fixed batch size of 500 divided by the number of composite keys.
column7:
In Filter Result of Standard Class with related ODBC external class having composite key, to fetch a large number of records at a time from external table, the number of records per DB Call equals to the fixed batch size of 500 divided by the number of composite keys.

No restriction has been imposed upon the maximum number of the composite keys, though depending upon the increasing number of the composite keys, the number of records per call will be decreased. Hence the number of DB interactions will increase.
Concurrency issues are not addressed by this fix. If required, such issues need to be taken up separately. This fix applies only to performance optimization.

If there are no composite keys, which means that only unique keys are present, the system will act as it did before this fix.

column6: Users using Hungarian date/time settings can now load WorkView filters regardless of the culture/regional settings.
column7: Users using Hungarian date/time settings were unable to load certain WorkView filters due to an error thrown. This fix allows them to display filter results successfully.

column6: Users using regional language settings (Hungarian) can successfully save selected date and time format.
column7: Users using Hungarian date/time settings were unable to load certain WorkView filters due to an error thrown. This fix allows them to display filter results successfully.

column6: Sequence of folders after re-arrangement/drag and drop is displayed in correct order now.
column7: After this fix, when the user completes the folder rearrangement by the drag and drop operation, subsequent calls involving the sequence ID is going to send over the correct display order.

column6: Previously attached documents will continue to be visible while trying to upload next document to an object.
column7: Due to one of previous developments (SBP- 414), in both Unity and Web Clients, when we try to see the Documents (already) attached to an object while trying to upload a next document, we encountered a null reference error. As a part of this fix, uploaded documents will continue to be visible on the viewer in this scenario.

column6: Culture Info and information like current, decimal & floating point are displayed appropriately for Portuguese language.
column7: Culture Info and information like current, decimal & floating point are displayed appropriately for Portuguese language.

column6: Fixed displayed results shown by WorkView Filter using the "~Workflow[<life cycle name>\<queue name>]" macro in a fixed constraint.
column7: The macro now functions successfully and fetches the expected items from the Workflow queue.

column6: Objects created within the Web Client successfully display the secondary class now.
column7: When user wanted to see the default screen of the extended class, the view was not refreshed properly. However, this has been fixed now. When creating an object within the Web Client and selecting the system task, the object automatically refreshes and displays the secondary class.

column6: Documents list in Object viewer can be viewed properly.
column7: Complete list of documents are displayed in Object viewer successfully. They are neither contracted nor having a scroll bar and can be viewed properly when uploaded or searched with/without filters.

column6: WorkView REST API generates correct response when Date and DateTime is fed, and generates correct response that is consumed by endpoints.
column7: The Workview Rest API generates correct reponse when supplied Date and DateTime in these formats respectively yyyy-MM-dd and yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ss. All endpoints consume the response correctly.

column6: Successful connection established between OnBase studio and Appserver for a migrated database.
column7: For a migrated database, given that  the template folder is removed from the resource path, user receives no error on diagnostics console any more when connecting OnBase studio with app server or when Display Template action is triggered in any of the client. The connection is established successfully.

column6: Dates in Date and Date/Time field can be properly selected.
column7: Dates on the Date and Date/Time fields can be successfully selected from the date picker and set on these fields, irrespective of the ribbon is maximized or minimized. The same behavior is happening when the fields are being manually typed in.

column6: Successful mapping to >>Document Type system keyword works and returns documents
column7: When opening a WorkView object that contains a Keyword Mapping for >>Document Type system keyword for a WorkView folder, the user is able to map a Document Type name of a Document Type to the Document Type attribute of a class.

column6: WorkView View highlights all messages of relevant mandatory fields now.
column7: All the required fields in a WorkView object give warning message upon saving the object without appropriate value, and all warning message highlights the relevant fields.

column6: Fixed an issue where duplicating a view that had rules configured would throw an error.
column7: The error would occur when the view had multiple nested rules and operations and the user tried to duplicate the view. It would essentially prevent creating copies of a view. This now works as expected.

column6: Two instances of Onbase studio both connected to the same app server or one functioning as thick client, the other as thin client, can purge the same application, class and filter successfully in both instances at same time, from Purge Deleted Objects Selectively and all other avenues.
column7: Two instances of Onbase studio both connected to the same app server or one functioning as thick client, the other as thin client, can purge the same application, class and filter successfully in both instances at same time, from Purge Deleted Objects Selectively and all other avenues. A text showing the incremental count of deleted objects being purged is displayed below the progress bar. Upon successful purging from Purging Objects Selectively, a success message is obtained in a popup box.

column6: Appropriate error message is displayed in the error message box when a mandatory search constraint is not filled while performing any operation like search
column7: In Web Client, in a User Entry Constraint filter, if a BOOLEAN type attribute is added as a required search constraint and not filled while performing the search then error message box is displayed with an appropriate error message.

column6: Appropriate search results are displayed when searched with alphabetical characters if a Document Type attribute is added as a search parameter.
column7: In Web Client, in a User Entry Constraint filter, if a Document Type attribute is added as a search parameter, then it accepts non-numeric characters, and displays proper search results if given search value as alphabetical characters.

column6: WorkView system task/action icons are now displayed successfully in the Web Client when using a port number at the end of the Web Server
column7: Fixed an error when WorkView system task/action icons were not displayed properly in the Web Client.

column6: External Classes with no Unique or Component Key index Attributes configured generate the expected filter count with no error when refreshed in Unity Client
column7: Resolved an issue when External Classes with no Unique or Component Key index Attributes configured generated an error when refreshing the filter count in Unity Client.

column6: Fixed issue when trying to drag/drop email documents from Outlook directly into a WorkView object
column7: When users drag and drop an email attachment (documents/presentation/audio/word/excel file) into the WorkView Object Viewer without the Import window open from Outlook, the client will not react. No error will be thrown on the client and the system will not break. If the user drags the attachment to the Upload window from local system, it attaches the same and uploads into OnBase.

column6: Objects are opening successfully from the calendar within the Web Client
column7: Objects are opening successfully from the calendar within the Web Client in any browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox). This works successfully for any view (month, week, day) and format (date only, date + time) of the calendar.

column6: Fixed a bug with Object Viewer ribbon of Unity Client
column7: User is able to navigate to the previous or next object using the 4 navigation buttons on the Object Viewer ribbon of Unity Client.

column6: Data Import functionality was fixed in OnBase Studio.
column7: The key value mapping selections made by the user, as well as the contents of the XML/CSV/Excel files are getting correctly imported using the 'Test Import' option in the WorkView Data Import functionality in OnBase Studio.

column6: Resolved exception when changing status step color in Studio
column7: Resolved an issue where an exception was displayed in OnBase Studio after administrators changed the color of a status step while configuring a custom capture process, forcing them to exit Studio without saving. This allows administrators to efficiently configure custom capture processes without being interrupted by unexpected exceptions.

column6: Unity Client now guarantees successful document generation without encountering any errors, empowering users with smooth uninterrupted packaging experiences.
column7: Before the fix, generating a package in the Unity Client would prompt an error. Now, this functionality is seamless for users using all document types, including PDFs with and without comments, href links.

column6: Foundation DBUtils - Clarified exception message when saving to a location that requires administrative privileges
column7: Improved exception message details when trying to save a configuration file to a location on the local computer that requires administrative privileges.

column6: The user is no longer displayed a message indicating that there are more search results available when the OnBase Database and Full-Text Index are out of sync.
Fixed an issue where users were unable to view Excel documents via the integrated office viewer due to changes made by Microsoft
column7: Users can now view and edit Excel workbooks

column6: OnBase WorkView using Oracle Databases - Fixed an issue where "ORA-00920: invalid relational operator"
column7: Fixed when leveraging an Oracle database the following error is thrown when trying to update a WorkView attribute that is using a View configured to search two different data sets: "ORA-00920: invalid relational operator". This error is only seen when using an Oracle database.

column6: Addressed an issue with the Document Auto Name string for Adobe Sign documents.
column7: When documents are returned from Adobe Sign, the document now maintains the Auto Name as set in OnBase.

column6: In case of missing template, error messages are displayed in proper format now.
column7: In case of missing template, error messages are displayed in proper format now.

column6: Object viewer is loading all CSS fine in Web and Unity Client.
column7: Object viewer is loading all CSS fine in Web and Unity Client.

column6: Messages to user related to purging process (during purging, on successful completion of purging, on failure of purging) are updated as below.
column7:
The message displayed on a pop-up box during purging is "Purged <n> out of <m> object(s)"
Message displayed on successful completion of purging is "The database has been purged of <n> object(s) out of <m> object(s) successfully."

Where <n> represents number of objects already purged at any instant. <m> represents number of objects to be purged in total.

column6: Message about how many objects are purged out of total objects to be purged is displayed within the failure or exception message.
column7: Using Studio as a thick client, the successfully purged data count upon total data is displaying within error message dialog box. However, when using Studio as Thin client, if the App Server gets stopped, there is no way we can show the Progress count information in the exception.

column6: Sporadic 403 error no longer occurs when attempting to display a Unity Form in the Unity Client when using a Load Balancer
column7: When configured to to use a Load Balancer, displaying a Unity Form in the Unity Client would sporadically produce a 403 error.

column6: Resolved issue to not allow orphaned rows in sqbatchcustom table when a scan queue is deleted.
column7: It's possible to have orphaned rows in the sqbatchcustom table even if scan queues are deleted.  This can become an issue when using Export or perhaps other modules where the extra irrelevant rows in sqbatchcustom table could prevent these features from working properly.  This has been resolved.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with System document types not getting added to Workflow
column7: The following System document types can now be added to Workflow after they are generated: SYS Document Purge Report, SYS List Content Reports, SYS Configuration Reports, and SYS Scan Reports.

column6: Full-Text Server will now be able to process documents larger than 2GBs.

Resolved issue with the HL7 Back-end Job Processor updating primary keyword values to be blank in standards AutoFill Keyword Sets, causing subsequent high memory usage and performance issues.
column7:
The HL7 Back-end Job Processor will no longer update primary keyword values in standard AutoFill Keyword Sets (AFKSs) to be blank. This prevents a subsequent issue, where the HL7 Back-end Job Processor would find a blank primary AFKS row and query the entire AFKS table. When this subsequent issue occurred, the HL7 Back-end Job Processor would consume a significant amount of memory and eventually the service would stop or would fail to continue processing other jobs. Changes were also made to prevent the HL7 Back-end Job Processor from querying AutoFill Keyword Sets without a keyword to filter results on.

More information: 
When performing certain jobs, the HL7 Back-end Job Processor will update AutoFill Keyword Sets (AFKSs) via reverse-lookup. A reverse-lookup update occurs when the secondary keyword being updated by the HL7 Import Process is assigned as a primary keyword on other standard AFKSs.  The HL7 Back-end Job Processor will then update other secondary keyword values that are also in the associated standard AFKSs. Also, as in the event of an AFKS merge, the primary keyword value may also be updated, which could cause this issue to occur. 

The issue mentioned above would update the primary keyword in associated standard AFKSs to be blank whenever that keyword was updated to be blank in the original AFKS being updated by the HL7 Import Process.

column6: Corrected issue with how the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor assigns Document Types in repeating segments.
column7: Prior to this fix, when processing an HL7 message with multiple documents, the Advanced Document Import processor would use the Document Type found in in the first segment for all documents in the repeating segments. Now, the HL7 Listener will correctly use the Document Type found in each segment for the document associated with that segment. If a Document Type value is not mapped in OnBase, the HL7 Listener will assign the default Document Type configured for the Advanced Document Import processor.

column6: In Filter Result of Standard Class with related ODBC external class having composite key, to fetch a large number of records at a time from external table, no. of records per DB Call equals to the fixed batch size of 500 divided by no. of composite keys
column7:
In Filter Result of Standard Class with related ODBC external class having composite key, to fetch a large number of records at a time from external table, no. of records per DB Call equals to the fixed batch size of 500 divided by no. of composite keys. 

Limitations:

No restriction has been imposed upon the maximum no of the composite keys, though depending upon the increasing no of the composite keys the no of records per call will be decreased. Hence the number of DB interactions will increase.

No implementation has been done to take care of the concurrency issues. These are pre existing issues and if required needs to be taken up separately. Only performance optimization has been taken into consideration.

For no composite keys present, that means that only unique key present, the system will follow the pre-existing execution path.

Please contact your OnBase administrator."

column6: Resolved issue with HL7 Listener and Sender failing to start after incorrectly attempting to whitelist invalid certificate thumbprints.
column7:
When "Any Trusted Certificate" is configured for Certificates or Authorities on an HL7 Connection Security Policy, certificates in the Authorized Certificates list will no longer be verified during startup of the HL7 Listener or Sender. Prior to this change, if any certificate thumbprints were in the list of Authorized Certificates, the HL7 Listener or Sender would attempt to whitelist/validate the configured certificates even when the "Any Trusted Certificate" setting was configured. This could cause the HL7 Listener or Sender to fail to start, if any invalid certificate thumbprints were in the list of Authorized Certificates. Now, the "Any Trusted Certificate" setting properly overrides or ignores the list of certificates in the Authorized Certificates list.

Note: If "Only Authorized Certificates" is configured instead of "Any Trusted Certificate", invalid certificates in the list of Authorized Certificates will still cause the HL7 Listener or Sender to fail to start. This is true for both Certificates and Authorities on an HL7 Connection Security Policy.

column6: Resolved an issue with the Image Retrieval API returning more pages than expected for some multi-page TIF documents.
column7: In some instances, it is possible for files in the OnBase disk groups to contain more pages than are specified in the OnBase database for those documents. When the Image Retrieval API (IRA) processed a request for one of these files, IRA returned all pages in the file in the disk groups, instead of only the pages that existed in the OnBase database. This change forces IRA to process/convert all TIF documents in order to return only the number of pages that are present in the OnBase database, regardless of how many pages are in the multi-page TIF file in the OnBase disk groups.

column6: Epic Integration Scanning: Resolved an issue in OnBase that was uncovered when Epic introduced a change to the FHIRCast process.
column7:
Resolved an issue where the SAS or FOS application would close with an error message shortly after loading. In FOS, the debuglog.txt file would log an error similar to the following: "Error processing FhirCast event. Error received invalid response when sending heartbeat."

The issue has been been resolved. It was discovered due to a change Epic introduced to the FHIRCast process. FHIRCast is used during integrated scanning to maintain a heartbeat connection between Epic and the OnBase scanning application while there is an active scanning session. While Epic's change aligns closely with FHIR industry standards, it exposed a bug in the OnBase code that broke the FHIRCast connection. The issue was not caused by Epic's change but was uncovered because of it.

column6: Fixed issue in Unity Forms document locks, which were not released correctly in the Unity Client.
column7: Users will now be able to properly work with Unity Forms document locks.

column6: Addressed the Workflow autofill execution slowness issue
column7: Improved Workflow autofill execution with enhanced functionality for "Expand Autofill" and "Check Autofill." Users will now experience improvement in execution time  when expanding selections, and improved validation checks ensure that autofilled data is consistent and reliable.

column6: A bug that caused Analysts to be inadvertently logged out of DeficiencyPop has been corrected
column7: An issue has been corrected that caused an Analyst user to be logged out of DeficiencyPop when they hit "Enter" after selecting the responsible Physician field while creating a deficiency.

column6: Resolved a performance issue related to Batch Indexing and Release of Information
column7: A performance issue related to Saving and Transitioning batches where the 'Append' action has occurred has been resolved.  This specific issue was experienced by customers who also leveraged Release of Information and the Virtual Print Driver listener to assist in releasing content external to OnBase.

column6: Addressed an issue with re-indexng documents with custom File format
column7: Users were unable to re-index custom image file format to regular image file format. Now Documents can be reconciled to the standard/built-in file format after being imported to a Custom File Format

column6: DeficiencyPop now supports printing when launched from Epic via the SMART on FHIR integration.
column7: Fixed printing OnBase documents from DeficiencyPop when integrated with Epic via SMART on FHIR launches.

column6: Resolved issue when scanning in the registration workflow via the SMART on FHIR integration for Epic Hyperdrive.
column7: The Integrated Scanning integration now links documents to the correct encounter when launching from one encounter but scanning to different encounter. An example of this workflow is when the scanning client is launched from a surgical encounter but the document is being scanned to the pre-admission encounter.

column6: Resolved issues with the HL7 Back-End Job Processor when using multiple services to process jobs concurrently.
column7: We identified and resolved several issues that could cause the HL7 Back-End Job Processor to stop processing jobs when more than one service was configured to process jobs concurrently.

column6: Addressed a performance issue in OnBase Studio when configuring the Send Notification action in Workflow.
column7: Fixed a performance issue with OnBase Studio when setting up the Send Notification action within Workflow. Previously, a database with a large number of users caused OnBase Studio to freeze when selecting the User recipient type from the Type drop-down list in the Recipients tab.

column6: Fixed an issue with open form template dialog which failed to display existing list of template
column7:
Fixed an issue with open form template dialog which failed to display existing list of template .
Users will now be able to view list of templates when they try to open form template from Open form template dialog

column6: Addressed an issue with importing large documents
column7: When importing larger documents import button was available prior to file being automatically set. This has been rectified.

column6: Addressed an issue where user is unable to import PDF documents into folder within the Web Client without selecting the Document Type first.
column7: Users are now able to import a file to a folder, and Import button is no longer greyed out when tries to without selecting a document type first.

column6: Addressed an issue with Workflow logging for security overrides
column7: Fixed an issue with Workflow logging for security overrides, which sometimes caused the application server to terminate unexpectedly.

column6: FIXED: Unity client may crash when dragging and dropping an email attachment to a WV object.
column7: The issue occurred when dragging an attachment from an email directly a WorkView object to attach it to the static folder. An error would be displayed and after that, the client would close.

column6: FIXED: Data for an encrypted attribute may be stored unencrypted in the database.
column7:
Fixed an issue that removed encryption for an encrypted attribute.

This occured when a new encrypted attribute was created, then modified (i.e. the name was changed), before configuration was saved. If that corrupt configuration was exported, it could be imported into another system. If that happens, data stored in the database would be stored in clear text.

column6: Resolved a condition where Client Certificate authentication cannot be used with the Optimized Soap Service Client.
column7: When Client Certificate authentication is used, typically with Unity API Scripts, the Optimized Soap Service Client does not function as expected. The behavior can easily been seen in Unity Scripts, as the Optimized Soap Service Client does not pass Client Credentials to the HTTPWebRequest in the SerializationContext.GetContext method. The result was that Unity Scripts created using the OptimizedSoapServiceClient receive a 403 forbidden. This has now been addressed and the Optimized Soap Service Client functions as expected when using Client Certificate authentication.

column6: Addressed an issue where only a single secondary keyword is expanded from the "matching instances" of the primary if the secondary values are non-unique
column7: Now for an Autofill even if the secondary values of the "matching instances" are non-unique all matching rows are expanded.

column6: The list of queues appearing in the Queue Name drop-down menu are now alphabetized
column7: The list of queue names was not appearing in an alphabetized order. With this fix, queues in the Queue Name drop-down menu are ordered alphabetically. The changes were made in the following layouts: Analysis, Coding, Analysis Admin, and Coding Admin.

column6: A correction has been made to the privileges required to Open or Create ROI Requests
column7: Release of Information was incorrectly checking for the 'Chart Search' privilege when users attempted to Open or Create a ROI Request from the ROI layout.  This requirement has been removed as the 'Chart Search' privilege should only be required for users who are creating ROI Requests from Chart Search.

column6: Reduced file size for CEFSharp
column7: Recent upgrades of the CEFSharp browser control caused additional files to be added making deployment sizes larger. The size has been reduced again.

column6: WorkView Attribute Validation Issue with Filter Dataset
column7:
In a View with an attribute set using a WorkView Filter dataset, an "Invalid dataset value" error occurred when more than 50 results shared the same value. This issue arose both when validating by manually typing in the value and when selecting it from a Lookup Filter. In previous versions (EP3), manual typing would fail, but selecting via the Lookup would validate correctly.
Users were unable to set proper values within the dataset, forcing them to allow values outside the dataset to proceed, thus compromising data integrity.
This defect has been resolved, restoring the functionality to validate values correctly within the dataset, even when there are more than 50 results with the same value.

column6: Addressed an issue where an unclear error message was displayed for a user attempting to access a locked document.
column7: Addressed an issue when one user has a document locked but another user attempts to import a revision of the same document, an incorrect error message is thrown, which causes confusion for the users. Now the error message given out to the user is: "The Document is locked and cannot be modified. Please try again later."

column6: Resolved issue with HL7 message end bytes not being read properly.
column7: The HL7 Listener now properly checks for the entire end byte sequence in order to ensure the end of a message has been reached. Prior to this fix, it was possible in very rare conditions for an HL7 message end byte sequence to be improperly read, which would cause the HL7 Listener to shut down the connection unexpectedly.

column6: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Advanced Document Import Processor creating empty documents.
column7: Prior to this fix, when a Document Type was configured with a default non-image File Format (e.g., PDF, Text, etc.), or was mapped in the HL7 Advanced Document Import Process to a non-image File Format, it was possible for the HL7 Listener to create 0kb files in the disk groups. This only occurred when using a Path Reference in the field mapped to $$FILEDATA. This could occur when either, A) no file path was specified in the HL7 message for the field mapped to $$FILEDATA, or B) there was no file in the directory specified by the file path in the field mapped to $$FILEDATA.

column6: Corrected an issue with IdP login when multiple service locations are configured in the Office Business Application configuration files.
column7: Login via IdP now works correctly when multiple service locations are configured in the Office Business Application configuration files. The login happens automatically when an Office document is opened from the Unity client and the Office Business Applications are installed. The login also happens automatically when an Office document is opened from the Web client and Desktop Host is configured to open the Office Business Applications.

column6: Addressed an issue when in the Batch Indexing Context and viewing a document, when attempting to perform a Send To | Create New Document, the feature is unavailable from the context menu.
column7: Now in Batch Indexing, when a user right-clicks an image or text document from the batch that has a Document Type and selects the Send To option, Create New Document is one of the options presented. When Create New Document is selected, the user is able to create a copy of the page being indexed as a separate document.

column6: Association class may throw an error if an object from one side of the association is missing from the database.
column7:
An error "System.AgrumentNullException" may be thrown when an object is viewed with an embedded filter for an association class and the object on the other side of the association is missing from the database.

The error can also happen in other areas of the software where associations are used (i.e. Security Attributes). Most likely it can happen with external classes when an external object is removed from the database without removing the orphaned association.

While the error is removed, it is still considered best practice to remove orphaned association classes.

column6: Resolved an issue with the keep alive configuration for the HL7 Listener and HL7 Auto Sender.
column7: The HL7KeepAliveSeconds keep alive INI configuration setting did not work as intended and has been replaced by the HL7KeepAliveTimeout INI setting. The HL7KeepAliveTimeout INI setting works the same as the KeepAliveTimeout setting in the Hyland Message Engine configuration file. Setting to 0 disables keep alive traffic from being sent. Setting to any positive integer defines the number of minutes between each keep alive packet sent by the HL7 service.

column6: Documents imported via the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor are now stored on multiple disk group mass storage copies.
column7: When importing documents that are assigned to a disk group that has multiple mass storage copies configured, the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor will now store the documents on all available mass storage copies instead of just Copy 1.

column6: Resolved issue with escaped return and new line sequences in Base64 not being handled properly by the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor.
column7: An issue was resolved with the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor not handling escaped return and new line sequences in Base64. Including these characters in Base64 is called line wrapping and would be \r\n (CRLF) characters, which would be escaped as \X0D\\X0A\ in an HL7 message. See the HL7 documentation for best practices regarding line wrapping.

column6: Corrected issue with the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic that could cause an error to appear even though the document loaded successfully.
column7:
Resolved an issue where an Epic error could display, stating that a document failed to load even though the document successfully loaded in the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration. 

Note: This does not address any potential issue with the SMART on FHIR integration viewer. If an issue exists with the SMART on FHIR integration, a separate software change will be completed to address it.

column6: Resolved issue with cache files not being cleaned up by the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic.
column7: An issue was identified and resolved with the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic where the WebView2 would continue to grow with each use. After this fix, the WebView2 cache should remain fairly consistent in size rather than continuing to grow.

column6: Corrected an issue with document preview during import when using the Integrated Office Viewer.
column7: Document preview now works as expected when previewing an Office document during import and the Integrated Office Viewer is enabled.

column6: Fixed an issue with the recording type selection not being visible when "Use Voting" option was enabled after resetting the media recording type.
column7: The media recording type selection is now visible when restarting a meeting after having reset the Recording type to "None" whether or not the "Use Voting" option is enabled.

column6: Resolved issue with cache files not being cleaned up by the DeficiencyPop for Epic COM Integration.
column7: An issue was identified and resolved with the DeficiencyPop for Epic (DP4E) COM Integration where the WebView2 would continue to grow with each use. After this fix, the WebView2 cache should remain fairly consistent in size rather than continuing to grow.

column6: Fixed an issue when executing a custom action "Highlight Element" on elements of other pages in a Unity Form then second page was not getting highlighted.
column7: Highlight Element custom actions now works fine with multi page form

column6: Path Reference HL7 Advanced Document Import processes no longer leave file in the directory once it is imported.
column7: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Advanced Document Import process leaving document files in the Path Reference directory once the import was complete.

column6: The Healthcare Web Viewer now properly prompts to download Office documents.
column7: When the Integrated Office Viewer is not configured in an OnBase environment, web-based clients such as the Healthcare Web Viewer will default to prompting to download the Office (such as Word and Excel) document, so that it can be viewed externally. This functionality was broken in the OnBase 22.1 Healthcare Web Viewer and has been corrected.

column6: The User Option "Open Microsoft Office documents using the Office Business Application" is now enabled for users with Microsoft Office 64bit installed.
column7: Users can now select or deselect the option to use the Office Business Applications when viewing Office documents. This option was previously disabled for users with Microsoft Office 64bit installed on their local machine.

column6: Fixed an issue where users were not able to edit unity forms if they had large document ID, i.e., more than 2B documents in their system
column7: Users will now be able to edit and print Unity Forms even if they have a large number of documents in their system.

column6: Fixed an issue with Unity Forms designer when opening form templates, filters are being held after they are cleared, and the Open Form Template window is closed until the Window is opened and closed again.
column7:
When opening Form Templates in the Unity Client Form Designer, Filters are being held after they are cleared, and the Open Form Template window is closed until the Window is opened and closed again.
Users will now be able to clear filters from the Forms Template dialog.

column6: Resolved race condition in the HL7 Back-end Job Processor when propagating patient demographics.
column7: A race condition was possible which could cause patient demographics not to propagate to all MRNs and Charts related to an MPI. This would, in turn, result in patient demographics not being updated on the related documents as well. A task force bulletin will be released with additional information. Please reach out to your first line of Support and reference HC-201497 to determine if your solution is impacted.

column6: An issue preventing Safari users from accessing the History and Property menus has been resolved.
column7: An issue preventing Safari users from accessing History and Properties has been resolved.  Prior to this fix, users attempting to access these menus would find that a new window would open but no information related to the content being accessed would be displayed.

column6: The Analysis layout will now display a message when no documents are available.
column7: A message will now display in the document viewer stating "No documents are available for analysis" when the user accesses a chart without documents.  Prior to this change, the user would see a set of ellipses, which made the application look as if it were still loading.

column6: Resolved an issue where the HL7 Listener could send negative acknowledgements (NACKs) with malformed HL7 after receiving HL7 messages with malformed content.
column7: When processing HL7 messages with malformed content, the HL7 Listener will now respond with properly formed HL7 content.

column6: The HL7 Auto Sender no longer gets stuck in a bad state when the connection is interrupted, or a timeout is reached waiting for an acknowledgement.
column7: Prior to this fix, it was possible for the HL7 Sender to get in a bad state when the connection was interrupted mid-message. This could also happen if the acknowledgement timeout was reached (see HL7 documentation on the -HL7WAITACKTIMEOUT switch). The sender would send invalid HL7 data and had to be restarted to resolve the issue. This issue only impacted OnBase 22.1 and higher.

column6: No Release Notes
Fixed a defect with font formatting in when Item summary sheet is generated.
column7: Sometimes, the last paragraph in a table lost the proper font format when the Item summary sheet was generated from a tag template. This has been corrected and the font formatting should be respected as expected.

column6: Fixed an issue when attempting to compose a document with templates having anchored images or embedded Excel tables
column7: Users are now able to compose documents with anchored images or embedded Excel tables.

column6: The issue has been fixed where in case of attempting to execute Ad-Hoc tasks on E-Forms, intermittent exception: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" was popping-up.
column7: When attempting to execute Ad-Hoc tasks on E-Forms, previously intermittent exception: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" was popping-up. It has been fixed and now onwards, executing Ad-Hoc tasks, there will not be any unwanted error pop-up.

column6: Resolved issue with Physician Acknowledgement stamps not being saved in the location that they were placed in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: Physician Acknowledgement stamps were moving to the upper left corner of the page they were placed upon closing the Healthcare Web Viewer. Stamps will now save correctly and remain in the location on the page they were placed.

column6: Corrected issue with the HL7 Patient Record Processor not applying patient confidentiality codes from incoming HL7 messages.
column7: An issue was discovered where the HL7 Patient Record Processor was not applying patient confidentiality codes when processing incoming HL7 messages. This affected additions, deletions, and updates to confidentiality codes at the patient level, when the Patient Record Processor processed the message. This issue did not impact the Medical Records Chart import processor, which would still apply confidentiality codes correctly at the chart and patient level when processing messages.

column6: Corrected issue with the HL7 Sender not logging acknowledgements.
column7: The HL7 Sender now correctly logs received acknowledgements to the export destination log file and/or the configured Document Type for sent messages.

column6: Resolved incorrect error being logged by the HL7 Sender when connection issues occur.
column7: In some circumstances, it was possible for the HL7 Sender to log the following error when connection issues occurred: "error formatting HL7 message". This error was misleading and has been corrected to only display if there is a problem formatting the outbound HL7 message.

column6: DeficiencyPop for Epic COM integration now launches successfully from Epic Hyperspace Classic.
column7: Resolved an issue with the DeficiencyPop for Epic COM integration not launching from Epic Hyperspace Classic. Note that this issue did not impact Epic Hyperdrive (COM or SMART on FHIR); only Hyperspace Classic COM integration was impacted.

column5: Public Access (Web Controls for Constituent Access)
column6: The document opens on the Safari browser without having to modify the browser's settings or use alternative browsers.
column7:
Previously, a defect hindered users accessing their city records on iPhones, resulting in greater difficulty compared to other methods. Users were required to either utilize an alternative browser or adjust Safari settings each time they wished to access a document, causing inconvenience.

The fix eliminates the need for users to resort to alternative browsers or make manual adjustments to Safari settings.

column6: FIXED: Unintended Clearing of Relationship Fields in Embedded Filters
column7: An issue has been fixed where saving filter data on an embedded filter could inadvertently clear the relationship between filter items and the base object. This problem was not reflected in the filter results, leading to unexpected behaviors and broken events.

column6: Fixed an issue with printing and send to file from hitlist for Unity Forms
column7: Users will now be able to print forms from the hitlist without any issue.

column6: OnBase Patient Window:  Fixed an issue where some right-click menu options caused the menu to close and error
Issues with AutoFill Keyword Sets using Unity Scripts were addressed.
column7: AutoFill Keyword Sets now populate keywords in core clients when using a Unity Script.

column6: Resolved an innocuous error that would be logged when importing non-medical record documents that were associated with the Medical Records Custom Query.
column7: This change resolved an object reference error that would be logged on the Application Server when uploading documents via Scan Acquistion Server. This only occurred when uploading documents indexed to a document type that was associated with the Medical Records Custom Query. This was an innocuous error - no issues actually occurred with the upload or the indexing of the imported document.

column6: Resolved issue with timeouts in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7:
The sessionTimeout attribute in the Hyland.Web.HealthcareWebViewer element in the Web Server web.config file is no longer used. The timeout attribute in the sessionState element is used instead. 

This was done to improve stability of the timeout functionality, particularly when Web Server or User Group Timeouts were also configured that were shorter than the Healthcare Web Viewer sessionTimeout. Prior to this fix, a shorter Web Server or User Group Timeout could be configured, and a user's session would be ended according to the timeout configuration, but the user was never notified of their session ending or prompted to extend their session. This could result in thumbnails failing to load (red X being displayed instead) and other actions on the document failing, such as attempting to view keywords, save page rotations, etc.

column6: The Medical Records Document Attachment Updater will no longer remove documents from closed charts.
column7:
Resolved an issue with the Medical Records Document Attachment Updater Unity Scheduler task.
The updater initially task removed documents from closed charts if it determined that required patients and/or chart identifiers were not present on the document. Currently, the updater task skips any documents associated with closed charts, as these documents cannot be modified, since they have a lock which cannot be removed.

column6: Resolved issue with the HL7 Sender when using the -HL7DEST switch and at least one Export Destination is configured to "Require Secure Connection Policy".
column7:
Prior to this fix, if one of the assigned Export Destinations was configured to "Require Secure Connection Policy", then the HL7 Auto Sender would require all assigned Export Destinations would need to be configured to "Require Secure Connection Policy". Alternatively, "Require Secure Connection Policy" could be disabled for all assigned Export Destinations. This would occur even when using the -HL7DEST switch to configure which Export Destination(s) the HL7 Sender would connect to. 

This fix ensures that secure connection policies are only required for Export Destinations that have "Require Secure Connection Policy" enabled.

column6: OnBase Patient Window: Fixed an issue where viewing revisions of a document was not working from the right-click menu
column7: Fixed an issue where viewing revisions of a document was not working from the right-click menu. Note that appropriate override privileges, based on document type, within OnBase Configuration are needed for these revisions to be available in OnBase Patient Window.

column6: The DeficiencyPop integration for Epic now shows all encounters associated with a deficiency episode in Analysis mode.
column7: Prior to this fix, the DeficiencyPop integration for Epic would only show the primary CSN for a deficiency episode when launched in Analysis mode. Now, DeficiencyPop correctly displays all encounters associated with the deficiency episode so that analysis can be performed on the related documents.

column6: Users passwords are updated successfully in the Web Client when the User Group has the Password Admin Configuration Right.
column7: Previously, passwords were not updated successfully when the User Group had the Password Admin Configuration Right. In the Web Client, after updating the password the Save button was inactive therefore the password change was not initiated.

column6: Improved performance for security attributes
column7: Fixed an issue where certain queries could cause a cast to varchar that wasn't necessary. The impact resulted in reduced query performance.

column6: Resolved issue when viewing some PDFs within Hyland Clinician Window.
column7: Updated the code, PDFs files are viewing correctly within Hyland Clinician Window.

column6: Unity Client tab order updated for External Autofill Expansion
column7: Tabbing after performing an External Autofill Expansion successfully targets the next expected keyword in the Keyword Panel

column6: Resolves a possible race condition in the HL7 Back-end Job Processor.
column7: Resolves a possible race condition in the HL7 backend Job Processor which could cause the Unity Scheduler Task to end up in an error status.

column6: Resolved high CPU usage issue in the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic.
column7: An issue that impacted some OnBase 22.1.13.1000 through 22.1.24.1000 environments was resolved. In these 22.1 builds, high CPU usage may occur on VM hosts and/or client workstations where the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic is used. The issue does not occur for all customers and cannot be replicated on demand. However, if higher than expected CPU usage is consistently occurring, it is recommended to upgrade to this or a later build of OnBase to resolve the issue.

column6: Resolved issue with Signature Deficiencies for Epic failing to launch more than once in a single Epic session.
column7: Starting in 22.1.15.1000, there was an issue that would cause SD4E to fail to launch successfully more than once during a single Epic session. This software change addresses that issue so that SD4E can be launched multiple times during the same session (sequentially, not concurrently).

column6: Addressed issue(s) with Workflow queue counts in the Unity Client
column7: Corrected an issue with Workflow queue counts when accessing a queue from a tile in the Unity Client. This fix ensures that the queue count is refreshed when accessing a Workflow queue from a tile while the Workflow interface is still open.

column6: Imported Platter Management Unity Scheduler Tasks were not visible in Unity Management Console
column7: An issue was found where any imported Platter Management Unity Scheduler tasks were then not showing up in the Unity Management Console. This has now been fixed so that any imported Platter Management scheduler task will then appear in the UMC.

column6: Resolved an issue when using the Send To | Create New Document action in Unity Client or Web Client where the original document's file type and size would not be retained.
column7: Updated the code to allow the Send To | Create New Document action in Unity Client or Web Client to retain the original document’s file type and size.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with authentication when logging into multiple domains
column7:
Fixed a defect in AD Enhanced SSO functionality, which occurred when AD Enhanced SSO is configured to allow OnBase logins from users in more than one AD Domain. 

Previously, an AD User with the same user name in more than one OnBase-associated AD Domain was only able to log in to OnBase using AD credentials from one of these domains. Attempts to login using AD credentials from any of the other domains failed.

After the fix, users with the same AD user name in more than one OnBase-associated AD Domain can successfully log in to OnBase using their AD credentials from any of these domain.

column6: Resolved issue when attempting to split jpeg documents within Document Separation (ad hoc or scan queue) in Unity Client. The new document(s) are converted to tiff and the file size on disk is inflated once saved.
column7: Update the code to allow split jpeg documents within Document Separation (ad hoc or scan queue) in Unity Client. The new document(s) retain the original document’s file type and size.

column6: The Scan Acquisition Server integration for Epic Hyperdrive now accurately retains the last-selected Document Type during indexing as well as the application window size and position
column7:
Prior to this fix, the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) retained neither the last-used Document Type during indexing nor the application window size and position that the user selected. These settings are now stored in the registry to ensure retention between launches of SAS. This primarily impacts the Hyperdrive integration for Epic (both COM and SMART on FHIR), as the Hyperspace integration managed the scan wrapper differently. So, Windows is now able to retain these settings between Hyperspace Classic SAS launches.

Please note that these settings will only be retained on the workstation/VM where the user is currently logged in.

column6: Copying and Deleting FHIR Mapping Templates now correctly copies or deletes the associated FHIR Paths within the mapping template.
column7:
Prior to this fix, FHIR Paths were not copied when a FHIR Mapping Template was copied, resulting in a FHIR Path being associated with both the original and copied Mapping Template. This meant that if a FHIR Path was modified in the original Mapping Template, the FHIR Path would also be modified in in the copied Mapping Template, and vice versa. 

Additionally, prior to this change, deleting a FHIR Mapping Template could result in FHIR Paths being orphaned in the database, as they were not deleted when the FHIR Mapping Template was deleted.

column6: Resolved a condition in Full-Text Search where indexing performance may be degraded for catalogs with many large documents.
Physician Acknowledgements in the Healthcare Web Viewer are saved after being placed instead of saving when the viewer is closing.
column7:
Hyland identified an issue where a browser can be exiting the Healthcare Web Viewer page so quickly, or in such a way that the save events triggered on close may not have a chance to execute properly. This could result in Physician Acknowledgements not saving as expected after being placed.

Since Physician Acknowledgements are now saved immediately after being dropped/placed, the acknowledgement note/text will no longer remain red until closing the Healthcare Web Viewer. Newly placed acknowledgements will turn blue, indicating that they have been saved.

column6: Version Stamp Dialog now displays the right Revision Number
column7: Previously version stamp dialog used to display the revision as one less than the actual value. Now this issue has been rectified.

column6: The Create PDF/TIFF action now renders images contained on HTML Forms
column7: Documents created by converting HTML forms to TIFFs render as they should when viewing the HTML form in Unity client, with the images included.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with queue count display in Workflow in the OnBase Client
column7: Fixed an issue to display correct queue count in Workflow in the OnBase Client. Previously, under certain circumstances the queue count did not display correctly for Workflow queues in the Thick Client.

column6: Fixed Defect: Workflow action error when creating a WorkView Notification that included the tag <<AttachAllDocumentsInFolder:Specifed Folder>>
column7: An issue was resolved where executing a Workflow action to create a WorkView Notification using the <<AttachAllDocumentsInFolder:Specified Folder>> function threw the following error: "Collection does not support finding items by name."

column6: Resolved issues when rendering some Old-Style JPEG images in OnBase.
column7: Updated the code and Jpg compressed tiffs are rendered correctly.

column6: Corrected an issue where accessing the "Customer Information Administration" within the Unity Client within an institutional environment was throwing an error
column7:
The Customer Information Administration window was failing with an error within institutional environments. This administration window is now accessible without error.
Previously, the error(s) seen were as follows:
"Parameter INSTITUIONS_PARAM was not in the correct format."
And Diagnostic Console showed:
"Institution string 410,0,412,413,3,414,4,2,415,411,1,5,99 was not in the expected format System.FormatException: Parameter INSTITUTIONS_PARAM was not in the correct format at Hyland.Core.ServiceHandlers.CustomerInformation.GetRecipientsForProcessClass()"

column6: Fixed an issue with Agenda Item Packets and Documents marked as Excluded from packet
column7: The issue was that if you marked a document to be excluded from the Agenda Item Packet, the previous supporting document would be duplicated in the item packet.  This has been fixed.

column6: Resolved an issue with the Scan Acquisition Server while sweeping Microsoft Office documents.
column7: When uploading a Microsoft Office document (Word, Excel, etc.) while the document is still open, the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) will no longer crash.

column6: Resolved issue with documents not linking to Epic Hyperdrive in specific SMART on FHIR integrated scanning workflows.
column7:
Prior to this fix, if Epic provided a document type in the initial SMART on FHIR context for scanning workflows that do not require scanning to that document type, any other document types selected by the user would not be linked in Epic. The only document type that would be linked in Epic is the document type that was included in the initial SMART on FHIR context. All other document types would not be sent to Epic for linking. 

Note: This issue is unlikely to occur in most solutions as Epic will usually not pass a document type in the SMART on FHIR context when a specific document type is not required by the scanning workflow.

column6: Resolved Healthcare Web Viewer printing issue for the SMART on FHIR Epic Hyperdrive Integration.
column7:
The Epic Hyperdrive SMART on FHIR scan viewer window does not allow browser PDF controls to be used to render PDF files, including print previews. This impacted the Healthcare Web Viewer - since print previews could not load, documents could not be printed. To resolve this issue, when printing a document, the Healthcare Web Viewer now launches an external window to load the PDF print preview, allowing the document to be printed. This change only impacts the SMART on FHIR integration - the COM integration for Hyperdrive and Hyperspace Classic will continue to successfully launch embedded print previews.

Note: Since the print preview is not contained within the scan viewer window, it is not automatically closed when the scan viewer window is closed. However, the print preview window will only be visible when viewing the patient for which it was launched and will close automatically when that patient context is closed in Epic.

column6: Resolved a memory issue with the Medical Records Orphan Documents Processor task.
column7: Under normal operating conditions, the Attachment Lock Processor that is part of the Orphan Document Processor works without any performance concerns. However, in certain customer environments, the Orphan Document Processor consumed large amounts of memory due to the number of rows in a specific database table. This fix resolves the issue by loading and processing the data from the table in smaller groups so that a large amount of memory is not consumed loading the entire table.

column6: "Open Microsoft Office documents in OBA" user setting is no longer being overridden when retrieving a document using the Outlook integration
License sharing fix
column7: Fixed an issue with license sharing that could result in a shared license being released prematurely, potentially causing downstream licensing errors.

column6: Notes word wrap restored
column7: Previously, stamp dialog used to display the revision as one less than the actual value. Now this issue has been rectified.

column6: Corrected an issue where .CR2 image files were unable to render in OBIMGLIB/Unity Client
