column7: An issue was corrected where .CR2 image files were unable to render in OBIMGLIB/Unity Client. Users would try to view images that were the .CR2 file type and the image would not render correctly. Users can now render these image types.

column6: Resolved a bug of signature being accepted as valid when user signs in the drop shadow.
column7:
Resolved an issue where signature is shown as valid even if no evidence of signature is shown when signature is applied in the drop shadow.
Users will now see a border beyond which user will not be able to sign.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with default column filters when navigating between life cycles
column7: Resolved an issue that caused default column filters to be removed when navigating between life cycles. Default column filters is now maintained when navigating between life cycles.

column6: Fixed: Importing data in Test Mode creates objects in the database.
column7: Fixed an issue where testing a data import in Studio would actually create active objects in the database.

column6: Fixed an issue where the conditional text wrapper tag was not working when using it with the item summary sheet Tag Templates.
column7: Conditional text wrapper tags can now be used in the item summary sheet Tag Templates.

column6: Resolved exceptions that caused the HL7 Back-end Job Processor to fail to load or process jobs.
column7: Fixed an issue with the HL7 Back-end Job Processor that caused it to fail to load or process jobs.

column6: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Back-End Job Processor when shutting down.
column7: Resolved an issue where the HL7 Back-End Job Processor could crash when shutting down, instead of shutting down gracefully.

column6: Resolved HL7 Listener crash for the Advanced Document Processor when the segment containing the document data is not present.
column7: The HL7 Listener no longer crashes when executing the Advanced Document Processor for a message that is missing the segment where OnBase expects the document data to be. This will occur when the message template assigned to the Advanced Document Processor HL7 import process has a field mapped to $$FILEDATA and the incoming message does not contain the segment with the mapped field.

column6: Declined envelopes will now be moved to the Completed envelopes list.
column7: When the status of the envelope is polled and returns with the status of Declined or Deleted, the envelope will be moved to the Completed Envelopes list in the DocuSign Administration portion of the Unity Client.

column6: An issue saving E-Forms created through the Outlook integration for OnBase has been resolved.
column7: E-Forms can now be saved as expected when using the Outlook integration for OnBase.

column6: Corrected an issue where note and standard not markups created in OnBase 17.0.1 would not display after upgrading to OnBase 21.1+
column7: Old note and standard note markups from OnBase 17.0.a and prior will now display in an OnBase 21.1 and above system.

column6: FIXED: Unable to enter 'Edit Mode' on an embedded filter.
column7: Fixed an issue where it was not possible to interact with the toolbar button on an embedded filter. For example, it was not possible to enable the 'edit' mode on the filter or add objects.

column6: Improved data validation for newer operating systems
column7: Improved data validation for the newer operating systems. Previously validation errors may have occurred in a variety of languages that may have prevented an object from being saved correctly.

column6: Corrected an issue causing XML Query Placeholders to inadvertently close Document Composition templates.
column7: When using copy/paste/delete/undo and redo on an XML query placeholder, the template may occasionally close without changes being saved. This issue was fixed.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with display of System Tasks in the Web Client
column7: Updated the user interface that displays System Tasks in the Web Client. Previously, a large number of system tasks caused the list to be cut off.

column6: The Healthcare Web Viewer now automatically saves note changes when being closed.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer no longer prompts to save note changes when being closed, if notes have unsaved changes. This results in a smoother experience for users while ensuring notes are still saved.

column6: The Healthcare Web Viewer now displays unburned Physician Acknowledgements for users that have View privileges.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer now displays unburned Physician Acknowledgements for users that have View privileges to the MedRec-Physician Acknowledgement note type. This brings the Healthcare Web Viewer into alignment with how the legacy ActiveX Epic integration viewer behaved.

column6: The Front Office Scanning COM integration for Epic now supports the "AllowLinkTypes" setting.
column7: The Front Office Scanning (FOS) COM integration for Epic now properly supports the "AllowLinkTypes" setting in the EpicIntegrations.config file. Previously, only the "PreventLinkTypes" and "PreventEpicLinks" settings were supported. This change brings the functionality of the FOS COM integration into parity with that of the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS).

column6: Resolved issue with the Web Blob Pass-through failing to update HTML/E-Forms.
column7: The Web Blob Pass-through will now update documents successfully, regardless of the file format/MIME type.

column6: The Integration for Epic installer now correctly copies the EpicIntegrations.config file if selected during installation.
column7: The Integration for Epic installer now correctly copies the EpicIntegrations.config file to the Front Office Scanning (FOS) program file directory, if selected during installation.

column6: Resolved issue with the Healthcare Web Viewer failing to display E-form documents.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer now displays E-form documents correctly. This issue impacted the standalone Healthcare Web Viewer (launched in a browser) as well as the COM/SMART on FHIR integrations for Epic.

column6: The HL7 Listener will now respond with a negative acknowledgement when processing ASCII-encoded HL7 messages containing non-ASCII characters.
column7: Prior to this change, the HL7 Listener would close the connection instead of responding with a NACK. This resulted in backlogs in HL7 interfaces, and no information was provided by the HL7 Listener to explain the cause of the issue. Now, the NACK will contain a reason, so the issue can be addressed in the interface (e.g., skipping the message, moving the message to an error queue, correcting the message encoding, etc.).

column6: Addressed issue(s) with transaction log when using the Create PDF/TIFF document workflow action
column7: Resolved issue(s) with transaction log when using the Create PDF/TIFF document workflow action. Create PDF/TIFF now logs the Document Handle of the original document and the new document. Previously, the transaction log showed only the Document Handle of the existing document.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with cursor position when using the Workflow action, Display HTML Form
column7: Corrected cursor position when using the Workflow action, Display HTML Form. This fix ensures that the cursor position is automatically in an input field when executing the Display HTML Form action.

column6: Corrected an issue causing XML Query Placeholders to inadvertently close Document Composition templates.
column7: When using copy/paste/delete/undo and redo on an XML query placeholder, the template may occasionally close without changes being saved. This issue was fixed.

column6: Resolved issue causing inaccurate reporting of document quantity in batches that contain documents with Security Keywords.
column7: Users previously encountered an issue after using Pre-Index or Full-Index Scan Mode to import batches that contain security keywords on the Document Type. When users viewed the imported batches, the interface erroneously reported a document quantity of 0. This has been resolved to display the correct number of documents in the batch.

column6: Resolved issue with the Acknowledge button text displaying incorrectly after placing an acknowledgement in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: Prior to this fix, the Acknowledge button text would display below the button icon in the Healthcare Web Viewer toolbar after placing an acknowledgement. Now, the button text will display properly.

column6: Resolved issue with Epic Welcome Kiosk not automatically registering the license on workstations.
column7: The Workstation Client for Epic Welcome Kiosk license will now correctly be automatically registered on workstations where Epic Welcome Kiosk is used for the first time, as long as there is an available license.

column6: Resolved issue where an error was received after indexing a batch using a Unity Form Keyword Panel when returning to Batch Processing with Auto Load Next Batch set.
column7: When attempting to fully index a document in Batch Processing with Unity Forms and Auto Load Next Batch, users were receiving the CefSharp error.  This has been resolved.

column6: Web Services Publishing will now correctly produce PDF/A-1B documents through Document Composition.
column7: An error was corrected that caused Web Services Publishing to produce a standard-PDF instead of PDF/A-1B.

column6: Multiline textboxes on Unity Forms now displays properly when used as part of a Workflow filter
column7: Multiline textboxes on Unity Forms now displays properly when used as part of a Workflow filter. Previously, word-wrap did not trigger, causing some texts to not be visible.

column6: Objects are opening successfully from the calendar within the Web Client
column7: Objects are opening successfully from the calendar within the Web Client in any browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox). This works successfully for any view (month, week, day) and format (date only, date + time) of the calendar.

column6: External Classes with no Unique or Component Key index Attributes configured are being refreshed properly in Unity Client
column7: External Classes with no Unique or Component Key index Attributes configured generate the expected filter count with no error when refreshed in Unity Client

column6: The Test System Creation Utility (TSCU) will now correctly copy values in the gender database table.
column7: The Test System Creation Utility (TSCU) will now correctly copy values in the gender database table. This software change will not correct issues with the gender database table in environments that were created by a prior version of the TSCU.

column6: Removed duplication of default font store location during Datalogics initialization
column7: If the default font store location is initialized manually, the LST font cache file can grow in size over time, causing performance issues in the application server.

column6: Addressed issue in failing to connect with Document Tracking Service.
column7: The Document Tracking Service now connects to the Application Server without displaying an error in the Diagnostics Console.  This allows the service to connect successfully and create records.

column6: Resolved issue with the Description not being retained by the Scan Acquisition Server SMART on FHIR integration for Epic.
column7: The Description is now properly indexed to documents uploaded to OnBase via the Scan Acquisition Server SMART on FHIR integration for Epic. Prior to this fix, the Description would be ignored and would not be added to the document keywords.

column6: Resolved an unhandled script exception in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: Resolved the following unhandled script exception in the Healthcare Web Viewer: "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'addEventListener')". This error would not be displayed to users, but would be visible in diagnostics logging output by Hyland.Logging when Trace logging is enabled.

column6: Fixed an intermittent timing issue where changes to an office document would sometimes not be saved when using the Integrated Office Viewer.
column7: Revisions will now always be created when editing an office document using the Integrated Office Viewer.

column6: Fixed an issue with checked out DocuSign documents failing to come back into OnBase when signed in DocuSign.
column7: The document checkout/document lock are now cleared right before the revision is made for the signed document allowing the sign document revision to happen successfully.

column6: Fixed issue where Template Role Mapping was incorrectly displaying the wrong DocuSign Template selected within OnBase Configuration.
column7: The correct DocuSign Template will now be displayed in Template Role Mapping within OnBase Configuration when a template is selected.

column6: Resolved memory error when that could occur when printing large documents directly from Epic Hyperspace Classic via the COM integration.
column7: Resolved memory error when that could occur when printing large documents directly from Epic Hyperspace Classic via the COM integration. Note: This does not affect the Hyperdrive COM or SMART on FHIR integration for Epic.

column6: Double-clicking notes in the Healthcare Web Viewer no longer attempts to open cross-references.
column7: Double-clicking notes in the Healthcare Web Viewer now opens the note instead of attempting to open cross-referenced documents.

column6: Corrected an issue with the Integration for Epic installer when attempting to add additional features/options.
column7: Corrected an issue with the Integration for Epic installer when attempting to add additional features/options to an existing installation. If a scanning feature (i.e., Scan Acquisition Server or Front Office scanning) was already installed, and an attempt was made to add the Healthcare Web Viewer or DeficiencyPop for Epic feature, selecting the "Next" button in the installer would not progress to the next step in the install process as expected.

column6: Resolved issue(s) with Mailbox Importer importing duplicate documents
column7: Resolved an issue where Mailbox Importer imported duplicate documents because of not marking emails as read. Previously, duplicate documents were loaded in OnBase from email through Mailbox Importer. This caused numerous duplicate documents to appear in Brainware and OnBase.

column6: Corrected an issue displaying property names in the Workflow action "Display HTML Form."
column7: Property names will now display correctly immediately after saving the action.

column6: Fixed issue which prompted a message 'System.OutOfMemoryException' in the Form Designer
column7: User will now be able to load a large Unity Form in the Form Designer and 'System.OutOfMemoryException' will not be displayed.

column6: Resolved an issue on Unity Forms with multiple pages for submitting Forms on Enter key press
column7: Unity Form wasn't submitted on pressing the Enter key if more than one page was added to it. This issue is now fixed.

column6: Sporadic 403 error no longer occurs when attempting to edit and publish a Unity Form in the Forms Designer when using a Load Balancer
column7: When configured to to use a Load Balancer, editing and publishing a Unity Form in the Forms Designer in the Unity Client would sporadically produce a 403 error. This issue is now fixed.

column6: Resolved the bug related to error reading "There was an error saving the form. XXXX Unable to set the Keyword value for one or more form fields" when the user attempted to send a Unity Form with missing keywords
column7:
Resolved the bug which appeared when users attempted to submit a Unity Form which has a missing keyword from an AutoFill Keyword Set, and the secondary keyword is marked as required. User was prompted with  "There was an error saving the form. XXXX Unable to set the Keyword value for one or more form fields".
Users are now able to submit a Unity Form without any error which has a missing keyword from an AutoFill Keyword Set and the secondary keyword is marked as required.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with notifications sent from Workflow Approval Management
column7: Corrected an issue that caused an exception when sending a notification from Workflow Approval Management. Email notifications are now properly sent even when a document has no previous approvers.

column6: Resolved issue(s) with sorting of WorkView attributes with a currency data-type
column7: WorkView attributes with a currency data-type are now sorted numerically when used on a Workflow inbox filter. Previously, the sorting was alphanumeric.

column6: Addressed issue(s) related to incorrect document display in the Workflow Web Client
column7: Addressed issue(s) related to incorrect document display in the Workflow Web Client. Previously and in very rare instances and with only one document left in a user's queue, the Web client displayed a related item for the previously worked document.

column6: Resolved error encountered when rotating pages in the Unity Client Document Viewer.
column7: When viewing a multi-page document in the Document Viewer, Unity users sometimes encountered the following error: “Value was either too large or too small for an Int32.” This occurred when selecting the thumbnail of any page other than the first one and attempting to rotate it. After bypassing the error multiple times, the Unity Client sometimes became unresponsive. This issue has been resolved.

column6: Fixed an issue that prevented meeting searches in the Agenda to Go iPad app
column7: Starting with the initial release build of 22.1, meeting search in the Agenda To Go iPad app was not working. An older OnBase Mobile Broker version (i.e 21.1.x) would need to be used as a workaround to the problem. This defect has been fixed and meeting search in Agenda to Go is now working.

column6: Issue fixed for inherited keyword with Multi Instance Keywords set up for Document Packaging in the Web Client and Unity Client
column7: Multi Instance Keywords (MIKGs) set up for Document Packaging in the Web Client and Unity Client now inherit all values of the primary document keyword field.

column6: Resolved an issue where word wrap was not respected for multi-line text fields when Unity and Image Forms are converted to or displayed as an image
column7: Users will now be able to view the entire content of the multi-line text fields correctly when Unity and Image Forms are converted to or displayed as an image.

column6: Resolved issue where clicking help button did not launch help file
column7: Resolved an issue where clicking the help button failed to launch the Application Client Connector help file. This fix allows users to access the help documentation from within the software, as expected.

column6: Updated Application Enabler's "Retrieve Document" Web Client context to launch in Microsoft Edge
column7: Updated Application Enabler's "Retrieve Document" context in the Web Client to launch automatically in Microsoft Edge. This allows the context to continue to function as expected in Windows 11, which does not support the previous behavior of launching in Internet Explorer.

column6: .Combined Viewer now supports searching for Workview Objects when an attribute is mapped to multiple keywords
column7: When an attribute on a Workview Object was mapped to more than one keyword, the Combined Viewer previously may not have executed the desired search.

column6: DeficiencyPop Display Column Configuration is now available for solutions licensed for Signature Deficiencies for Epic
column7: DeficiencyPop Display Column Configuration is now available for solutions licensed for Signature Deficiencies for Epic.  Prior to this change this configuration required the Medical Records Management Solutions license to be present.  This prevented the Display Column configuration from being accessible to customers with solutions that use Signature Deficiencies for Epic and not Medical Records Management Solution.

column6: Changed settings for Front Office Scanning debug logging when launched via Epic Integration Scanning.
column7: The WriteLog setting in the EpicIntegrations.config file now determines whether Front Office Scanning (FOS) writes logs. When enabled, FOS writes logs to the location specified in the Diskgroup Path setting. These are the same settings used by the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS). Starting with this build, the debugging instructions for FOS are now the same as the SAS instructions. See “Writing Events to the Debug Log” in the “Debug Modes” section of the Integration for Epic documentation. For builds prior to this release, continue to follow the instructions in “Running the Front Office Scanning Integration in Debug Mode.”

column6: The Document Viewer within the Folder layout no longer becomes stuck on a certain document when a user creates a Revision or Rendition
column7: Within the Unity Client, when creating a Revision or Rendition from a re-index action within the Folder layout, the Folder Viewer could become frozen on a certain document forcing the user to re-launch the client

column6: Resolved issue with re-indexing documents retrieved by custom query in the Front Office Scanning COM integration for Epic.
column7: Front Office Scanning (FOS) supports re-indexing documents retrieved via Custom Query. Prior to this fix, when the following criteria were met, FOS would not re-index the document to the selected document type. The criteria for this issue to occur were, A) the "ask" document type button was used to re-index the document, B) the document type name selected from the drop-down menu contained lowercase letters, and C) the document type did not have its own button configured in the EPICFOSCONFIG.xml file. FOS now correctly re-indexes documents regardless of the document type name containing lower or uppercase letters.

column6: Full-Text documents would run into a situation where they would get stuck in the 'In Progress' state. They will now progress through the states correctly and complete processing.
Resolved issue with the Dump to File HL7 Import Process intermittently causing the HL7 Listener to stop unexpectedly.
column7: Prior to this fix, the following error could occur intermittently when the HL7 Listener was executing a Dump to File process: "Unable to process message due to lock collision". This would cause the HL7 Listener service to stop.

column6: Resolved race condition with the HL7 Sender.
column7: Corrected a race condition that could cause the HL7 Sender to erroneously report “HL7 Bad Ack Message”. In addition, verbose logging was updated to properly log the message received as well as any errors found when interpreting the response.

column6: Resolved issue with the Add Correction button text displaying incorrectly after the corrections form is displayed in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: Prior to this fix, the Add Correction button text would partially disappear and display below the button icon in the Healthcare Web Viewer toolbar when the corrections form was displayed. Now, the button text will display properly.

column6: Resolved an issue with the Integration for Epic installer when deploying a pre-configured Epic FOS configuration file.
column7: Corrected the file path stored in the registry when deploying a pre-configured Epic Front Office Scanning (FOS) configuration file in the Integration for Epic installer. Prior to this change, a trailing backslash ("\") would be added to the file path for the EPICFOSCONFIG.xml file, which would cause FOS to be unable to locate the file correctly.

column6: Resolved session loss issue with the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic when multiple viewer windows are open at the same time.
column7: Prior to this fix, if multiple instances of the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic were open at the same time, closing one of the windows would cause the session to be lost for all other open viewer windows. This would cause issues when viewing page thumbnails or performing other document actions such as displaying document properties or keywords.

column6: The current page now updates correctly in the Page Status dialog in Resolute in Epic Hyperdrive when using the COM Healthcare Web Viewer integration for Epic.
column7: The current page now updates correctly in the Page Status dialog in Resolute in Epic Hyperspace Classic when scrolling between pages or selecting different page thumbnails in the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic. Prior to this fix, the current page would be off by 1, as the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration was using a 0-based page numbering schema, instead of 1-based.

column6: The current page now updates correctly in the Page Status dialog in Resolute in Epic Hyperspace Classic when using the COM Healthcare Web Viewer integration for Epic.
column7: The current page now updates correctly in the Page Status dialog in Resolute in Epic Hyperspace Classic when scrolling between pages or selecting different page thumbnails in the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic.

column6: Overlays can now be printed from the Unity Client when using an institutional database
column7: When an overlay was applied to a document type within an individual institution, and if there are multiple institutions with different overlay settings applied to the same document type, there was no option to apply that overlay to a desired document being printed

column6: Resolved issue preventing the Virtual Print Driver from importing documents into Unity Client.
column7: Users were unable to use the Virtual Print Driver on workstations without the Microsoft Visual C++ 2010/2012 redistributable files installed. This issue has been resolved.

column6: The DocPop hitlist refreshes after re-indexing a document.
column7: DocPop hitlist with Custom Query results correctly refreshes and loads once a document has been re-indexed within the web client.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with keywords on related documents
column7: Resolved an issue where keywords were not updated on related documents when security keywords were in use. So, when a Workflow process searches for related items to perform keyword updates, security keywords are now ignored, in-line with other Workflow overrides.

column6: Addressed issue(s) related to the execution of the Workflow action, "Replace Keyword"
column7: The Workflow action, "Replace Keyword" will no longer re-trigger Full-Text listener. Prior to this change, this Workflow action incorrectly caused documents to be re-processed by the Full-Text listener.

column6: Fixed the issue when the Select List drop-down arrow on Unity Forms was misaligned with the text box
column7: Users will now be able to view the Select List drop-down arrow aligned properly with the text box.

column6: Fixed issue for inherited keywords for Currency and Masking for Document Packaging
column7: Users can generate a package in the Web Client or Unity Client with inherited keywords for Currency and/or Masking keyword types.

column6: Resolved an issue where memory leaks would occur when attempting to render annotations on PDF documents.
column7: Resolved a memory leak when Annotations were being burned into a document being saved to file. Customers were only able to process about 3300 documents at a time until they would receive a GDI error message. Now we are able to reach the full GDI limit of over 26000 documents.

column6: Resolved an issue with deficiencies not remaining in the location they were last placed through DeficiencyPop.
column7: Resolved issue where deficiencies that were originally placed in Signature Deficiencies for Epic, then later moved through DeficiencyPop for Epic, would not remain in the location they were last placed in DeficiencyPop.

column6: Resolved issue causing the cursor not to return to the current batch after completing work in the Scan Queue Layout in Batch Processing.
column7: Previously, users sometimes encountered an issue in Batch Processing that occurred when multiple users were working in the same scan queue at the same time. When returning to the Scan Queue Layout after completing a process on a particular batch, the cursor sometimes pointed to a different batch, causing confusion for the end user. This occurred when another user was creating a batch in the same scan queue. This issue has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue preventing successful launching of integrated scanning from Epic via SMART on FHIR.
column7: Corrected a URL decoding issue with FHIRCast, which is used for communication with Epic Hyperdrive when launching Front Office Scanning (FOS) via the SMART on FHIR integration with Epic. This issue prevented FOS from successfully launching and has now been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue causing a communication loss error to incorrectly display when using Epic Integrated Scanning via the SMART on FHIR integration.
column7:
When performing integrated scanning with Front Office Scanning or Scan Acquisition Server, the following error message appeared intermittently: "Communication with Epic has been lost. The application will now exit, work will not be saved."

Contrary to this statement, the document was successfully uploaded to OnBase and linked in Epic. The issue causing this incorrect error message has been resolved.

column6: The Healthcare Web Viewer can now retain the last used zoom level when displaying documents.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer can now retain the last used zoom level when displaying documents. This behavior can be overriden by the zoomLevelOR variable in the appsettings element, in the Viewer Vars section, in the Web Server web.config. If no value is specified for the zoomLevelOR variable, the Healthcare Web Viewer displays documents at the last zoom level at which the user viewed a document.

column6: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Back-End Job Processor creating duplicate standard AutoFill Keyword Sets.
column7: Prior to this fix, the HL7 Back-End Job Processor could incorrectly create duplicate Standard AutoFill Keyword Set rows. This could occur when a secondary keyword in one AutoFill Keyword Set is being updated by an HL7 AutoFill Keyword Set Merge Processor, and that keyword is also a primary keyword in another keyset. When performing updates via reverse-lookup, the HL7 Back-End Job Processor did not verify if the primary value being updated already existed as a primary value in another keyset. This has been corrected.

column6: Resolved issue with the HL7 Listener not triggering updates to MRN-based and Chart-based AutoFill Keyword Sets.
column7: Prior to this fix, when the Medical Records Chart Processor processed an HL7 message that was intended to update MRN or Chart-level keywords, a job was not queued for the HL7 Back-end Job Processor to perform that work. Therefore, those keywords were not updated on documents that utilize the MRN and Chart-based AutoFill Keyword Sets. A technical bulletin should be available on Community related to this issue. If you have any additional questions or concerns, please contact Hyland Technical Support and reference Jira # HC-173178.

column6: Fixed an issue with the Esri login window not loading. and removed the non functional default base map URL.
column7: The Esri login screen now loads properly and the provided default BaseMapUrl was removed because it was no longer functional. The BaseMapUrl option now defaults to blank and will retrieve a functioning default base map from Esri if left blank.

column6: fixed an issue that would cause an application server crash when attempting to load an excel file that was too large in the integrated office viewer.
column7: When using integrated office viewer, users will now see a message display explaining the file size limitation when attempting to view an Office document that exceeds the file size limit set by Microsoft.

column6: Correction to Licensing and Privilege requirements for Epic Physician Acknowledgement
column7:
A change was made to correct the Licensing and Privileges required to assign Physician Acknowledgement tasks through an OnBase Scan Queue.  

With this change the software will require that:
1. The solution is licensed for the Epic Integration
2. The user has the Indexing privilege
3. The user is accessing a Scan Queue that is configured for Epic Physician Inbox Linking Support

column6: The Event Date settings now properly save the selected value for the Post Event on Managed Folder Workflow action.
column7: This change will also help ensure that the correct date is used when the action is executed.

column6: Publishing Export Workstation Configuration installer works properly
column7: Publishing Export Workstation Configuration installer works properly

column6: Documents from an external Meditech System were being excluded from the OnBase Release of Information packet
column7: An issue has been resolved that was resulting in documents attached to the OnBase Release of Information packet using the Meditech Report Capture integration not being included in the final release.  These documents appeared to be present on the Attachments screen but were missing from the Release Order screen and the final PDF that was generated as part of the release.

column6: Resolved an issue with signature deficiencies not remaining where they were placed in the Signature for Deficiencies for Epic client.
column7: Prior to this fix, some signature deficiencies would move immediately after being placed and had to be redropped (moved) back to the original location. This only affected the Signature Deficiencies for Epic (SD4E) client, not other deficiency applications such as DeficiencyPop and MRM Unity.

column6: Resolves Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) issues in the Web Client and Healthcare Web Viewer when used in an iframe.
column7: Updated the Web Client and Healthcare Web Viewer to correctly handle Cross-Origin Resource Sharing when opened in an iframe. Prior to this change, symptoms of this issue include, but are not limited to, PDFs failing to open in the viewer as well as document properties, keyword, and history dialog windows failing to open.

column6: Addressed issues(s) with the Workflow rule "Prompt User with Question Box" in the Unity Client
column7: Updated an underlying web component used to render the interactive window. This enabled the Unity Client to respond as expected when executing the Workflow rule, "Prompt User with Question Box".

column6: Addressed issue(s) with Queue Administration access in the Workflow tab in OnBase Client
column7: Resolved an issue where an incorrect exception message appeared when a user with appropriate rights accessed Queue Administration in the Workflow tab of OnBase Client.

column6: Addressed mapping issue(s) when using the Call REST API Workflow action
column7: Corrected a mapping issue using the Workflow action, Call REST API. Users can now map a field contained in a nested object when the parent object is an array.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with display column operators in Workflow's Unity Client interface
column7: Corrected an issue that caused display column operators to be cleared after re-indexing two or more documents within Workflow's Unity Client interface. The filter columns now persists until cleared by a user.

column6: Addressed issue(s) with persistence of Workflow custom layout
column7: Corrected an issue with persistence of Workflow custom layout. Previously, the user needed to reload the layout every time they navigated from Workflow. This fix ensures that Workflow's custom layout will persist even if the user closes the interface.

column6: Improved performance of the Workflow action, "Re-Indexing Document" in the Unity Client
column7: For document types that are configured for renditions but not revisions, there is now a noticeable performance improvement in the Unity Client. Previously, Unity Client responded slowly when reindexing documents that were configured for renditions but not revisions.

column6: WorkView REST API generates correct response when Date and DateTime is fed, and generates correct response that is consumed by endpoints.
column7:
The Workview Rest API generates correct reponse when supplied Date and DateTime in these formats respectively yyyy-MM-dd and yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ss. All endpoints consume the response correctly.

The changes have been implemented in 23.1 and 22.1 (backport.)

column5: Public Access (Web Controls for Constituent Access)
column6: NextGen Public Access log document views to document history
column7: During retrieving from NextGen Public Access site, the retrieval is written to the OnBase transaction log and included properly in Document History

column6: Reduced screen flicker scenarios while using Advanced Capture
column7: Significantly reduced the scenarios that caused the screen to flicker when using Advanced Capture. This mitigates interruptions and improves the overall user experience.

column6: Performance Improvements when opening meetings in the Agenda Client.
column7:
Two performance issues when loading a meeting in the Agenda Client were identified and corrected:
- We are now handling the retrieval of Pending Unassigned items after the meeting is loaded, which helps to reduce the loading time of a Meeting if there are several unassigned items for that meeting type in the database.
- We updated code that syncs the order of attachments in the Meeting tree to the order displayed in the Unity Form, and vice versa, which also reduces the loading time of a meeting.

column6: Addresses an issue when the Section Title displays an old value in the Agenda Online Item Details.
column7: Fixed issue where Section Title still displays old value in the Agenda Online Item Details panel after the Section Title is edited in the Agenda Client.

column6: Fixed an issue where you were unable to access an agenda item form if the item was targeted for a meeting that had been deleted.
column7: If a meeting is deleted, all agenda items that are targeted for that meeting can still be accessed. You will see a value of "Original target meeting not found" indicating that it was set at one point, but is now no longer in the system.  You would be able to change the target meeting to another valid meeting if needed, and if it hasn't already been added to a meeting agenda.  Once the item is added to a meeting, the field is locked. You can move it back to "pending items" to unlock the field to change it if needed.

column6: Fixed an issue in the Forms tab of Document History that was preventing you from seeing the full multiline text value because it was being cut off.
column7: The cell now expands its height automatically so you can view all the lines of the multiline text value in both the Old and New Value columns.

column6: Addressed an incorrect success message on the summary document generation.
column7: Summary Document generation was generating a "Minutes document generation" toaster message on success. It now shows the correct message indicating that the "Summary document generation" was successful.

column6: Addressed an Index was out of range error when generating a meeting document
column7: It was possible for the tag template word doc for a meeting document to have an invalid xml layout. This was fixed and the index out of range error no longer occurs in that particular scenario.

column6: Added ability to use the "Create or Update Agenda Item from Document" workflow action to create Unity form based agenda items. this will allow you to easily create unity form based agenda items from an existing document in OnBase and automatically pull in keyword values from the originating document to be added to fields on the unity form agenda item.
column7:
- This action will only create new Unity form agenda items from an existing OnBase document. You cannot edit existing unity form items with it.
- We added the ability to attach the originating document to the newly created unity form agenda item.
- We added the ability to set attached supporting documents as Confidential at the time of item creation.

column6: Fixed an issue where the item title in the Accessible view only displayed the short item title, which did not match the long item title in the standard view of Agenda Online.
column7: This fix allows for the Accessible view to show the long item title if one exists. If a long title does not exist, the short item title is used. In both cases, the Standard view and Accessible view now shows the same item title, either long or short.

column6: Addressed an issue with Agenda Online Full Text search did not return the complete list of results is some cases.
column7: A problem existed where if a large number of Agenda Online documents were in the system the Full Text Search would not show the proper document result set. It will now behave the same as it does today in the Unity an Web clients. It will filter then search the documents correctly to display the proper document result hit list. This should match what you find when you run the same search in Unity or Web client.

column6: Addressed an issue in Agenda Online that prevented downloading supporting documents with the "&" in the file name after performing a full text search for the file.
column7: Agenda Online users should now be able to download files from the full text search results with any special characters in the file name.

column6: Service profile logging is now enabled by default with a logging level set to "Information".
column7: Service profile logging will always log at the "Information" level regardless of what the log level is set as.

column6: Added support for RightFax v21.2
column7: The Integration for RightFax will function correctly with RightFax Server v21.2 since the RightFax product underwent some technical refactoring.

column6: OnBase Application Server - Fixed an issue where a duplicate row was being inserted into the database when it should not be.
The F6 shortcut successfully expands Autofills within the Unity Client.
Reverse autofill keyset exclusions are now respected in the Web Client
column7: Keywords marked with exclusions will not be populated with values from the AutoFill Keyset when using Reverse AutoFill lookup during Document Import.

column6: Select Keyset pop-up dialog now has "Toggle Column Filters" option
column7: Select Keyset pop-up dialog now has "Toggle Column Filters" option

column6: Reverse Autofill Keyword Sets are now available to restricted users when restricted keywords exist on the doc type outside of the keyset group
column7: In the Unity Client, restricted users are unable to use a Reverse Autofill Keyword Set in the Unity Client if the RAFKS's has "Keyword Exclusions", "Lock Applied Keywords", or "Insert Primary as Secondary" configured or the "Replace Existing Keywords" setting on the RAFKS does not match the "Replace Existing Keywords" on its AFKS and a keyword outside of the keyset is restricted on the document type. This allows for greater security around keyword information available to users.

column6: Addressed an issue in Unity Client Import where expanding an AutoFill Keyset using F6 caused an error.
column7: Expanding an Autofill Keyword Set in Unity Client Import using F6 to create a second instance of the Keyword no longer causes an "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" error message.

column6: Tab successfully expands Primary Keywords which have Read-Only Secondary Keywords

column6: Fixed an issue with clearing Hidden/Read-Only Keywords
column7: End users are no longer prompted with "A keyword that cannot be modified has been changed." message when clearing Hidden or Read-Only Keywords.

column6: Hidden keywords are no longer sent down to the client when an autofill has multiple keyset matches on a given primary.
column7: Hidden keywords are no longer sent down to the client when an autofill has multiple keyset matches on a given primary to match other hidden keyword functionality. If access to these keywords is needed, provide users with the "Access Restricted Keywords" override for the affected Document Type(s).

column6: Addressed an issue where the AutoFill Keyset selection dialog could erroneously display multiple times.
column7: Updated the AutoFill Keyset selection dialog to only display one time. This allows users to avoid making the Keyset selection multiple times.

column6: Users without the the "Access Restricted Keywords" privilege are able to expand a Reverse AutoFill Keyword Set that contains a Read-Only Primary Keyword.
column7: If a Reverse AutoFill Keyword Set contains a Read-Only primary keyword, but one or more unrestricted secondary keywords, users without the the "Access Restricted Keywords" privilege are able to expand the Reverse AFKS by performing a Reverse AFKS lookup on one of the unrestricted secondary keywords.

column7: The Web Integration Settings dialog now displays the correct configuration settings for the selected Integration Profile when the database contains settings for an Integration that is not configurable in the Configuration Client.

column6: The HTML and ActiveX Web Client now no longer automatically convert .html documents to a .htm file format in most circumstances
column7:
In the HTML Web Client, the 'Send To | File' and 'Send To | Mail' options now no longer automatically convert a .html document to a .htm file format. 

In the ActiveX Web Client, the 'Send To | File' functionality now no longer converts a .html document to a .htm format. 

The 'Send To | Mail' functionality in the ActiveX Web Client does still convert 
 .html documents to a .htm format.

However, as a workaround for sending to mail in the ActiveX Web Client, the  
 OnBase Administrator can configure their Web Server to use the "UseWebMail" configuration option.  This will cause the ActiveX Web Client to use the HTML code for 'Send To | Mail' and they will see the updated behavior.

column6: Addressed issue with email sending when attaching a document using a long document Auto-Name string
column7: Updated field to allow 255 characters when attaching a document using document Auto-Name string

column6: Resolved an issue with FHIR that can occur when an Assigning Authority value is hard coded in a FHIR Mapping Template.
column7:
A memory issue could occur with the OnBase Application Server in the following conditions:
1) A patient and/or chart identifier chart data field (e.g. MPI, MRN, or CSN) is configured to require Assigning Authority. 
2) One or more of the Assigning Authority keyword types are mapped to a FHIR Path configured with a hard coded value (e.g. 'AA') in the FHIR Mapping Template. 
3) A FHIR message is received that does not contain one or more of the patient/chart identifiers configured to require Assigning Authority.
4) This would cause the OnBase Application Server to attempt to lookup all patient/chart identifiers with the hard coded Assigning Authority value from the FHIR path, as no corresponding primary identifier was provided. 

This change ensures that OnBase will not attempt to perform a lookup if no primary identifier is provided, even if the Assigning Authority is either provided in the FHIR message or hard coded as a value in a FHIR path.

column6: Blank or duplicate file extensions can no longer be added for FHIR MIME types.
column7: Updated the OnBase Configuration client to no longer accept blank file extensions or duplicate file extensions.

column6: Addressed issue with FolderPop not loading HTML Folder Filters.
column7: Fixed a session context issue that prevented FolderPop sessions from being able to retrieve an HTML Folder Filter. This prevented FolderPop users from seeing folder contents if that folder used an HTML filter.

column6: Repeating/duplicate folder query select statements are prevented from running against the database.
column7: Repeating/duplicate folder query select statements are prevented from running against the database. This will prevent unneeded system stress and, in the case of larger batches, chances for a system timeout.

column6: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Listener failing to start in some environments with large numbers of user accounts and physicians.
column7: The HL7 Listener could fail to start in some OnBase solutions that have very large numbers of users (200,000+) and physicians (90,000+). Memory usage has been reduced so that the HL7 Listener will start successfully, even when there are a large number of users and physicians.

column6: Resolved an issue with the Patient Record Administrator where it would not refresh after deleting a record.
column7: The Patient Record Administrator now refreshes after deleting a record.

column6: Enforced a 32 character limit in the HL7 Export Destination Configuration UI for the Network Address.
column7: Previously, it was possible to enter a Destination Network Address that had greater than 32 characters in the HL7 Export Destination settings dialog. This would cause an error to occur when attempting to save the Export Destination as the database can only accept 32 character export destinations. The UI was updated to enforce the 32 character limitation to prevent this error from occurring. Additionally, a horizontal scroll bar was added in order to correctly view Destination Network Addresses that would have previously been cut off.

column6: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Listener crashing during start-up when multiple ports were specified in the Command Line switches.
column7: Depending on the number of ports specified in the -HL7PORT Command Line switch, as well as the number of Import Processes configured, it was possible for the HL7 Listener to fail to start properly. Changes were made to improve memory pool utilization in order to prevent this issue from occurring.

column6: Resolved an issue where HL7 Back-end Job Processor jobs can be processed out-of-order when running multiple HL7 Listeners to process patient-centric messages.
column7:
When sending patient-centric messages (e.g. ADT or SIU) to multiple OnBase HL7 Listeners, it was possible for the listeners to insert jobs into the queue with non-sequential jobnums. This would cause the HL7 Back-end Job Processor to potentially process jobs in the wrong order and/or skip jobs due to way the HL7 Back-end Job Processor queried the queue for jobs. This change addresses the issue with jobs being inserted into the queue with non-sequential jobnums. The issue with jobs potentially being skipped is being addressed by HC-166959 (also fixed in 21.1.15.1000). 

Note: Prior to EP5, it was a documented best practice to have all patient-centric feeds processed on a single HL7 Listener service. Please see the EP3 or older HL7 MRG under the -HL7LISTENER command line switch section for this documentation.

column6: Removed HL7ListenerSocketTimeoutSeconds from the INI settings file.
column7: Removed HL7ListenerSocketTimeoutSeconds from the INI settings file as it is no longer used by the OnBase Thick Client.

column6: Resolved an issue with various chart attributes not being accurately reflected in Patient Record Administration.
column7: Prior to this fix, the VIP Flag, Discharge Status, and Chart Analysis Required chart attributes would display as checked when viewing charts in Patient Record Administration, even though the attributes were not set in the database. The opposite would also occur - the attributes would display as unchecked when the attributes were set in the database. This fix ensures the checkboxes correctly indicate the status of these chart attributes.

column6: Resolved an issue where the OnBase Client could crash when attempting to merge or move patients to a patient with a blank Assigning Authority in Patient Record Administrator.
column7: Resolved an issue with the Patient Record Administrator in the OnBase Client that only applies to solutions which require Assigning Authority at the MPI or MRN level/Chart Data Field. When attempting to merge or move patients to a patient with an blank Assigning Authority, the Client would crash. Now, Patient Record Administrator will make the invalid record unavailable (it will be grayed out) so that patient records cannot be merged into it.

column6: Fixes an issue where two buttons would appear to be active/outlined in the HL7 Import Processor Settings dialog.
column7: Resolve an issue where both the "Save" and "Edit Description" buttons would appear to be active/outlined in the HL7 Import Processor Settings dialog.

column6: Only the first page of multi-page image documents is retrieved when using the Image Retrieval API.

With this bug fix, IRA will now properly convert multi-page image files to TIF so that all pages are visible in Epic. This will occur regardless of the "Retrieve Native Image in Epic IRA" Epic External System setting.

column6: Addressed an issue where the interface could freeze when uploading a document as a revision via the Word add-in application.
column7: Uploading document revisions with the Word add-in and other Office add-in apps should work as expected and not freeze the application.

column6: Addressed an issue with the focus shifting from the keyword field to excel document when indexing an Excel document imported via the OBA integration.

column6: The Unity Client and Office Business Application Add-ins display Office documents as expected.
column7: An issue was addressed that was causing Office documents to not display as expected in the Unity Client and Office Business Application Add-ins.

column7: Users are able to toggle between View and Edit modes when using the Integrated Office Viewer in the OnBase Web Client

column6: Corrected an error when the WOPI viewer in the web client reloads a document after editing and added some configuration option to control the document refresh behavior.

Added two new config settings in the web client web.config to control the autorefresh behavior of a document after it has been edited in the web client:
<add key="disableWopiAutoRefreshAfterEdit" value ="true"/>
<add key=”wopiRefreshAttemptsAfterEdit” value=”2”/>
These settings do not display in the web.config file by default, but can be added manually. See Integrated office viewer MRG for more details.

column7: In Unity Client, the warning message will display when importing or re-indexing a document with a double byte characters if keyword values exceed keyword length.

column7: Updated the HTML Web Client to allow users without the "Access Restricted Keywords" privilege to save documents with hidden keywords in the Add/Modify, Re-Index, and HTML Send To > Create New Document contexts.

column7: Updated the Unity Client to allow AutoFill Keysets to expand properly on the re-index dialog when multiple documents are selected for re-index.

column7: Unity Client successfully loads the keyword panel within the Batch Scanning window.

column6: Removed incorrect log in Document History.
column7: Source Document will no longer log a Document Type Change Request when changing Document Types within the Create New Document dialog.

column6: Retrieval keywords appear in custom queries in the Web Client
column7: Even if space is set as a static character, Retrieval keywords appear in custom queries under the 'Search Type' in the Web Client, which is similar to functionality in the Unity Client.

column7: MIKG's instances created on Documents during the Import process will respect the the user's Access Restricted Keyword privilege

column6: Addressed an issue where Default Keyword values could be removed on Re-Index in the Unity Client under certain conditions
column7: When re-indexing a document from one Document Type to another in the Unity Client, the Default Keyword value could be removed if the same default value is configured on both Document Types. This would only occur if there are multiple instances of that Keyword Type on the document and if the Default Value is in the first position in the keyword load order.

column6: Addressed issue where certain document dates were not displayed.
column7: Documents with the date of 1/1/1964 now display their date properly.

column6: Addressed issue with CR/LF characters indexed as part of the MAIL Subject system keyword value
column7: CR/LF characters are now trimmed from the Email Subject before indexing the value to the MAIL Subject system keyword. This prevents the MAIL Subject keyword value from folding or appearing on multiple lines.

column6: Addressed issue with MAIL Attachment Count Keyword Value being incremented when embedded attachments are excluded
column7: Updated the MAIL Attachment Count Keyword Value to only increment for standard email attachments when the process is configured to exclude embedded attachments from import.

column6: Addressed issue with embedded attachments not being imported via Mailbox Importer when the attachments do not have names
column7: The Attachment ID is now used as the embedded attachment name when the attachment name is absent, thus allowing it to be successfully imported using Mailbox Importer.

column6: Documents may now be successfully rotated/flipped and saved in Analysis after placing Deficiency notes
column7: Previously, an issue existed where attempting to rotate a document in Analysis after placing Deficiency note(s) could result in an exception error message appearing. This no longer occurs.

column6: Made performance improvements when opening a meeting in Minutes and Voting clients.
column7: Corrected an issue where it was taking too long to retrieve the Long Titles of agenda items from the unity form xml on the disk. The Long Titles are now stored in the database and are retrieved much faster, making the meeting loading process much quicker for both the Minutes Client and the Voting Client.

column6: Fixed an issue where the "Do not require seconder" option was not being respected in certain scenarios.
column7:
Motions will now respect the "Do not require seconder" option pre-meeting, during meeting, and post-meeting for any motion that has no seconder defined.

If you add a seconder to a motion even with the "Do not require seconder" option enabled, the option will be overridden in subsequent meeting session instances as it now assumes that it will require a seconder moving forward given that a seconder was added previously.

column6: For a meeting that has already been run with media, you now have the ability to continue running the meeting without media.
column7: This is helpful in cases where either media publisher or the encoder go offline during the meeting. This prevents minutes client from freezing up and it will allow you to continue to use the minutes UI for creating minutes, motions votes, setting timestamps, etc during the meeting even with media being down.  The end result is that there is minimal interruption in the minutes UI and that disconnects and reconnects of media are handled seamlessly without affecting the flow of the live meeting.

column6: You are now able to reset the Recording Type of a meeting if it was set to "None".
column7: You may need to do this if the meeting has been run with the "None" media option and you need to run the meeting with Media later. If the recording type is set to "None" then you can't add media to the meeting so in the past you would have to create a new meeting or call support to reset it for you because it was not possible to reset it on you own in the UI. Now you can right click on the meeting in the list and select the Reset Recording Type option.

column6: Two new features were added to address an issue with Minutes timing out when starting large meetings.
column7:
First, a new Minutes config setting, RequestTimeoutSeconds, was added to the obminutes.config file. By default it is set to 300 seconds (5 minutes). Increasing this setting can prevent a timeout error from occurring when starting large meetings. 

Second, a new feature was added to the OnBase Minutes client that will allow you to create the minutes outline before starting the meeting.

Because larger meetings can take a fair amount of time to create the minutes outline, we wanted to provide a way to prepare that meeting data before the meeting start time.  Prior to this change the minutes outline was created by starting the meeting. This was problematic with larger meetings because you would have to wait for the process to complete before the live stream, recording, or any other functions in the Minutes client were available, causing delays in your live meeting. 

You will now see a new menu option in the Minutes client, Meeting > Generate Minutes View. It will be enabled in pre-meeting mode. Also, the Meeting panel title will identify the View that is being displayed so you can know for sure which view you are working with; "Meeting - Agenda View" for pre-meeting mode or "Meeting - Minutes View" once the minutes outline has been generated. 
Note: once you generate the minutes view, the meeting state will show as post meeting even if you have not started the meeting yet. This is expected.

This feature will allow you to easily prepare the minutes outline earlier in the day before the meeting starts so you don't experience a delay at meeting start time.  We would recommend using this if you have large meetings with many agenda items and are seeing a considerable delay when clicking the start button. 

Note: for ease of meeting administration, it is best to run this function only after you know that all the agenda items have been finalized for the meeting and there will be no new items added. If items need to be added after you have created the minutes view then you would need to add those items directly to the Minutes View either in the Agenda client or Minutes client.  If you also want the item to display in the original Agenda view, a user with agenda administrator privilege would be able to create the item in the Agenda view using the Agenda client.

column6: Addressed an issue where the time stamp for a speaker minute entry is not adding properly
column7: Using the setting to add a minute when a speaker has spoken will now include the time stamp in the meeting tree in Minutes and also in a minutes document when using the <timestamp> tag.

column6: Note text not saving in Unity Client when scrolling to different pages.
column7: Fixed an issue where note text would not be saved after entering note text in the Unity Client and scrolling to a different page on the document without performing any other interaction, such as clicking outside of the note.

column6: Unity Workflow Ad Hoc Task Add Note correctly prints text.
column7: An error has been corrected so that now, when adding a note through an ad hoc task in Workflow, note text will print on document correctly.

column6: Suppressed duplicate notification dialogs when entering invalid value.
column7: Within the Workflow action 'Custom Workflow Trace' duplicate warning dialogs would appear when a repeat value of '0' was entered. This has been corrected.

column6: "Assigned User, Group or Role" no longer appears blank after relaunching or reloading the repository in OnBase Studio.
column7: Corrected an issue in OnBase Studio where "Assigned User, Group or Role" would appear blank after relaunching or reloading the repository, despite the values being saved correctly. This issue did not affect execution in the clients but was unclear to administrators in OnBase Studio that the values were actually saved.

column6: Resolved an issue with Patient Record Administration not displaying the correct information when the next record is auto-selected after a deletion.
column7: When deleting a record in Patient Record Administration, if another record is next in the list, it will be auto-selected to display and the tab information will update accordingly.

column6: Resolved an issue with the Patient Record Administrator where the incorrect Assigning Authority could display in a specific error.
column7: Previously, it was possible for the incorrect Assigning Authority to display when the Patient Record Administrator was displaying an error about mismatched/conflicting records. This has been corrected to ensure the correct Assigning Authority is always specified in the error text.

column6: Corrected issue that caused PCL Macro overlays to render incorrectly on PCL documents.
column7: Corrected issue that caused PCL Macro overlays to render incorrectly on PCL documents.

column6: Addressed issue with dot-shading appearing as black bars on PCL documents when viewed in the Unity and Web Clients.
column7: Dot-shading displays as intended on PCL documents when viewed within the Unity and Web Clients and no longer appears as solid black bars.

column6: Addressed issue with misalignment of PCL macro-overlays in the OnBase Client.
column7: PCL macro-overlays are aligned with the text on PCL documents when viewed within the OnBase Client.

column6: Fixed multiple issues related to saving note markups and comments on multi-page plan documents in the engineering viewer.
column7:
- Save button now works when you add a second note markup and/or comment on a different page of the document.
- The viewer now recognizes changes on each page of the document, and prompts you to save before moving on to the next page. This prevents two other reported issues where you would have to click the Save button multiple times to apply all the comments, and eliminates the possibility of losing note markups and comments as you add several across multiple pages.

column6: Removed Delete and Copy/Paste limitations when multiselecting markups in the  engineering viewer.
column7: You can now multiselect markups including notes and standard notes and the Delete and Copy/Paste markup buttons are enabled function accordingly.

column6: Fixed an issue in the Engineering viewer where the Centimeter-Millimeter unit was displaying kilometer-Meter units when drawing a line or area measure markup and visa versa.
Fixed a copy and paste issue with note markups.
column7: Corrected an issue where you would see an error trying to copy and paste a newly created note markup.

column6: Corrected an issue with Review Comments where in some cases double clicking within the text box when editing or creating a new comment would delete any new unsaved text you entered.
column7: When entering text while editing or creating a new Review Comment, double clicking anywhere in the text field will no longer delete the text you have entered.

column6: Added messages with additional details if a Plan Set fails to generate or if a Plan Set generates but excludes something.
column7:
If you attempt to generate at Plan Set with only non PDF files it will fail to generate. We now show a message with additional details that includes a reference to the non PDF documents that caused the failure.  If a Plan Set contains a mix of non PDF and PDF files the Plan Set will generate with a warning message listing any non PDF files that weren't included.

We also fixed an issue where Comments on pages that are no longer on the current revision of the document would cause a plan set to fail. We now generate the Plan Set and give a warning with details of the problematic comment IDs so you can go and review the Comments and re add them to the appropriate pages on the current revision.

column6: Corrected an issue whereby the project information is not automatically updated after executing an ad hoc task in workflow.
column7: When changing the Project Information,  such as Project ID or Name, via a workflow ad hoc task, the Project Information is automatically updated.

column6: Fixed an issue where duplicate project documents could be created.
column7: After importing a document from the plan review portal and then re-indexing that document in the Unity client, then importing a revision of the original document via the Add a plan document function in the unity client, the plan document would be duplicated on the project. This issue has been fixed.

column6: Patient Portal Link can now render all pages of a Unity Form regardless of the default file format or extension.
column7:
Patient Portal Link users now can view all pages of a Unity Form that has been converted to an image, even if the default file format of the Document Type is set to Image File Format.  Previously, Patient Portal Link displayed only the first page of the image, along with a message saying the user could sign the document upon arrival at the facility for treatment. This issue has been resolved.

The signing of third-party forms is no longer supported in Patient Portal Link. Third-party forms can be signed upon arrival at the facility for treatment. 

Internet Explorer is no longer supported for use with Patient Portal Link.

column6: Close Events in the Unity Client allow entering dates prior to 01/01/1964
column7: The date picker for a Close Event in the Unity Client allows for dates to be selected or manually entered for dates prior to 01/01/1964. This lets users have more complete data for sites with records before 1964.

column6: Fixed an issue when placing a hold on a folder in Records Management Administration when search results are grouped by column
column7: Placing a hold in the Records Management Administration screen will no longer throw an exception when the search results are grouped by a specific column.

column6: The Workflow Inbox data provider now filters out results that are tied to keywords that don't exist on a document type.
column7: When using the Workflow Inbox Data Provider in Reporting Dashboards, documents that did not contain keywords were incorrectly being returned and inflating the number of results. This has been corrected.

column6: Adding redactions or markups to the first page of a multi-page TIFF converted from PDF in core clients no longer causes other pages to be removed from the document
column7: An issue in the core clients that caused pages to be removed from a document has been corrected. Pages are no longer removed from a document when a multi-page PDF with a redaction or markup on the first page is converted to a TIFF image.

column6: Addressed an issue in the Unity Client to allow a document to be re-indexed as a revision of another document.
column7: Updated the Unity Client so that re-indexing a document to be a revision of another existing document functions as expected and no longer displays an error.

column6: Addressed an issue with default Keyword Values not being populated in the StatusView File Upload portlet.
column7: Updated the StatusView File Upload portlet to display default Keyword Values. This allows default Keyword Values to be used for indexing when importing files through the StatusView File Upload portlet.

column6: External text search results now respect Retrieval List Sort Order options
The Unity Client now logs out of the Hyland Idp using the system browser when relaunching the client in service mode
column7: The Unity Client now logs out of the Hyland Idp using the system browser when relaunching the client in service mode

column6: Users with rights cannot "Retrieve by Document Handle" in the Unity Client unless they also have the "Retrieve / View" privilege.
column7: Addressed an issue where, in the Unity Client, users with the "Product Right -> Administrative Privilege -> Retrieve by Document Handle / File Name" rights were unable to "Retrieve by Document Handle" unless they also had the "Retrieve / View" Privilege assigned. Attempting to do so without the "Retrieve / View" privilege would result in nothing appearing to happen.

column6: Global Client Setting, 'Disable CSV export sanitization', not being respected in the Unity Client
column7: Addressed an issue where the Global Client Setting, 'Disable CSV export sanitization', was being respected in the Unity Client when exporting a CSV file.

column6: Documents no longer appear blurry after being rotated
column7: Updated the Unity Client so that documents no longer appear blurry after being rotated 270 degrees. This ensures that users will not worry about losing image quality while editing their documents in the Unity Client.

column6: Unity Client is not prompting users when the file size limit is with mail attachments
column7: Addressed an issue where the Unity Client would not display an exception dialog alerting the user that that the attachment file size was exceeded when attempting to send a document via "send to mail" and the attachment exceeded Outlooks attachment size restrictions.

column6: Improved the visibility of focused page thumbnails.
column7: Changed the selected thumbnail to retain the blue bounding box when it is not actively focused. To distinguish between the actively selected thumbnail and the thumbnail that is moused over, there is a slightly lighted blue shade to represent the moused over thumbnail.

column6: Fixed a color issue with imports in the Unity Client
column7: Resolved an issue where there were certain cases when a multi-page tiff would be imported without color in the Unity Client.

column6: Users are able to select an image file to append to after incorrectly selecting a non-image file.
column7: End users are able to append pages to an image during import if they had initially selected a non-image file before selecting the images. This allows for greater stability and flexibility when importing files in the Unity Client.

column6: A user is able to continue with an import after a system loses connection to the Application Server and reconnects.
column7: If the Application Server connection is lost during document import, the user is notified and is given the option to reconnect to the application server. Upon reconnecting, the keyword panel is reset and the user may try their import again.

column6: The Document Import queue is now refreshed and cleared when a user navigates to another layout.
column7: Queueing multiple documents for import and then exiting the Import process by navigating to another layout, such as Document Retrieval, clears the entire Import queue. This prevents documents being erroneously imported when a separate, unrelated import process is started.

column6: Resolved the document history incorrectly logging in the unity client.
column7: Fixed an issue when a user did not not have rights to view the document the action was being logged incorrectly. This has been resolved so that we no longer log this in the Document History.

column6: Unprivileged users no longer able to purge documents
column7: Users that do not have the Document Maintenance and API Administration privileges, but have Re-Index privileges, are able to Re-Index a document and save as a Rendition of an existing document.

column6: The "Mail Recipient (as Link)" and "Create Hyperlink" options now include checksums in the generated DocPop URL.
column7: The "Send to -> Mail Recipient (as Link)" and "Send to -> Create Hyperlink" context menu actions now include checksums in the generated DocPop URL if the OnBase instance is configured to use DocPop Checksums

column6: The Assume Revision doctype option is now respected on thumbnail delete
column7: An issue was addressed where, when deleting (from a thumbnail) a page from a multi-page image document and after clicking “Yes” to delete the page, a prompt for Document Revisions did not appear.

column6: Resolved an issue where exception was thrown when attempting to clear out a Masked Unity Form field using a Unity Script
column7: User will now be able to clear Masked Unity Form fields using a Unity Script. This will allow clearing of both masked keyword or non-keyword fields by using a Unity Script without an exception.

column6: Resolved an issue where an exception was thrown when trying to publish a form using a Calculated Field SUM() expression with Nested Table fields.
column7: User will now be able to publish and submit forms that have Calculated Field expressions used with Nested Tables, Tables, Sections, Repeating Sections, and Panels without an exception.

column6: Resolved the issue of exception thrown when using + or * as a Static Character at the start of a Masking Field.
column7:
Resolved an issue where an exception was thrown when using + or * as a Static Character at the start of a Masking field.

Users will now be able to use Static Characters at any position in a Masking Field without an exception.

column6: Resolved an issue where exception was thrown when trying to test drive, publish, or submit forms that uses a Calculated Field having AVG() expression involving Repeater Control fields and another field outside the Repeater Control
column7: Users will now be able to test drive, publish, or submit forms that uses a Calculated Field having AVG() expression involving Repeater Control (Nested Table, Table, or Repeating Section) fields and another field outside the Repeater Control

column6: Resolved an issue where secondary keyword fields would retain the previous values, even if a primary keyword value was entered that does not have an existing entry in an Autofill Keyword table.
column7: Users will now be able to see all the secondary keyword fields cleared when a primary keyword is entered that does not have an existing entry in an Autofill Keyword table. The same behavior is noticed if the the primary keyword field is cleared.

column6: Addressed an issue in the Web Application Management Console to help prevent invalid ADFS configurations.
column7: The Web Application Management Console has been updated to require the "Configure for ADFS" menu item to be executed from the Tools menu before changes can be made to settings on the "ADFS Settings" tab.

column6: Applications under subfolders will now be displayed within WAMCOM
column7: If a web application is under a subfolder it will now be in the list of applications to select from.

column6: Optimize for Windows Authentication adds a location block for loginformproc.aspx
column7: WamCon successfully updates loginformproc within the web.config when Optimized for Windows Authentication

column6: Users who viewed unread Document Knowledge Transfer documents from the unread documents pop-up option upon logging in will now be able to open Workflow as expected.
column7: Users who viewed unread Document Knowledge Transfer documents from the unread documents pop-up option upon logging in will now be able to open Workflow as expected.

column6: Addressed issue with creating new revisions in the Send To > Create New Document dialog.
column7: Updated the HTML Web Client to respect the "Create as revision of original document" option on the Send To > Create New Document dialog. This allows a revision to be created as expected when selecting the option.

column6: Documents and Folders do not open in new windows when using FolderPop
column7: Documents and Folders do not open in new windows when using FolderPop

column6: Window size settings are now properly saved and stored when opening DocPop or PDFPop through Internet Explorer
Fixed scroll behavior for image files in the Web Client when web browser zoom is changed from default
column7: When viewing image documents in the Web Client and using Firefox, Edge, or Chrome, and using a increased zoom level in the browser, users will now be able to scroll to the next page in the document.

column6: When the New Form context is set as a Home Page within the HTML Web Client, Workflow will be correctly displayed.
column7: When the New Form context is set as a Home Page within the HTML Web Client, Workflow will be correctly displayed.

column6: Administrators can now set custom column widths for the Reverse Autofill Keyset dialog within the OnBase Web Client.
