column7: Specifying the default column widths for a Reverse AutoFill Keyset within OnBase Configuration allows the Web Client to display Reverse AutoFill Keyset lookup results with custom column widths.

column6: Files downloaded after a disconnect correctly prompt the Re-Authentication dialog, and downloads continue after a successful Re-Authentication
column7: File downloads successfully continue after a Nist Re-Authentication request

column6: Removed a redundant Notes pane in the Web Client.
column7: When a document is displayed using the Show Document button from the Notes dialog box, it no longer includes a redundant Notes pane.

column6: More user friendly error message is displayed in the Web client when a new user is created and not assigned to a user group.
column7: While creating a new user in the Web Client Administration panel, if no user group is selected, a more intuitive error is shown telling the user to select a user group before continuing.  Thus, providing a more user friendly dialog.

column6: Workflow life cycle icons and ad hoc user task icons are displayed as expected
column7: Workflow life cycle icons and ad hoc user task icons are displayed as expected within the OnBase Web Client.

column6: The Web Client main window remains enabled after a user deletes a note from the Notes dialog box within the Workflow window
column7: The Web Client main window remains enabled after a user deletes a note from the Notes dialog box within the Workflow window

column6: Using the Send To | File feature to export a JPEG file (.jpeg) Document as "Rendered Format" in the Web Client no longer causes an error
column7: Using the Send To | File feature to export a JPEG file (.jpeg) Document as "Rendered Format" in the Web Client no longer causes an error. If the source OnBase document is single-page it will be downloaded as a JPEG file. If the source OnBase document is multi-page it will be download as a TIFF file.

column6: Report file exported by Create List Report option in Web Client as Excel 2003 (.xls) can be opened without any issues
column7: The report file exported by the Create List Report option in the Web Client as Excel 2003 (.xls) document can now be opened without any issues. A warning message will be displayed since the generated file is an XML file but uses an .xls extension, but the file can be opened after closing the warning.

column6: In the HTML Web Client, an error is no longer displayed in Diagnostics Console when an end user creates a document using the Send To -> Create New Document option.
column7: Previously, end users would select Send To -> Create New Document from the Document hitlist in the HTML Web Client, which would result in an error.  This issue no longer occurs and documents are able to be created in this manner.

column6: Addressed a licensing issue when using DocPop in the IAM environment
column7: Added enableCoreQueryAPILicense setting to the Hyland IdP login path. This ensures that when users use the default login for DocPop, the Core Query API license is consumed instead of named or concurrent licenses.

column6: Unsupported Send To | File Option Removed
column7: The PDF option is no longer available when a Web Client user attempts to use the Send To | File option for Office documents that are configured to use a custom file format.

column6: Send To File Content Type Fix
column7: For Web Client users performing a Send To | File action, the Rendered Format and Original Format options are now available within the Save As menu when exporting either a PDF document, or an Office document that uses the system office file format or a custom file format.

column6: Web Client Users Can Save Multiple Documents to ZIP File
column7: Web Client Users can correctly save multiple documents to a ZIP file, even for documents that use a custom file format.

column6: WAMCON deprecated settings list updated
column7: WAMCON properly removes deprecated settings when upgrading a web.config file from a previous version

column6: In the Web Client, the Document Name column for Custom SQL Custom Query will now expand and use all of the available space in the grid
column7: The Document Name column was previously being set to a static width.  Now, the Document Name column for Custom SQL Custom Query will expand and use all of the available space in the grid.

column6: Web Client loading background updated for better visibility
column7: Web Client loading background contrast has been changed for better visibility when using light mode

column6: Ensure document locks are respected
column7: Regardless of client, when attempting to import, re-index, or workflow re-index a locked document, ensure document locks are respected and they can still be checked out and checked back in without issue.

column6: Language-Specific local culture format settings respect display date, datetime, floating point, and currency fields appropriately.
Ensure that a document's name is properly formatted when choosing the "Send To" -> "File" option within Document Retrieval.
Images load properly when viewed within the document viewer on Internet Explorer 11.
column7: The image viewer no longer displays a null reference when viewing documents in Internet Explorer 11.

column6: Error is no longer thrown when viewing an E-Form in Workflow and exiting out of Workflow
Exception is no longer thrown when routing E-Forms out of a Workflow queue using an Ad-Hoc Task
End-users who do not have access to restricted keywords are able to use Autofill Keyword Sets to modify the keyword values.
column7: When restricted users use the Split Document ad hoc action on a document with restricted keywords, they are able to use Autofill Keyword Sets to modify the keyword values on the newly created document.

column6: Resolved an issue where certain Workflow rules/actions would not operate correctly in Oracle Databases.
column7: In Oracle Databases, certain Workflow rules/actions would not properly save their configuration with OnBase Studio. As a result, these rules/actions would throw an exception when executed in a client. This has now been resolved.

column6: Users of institutional databases will now see correct user accounts in the Unity Client's load balancing administration screen.
column7: This change makes the Unity Client reflect what is available in the Thick Client.

column6: Adjustments to Related Document list viewer to correct error under very specific circumstances.
column7: In the Unity Client the Related Document list viewer would not consistently open items if the list viewer was sized in a specific way. This has been corrected.

column6: Added wfcontenttype value when converting a legacy lifecycle to a unity lifecycle.
column7: Previously, wfcontenttype was not being set to 1.

column6: Updated localization for Default Property Bag setting in OnBase Studio.
column7: This bug corrects an untranslated string for Default Property Bag setting in OnBase Studio.

column6: Updated the Web Client to properly display "System Task Name" after the execution of a System Task.
column7: Previously, the Web Client's confirmation message would display the "System Task ID" rather than "System Task Name." This change will make it simpler for end users to understand the confirmation message.

column6: System Task interface translations are now consistently reflected in all right-click menus.
column7: Previously, interface translations did not apply when selecting System Tasks from the right-click menu directly from a document (as opposed to from the results' hitlist.)

column6: Changing the Content Type of a Portfolio Relation will now be maintained, and the Source Content Type will reflect that change.
column7: In prior versions the Source Content Type of a portfolio relation should be changed, but the change did not persist after saving the repository.

column6: When using a Workflow System Task and the Document Separation action, the viewer now refreshes to show the latest revision.
column7: The Document Viewer now refreshes, after a System Task using Document Separation is run, to display the latest Revision.

column6: Workflow queues containing Workview items with a queue filter will now respect a floating point attribute's precision value.
column7: Previously, the precision was not respected and padded with trailing 0's.

column6: In the web client, system tasks were previously unable to transition Workview objects between queues.
column7: Diagnostics would incorrectly report that a Workview object was not in an associated queue, and this would result in the System Task not transitioning the object to the destination queue. This has been corrected.

column6: OnAbort task lists now support drag and drop within OnBase Studio.
column7: Corrected a bug that prevented the ability to drag and drop Workflow rules, actions, and task lists within OnAbort task lists.

column6: Workflow custom layouts for users will now persist between sessions even if a custom banner is enabled in the Unity Client.
column7: This resolves an error where a custom banner in the Unity Client would cause a user's custom Workflow layout to intermittently reset between user sessions.

column6: Users can now 'Release Ownership' of a WorkView object in 'My Owned Items' without receiving an exception message.
column7: Prior to this users would receive an incorrect exception message.

column6: Resolved an exception that occurred when the Workflow action 'Copy Document' was used on a Document Type with 'Enable Geolocation Storage.'
column7: This change allows Workflow action 'Copy Document' to execute successfully on Document Types with 'Enable Geolocation Storage.'

column6: When an exception occurs due to the lack of a valid session after attempting an execute an ad hoc task, the error message has been clarified to indicate a possible cause is session loss.
column7: Prior to this change the exception message was unclear and lead to confusion.

column6: Corrected an issue where documents did not always display after selecting a Unity Pop link.
column7: Successive attempts at selecting Unity Pop Links that direct a user to the same Workflow queue would sometimes result in the document viewer not loading the document, requiring the user to manually refresh the inbox. This has been corrected.

column6: Notifications can now be successfully sent when they contain a MIKG with a blank value.
column7: Notifications can now be successfully sent when the notification contains a MIKG with a blank value. Prior to this the 'Send Notification' action would fail to execute.

column6: Addressed issue with "source value" in Workflow action "Create Form"
column7: The "Create Form" Workflow action now properly saves the "source value" option when mapping keyword values.

column6: Improvements made in the Web Client to Approval Delegation user list when the list contains a very large number of users.
column7: The type-ahead filter will limit results to the top 500 matching users, which will increase the speed at which results are returned. End users will need to continue to provide additional characters to filter the list below that number.

column6: Users will no longer receive duplicate WAM notifications at improper intervals.
column7: Corrected an issue that caused the Send Reminder Notification Workflow action to incorrectly send the Initial Notification immediately upon execution, rather than at the configured interval level.

column6: Corrected various issues that could occur in Workflow Approval Management caused by de-activated users in Approval Roles.
column7: Administrators of Approval Roles will now be able to correctly replace Approvers who have had their accounts de-activated.

column6: Folders now prompt with 'Load more' to allow loading more than 20 documents.
column7: In the past folders loaded more than 20 documents automatically when the user scrolled down the list, but this didn't work with all configurations. To make sure we always display the remaining documents in a folder, that functionality was replaced with a 'load more' prompt.

column6: FIXED: Filter not loading after adding columns via an OnAfterExecuteFilter event.
column7: Addressed an issue that would stop filters from loading when a column was added to the results via the OnAfterExecuteFilter Unity Script.

column6: FIXED: 'Invalid object name' Exception when connecting to an external class.
column7: Addressed an 'Invalid Object Name' exception that occurred when connecting to an external class via ODBC connection when the database was case sensitive and table names where upper case.

column6: Fixed an issue with values not being evaluated correctly in report templates.
column7: Fixed an issue with values not being evaluated correctly in report templates. This would occur when using math functions inside a filter block when using the report processor with the tag RP-2.0.

column6: Fixed an issue with the Workflow inbox not being refreshed after executing a Workflow task.
column7: Fixed an issue with the Workflow inbox not being refreshed after executing a Workflow task.

column6: Fixed an issue that would allow objects to be saved without a value for a required attribute in embedded filter in edit mode.
column7: Fixed an issue that would allow objects to be saved without a value for a required attribute in embedded filter in edit mode.

column6: Fixed an issue with the "An item with the same key has already been added" exception being thrown in the Unity API.
column7: Fixed an issue with the "An item with the same key has already been added" exception being thrown in the Unity API.

column6: FIXED: ReadOnly Property set via client side script is not respected
column7: Fixed issue where an attribute with a dataset that was set to read-only via a script could still be populated through the UI.

column6: FIXED: Default Static Folder Sort is not Respected
column7: The static default sort order is now respected.

column6: FIXED: Unable to remove an Extension Class from life cycle
column7: Resolved an issue where objects in an extension class could not be removed from a life cycle.

column6: FIXED: Objects are no longer prompting to save changes after hiding lookup filter.
column7: Fixed an issue where hiding a lookup filter would mark related boolean attributes as modified.

column6: FIXED: Currency, Floating Point and Decimal values are not displayed correctly when using the regional setting fr-CH
column7: Fixed an issue where attribute values may not be displayed correctly on an object when the local is set to fr-CH.

column6: FIXED: WorkView date field cannot be filled manually with the German ISO time format dd.mm.yyyy
column7: Fixed an issue that prevented typing in a date using a German time format.

column6: FIXED: Data in embedded filter may revert when saving
column7: Fixed an issue where data in an embedded filter may revert back after it was changed. This could happen when the user created at least two new rows in an editable filter and after creating the 2nd row, changes data in the first row and saves the editable filter (save or toggle edit mode off).

column6: FIXED: Column order for user defined filters may not be saved correctly.
column7: Fixed an issue where columns were not stored in the correct order when saving a user defined filter with more than 16 display columns.

column6: FIXED: An error is thrown when composing a document for an external class.
column7: Composing a document for an external class no longer throws the exception "External Data Objects must specify the Class they are associated to".

column6: FIXED: External Object will not display in the Web Client if the Object ID matches that of another External Object
column7: External objects now display properly even if the object ID matches that of an object in a different class.

column6: Fixed an issue where currency symbols were not displayed correctly.
column7: Fixed an issue where currency symbols were not displayed correctly.

column6: FIXED: Error: Parameters in database command exceeds the maximum (2000) parameters allowed.
column7: Fixed an issue that would cause this error message when a filter returned more than 2000 results.

column6: FIXED: Component Doctor no longer times out.
column7: Fixed an issue where the component doctor may time out on very large databases.

column6: Addressed issue with creation of 'Import File Name' and 'Index File Name' system Keyword Types with XML DIP.
column7: The 'Import File Name' and 'Index File Name' system Keyword Types are now created within the database when licensed for XML DIP.

column6: The Configuration Client no longer closes unexpectedly when deleting security keywords
column7: A System Administrator in OnBase is now able to delete multiple security keywords from a user or user group without the Configuration Client closing unexpectedly.

column6: Filter rows now persist their state between user sessions.
column7: When enabling/disabling filter rows in Diagnostics Console, the state will persist to the next time that the user launches Diagnostics Console.

column6: Saved preferences of column ordering now functions correctly when new columns are added in Diagnostics Console.
Documents purged using the "Retain Keywords" now have a right-click context menu in document lists
column7: In versions of OnBase before Foundation EP1, documents purged using the "Retain Keywords" purge processor option now have a right-click context menu in document lists.

column6: A warning message is now displayed to an end user when the file is too large
column7: A warning message will now be displayed to an end user preventing them from sending an e-mail through the Web Client using Office 365 messages that contain attachments that are over 3MB in size

column6: The Temporary File Service, when used with the API Server, will now properly remove files marked as expired.
column7: The Temporary File Service, when used with the The API Server, has the ability to mark the files in the temporary directory as expired. This is so that instead of immediate deletion, background processors will clean up these files on a schedule. It was found that these background processors were not properly removing the files, and this has now been fixed.

column6: Fixed an issue where in certain situations Foundation DBUtils will fail to upgrade the OnBase Database to 21.1 EP5
column7: If the "Create Check Document Types" option was used to upgrade the OnBase database to 20.3 EP3, when attempting to upgrade to 21.1 EP5 the upgrade would fail. After this change Foundation DBUtils will properly identify "Create Check Document Types" has been run previously and will not attempt to create them again.

column6:
The 'DurableBufferPath' key was added to allow Durable HTTP buffer files to be written to a non-default file path.
The 'DurableBufferRollingInterval ' key was added to allow the user to specify the Durable Buffer file rolling interval.
Addressed an issue in which files over 1GB would fail to download from the Plan Review Portal.
column7: Plan Review portal users are now able to download files over 1GB in size from both the published Review Cycle documents and the published Approved documents tabs.

column6: doLogin parameter no longer causes infinite log in loop when included in a unencrypted Reporting Viewer link.
column7: Before this fix, including the doLogin parameter in an unencrypted Reporting Viewer link would cause the log in page to reappear continuously after submitting valid credentials. This has now been fixed, and log in is now successful with the item displaying when including this parameter (set to any value) in the unencrypted link.

column6: Send to > Create New Document - Unity - While attempting to expand an autofill over Readonly Keywords without changing DocType ,  autofill expands successfully.
column7: Send to > Create New Document - Unity - While attempting to expand an autofill over Readonly Keywords without changing DocType , autofill expands successfully.

column6: E-forms opened or created from the Personal Page will now close out of their new window automatically
column7: When opening an E-Form from the Personal page, the Unity Client opens a new window to show the E-Form. Once the form is saved or submitted, the window will now close automatically.

column6: Addressed an issue with the Document Auto Name string for Adobe Sign documents.
column7: When documents are returned from Adobe Sign, the document now maintains the Auto Name as set in OnBase.

column6: HTML files are no longer converted to a HTM file format when exported from the Unity Client using Sent To | Mail Recipient or Send To | File.
column7: HTML files that have been imported into OnBase will now retain their original file format when exported out of OnBase from the Unity Client using either the Send To | Mail Recipient or Send To | File functionality

column6: Addressed an issue where the interface could freeze when uploading a document as a revision via the Word add-in application.
column7: Uploading document revisions with the Word add-in and other Office add-in apps should work as expected and not freeze the application.

column6: Hidden System Keywords are now populated when a user lacks the Access Hidden Keywords privilege
column7: Hidden System Keywords are now populated when a user lacks the Access Hidden Keywords privilege. Previously, this privilege was required to have System Keywords, that were also Hidden, populated on the document, such as the Mail System ID keyword for an imported email.

column6: Corrected an issue with Review Comments where in some cases double clicking within the text box when editing or creating a new comment would delete any new unsaved text you entered.
column7: When entering text while editing or creating a new Review Comment, double clicking anywhere in the text field will no longer delete the text you have entered.

column6: When Unity Client is in Service Mode, reconnection is now logged as "logon to Unity Client" in the his.securitylog.
column7: When the Unity Client is running in Service Mode for an extended period of time and it loses connection to the Application Server, re-establishing a connection will be logged as "logon to Unity Client" (subactionnum 15).   Previously, this was logged as  "logon to Core Services" (subactionum 9).  This change provides more accurate reporting for audit purposes.

column6: Resolved an issue where exception was thrown when submitting a Unity Form after modifying an existing field associated to a security keyword
column7: User will now get a message “User’s permission is revoked and one document has been removed from the document list” when access to the document is limited for the user after modifying the value of an existing field associated to a security keyword. The modified value should be according to the condition set for the Security Keyword in the OnBase Configuration. This will allow the user to submit a Unity Form after modifying an existing field associated to a security keyword without an exception.

column6: Added the ability to allow users to disable Chromium caching for Unity Forms in Unity Client
column7: Users will now be able to see a new configuration property <DisableUnityFormChromiumCache> added in obunity.exe.config. On setting this property to True, the form cache will be fetched from the App Server instead of looking into the Chromium Cache. Currently the cache for Unity Forms is fetched from <ChromiumCache> which can sometimes throw Exception. This new configuration will allow users to disable Chromium caching for Unity Forms in Unity Client.

column6: Using the Send To | File feature to export a JPEG file (.jpeg) Document as "Rendered Format" in the Web Client no longer causes an error
column7: Using the Send To | File feature to export a JPEG file (.jpeg) Document as "Rendered Format" in the Web Client no longer causes an error.  If the source OnBase document is single-page it will be downloaded as a JPEG file.  If the source OnBase document is multi-page, it will be downloaded as a TIFF file.

column6: Resolved an issue where certain Workflow rules/actions would not operate correctly in Oracle Databases.
column7: In Oracle Databases, certain Workflow rules/actions would not properly save their configuration with OnBase Studio. As a result, these rules/actions would throw an exception when executed in a client. This has now been resolved.

column6: Addressed memory usage issue that occurred during large DIP batch commits
column7: Decreased memory usage of the OnBase Client when large DIP batches are committed.

column6: When thumbnails are disabled, paging through an image rendered document while using DDS will now cache the images correctly.
column7: Caching the images will allow for increased performance, and should match the performance of when thumbnails are enabled.

column6: The Web Client main window remains enabled after a user deletes a note from the Notes dialog box within the Workflow window
column7: The Web Client main window remains enabled after a user deletes a note from the Notes dialog box within the Workflow window

column6: Restricted users are able to use the Split Document ad hoc action on documents with restricted keywords present.
column7: When users without access to restricted keywords use the Split Document ad hoc action on a document with restricted keywords, they are able to use Autofill Keyword Sets to modify the keyword values on the newly created document.

column6: Addressed issue with "source value" in Workflow action "Create Form"
column7: The "Create Form" Workflow action now properly saves the "source value" option when mapping keyword values.

column6: Keyword Panel does not display the correct document type's keywords when when re-indexing from a Workflow Re-index task
column7: Resolved an issue when re-indexing a document using the Workflow Re-index action that caused the primary document's keywords display instead of the default select within the action.

column6: Improper tag displayed in Assigned Users column of the Workflow Locations Dialog in Web Client.
column7:
Line break tag
has been removed from the Assigned Users column of the Workflow Locations Dialog in Web Client.

column6: Corrected unsupported configuration in the Set Property Value action.
column7: When using the User group name(s) of current user setting in the Set Property Value action - the Set property to all value instances check box is now set and disabled.

column6: Addressed an issue with Agenda Online Full Text search did not return the complete list of results is some cases.
column7: A problem existed where if a large number of Agenda Online documents were in the system the Full Text Search would not show the proper document result set. It will now behave the same as it does today in the Unity an Web clients. It will filter then search the documents correctly to display the proper document result hit list. This should match what you find when you run the same search in Unity or Web client.

column6: Allowed Application Enabler's Smart-Screen application to read ComboBox value in Chrome
column7: Included additional logic to ensure InfoTree captures ComboBox values from Chrome

column6: Fixed issue with Nested XML Query Placeholders for Repeating XML Query Placeholder
column7: When creating Nested XML Query Placeholders for Repeating XML Query Placeholder, a NullReferenceException is no longer thrown.

column6: Fixed: Certain Document Composition documents' size may be incorrectly inflated.
column7: Reduced size of Document Composition referencing fragments from another template that includes images

column6: Addressed an issue when generating a document packet that indexes a multi-instant keyword group populated by an autofill keyword set.
column7: An error is no longer displayed and makes the client unresponsive when a user generates a document packet that indexes a multi-instance keyword group populated by an autofill keyword set.

column6: For a meeting that has already been run with media, you now have the ability to continue running the meeting without media.
column7: This is helpful in cases where either media publisher or the encoder go offline during the meeting. This prevents minutes client from freezing up and it will allow you to continue to use the minutes UI for creating minutes, motions votes, setting timestamps, etc during the meeting even with media being down.  The end result is that there is minimal interruption in the minutes UI and that disconnects and reconnects of media are handled seamlessly without affecting the flow of the live meeting.

column6: Added a more intuitive message when attempting to create a new user in Web Client Administration without selecting any user groups.
column7: While creating a new user in the Web Client Administration panel, if no user group is selected, a more intuitive message is shown telling the user to select a user group before continuing.  Thus, providing a more user friendly dialog.

column6: File downloads successfully continue after a NIST Re-Authentication request
column7: Files downloaded after a disconnect correctly prompt the Re-Authentication dialog, and downloads continue after a successful Re-Authentication

column6: Epic Integration Scanning: Resolved an issue in OnBase that was uncovered when Epic introduced a change to the FHIRCast process.
column7:
Resolved an issue where the SAS or FOS application would close with an error message shortly after loading. In FOS, the debuglog.txt file would log an error similar to the following: "Error processing FhirCast event. Error received invalid response when sending heartbeat."

The issue has been been resolved. It was discovered due to a change Epic introduced to the FHIRCast process. FHIRCast is used during integrated scanning to maintain a heartbeat connection between Epic and the OnBase scanning application while there is an active scanning session. While Epic's change aligns closely with FHIR industry standards, it exposed a bug in the OnBase code that broke the FHIRCast connection. The issue was not caused by Epic's change but was uncovered because of it.

column6: DeficiencyPop now supports printing when launched from Epic via the SMART on FHIR integration.
column7: Fixed printing OnBase documents from DeficiencyPop when integrated with Epic via SMART on FHIR launches.

column6: A bug that caused Analysts to be inadvertently logged out of DeficiencyPop has been corrected.
column7: An issue has been corrected that caused an Analyst user to be logged out of DeficiencyPop when they hit "Enter" after selecting the responsible Physician field while creating a deficiency.

column6: Resolved issue when scanning in the registration workflow via the SMART on FHIR integration for Epic Hyperdrive.
column7: The integrated scanning integration now links documents to the correct encounter when launching from one encounter but scanning to a different encounter. An example of this workflow is when the scanning client is launched from a surgical encounter but the document is being scanned to the pre-admission encounter.

column6: Front Office Scanning temporary files now write to the correct directory.
column7: Prior to this fix, Front Office Scanning (FOS) could attempt to write some types of temporary files to the directory where FOS was installed which, depending on the user's permissions, could result in a failure to write the temporary file. Now, FOS will correctly use the environment variables to determine the correct location to write temporary files.

column6: Documents imported via the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor are now stored on multiple disk group mass storage copies.
column7: When importing documents that are assigned to a disk group that has multiple mass storage copies configured, the HL7 Advanced Document Import processor will now store the documents on all available mass storage copies instead of just Copy 1.

column6: Resolved race condition in the HL7 Back-end Job Processor when propagating patient demographics.
column7: A race condition was possible which could cause patient demographics not to propagate to all MRNs and Charts related to an MPI. This would, in turn, result in patient demographics not being updated on the related documents as well. A task force bulletin will be released with additional information. Please reach out to your first line of Support and reference [HC-201497] to determine if your solution is impacted.

column6: Corrected issue with the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic that could cause an error to appear even though the document loaded successfully.
column7:
Resolved an issue where an Epic error could display, stating that a document failed to load even though the document successfully loaded in the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration. 

Note: This does not address any potential issue with the SMART on FHIR integration viewer. If an issue exists with the SMART on FHIR integration, a separate software change will be completed to address it.

column6: A bug that caused an incorrect filter to be applied to DeficiencyPop when integrated with Epic has been resolved
column7: An issue has been resolved that was causing the incorrect Chart to be filtered to when DeficiencyPop was launched from Epic using the COM integration.  This issue was present in the layout used by Physicians to complete deficiencies.

column6: The Analysis layout will now display a message when no documents are available.
column7: A message will now display in the document viewer stating "No documents are available for analysis" when the user accesses a chart without documents.  Prior to this change, the user would see a set of ellipses which made the application look as if it were still loading.

column6: Resolved issue with Physician Acknowledgement stamps not being saved in the location that they were placed in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: Physician Acknowledgement stamps were moving to the upper left corner of the page they were placed upon closing the Healthcare Web Viewer. Stamps will now save correctly and remain in the location on the page they were placed.

column6: Corrected issue with the HL7 Patient Record Processor not saving patient confidentiality codes.
column7: An issue was discovered where the HL7 Patient Record Processor was not applying patient confidentiality codes when processing incoming HL7 messages. This issue did not impact the Medical Records Chart import processor.

column6: Addressed an issue in the Forms tab of Document History that was preventing you from seeing the full multi-line text value because it was being cut off.
column7: The cell now expands its height automatically so you can view all the lines of the multi-line text value in both the Old and New Value columns.

column6: Reverse Autofill Keyset exclusions are now respected in the Web Client
column7: Keywords marked with exclusions will not be populated with values from the AutoFill Keyset when using Reverse AutoFill lookup during Document Import.

column6: The 'Use Local File Cache' configuration option for Distributed Disk Services now also improves the performance of image rendered PDFs.
End-users without the Access Restricted Keywords privilege are able to save documents with Hidden Keywords.
column7: An issue was fixed in the HTML Web Client to allow users without the "Access Restricted Keywords" privilege to save documents with hidden keywords. This issue was addressed in the Add/Modify, Re-Index, and HTML Send To > Create New Document contexts.

column6: You are now able to reset the Recording Type of a meeting if it was set to "None".
column7:
You may need to do this if the meeting has been run with the "None" media option and you need to run the meeting with Media later. If the recording type is set to "None" then you can't add media to the meeting so in the past you would have to create a new meeting or call support to reset it for you because it was not possible to reset it on you own in the UI. Now you can right click on the meeting in the list and select the Reset Recording Type option.

This option will only be available if the Recording Type status is "None"

You cannot reset the recording type if there is audio or video recorded for the meeting.

column6: Corrected an issue when using copy and paste with a newly created note markup.
column7: Corrected an issue where you would see an error when doing a copy and paste with a newly created note markup in the engineering viewer.

column6: Removed Delete and Copy/Paste limitations when multiselecting markups in the engineering viewer.
column7: You can now multiselect markups including notes and standard notes and the Delete and Copy/Paste markup buttons are enabled function accordingly.

column6: The column width can be customized in the Reverse Keyset Lookup dialog box
column7: Specifying the default column widths for a Reverse AutoFill Keyset within OnBase Configuration allows for the Web Client to display Reverse AutoFill Keyset lookup results with custom column widths.

column6: DeficiencyPop for Epic COM integration now launches successfully from Epic Hyperspace Classic.
column7: Resolved issue with the DeficiencyPop for Epic COM integration not launching from Epic Hyperspace Classic. Note: this issue did not impact Epic Hyperdrive (COM or SMART on FHIR) - only Hyperspace Classic COM integration was impacted.

column6: Unity Client tab order updated for External Autofill Expansion
column7: Tabbing after performing an External Autofill Expansion successfully targets the next expected keyword in the Keyword Panel.

column6: Resolved issue when attempting to split jpeg documents within Document Separation (ad hoc or scan queue) in Unity Client. The new document(s) are converted to tiff and the file size on disk is inflated once saved.
column7: Update the code to allow split jpeg documents within Document Separation (ad hoc or scan queue) in Unity Client. The new document(s) retain the original document’s file type and size.

column6: The Scan Acquisition Server integration for Epic Hyperdrive now accurately retains the last-selected Document Type during indexing as well as the application window size and position.
column7:
Prior to this fix, the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) did not retain either the last-used Document Type during indexing, nor the application window size and position that the user selected. These settings are now stored in the registry to ensure retention between launches of SAS. This primarily impacts the Hyperdrive integration for Epic (both COM and SMART on FHIR), as the Hyperspace integration managed the scan wrapper differently. So, Windows is now able to retain these settings between Hyperspace Classic SAS launches.

Please note that these settings will only be retained on the workstation/VM where the user is currently logged in.

column6: Resolved issue with timeouts in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7:
The timeout attribute in the sessionState element is used instead of the sessionTimeout attribute in the Hyland.Web.HealthcareWebViewer element in the Web Server web.config file.
This improves the stability of the timeout functionality when the configured Web Server or User Group Timeouts are shorter than the Healthcare Web Viewer sessionTimeout. 
Earlier, a shorter Web Server or User Group Timeout could be configured, and a user's session would be ended according to the timeout configuration. However, the user was neither notified when their session ended, nor prompted to extend their session. As a result, thumbnails would not load and different actions on the document, such as attempting to view keywords, save page rotations, and others also failed.

column6: Resolved high CPU usage issue in the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic.
column7: An issue that impacted some OnBase 21.1.38.1000 through 21.1.45.1000 environments was resolved. In those EP5 builds, high CPU usage may occur on VM hosts and/or client workstations where the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic is used. The issue does not occur for all customers and cannot be replicated on demand. However, if higher than expected CPU usage is consistently occurring on virtual machines or workstations where the Healthcare Web Viewer COM Integration for Epic is installed and used, it is recommended to upgrade to this or a later build of OnBase to resolve the issue.

column6: Physician Acknowledgements in the Healthcare Web Viewer are saved after being placed instead of saving when the viewer is closing.
column7:
Hyland identified an issue where a browser can be exiting the Healthcare Web Viewer page so quickly, or in such a way that the save events triggered on close may not have a chance to execute properly. This could result in Physician Acknowledgements not saving as expected after being placed. 

Since Physician Acknowledgements are now saved immediately after being dropped/placed, the acknowledgement note/text will no longer remain red until closing the Healthcare Web Viewer. Newly placed acknowledgements will turn blue, indicating that they have been saved.

column6: Imported Platter Management Unity Scheduler Tasks were not visible in Unity Management Console
column7: An issue was found where any imported Platter Management Unity Scheduler tasks were then not showing up in the Unity Management Console. This has now been fixed so that any imported Platter Management scheduler task will then appear in the UMC.

column6: The DeficiencyPop integration for Epic now shows all encounters associated with a deficiency episode in analysis mode.
column7: Prior to this fix, the DeficiencyPop integration for Epic would only show the primary CSN for a deficiency episode when launched in analysis mode. Now, DeficiencyPop correctly displays all encounters associated with the deficiency episode so that analysis can be performed on the related documents.

column6: Resolved an innocuous error that would be logged when importing non-medical record documents that were associated with the Medical Records Custom Query.
column7: This change resolved an object reference error that would be logged on the Application Server when uploading documents via Scan Acquistion Server. This only occurred when uploading documents indexed to a document type that was associated with the Medical Records Custom Query. This was an innocuous error - no issues actually occurred with the upload or the indexing of the imported document.

column6: Resolved issue when using the Send To | Create New Document action in Unity Client or Web Client, which would fail to retain the original document’s file type and size.
column7: Update the code to allow the Send To | Create New Document action in Unity Client or Web Client retain the original document’s file type and size.

column6: Resolved an issue with the Scan Acquisition Server while sweeping Microsoft Office documents.
column7: While uploading a Microsoft Office document while the document is still open (e.g., in Word, Excel, etc.)., the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) will not crash anymore.

column6: Resolved issue with documents not linking to Epic Hyperdrive in specific SMART on FHIR integrated scanning workflows.
column7:
Prior to this fix, if Epic provided a document type in the initial SMART on FHIR context for scanning workflows that do not require scanning to that document type, any other document types selected by the user would not be linked in Epic. The only document type that would be linked in Epic is the document type that was included in the initial SMART on FHIR context. All other document types would not be sent to Epic for linking. 

Note: This issue is unlikely to occur in most solutions as Epic will usually not pass a document type in the SMART on FHIR context when a specific document type is not required by the scanning workflow.

column6: Resolved a condition in Full-Text Search where indexing performance may be degraded for catalogs with many large documents.
column7: Catalogs with many documents would previously experience degraded indexing performance. This has been addressed and performance has improved regardless of the number of documents in the catalog.

column6: Resolved a memory issue with the Medical Records Orphan Documents Processor task.
column7: Under normal operating conditions, the Attachment Lock Processor that is part of the Orphan Document Processor works without any performance concerns. However, in certain customer environments, the Orphan Document Processor consumed large amounts of memory due to the number of rows in a specific database table. This fix resolves the issue by loading and processing the data from the table in smaller groups so that a large amount of memory is not consumed loading the entire table.

column6: License sharing fix
column7: Fixed an issue with license sharing that could result in a shared license being released prematurely, potentially causing downstream licensing errors.

column6: Resolving the bug where the Select List drop down arrow on Unity Forms was misaligned with the text box.
column7: Users are now able to view the Select List drop down arrow aligned with the text box.

column6: Fixed Defect: Workflow Action error when creating a WorkView Notification that included the tag <<AttachAllDocumentsInFolder:Specifed Folder>>
column7: An issue was resolved where executing a Workflow action to create a WorkView Notification using the <<AttachAllDocumentsInFolder:Specified Folder>> function threw the following error: "Collection does not support finding items by name."

column6: Resolved Healthcare Web Viewer printing issue for the SMART on FHIR Epic Hyperdrive Integration.
column7:
The Epic Hyperdrive SMART on FHIR scan viewer window does not allow browser PDF controls to be used to render PDF files, including print previews. This impacted the Healthcare Web Viewer - since print previews could not load, documents could not be printed. To resolve this issue, when printing a document, the Healthcare Web Viewer now launches an external window to load the PDF print preview, allowing the document to be printed. This change only impacts the SMART on FHIR integration - the COM integration for Hyperdrive and Hyperspace Classic will continue to successfully launch embedded print previews.

Note: Since the print preview is not contained within the scan viewer window, it is not automatically closed when the scan viewer window is closed. However, the print preview window will only be visible when viewing the patient for which it was launched and will close automatically when that patient context is closed in Epic.

column6: Resolved exceptions that caused the HL7 Back-end Job Processor to fail to load or process jobs.
column7: Fixed an issue with the HL7 Back-end Job Processor that caused it to fail to load or process jobs.

column6: Resolved HL7 Listener crashes related to segment length and carriage returns.
column7: The HL7 Listener no longer crashes when any segment contains fewer than 3 characters or contains multiple carriage returns in the segment containing the $$FILEDATA field (typically OBX).

column6: Corrected an issue where .CR2 image files were unable to render in OBIMGLIB/Unity Client
column7: An issue was corrected where .CR2 Image files were unable to render in OBIMGLIB/Unity Client. Users would try to view images that were the .CR2 file type and the image would not render correctly. Users can now render these image types.

column6: Corrected an issue where accessing the "Customer Information Administration" within the Unity Client within an institutional environment was throwing an error
column7:
The Customer Information Administration window was failing with an error within institutional environments. This administration window is now accessible without error.
Previously, the error(s) seen were as follows:
"Parameter INSTITUIONS_PARAM was not in the correct format."
And Diagnostic Console showed:
"Institution string 410,0,412,413,3,414,4,2,415,411,1,5,99 was not in the expected format System.FormatException: Parameter INSTITUTIONS_PARAM was not in the correct format at Hyland.Core.ServiceHandlers.CustomerInformation.GetRecipientsForProcessClass()"

column6: Resolved an issue where hidden fields were displayed to the user when the form was still loading.
column7: Users now see the loading of spinner and a blank page with no fields being displayed until the form is fully loaded.

column6: Resolved issue where users could not select Document Type for "Re-Index Document" Workflow Action
column7: Resolved an issue where users were no longer being prompted to select the Document Type when using the "Re-Index Document" Workflow Action, even when the "User Must Resolve Doc Type" AppEnabler Option was selected. This fix allows users to select the appropriate Document Type when multiple Document Types are configured for the enabled application, as expected.

column6: Corrected an issue where note and standard not markups created in OnBase 17.0.1 would not display after upgrading to OnBase 21.1+
column7: Old note and standard note markups from OnBase 17.0.a and prior will now display in an OnBase 21.1 and above system.

column6: Resolved issue causing the Document Separation window to appear behind the Unity Client window.
column7: Previously, an issue was encountered when processing documents in the “Awaiting Index” status in the Batch Processing layout of the Unity Client. When users right-clicked a document within the Document Viewer and selected to send it to Document Separation, the Document Separation window appeared behind the Unity Client window. This has been resolved.

column6: The Healthcare Web Viewer now automatically saves note changes when being closed.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer no longer prompts to save note changes when being closed, if notes have unsaved changes. This results in a smoother experience for users while ensuring notes are still saved.

column6: The Healthcare Web Viewer now displays unburned Physician Acknowledgements for users that have View privileges.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer now displays unburned Physician Acknowledgements for users that have View privileges to the MedRec-Physician Acknowledgement note type. This brings the Healthcare Web Viewer into alignment with how the legacy ActiveX Epic integration viewer behaved.

column6: Resolved issue with the Acknowledge button text displaying incorrectly after placing an acknowledgement in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: Prior to this fix, the Acknowledge button text would display below the button icon in the Healthcare Web Viewer toolbar after placing an acknowledgement. Now, the button text will display properly.

column6: Resolved issue with Epic Welcome Kiosk not automatically registering the license on workstations.
column7: The Workstation Client for Epic Welcome Kiosk license will now correctly be automatically registered on workstations where Epic Welcome Kiosk is used for the first time, as long as there is an available license.

column6: Sporadic 403 error no longer occurs when attempting to edit and publish a Unity Form in the Forms Designer when using a Load Balancer
column7: When configured to to use a Load Balancer, editing and publishing a Unity Form in the Forms Designer in the Unity Client would sporadically produce a 403 error. This issue is fixed now.

column6: Sporadic 403 error no longer occurs when attempting to preview a Unity Form in a test drive view when using a Load Balancer
column7: When configured to to use a Load Balancer, previewing a Unity Form in the Test Drive view in the Unity Client would sporadically produce a 403 error. This issue is fixed now.

column6: Updated the Unity Client's Workflow interface to add previously unavailable options when using “Match Keyword to User Name” load balancing.
column7: In the Unity Client's Workflow interface, two items have been added in relation to “Match Keyword to User Name” load balancing: “Remove All Existing Assignments” and the "<Unassigned>" option.

column6: Resolved issue with HL7 Sender crashing when sending documents with non-square DPI.
column7: The HL7 Sender no longer crashes when attempting to send Base64-encoded documents with a non-square DPI (e.g. 204x98 DPI). A non-square DPI is common with faxed documents, so this issue may impact those documents more than scanned documents. This issue only impacted EP5/21.1.

column6: Resolved issue with multi-instance Keyword Values not being applied in proper sequence
column7: Resolved an issue to ensure Keyword Values from Multi-Instance Keyword Type Groups (MIKGs) are now assigned to documents in the appropriate sequential order during Advanced Capture processing. This improves data integrity for documents with any sequence number.

column6: Addressed an issue where you were unable to access an agenda item form if the item was targeted for a meeting that had been deleted.|Fixed an issue where you were unable to access an agenda item form if the item was targeted for a meeting that had been deleted.
column7: If a meeting is deleted, all agenda items that are targeted for that meeting are able to be accessed. You will see a value of "Original target meeting not found," indicating that it was set at one point, but is now no longer in the system. You would be able to change the target meeting to another valid meeting if desired if it hasn't been added to a meeting agenda yet. Once the item is added to a meeting, the field is locked. You can move it back to pending items to unlock the field to change it if needed.|If a meeting is deleted, all agenda items that are targeted for that meeting are still be able to be accessed. You will see a value of "Original target meeting not found," indicating that it was set at one point but is now no longer in the system. You change the target meeting to another valid meeting if needed, but only if it hasn't been added to a meeting agenda. Once the item is added to a meeting, the field is locked. You can move it back to pending items to unlock the field to change it if needed.

column6: Addressed an Fixed issue where Section Title still displays old value in the Agenda Online Item Details panel. after the Section Title is edited in the Agenda Client.|Addressed issue where Section Title still displays old value in the Agenda Online Item Details panel.
column7: Fixed issue where Section Title still displays old value in the Agenda Online Item Details panel after the Section Title is edited in the Agenda Client.|Updated Agenda Online so that once the Section Title has been edited, the edited value is displayed in the Agenda Online Details panel.

column6: Addressed an issue where the item title in the Accessible view only displayed the short item title, which did not match the long item title in the standard view of Agenda Online.|Fixed an issue where the item title in the Accessible view only displayed the short item title. This title not match the long item title in the Standard view of Agenda Online.
column7: This fix allows for the Accessible view to show the long item title if one exists. If a long title does not exist, the short item title will be used instead. In both cases the Standard view and Accessible view will now show the same item title, either long or short.|This fix allows for the Accessible view to show the long item title if one exists. If a long title does not exist, then the short item title will be used. In both cases the Standard view and Accessible view will now show the same item title, either long or short.

column6: The F6 shortcut successfully expands Autofills within the Unity Client.
column7: The F6 shortcut key successfully expands Autofill Keyword Sets within the Unity Client. This fixes a previously found error, allowing users to quickly and easily expand Autofill Keyword Sets in the Unity Client with one keystroke.

column6: End users are able to access the Reverse Autofill Lookup button even if the document type has a Hidden keyword on it.
column7: Previously end users were not able to access the Reverse Autofill Lookup button if the document type they are indexing into has a Hidden keyword value on it.

column6: The Unity Client now respects the "Use JPEG compressed TIFF as default color image format" global client setting
Distribution Service supports non-ascii characters allowing for internationalized email support.
column7: Prior to this change, even if the SMTP Server was configured to allow for non-ascii characters as the recipient (or sender, or reply-to), Distribution Service was not able to properly send those emails.

column6: Resolved a hanging issue when scanning multiple pages in the Unity Client
column7: Fixed an issue where the Unity Client would hang while scanning multiple pages from the "Import" layout. This allows users to view scanned pages in the Document Viewer as expected.

column6: Multipage documents can be appended to Document Types configured with a Default File Format of Image File Format.
column7:
All pages are correctly appended when a Web Client user attempts to append a multipage document to a non-revisable Document Type 
with a Default File Format of Image File Format.

column6: The Update Document Retention Status Workflow action now behaves as expected when executed as System Work of Evaluation Processor Life Cycle
column7: The Update Document Retention Status Workflow action now behaves as expected when executed as System Work for an Evaluation Processor Life Cycle.

column6: EDM Briefcase users are now able to scroll horizontally and vertically within the Briefcase Document dialog box.
column7: When the user has a large number of documents in their EDM briefcase, the list of documents is contained within a table that can be navigated with a scroll bar.

column6: EDM Briefcase users are notified of how many characters over the limit they are in the Check In Comment field within the Document Check-In dialog box.
column7: While leaving a check in comment for a document, if the user enters too many characters, they will see the message "Characters Over Limit: [n]", with [n] being the number of characters that are over the limit.

column6: Users without the Access Restricted Keyword privilege are now able to execute the Save as New Document option.
column7: The Save as New Document option will create a new document containing hidden and/or read-only Keyword Values when a User Group is not assigned the Access Restricted Keyword privilege when checking in a document.|When a user checks in a document that contains hidden or read-only Keyword Types as a member of a User Group that is not assigned the Access Restricted Keyword privilege, the Save as New Document option will create a new document that contains the hidden or read-only Keyword Types.

column6: The Check In Document from Briefcase dialog box correctly displays the Auto-Name String for the document being checked in|The Check In Document from Briefcase dialog box correctly displays the Auto-Name String for the document being checked in.
column7: When checking in documents via the EDM briefcase (both in the Check In Documents dialog box displayed on login and the Check In Document from Briefcase dialog box) the document's Auto-Name String is correctly displayed.

column6: Repeating/duplicate folder query select statements are prevented from running against the database.
column7: Repeating/duplicate folder query select statements are prevented from running against the database. This will prevent unneeded system stress and, in the case of larger batches, chances for a system timeout.

column6: Addressed an issue in the Unity Client where AutoFill Keysets may not expand when re-indexing multiple documents
column7: Updated the Unity Client to allow AutoFill Keysets to expand properly on the re-index dialog when multiple documents are selected for re-index.

column6: Multiple Documents with Restricted Keywords can be queued for upload.
column7:
End users are able to simultaneously queue multiple Documents with Required Keywords without entering Required Keyword values.
Values must be entered before executing the import.

column6: In Unity Client, the warning message will display when importing or re-indexing a document with double-byte characters if keyword values exceed keyword length.
column7: In Unity Client, the warning message will display when importing or re-indexing a document with double-byte characters if keyword values exceed keyword length.

column6: A more informative error is displayed in the Unity Client UI and detailed error is logged to Unity Diagnostics when keyword configurations differ.|A more informative error is displayed in the Unity Client UI and detailed error is logged to Unity Diagnostics.
column7: There is improved error handling when re-indexing from a document type to another document type with the same MIKG but different keyword configurations as a restricted user.|There is improved error handling when re-indexing from a document type to another document type with the same MIKG but different keyword configurations as as restricted user.

column6: Addressed an issue where the "Do not require seconder" option was not being respected in certain scenarios.|Fixed an issue where the "Do not require seconder" option was not being respected in certain scenarios.
column7:
Motions will now respect the "Do not require seconder" option during pre-meeting, meeting, and post-meeting for any motion that has no seconder defined.

However, If you add a seconder to a motion, even with the "Do not require seconder" option enabled, the option is overridden in subsequent meeting session instances as it now assumes that it will require a seconder moving forward given that a seconder was added previously.|Motions will now respect the "Do not require seconder" option during pre-meeting, meeting, and post-meeting scenarios for any motion that has no seconder defined.

However, if you add a seconder to a motion, even with the "Do not require seconder" option enabled, the option will be overridden in subsequent meeting session instances as it now assumes that it will require a seconder moving forward since a seconder was previously added.

column6: Tokens can be included in Notification Hyperlinks
column7: Previously, when editing a Notification that had a link that included Tokens, the link would become invalid on save. e.g. %K would be replaced with %25K. With this change, the tokens remain as desired, creating a valid link for users.

column6: Addressed multiple issues related to saving note markups and comments on multi-page plan documents in the engineering viewer.
column7:
- Save button now works when you add a second note markup and/or comment on a different page of the document.
- The viewer now recognizes changes on each page of the document and prompts you to save before moving on to the next page. This prevents two other reported issues where you would have to click the Save button multiple times to apply all the comments and eliminates the possibility of losing note markups and comments as you add many across multiple pages.

column6: Addressed an issue in the Engineering viewer with the Centimeter-Millimeter unit displaying incorrect units. |Addressed an issue with markups in the Engineering Viewer.
column7: Fixed an issue in the Engineering Viewer where the centimeter-millimeter unit was displaying kilometer-meter units when drawing a line or area measure markup.|Fixed an issue where the Centimeter-Millimeter unit was displaying kilometer-Meter units when drawing a line or area measure markup and visa versa.

column6: Splitting an oversized volume no longer causes an error to occur.
PDF files that contain edit protection can be printed when using the HTML Web Client.|PDF files that contain edit protection can be printed when using the Web Client.
column7: When viewing an edit protected PDF in the HTML Web Client, the file should is able to be printed without issue.|When viewing an edit protected PDF in the Web Client, the file is able to be printed without issue.

column6: Option to not switch margin values when vertical or horizontal duplexing is selected.
column7:
When a print format duplex setting is set to vertical or horizontal, for the back of the pages, margin values are swapped top/bottom or left/right in order to allow for margins to line up on the physical page. This ensures enough space in the margins of generally one side of the page.

This may be seen as an unwanted feature when the output is to an electronic medium, like a pdf or xps document. An option has been added to not switch the margin values.

column6: Addressed an issue with default Keyword Values not being populated in the StatusView File Upload portlet.
column7: Updated the StatusView File Upload portlet to display default Keyword Values. This allows default Keyword Values to be used for indexing when importing files through the StatusView File Upload portlet.

column6: The incorrect revision is no longer made read-only when signing an E-form
column7: A correction was made to a previous error that caused the original revision to be made read-only when signing an E-form instead of the latest revision. This change brings greater stability for E-forms in the Unity Client.|If an e-form is modified by adding keywords and saving during the process of signing the e-form with a digital signature, the digital signature is added to the correct revision.

column6: Fixed editable note text focus
column7: Resolved the issue when the note text not setting the focus on the editable note text field.

column6: Documents no longer appear blurry after being rotated
column7: Updated the Unity Client so that documents no longer appear blurry after being rotated 270 degrees. This ensures that users will not worry about losing image quality while editing their documents in the Unity Client.

column6: During the page append process, end users can successfully choose an image file type if the incorrect file type was previously chosen.
column7: An error was fixed that prevented end users from being able to append pages to an image during import if they had initially selected a non-image file before selecting the images in the Unity Client.

column6: A user is able to continue with an import after a system loses connection to the Application Server and reconnects.|An end user is able to retry an import after an Application Server reconnect occurs.
column7: If the application server connection is lost during document import, the user is notified and is given the option to reconnect to the application server. Upon reconnecting, the keyword panel is reset and the user may try their import again.|If the Application Server connection is lost during document import, the user is notified and is given the option to reconnect to the application server. Upon reconnecting, the keyword panel is reset and the user may try their import again.

column6: The Document Import queue is now refreshed and cleared when a user navigates to another layout.
column7: Queueing multiple documents for import and then exiting the Import process by navigating to another layout, such as Document Retrieval, clears the entire Import queue. This prevents documents being erroneously imported when a separate, unrelated import process is started.

column6: Changed the selected thumbnail to retain the blue bounding box
column7: Changed the selected thumbnail to retain the blue bounding box when it is not actively focused. To distinguish between the actively selected thumbnail and the thumbnail that is moused over, there is a slightly lighted blue shade to represent the moused over thumbnail.

column6: Addressed an issue in the Web Application Management Console to help prevent invalid ADFS configurations.|Addressed an issue in the Web Application Management Console to prevent invalid ADFS configurations.
column7: The Web Application Management Console has been updated to require the "Configure for ADFS" menu item to be executed from the Tools menus before changes can be made to settings on the "ADFS Settings" tab.

column6: Error is no longer thrown when viewing an E-Form in Workflow and exiting out of Workflow
Exception is no longer thrown when routing E-Forms out of a Workflow queue using an Ad-Hoc Task
FIXED: Studio "Validate" options for "Consistency" and "Fix Issues" don't identify or fix missing attribute columns.
column7: Addressed an issue in OnBase Studio where the Validate/Fix Issues option would not correctly identify and fix missing columns for attributes.

column6: FIXED: Unable to delete an action that is shared with other classes
column7: Addressed an issue that prevented removing actions from a class if the same action was used on another class.

column6: Improved performance on filters with calculated attributes.
column7: Addressed an issue where some calculated attribute configurations could impact performance on filter queries.

column6: FIXED: Navigation buttons are no longer grayed out when the object was opened from an embedded filter.
column7: Navigation buttons are no longer grayed out when the object was opened from an embedded filter.

column6: FIXED: Formatted Text attributes were not accessible via the Client Side API.
column7: Fixed an issue that prevented accessing values in related formatted text attributes via the JavaScript API.

column6: Service profile logging is now enabled by default, with a logging level set to "Information".
column7: Service profile logging will always log at the "Information" level regardless of what the log level is set as.

column6: Addressed issue in failing to connect with Document Tracking Service.
column7: The Document Tracking Service now connects to the Application Server without displaying an error in the Diagnostics Console. This allows the service to connect successfully and create records.

column6: The Front Office Scanning COM integration for Epic now supports the "AllowLinkTypes" setting.
column7: The Front Office Scanning (FOS) COM integration for Epic now properly supports the "AllowLinkTypes" setting in the EpicIntegrations.config file. Previously, only the "PreventLinkTypes" and "PreventEpicLinks" settings were supported. This change brings the functionality of the FOS COM integration into parity with that of the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS).

column6: Resolved issue with the Web Blob Pass-through failing to update HTML/E-Forms.
column7: The Web Blob Pass-through will now update documents successfully, regardless of the file format/MIME type.

column6: The Test System Creation Utility (TSCU) will now correctly copy values in the gender database table.
column7: The Test System Creation Utility (TSCU) will now correctly copy values in the gender database table. This software change will not correct issues with the gender database table in environments that were created by a prior version of the TSCU.

column6: Fixed an issue with checked out DocuSign documents failing to come back into OnBase when signed.
column7: The document checkout/document lock are now cleared right before the revision is made for the signed document, allowing the sign document revision action to happen successfully.

column6: Resolved memory error that could occur when printing large documents directly from Epic Hyperspace Classic via the COM integration.
column7: Resolved memory error that could occur when printing large documents directly from Epic Hyperspace Classic via the COM integration. Note: This does not affect the Hyperdrive COM or SMART on FHIR integration for Epic.

column6: Resolved issue with the Healthcare Web Viewer failing to display E-form documents.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer now displays E-form documents correctly. This issue impacted the standalone Healthcare Web Viewer (launched in a browser) as well as the COM/SMART on FHIR integrations for Epic.

column6: Double-clicking notes in the Healthcare Web Viewer no longer attempts to open cross-references.
column7: Double-clicking notes in the Healthcare Web Viewer now opens the note instead of attempting to open cross-referenced documents.

column6: The Integration for Epic installer now correctly copies the EpicIntegrations.config file if selected during installation.
column7: The Integration for Epic installer now correctly copies the EpicIntegrations.config file to the Front Office Scanning (FOS) program file directory, if selected during installation.

column6: Corrected issue with the Integration for Epic installer when attempting to add additional features/options.
column7: Corrected an issue with the Integration for Epic installer when attempting to add additional features/options to an existing installation. If a scanning feature (i.e., Scan Acquisition Server or Front Office scanning) was already installed, and an attempt was made to add the Healthcare Web Viewer or DeficiencyPop for Epic feature, selecting the "Next" button in the installer would not progress to the next step in the install process as expected.

column6: Resolved issue(s) with Mailbox Importer importing duplicate documents
column7: Resolved an issue where Mailbox Importer imported duplicate documents because of not marking emails as read. Previously, duplicate documents were loaded in OnBase from email through Mailbox Importer. This caused numerous duplicate documents to appear in Brainware and OnBase.

column6: Fixed issue which prompted a message 'System.OutOfMemoryException' in the Form Designer
column7: User will now be able to load a large Unity Form in the Form Designer and 'System.OutOfMemoryException' will not be displayed.

column6: Web Services Publishing will now correctly produce PDF/A-1B documents through Document Composition.
column7: An error was corrected that caused Web Services Publishing to produce a standard-PDF instead of PDF/A-1B.

column6: Updated Application Enabler's "Retrieve Document" Web Client context to launch in Microsoft Edge
column7: Updated Application Enabler's "Retrieve Document" context in the Web Client to launch automatically in Microsoft Edge. This allows the context to continue to function as expected in Windows 11, which does not support the previous behavior of launching in Internet Explorer.

column6: Removed duplication of default font store location during Datalogics initialization
column7: If the default font store location is initialized manually, the LST font cache file can grow in size over time, causing performance issues in the application server.

column6: Resolved issue preventing the Scan Acquisition Server from uploading the document Description.
column7: The Description is now properly indexed to documents uploaded to OnBase via the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS) SMART on FHIR integration for Epic. Prior to this fix, the Description was ignored and not added to the document Keywords.

column6: Changed settings for Front Office Scanning debug logging when launched via Epic Integration Scanning.
column7: The WriteLog setting in the EpicIntegrations.config file now determines whether Front Office Scanning (FOS) writes logs. When enabled, FOS writes logs to the location specified in the Diskgroup Path setting. These are the same settings used by the Scan Acquisition Server (SAS). Starting with this build, the debugging instructions for FOS are now the same as the SAS instructions. See “Writing Events to the Debug Log” in the “Debug Modes” section of the Integration for Epic documentation. For builds prior to this release, continue to follow the instructions in “Running the Front Office Scanning Integration in Debug Mode.”

column6: Resolved issue occurring when users re-index documents retrieved by custom query in Front Office Scanning.
column7:
The COM Integration for Epic allows Front Office Scanning (FOS) users to re-index documents retrieved via Custom Query. Prior to this fix, however, FOS did not re-index documents to the selected Document Type whenever all the following conditions existed:
1.	The "ask" Document Type button was used to re-index the document.
2.	The Document Type name selected from the drop-down list contained lowercase letters.
3.	The Document Type did not have its own button configured in the EPICFOSCONFIG.xml file. 
This issue has been resolved. FOS now correctly re-indexes documents regardless of the letter case in the Document Type name.

column6: Full-Text documents would run into a situation where they would get stuck in the 'In Progress' state. They will now progress through the states correctly and complete processing.
Publishing Export Workstation Configuration installer works properly
column7: Publishing Export Workstation Configuration installer works properly

column6: Resolved issue with the Add Correction button text displaying incorrectly after the corrections form is displayed in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: Prior to this fix, the Add Correction button text would partially disappear and display below the button icon in the Healthcare Web Viewer toolbar when the corrections form was displayed. Now, the button text will display properly.

column6: Resolved an issue with the Integration for Epic installer when deploying a pre-configured Epic FOS configuration file.
column7: Corrected the file path stored in the registry when deploying a pre-configured Epic Front Office Scanning (FOS) configuration file in the Integration for Epic installer. Prior to this change, a trailing backslash ("\") would be added to the file path for the EPICFOSCONFIG.xml file, which would cause FOS to be unable to locate the file correctly.

column6: Resolved issue with the Healthcare Web Viewer displaying an error when attempting to view non-image documents in Epic.
column7: After making changes to improve the Healthcare Web Viewer in 21.1.34.1000 with HC-172566, a bug was introduced which caused an error to occur in Epic Hyperspace and Hyperdrive when attempting to view a non-image document. The document would display, even though the error would indicate that the document failed to display. This behavior also caused a significant delay in displaying the document in Hyperspace.

column6: Overlays can now be printed from the Unity Client when using an institutional database
column7: When an overlay was applied to a document type within an individual institution, and if there are multiple institutions with different overlay settings applied to the same document type, there was no option to apply that overlay to a desired document being printed

column6: Resolved issue preventing the Virtual Print Driver from importing documents into Unity Client.
column7: Users were unable to use the Virtual Print Driver on workstations without the Microsoft Visual C++ 2010/2012 redistributable files installed. This issue has been resolved.

column6: The DocPop hitlist refreshes after re-indexing a document.
column7: DocPop hitlist with Custom Query results correctly refreshes and loads once a document has been re-indexed within the web client.

column6: Resolved error encountered when rotating pages in the Unity Client Document Viewer.
column7: When viewing a multi-page document in the Document Viewer, Unity users sometimes encountered the following error: “Value was either too large or too small for an Int32.” This occurred when selecting the thumbnail of any page other than the first one and attempting to rotate it. After bypassing the error multiple times, the Unity Client sometimes became unresponsive. This issue has been resolved.

column6: The current page now updates correctly in the Page Status dialog in Resolute in Epic Hyperspace Classic when using the COM Healthcare Web Viewer integration for Epic.
