column7: The current page now updates correctly in the Page Status dialog in Resolute in Epic Hyperspace Classic when scrolling between pages or selecting different page thumbnails in the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic.

column6: The current page now updates correctly in the Page Status dialog in Resolute in Epic Hyperdrive when using the COM Healthcare Web Viewer integration for Epic.
column7: The current page now updates correctly in the Page Status dialog in Resolute in Epic Hyperspace Classic when scrolling between pages or selecting different page thumbnails in the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic. Prior to this fix, the current page would be off by 1, as the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration was using a 0-based page numbering schema, instead of 1-based.

column6: Combined Viewer now supports searching for Workview Objects when an attribute is mapped to multiple keywords
column7: When an attribute on a Workview Object was mapped to more than one keyword, the Combined Viewer may not have executed the desired search.

column6: Resolved a memory leak when Annotations were being burned into a document being saved to file.
column7: Resolved a memory leak when Annotations were being burned into a document being saved to file. Customers were only able to process about 3300 documents at a time until they would receive a GDI error message. Now we are able to reach the full GDI limit of over 26000 documents.

column6: DeficiencyPop Display Column Configuration is now available for solutions licensed for Signature Deficiencies for Epic
column7: DeficiencyPop Display Column Configuration is now available for solutions licensed for Signature Deficiencies for Epic.  Prior to this change, this configuration required the Medical Records Management Solutions license to be present. This prevented the Display Column configuration from being accessible to customers with solutions that use Signature Deficiencies for Epic and not Medical Records Management Solution.

column6: Resolved an issue with deficiencies not remaining in the location they were last placed through DeficiencyPop.
column7: Resolved issue where deficiencies that were originally placed in Signature Deficiencies for Epic, then later moved through DeficiencyPop for Epic, would not remain in the location they were last placed in DeficiencyPop.

column6: Resolved issue that prevented Epic integrated scanning applications from closing when launched via SMART on FHIR.
column7: An FHIRCast communication error sometimes occurred when attempting to close Front Office Scanning (FOS) or Scan Acquisition Server (SAS). Whenever the communication error occurred, it caused the integrated scanning application to fail to close (requiring additional effort to force it to close). This only affected the SMART on FHIR Integration for Epic; the issue has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue with the HL7 Listener not triggering updates to MRN-based and Chart-based AutoFill Keyword Sets.
column7: Prior to this fix, when the Medical Records Chart Processor processed an HL7 message that was intended to update MRN or Chart-level keywords, a job was not queued for the HL7 Back-end Job Processor to perform that work. Therefore, those keywords were not updated on documents that utilize the MRN and Chart-based AutoFill Keyword Sets. A technical bulletin should be available on Community related to this issue. If you have any additional questions or concerns, please contact Hyland Technical Support and reference Jira # HC-173178.

column6: Resolved issue with the Healthcare Web Viewer displaying an error when attempting to view non-image documents in Epic.
column7: After making changes to improve the Healthcare Web Viewer in 21.1.34.1000 with HC-172566, a bug was introduced which caused an error to occur in Epic Hyperspace and Hyperdrive when attempting to view a non-image document. The document would display, even though the error would indicate that the document failed to display. This behavior also caused a significant delay in displaying the document in Hyperspace.

column6: Resolved session loss issue with the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic when multiple viewer windows are open at the same time.
column7: Prior to this fix, if multiple instances of the Healthcare Web Viewer COM integration for Epic were open at the same time, closing one of the windows would cause the session to be lost for all other open viewer windows. This would cause issues when viewing page thumbnails or performing other document actions such as displaying document properties or keywords.

column6: fixed an issue that would cause an application server crash when attempting to load an excel file that was too large in the integrated office viewer.
column7: When using integrated office viewer, users will now see a message display explaining the file size limitation when attempting to view an Office document that exceeds the file size limit set by Microsoft.

column6: Correction to Licensing and Privilege requirements for Epic Physician Acknowledgement
column7:
A change has been made to correct the Licensing and Privileges required to use assign Physician Acknowledgement tasks through an OnBase Scan Queue.  

With this change the software will require that:
1. The solution is licensed for the Epic Integration
2. The user has the Indexing privilege
3. The user is accessing a Scan Queue that has been configured for Epic Physician Inbox Linking Support

column6: Publishing Export Workstation Configuration installer works properly
column7: Publishing Export Workstation Configuration installer works properly

column6: Documents from an external Meditech System were being excluded from the OnBase Release of Information packet
column7: An issue has been resolved that was resulting in documents attached to the OnBase Release of Information packet using the Meditech Report Capture integration not being included in the final release.  These documents appeared to be present on the Attachments screen but were missing from the Release Order screen and the final PDF that was generated as part of the release.

column6: Resolved an issue with signature deficiencies not remaining where they were placed in the Signature for Deficiencies for Epic client.
column7: Prior to this fix, some signature deficiencies would move immediately after being placed and had to be redropped (moved) back to the original location. This only affected the Signature Deficiencies for Epic (SD4E) client, not other deficiency applications such as DeficiencyPop and MRM Unity.

column6: Resolved a Web Client ActiveX CAB installation issue
column7: Previously, the installation would fail with the error:  "The system cannot find the file specified." This was due to mismatched file names.

column6: Addressed issue(s) related to changing the content type of a portfolio relation
column7: Resolved an issue where changing the content type of a portfolio relation is now maintained, and the source content type reflects that change. In prior versions, the source content type of a portfolio relation was expected to change, but the change did not persist after saving the repository.

column6: An error preventing Physicians from being able to cover for others has been resolved
column7: An issue has been resolved related to Physicians in Medical Groups who were configured to Complete Non-Signature Deficiencies.  Upon attempting to cover chart-level deficiencies, these users were receiving errors stating "An error occurred, please try again."

column6: Resolved a 'string literal too long' exception that occurred when updating scan formats in Oracle Databases.
column7: When updating scan formats in Oracle Databases, users encountered a 'string literal too long' exception. Scan sources for particular scan queues could not be changed, resulting in the creation of a new scan format every time a new scan source was desired. This issue has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue causing the cursor not to return to the current batch after completing work in the Scan Queue Layout in Batch Processing.
column7: Previously, users sometimes encountered an issue in Batch Processing that occurred when multiple users were working in the same scan queue at the same time. When returning to the Scan Queue Layout after completing a process on a particular batch, the cursor sometimes pointed to a different batch, causing confusion for the end user. This occurred when another user was creating a batch in the same scan queue. This issue has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue with Double-Click Queue Operation in the Document Preparation queue in Unity Batch Processing.
column7: Previously, the "Double-Click Initiates Queue Operation" setting in the Scan Queue Configuration (Process Options) did not function as intended in the Document Preparation queue in Unity Batch Processing.  This issue forced users to right-click the batch or to select the operation from the Unity ribbon. This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue preventing the BarcodeDocumentUpdates.AddKeyword method from expanding an AutoFIll Keyword Set if the value does not match the original barcode value.
column7: When a value other than the original barcode value was passed to the BarcodeDocumentUpdates.AddKeyword method, the AutoFill Keyword Set would not expand even if a matching primary keyword exists. This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue causing a communication loss error to incorrectly display when using Epic Integrated Scanning via the SMART on FHIR integration.
column7:
When performing integrated scanning with Front Office Scanning or Scan Acquisition Server, the following error message appeared intermittently: "Communication with Epic has been lost. The application will now exit, work will not be saved."

Contrary to this statement, the document was successfully uploaded to OnBase and linked in Epic. The issue causing this incorrect error message has been resolved.

column6: Full-Text Search will now correctly process documents that fall under Records Management.
Full-Text Server will now be able to process documents larger than 2GBs.
Resolved issue with Healthcare Web Viewer not releasing concurrent client licenses.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer now releases concurrent client licenses after attempting to view an invalid document handle that does not exist in OnBase.

column6: Resolved session errors in the Healthcare Web Viewer when saving Document Type Redactions.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer no longer displays the following error when saving Document Type Redactions: "Your session has expired. You are required to log in again."

column6: The Healthcare Web Viewer can now retain the last used zoom level when displaying documents.
column7: The Healthcare Web Viewer can now retain the last used zoom level when displaying documents. This behavior can be overriden by the zoomLevelOR variable in the appsettings element, in the Viewer Vars section, in the Web Server web.config. If no value is specified for the zoomLevelOR variable, the Healthcare Web Viewer displays documents at the last zoom level at which the user viewed a document.

column6: Re-ordering/moving pages will no longer cause a session error in the Healthcare Web Viewer.
column7: It is now possible to re-order/move pages in the Healthcare Web Viewer without losing the session.

column6: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Back-End Job Processor creating duplicate standard AutoFill Keyword Sets.
column7: Prior to this fix, the HL7 Back-End Job Processor could incorrectly create duplicate Standard AutoFill Keyword Set rows. This could occur when a secondary keyword in one AutoFill Keyword Set is being updated by an HL7 AutoFill Keyword Set Merge Processor, and that keyword is also a primary keyword in another keyset. When performing updates via reverse-lookup, the HL7 Back-End Job Processor did not verify if the primary value being updated already existed as a primary value in another keyset. This has been corrected.

column6: Blank FHIR Resource URLs can now be modified or deleted when using Oracle databases with OnBase.
column7: When importing settings from another OnBase system, it's possible that the imported FHIR Resource URLs can be blank and they need to be updated to the correct value. Prior to this fix, when using an Oracle database with OnBase, an error would occur when attempting to delete or modify blank FHIR Resource URLs. This error no longer occurs and the FHIR Resource URLs can be deleted or modified successfully.

column6: Addressed a permission issue with viewing and creating Unity Forms in OnBase Mobile for iPhone.

column7:

This issue would occur when a Patient Level document type was configured in either way in the Medical Records Unity Client:

column6: Addressed issue(s) related to increased memory consumption
column7: Resolved an issue that led to increased memory consumption in Workflow. Previously, in certain situations, Workflow retained additional memory that was not needed.

column6: "~Workflow[<life cycle name>\<queue name>]" macro functions successfully in a Fixed Constraint and fetches expected items from Workflow queue.
column7:
The issue was caused due to a previous performance improvement in this macro.
The function of this macro is to fetch all items from any workflow queue to WorkView when the macro is applied to Fixed Constraints. With this fix, the macro functions successfully and fetches items from workflow queue.

column6: Resolved issue(s) with XML DIP when CDATA was within the XML element
column7: Resolved an issue that caused XML DIP to fail when CDATA was within the XML element. Previously, when CDATA was in the XML element, XML DIP failed to read the document type from the correct line.

column6: Corrected an issue where a null reference exception occurs when the webBrowserRenderMode in the Unity Client config is set to “chromium” in PacketPreview, OleAsPDF, and Zip.
column7: Corrected an issue where a null reference exception occurs when the webBrowserRenderMode in the Unity Client config is set to “chromium” in PacketPreview, OleAsPDF, and Zip. These 3 areas forced IE and there is a conditional in the code that wasn’t accounting for viewers that may force IE and a certain casting fails. Now when opening a document in packet preview, an OleAsPDF document, and a zip document, no null reference exceptions occur.

column6: Resolved issue to not allow orphaned rows in sqbatchcustom table when a scan queue is deleted.
column7: It's possible to have orphaned rows in the sqbatchcustom table even if scan queues are deleted.  This can become an issue when using Export or perhaps other modules where the extra irrelevant rows in sqbatchcustom table could prevent these features from working properly.  This has been resolved.

column6: Resolved issue that prevented the Data Capture Server from releasing memory consumed during Bar Code Processing.

column6: Fixed an issue with workflow adhoc task, when viewing a submitted HTML E-Form within workflow using the "Display HTML Form" adhoc task, the date selection field does not load its existing value.

A Bug has been reported wherein when trying to view a already submitted HTML E-form within workflow using the "Display HTML Form" Adhoc task, date field returned a blank value.

column6: Made Changes to Address Memory Leak Issues
column7:
In Unity Client, customers were sometimes seeing the following error:
DocumentViewer Error: 0 : Insufficient memory to continue the execution of the program.
Exception: System.OutOfMemoryException: Insufficient memory to continue the execution of the program.
We made changes in the code to address this error, specifically to the batch Indexing layout, such as the thumbnail memory leaks and leaking web browser (likely introduced with CefSharp). "Triangle Errors" in Unity Client should no longer be popping up for the user and the memory leaks are no longer causing problems.

column6: Resolved issue where documents were not separated correctly when the Data Capture Server performed bar code processing when patch code separation was used.
column7:
When Data Capture Server executes bar code processing on a batch of documents when the documents are in a specific order, each document is separated into individual documents when a bar code is processed even though the bar code process/scan queue itself is only set to separate via patch codes.

This has been resolved and will perform separation consistent with the Thick Client.

column6: Fixed a color issue with imports in the Unity Client
column7: Resolved an issue where there are certain cases when a multi-page tiff would be imported without color in the Unity Client.

column6: Resolved an issue with Epic SMART on FHIR integrated Front Office Scanning that occurred when scanning to pre-populated documents.
column7:
When performing integrated scanning with FOS or SAS in Epic Registration (Prelude) and selecting a pre-populated document in the Epic Documents Table, the following error may occur: "Document was uploaded to OnBase successfully but did not attach properly in the Epic system. Please contact your OnBase administrator."

Although this message is valid in some cases, other times it is caused by an issue with the FHIR communication with Epic that prevented OnBase from correctly linking the document in Epic. The OnBase Application Server has been updated to resolve the FHIR communication issue with Epic.

This issue is most likely to affect any registration scanning that will scan a document to a pre-populated entry in the Prelude Documents Table in Epic. This should not affect scans to new rows in the Documents Table from the Document Type buttons in the Menu bar.

column6: Resolved issue causing a communication loss error to incorrectly display when using Epic Integrated Scanning via the SMART on FHIR integration.
column7:
Previously, the following error message was incorrectly displayed: "Communication with Epic has been lost. The application will now exit, work will not be saved." This occurred intermittently when performing integrated scanning with FOS or SAS and was due to a possible race condition.

Note: Despite the claimed error, whenever this specific race condition occurred, the document was successfully uploaded to OnBase and correctly linked in Epic.

column6: In Filter Result of Standard Class with related ODBC external class having composite key, to fetch a large number of records at a time from external table, the number of records per DB Call equals to the fixed batch size of 500 divided by the number of composite keys.
column7:
In Filter Result of Standard Class with related ODBC external class having composite key, to fetch a large number of records at a time from external table, the number of records per DB Call equals to the fixed batch size of 500 divided by the number of composite keys.

No restriction has been imposed upon the maximum number of the composite keys, though depending upon the increasing number of the composite keys, the number of records per call will be decreased. Hence the number of DB interactions will increase.
Concurrency issues are not addressed by this fix. If required, such issues need to be taken up separately. This fix applies only to performance optimization.

If there are no composite keys, which means that only unique keys are present, the system will act as it did before this fix.

column6: Successful creation document composition documents from an external class in version 21.1.31.1000
column7: Customers will now be able to create document composition documents from the external class successfully, without any error message thrown.

column6: Made performance improvements when opening a meeting in Agenda, Minutes, and Voting clients.
column7:
Two performance issues when loading a meeting in the Agenda Client were identified and corrected:
- We are now handling the retrieval of Pending Unassigned items after the meeting is loaded, which helps reduce the time of loading a meeting if you have many unassigned items for that meeting type in your database.
- We updated code that syncs the order of attachments in the meeting tree to the order displayed in the Unity Form, and vice versa, which also reduced the time of loading a meeting.

Regarding the Minutes and Voting Client meeting loading:
- We corrected an issue where it was taking too long to retrieve the Long Titles of agenda items from the unity form xml on the disk. The Long Titles are now stored in the database, and are retrieved much faster, making the meeting loading process much quicker for both the Minutes Client and the Voting Client.

column6: Resolved issue with the HL7 Back-end Job Processor updating primary keyword values to be blank in standards AutoFill Keyword Sets, causing subsequent high memory usage and performance issues.
column7:
The HL7 Back-end Job Processor will no longer update primary keyword values in standard AutoFill Keyword Sets (AFKSs) to be blank. This prevents a subsequent issue, where the HL7 Back-end Job Processor would find a blank primary AFKS row and query the entire AFKS table. When this subsequent issue occurred, the HL7 Back-end Job Processor would consume a significant amount of memory and eventually the service would stop or would fail to continue processing other jobs. Changes were also made to prevent the HL7 Back-end Job Processor from querying AutoFill Keyword Sets without a keyword to filter results on.

More information: 
When performing certain jobs, the HL7 Back-end Job Processor will update AutoFill Keyword Sets (AFKSs) via reverse-lookup. A reverse-lookup update occurs when the secondary keyword being updated by the HL7 Import Process is assigned as a primary keyword on other standard AFKSs.  The HL7 Back-end Job Processor will then update other secondary keyword values that are also in the associated standard AFKSs. Also, as in the event of an AFKS merge, the primary keyword value may also be updated, which could cause this issue to occur. 

The issue mentioned above would update the primary keyword in associated standard AFKSs to be blank whenever that keyword was updated to be blank in the original AFKS being updated by the HL7 Import Process.

If the user configures “DisableEFormChromiumCache“ to "false", then the resources will be fetched from cache in the next subsequent requests.  The default setting for DisableEFormChromiumCache is "false". This feature is added in version 21.1 only.

column6: Resolved an issue where the HL7 Listener could fail to create patients if patient demographics are not included in the HL7 message.
column7:
Resolved an issue that could cause the Patient Record Processor HL7 Import Process to fail to create a patient record when the HL7 message contains only identifiers (e.g. MPI, MRN, Assigning Authority) and no demographics (Name, Gender, Date of Birth).

Note: This issue does not affect the Medical Records Chart Processor HL7 Import Process.

column6: Resolved an issue where the HL7 Back-end Job Processor could fail to process some AutoFill Keyword Set merge jobs.
column7:
Resolved an issue with the HL7 Back-end Job Processor where AutoFill Keyword Set merge jobs fail to process when no secondary keywords are being updated by that job.

Note: The HL7 Listener services and the Unity Scheduler running the HL7 Back-end Job Processor must be updated to 21.1.20.1000 or later for this fix.

column6: Resolved an issue with MRN-based AutoFill Keyword Sets not expanding when using a specific Chart Data Field configuration.
column7: When the same keyword type is configured for both the MRN (Chart Data Field # 7) and MPI (Chart Data Field # 1), MRN-based AutoFill Keyword Sets (AFKS) were not expanding as expected. This fix resolves that issue so that MRN-based AFKS values are populated as expected when expanded. A workaround or alternative solution is to use an MPI-based AFKS instead.

column6: Corrected an issue where DocPop could display the incorrect document when launched from Epic.
column7: Resolved an issue when a user launched a DocPop URL from Epic, subsequent DocPop launches would open the same document and not the new document that was selected by the user. Now, subsequent DocPop launches will launch the selected documents as expected.

column6: When indexing a document, the cursor no longer enters text on the Excel spreadsheet instead of the keyword panel.

column6: Addressed issue where Unity jumped to page containing a note when opening the Notes List and only one note exists.
column7: Previously, if a user opened the notes pane and only one note existed, the system would automatically take the user to the page that contained the note. This caused the user to have to scroll back to the page they were on before toggling the notes on. Now the user remains on the page they are currently on and only jump to a different page if they select a specific note they would like to look at.

The signing of third-party forms is no longer supported in Patient Portal Link. Third-party forms can be signed upon arrival at the facility for treatment.

column6: Resolved an issue with the Image Retrieval API returning more pages than expected for some multi-page TIF documents.
column7: In some instances, it is possible for files in the OnBase disk groups to contain more pages than are specified in the OnBase database for those documents. When the Image Retrieval API (IRA) processed a request for one of these files, IRA returned all pages in the file in the disk groups, instead of only the pages that existed in the OnBase database. This change forces IRA to process/convert all TIF documents in order to return only the number of pages that are present in the OnBase database, regardless of how many pages are in the multi-page TIF file in the OnBase disk groups.

column6: Corrected an error that could occur when moving/merging Patients via HL7 with the "Allow implicit chart moves" HL7 Setting disabled.
column7:
When the "Allow implicit chart moves" HL7 Setting was disabled, it was possible for some patient move/merge messages to fail to process. This would occur whenever the chart identifier in the HL7 message was associated with the source/losing patient in the MRG segment of the HL7 message, and was not already associated with the target/winning patient in the PID segment. 

Context: The "Allow implicit chart moves" option in the HL7 Settings dialog was added in EP5 in order to support legacy configurations during upgrades. New installations of OnBase should have this setting disabled by default. This setting should be disabled whenever more than one HL7 Listener will be processing patient/chart-related messages (e.g. ADT/SIU) in a single OnBase environment. Reference the "Allowing Implicit Chart Moves" section of the HL7 MRG or reach out to your first line of support for more information.

column6: Resolved an issue where HL7 Back-end Job Processor jobs can be processed out-of-order when running multiple HL7 Listeners to process patient-centric messages.
column7:
When sending patient-centric messages (e.g. ADT or SIU) to multiple OnBase HL7 Listeners, it was possible for the listeners to insert jobs into the queue with non-sequential jobnums. This would cause the HL7 Back-end Job Processor to potentially process jobs in the wrong order and/or skip jobs due to way the HL7 Back-end Job Processor queried the queue for jobs. This change addresses the issue with jobs being inserted into the queue with non-sequential jobnums. The issue with jobs potentially being skipped is being addressed by HC-166959 (also fixed in 21.1.15.1000). 

Note: Prior to EP5, it was a documented best practice to have all patient-centric feeds processed on a single HL7 Listener service. Please see the EP3 or older HL7 MRG under the -HL7LISTENER command line switch section for this documentation.

column6: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Listener crashing during start-up when multiple ports were specified in the Command Line switches.
column7: Depending on the number of ports specified in the -HL7PORT Command Line switch, as well as the number of Import Processes configured, it was possible for the HL7 Listener to fail to start properly. Changes were made to improve memory pool utilization in order to prevent this issue from occurring.

column6: Resolved issue with the HL7 Listener attempting to store invalid dates received in messages.
column7:
Changes made as part of HC-144952 (released in 21.1.3.1000) caused an issue where the HL7 Listener failed to perform validation of date values received in messages before attempting to store them. 
This resolves that issue and the HL7 Listener will again perform validation of date values received in messages. If they are invalid, the HL7 Listener will not attempt to store the date values and will instead store a blank value, as before. (A valid date format is one that adheres to the HL7 standard.)

column6: Resolved an issue with the HL7 Listener failing to start in some environments with large numbers of user accounts and physicians.

column6: The Unity Scheduler can fail to expand MPI/MRN/Chart-based AutoFill Keyword Sets.
column7:
The Unity Scheduler would fail to expand MPI/MRN/Chart-based AutoFill Keyword Sets when the HL7 Back-end Job Processor task exists in the same scheduler task group as the Workflow action to expand the AFKS. An exception would be logged in the diagnostics logging and the secondary keywords in the AFKS would not be populated. The workaround was to move the HL7 Back-end Job Processor to a separate task group from the Workflow action that was expanding the AFKS.

This bug has been corrected and AutoFill Keyword Sets will expand properly even when on the Workflow action expanding the AFKS is in same task group as the HL7 Back-end Job Processor.

Note: It is still considered best practice to place the HL7 Back-end Job Processor in its own scheduler task group.

column6: Some template anchor tabs may be impacted from changes that DocuSign has implemented in their newer API endpoint that the Integration for DocuSign eSignature now uses for creating an envelope using templates.
column7:
DocuSign implemented a new API endpoint for creating an envelope using templates and ended support for the previous endpoint. The Integration for DocuSign eSignature uses the new endpoint as of EP4 (20.8). This new endpoint enables support of DocuSign Payment Fields, Stamps, and other advanced features but unknowingly changed the way anchor tabs that are part of the referenced Templates are applied.

Previously, an anchor tab on a DocuSign Template would only apply to the one document in the Envelope it was mapped to in OnBase. Using the newer DocuSign method, the anchor tab is applied to every document in the Envelope. As a result, if the Envelope contains multiple documents, each with a Template with an anchor tab using the same anchor text, a signature field will be placed multiple times in the same location, causing a recipient to have to sign the same location multiple times.

If you are using anchor tabs and experience this issue, you should update the anchor tabs in your DocuSign Templates and the corresponding text string identifier (AnchorTabString) in the documents for all the documents that are going into that specific type of envelope so that there is a unique Anchor Tab/Text String combo per template/document mapping.

For more information about using anchor tabs in DocuSign Templates, please refer to DocuSign documentation:
https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-soap-api/esign101/anchor-tagging/

column6: Addressed an issue where the "Word Document Composition" option was not always visible to check in user group privileges window.

Plan Review allows for that functionality without the Doc Comp license being purchased.  The problem was that without the "Word Document Composition" check box checked, Plan Review wouldn't enable the Create Letter button. In the past you would have to be licensed for Doc Comp to see that check box privilege in the user group. We addressed the issue so now whether you are licensed for Document Composition or not you will see the "Word Document Composition" privilege available to check in the user group so that you can use the "Create Letter" button in Plan Review to generate comment letters.

column6: Resolved issue with the HL7 Back-end Job Processor updating primary keyword values to be blank in standards AutoFill Keyword Sets, causing subsequent high memory usage and performance issues.
column7:
The HL7 Back-end Job Processor will no longer update primary keyword values in standard AutoFill Keyword Sets (AFKSs) to be blank. This prevents a subsequent issue, where the HL7 Back-end Job Processor would find a blank primary AFKS row and query the entire AFKS table. When this subsequent issue occurred, the HL7 Back-end Job Processor would consume a significant amount of memory and eventually the service would stop or would fail to continue processing other jobs. Changes were also made to prevent the HL7 Back-end Job Processor from querying AutoFill Keyword Sets without a keyword to filter results on.

More information: 
When performing certain jobs, the HL7 Back-end Job Processor will update AutoFill Keyword Sets (AFKSs) via reverse-lookup. A reverse-lookup update occurs when the secondary keyword being updated by the HL7 Import Process is assigned as a primary keyword on other standard AFKSs.  The HL7 Back-end Job Processor will then update other secondary keyword values that are also in the associated standard AFKSs. Also, as in the event of an AFKS merge, the primary keyword value may also be updated, which could cause this issue to occur. 

The issue mentioned above would update the primary keyword in associated standard AFKSs to be blank whenever that keyword was updated to be blank in the original AFKS being updated by the HL7 Import Process.

column6: Resolved an issue where HL7 Back-end Job Processor jobs can be processed out-of-order when running multiple HL7 Listeners to process patient-centric messages.
column7:
When sending patient-centric messages (e.g. ADT or SIU) to multiple OnBase HL7 Listeners, it was possible for the listeners to insert jobs into the queue with non-sequential jobnums. This would cause the HL7 Back-end Job Processor to potentially process jobs in the wrong order and/or skip jobs due to way the HL7 Back-end Job Processor queried the queue for jobs. This change addresses the issue with jobs being inserted into the queue with non-sequential jobnums. The issue with jobs potentially being skipped is being addressed by HC-166959 (also fixed in 21.1.15.1000). 

Note: Prior to EP5, it was a documented best practice to have all patient-centric feeds processed on a single HL7 Listener service. Please see the EP3 or older HL7 MRG under the -HL7LISTENER command line switch section for this documentation.

column6: Resolved issue with the HL7 Listener attempting to store invalid dates received in messages.
column7:
Changes made as part of HC-144968 (released in 20.3.37.1000) caused an issue where the HL7 Listener failed to perform validation of date values received in messages before attempting to store them as keywords. 
This resolves that issue and the HL7 Listener will again perform validation of date values received in messages. If they are invalid, the HL7 Listener will not attempt to store the date values and will instead store a blank value, as before. (A valid date format is one that adheres to the HL7 standard.)

column6: Performance improvements opening a Meeting in Agenda, Minutes, and Voting clients
column7:
Two performance issues when loading a meeting in the Agenda Client were identified and corrected:
- We are now handling the retrieval of Pending Unassigned items after the meeting is loaded which will help reduce the time of loading a Meeting if you have many unassigned items for that meeting type in your database.
- We updated code that syncs the order of attachments in the Meeting tree to the order displayed in the Unity Form and vice versa which also reduced the time of loading a meeting.

Regarding the Minutes and Voting Client meeting loading:
- We corrected an issue where it was taking too long to retrieve the Long Titles of agenda items from the unity form xml on disk. column7: Document Queries by document type or document type group query the types according to applicable keywords and therefore return documents that do not contain combined view search keyword values if the keyword types do not exist on the document type.  While the backend database query behavior is not changed the results are now filtered from the response from the application server so that only documents matchin all applied combined view search keyword values and one or more values of any applied source filter keyword types are returned.  The limitation applies only to those combined view type search and source filter keyword types for which the user has specified values.  If a keyword type is assigned as a search or source filter keyword type to the combined view type but not used in the search then it will be ignored.

Assigning Authority values passed on the Deficiency Pop URL are no longer treated as case sensitive when filtering
column7:
Deficiency Pop has been updated to no longer treat Assigning Authority values passed on the Deficiency Pop URL as case sensitive.  Assigning Authorities configured for use in OnBase are treated in a case insensitive manner.  This change was made to align Deficiency Pop with behavior seen in the OnBase Patient Window and HL7 modules.

Prior to this change if HL7 ADT sends a mixed case Assigning Authority value (e.g. "Vst") and the configured coded entry is upper case (e.g. "VST") users will see a "No deficiencies remaining for:" message in the filter when filtering on the chart.  This fix ensures that filtering is applied correctly, regardless of the case used to construct the Assigning Authority query string parameter.

The OnBase Patient Window will no longer check keyword filter/display column configuration when loading
column7:
The OnBase Patient Window (OPW) previously included a step upon login in order to check configuration relative to the number of loose keywords available for filtering as well as display columns in the document list. If the environment was configured with 4+ loose keyword/Keyword Type Group (KTG) combinations in either filter criteria or document list display columns, the application would error out and prevent access. This check was added in order to allow for opportunities to make configuration changes to help improve performance of the OPW. 

Due to other improvements added to the OPW, this check has been removed as it no longer provides any benefit.

This prevented any charts that did not have documents from being available within the selection dialog when creating the form. 

This functionality has been updated so that all charts (those with and without documents) are available for selection when creating chart based forms within the OPW.

There is a new setting in the application server's web.config file for setting the minimum time a temporary file exists before it expires.
column7:
Prior to this change, temporary files created by the application server for images sized greater than 100 kB retrieved by Distributed Disk Groups or from encrypted Disk Groups were not expired from the temp directory until the current user's session ended. This potentially could cause problems when users' sessions did not expire frequently. Those temp files now expire after five minutes after them by default.  If desired the time is configurable in the following new node in the application server's web.config file:

<TemporaryFiles>
      <!-- The minimum amount of time a temporary file should exist without being accessed before it expires. Default is 00:05:00 (five minutes). -->
      <TempFileExpirationThreshold value="" />
</TemporaryFiles>

column6: Display Actions will now be removed when their event data is removed from the dashboard item
column7: Fields are required to be placed in the dimension or hidden dimension section within the Dashboard Designer to be considered valid for mapping to a Display Action. Before this change, if you mapped a Display Action to a valid data item and then moved it to and invalid location, the display action would still be available and functional. This has been fixed, and if a previous mapped action has its data item moved to an invalid location, the display action is then removed.

This enhancement adds functionality for field configurations in the Visual RCM Batch File Fixed-Width processor. 

New field configurations for:
Currency formats
Date formats
Trimming spaces
Trimming certain characters
Trimming leading characters
Trimming trailing characters

column6: Global Fields can be defined for Visual Batch Header Import
column7: In the OnBase Thick Client >  Processing > Revenue Cycle Management > Batch File Import Processor > Visual Fixed Width Configuration, users have the ability to configure Global Fields for RCM Line Types.  Some Global Fields have character limits and therefore appropriate dialogs will be presented to a user when the character limit has been exceeded.  The values will then be truncated to adhere to the character limit of that particular field.  Global values can be edited by right clicking or double clicking the [VALUE] as well.

column6: New "RCM Line Type" configuration for Visual RCM Batch File Fixed Width Import Processor

These new types help to determine the type of data coming from the import file, for instance being a Batch, EOB, Claim, or Detail.

column6: Host Importer "Identify Posted Claims" configuration respects file posted dates
column7: The Host Importer configuration for "Identify Posted Claims" will now set the posted date for claims and details to the value provided within the import file. If no value is provided or is invalid, then the posted date will be set to the date the import is processed.

column6: Users can now import batch data via delimited files

Safety improvements when a computer running the ActiveX controls runs low on memory
column7:
When a computer running the ActiveX controls runs low on memory, rendering can be affected.
The warning on the viewer status line, "Image quality reduced due to available resources" indicates the following:
	1: A "low memory" condition was detected.
	2: Memory is not so low (yet) that images can not be rendered at all.
	3: To preserve memory, image scales are reduced.
	4: Certain operations that are affected by image scale, for example, note creation or modification or document rotation operations, are prevented.
	5: Forbidden operations are removed from the viewer menus and toolbars.
	6: No note changes can be saved.

column7:
Deficiency Pop has been updated to no longer treat Assigning Authority values passed on the Deficiency Pop URL as case sensitive.  Assigning Authorities configured for use in OnBase are treated in a case insensitive manner.  This change was made to align Deficiency Pop with behavior seen in the OnBase Patient Window and HL7 modules.

Prior to this change if HL7 ADT sends a mixed case Assigning Authority value (e.g. "Vst") and the configured coded entry is upper case (e.g. "VST") users will see a "No deficiencies remaining for:" message in the filter when filtering on the chart.  This fix ensures that filtering is applied correctly, regardless of the case used to construct the Assigning Authority query string parameter.

column6: Number of documents displayed in Combined View are set by DocumentQueryLimit setting in the AppServer configuration
column7:
DocumentQueryLimit is respected by Combined Viewer. Documents are limited based on the number in the DocumentQueryLimit setting in the AppServer configuration. The default limit is set to 2000 documents. Adjusting this number has a direct effect on the number of documents listed in specific Combined Viewer View Types. 

Note:  The document count, if set to display on the View Tab, will display the total number of documents. Regarless of the DocumentQueryLimit. eg: There are 5000 documents within a View Type group. The count next to the View Type will display there are 5000 documents, but only 2000 of the documents will be displayed within that group by default. Adjusting the DocumentQueryLimit in the AppServer config to 5000 documents or greater will display all 5000 documents.

column6: A Sender Address must be defined when sending notifications/emails through the Distribution Service and Document Distribution
column7:
Users sending a notification/email through Distribution Service must define a Sender Address.  This can be achieved through one of three ways…

	1. Entering a Default Sender Email Address in the Distribution Service Settings in OnBase Configuration.
	2. Configuring a User Email Address in Names/Passwords Settings in OnBase Configuration.
	3. Entering an email address when configuring an email sending process such as Workflow.

If at least one of these is not configured, the Distribution Service will not send the notification/email.


Users sending documents through Document Distribution must have a Default Sender Address configured in Distribution Service Settings.  If this sender field is not populated with a valid email address, the documents will stay in the Distribution Queue with a status of 'Waiting'.

Datasets will display in Unity Forms and Image Forms in the Unity Briefcase
An exception will no longer be thrown when making a table read only using the "Change Element Editability" Custom Action
The signature box will appear instantly when clicked on as expected.
Context Menu options for Digital Signatures added to the Document Search Results List
column7:
The following sub-menu options are now available for Digital Signatures from the Document Select Context Menu in the ActiveX Web Client:
 - Sign Document
 - Verify Document
 - View Signatures

column6: Full Support Mode now Enabled for Edge HTML 16 & 17 browsers
column7: The latest build for OnBase 18.0 now has Full Support for Edge HTML 17.

column6: Grouping by a Entry Date header now persists after refreshing the Workflow Inbox in the Web Client
Display Filtered Document Count is now respected when a Workflow filter has been applied to a queue
The Create Note action now places the note on the correct page when using the "Current Page" option
In the Unity Client Workflow interface, related items for the most recently selected primary document will now display as expected in the viewers
column7: The viewers will now accurately display the corresponding primary and related documents when a suspended task is executed on multiple documents.

column6: When the inbox is undocked in Unity Client, the Document Information Panel and Re-Index panel are not locked
Timers with multiple child task lists executing against a large number of items no longer cause out of memory exceptions
Shift-JIS character encoding is now displayed correctly in HTML Form Workflow Filters
The On Demand Diagnostics (ODD) Console will now log all Message Sources for users that are temporarily granted access through User Administration
Fixed an issue where an invalid child dataset attribute value can be saved if the parent value is changed without changing the child dataset
Objects utilizing WorkView notifications set to send only on Create can be properly deleted.
Newly created Views that are redirected from an existing View will use the View Template instead of the View
A Task has been added to the WorkView Doctor, to remove or modify Data Set component properties for existing attribute components
column7:
-A"Check for Field Components that have a different Data Set Value data source from its Attribute" task has been added to the WorkView Doctor.
[img: 306475.jpg]
-The WorkView Doctor will now also compare the database version to the OnBase build being used, before allowing the WorkView Doctor to be ran.

column6: Previously configured filter user overrides will be respected in Version 3 Views
column7: This applies to both filter bar items and embedded filters.

column6: Improved error handling when importing an EIS off-ramp with a message item type
column7: Importing an EIS off-ramp with a message item type no longer throws an error in the OnBase Studio import wizard

column6: Keyword Types can be mapped to the "DICOM - Study Date" and "Clinical - Procedure Date" chart data fields
Performing a bar code append with a revisable document type now correctly logs the action in document history
Combined Viewer search panel keywords are now being displayed in alphabetical order.
Filter respects display column keyword type and the filter keyword type.
OnBase Studio can now import multiple Unity scripts referencing two versions of same library.
When importing new filter bars, classes, and calendar filters, the sequences will be updated
Attributes can now be mapped when replacing classes via the Importer
Improved error handling when attempting to publish a Web Services Publishing receive operation for <Any Message Item Type>
When burning a 'Line Measure' and/or 'Area Measure' markup into an approved document, the text portion of the markup is burned into the correct position
An exception will no longer occur when using the Ctrl + V shortcut to paste images in the Unity Client and IE11 Web Client
column7:
- Images pasted in IE11 will properly display without selecting the Italics option. 
- Pressing the Enter key after pasting will now move the cursor to the next line.
- The Unity Client context menu will still allow copy/paste functionality in applicable attribute fields.

column6: The action types Set Properties from Message Item and Call Soap Service are now available in Workflow
Fix: Corrected icon placement of the Front Office Scanning rotate document buttons in the toolbar.
Negative or whole number currency values no longer mark an unchanged object as dirty when opened in an Oracle database.
The Main Enter Key now functions as expected in the Web Client when it is configured for any of the possible options at the Scan Queue Level
column7: The Main Enter Key can be configured to perform three different functions within the Indexing Layout ( No Action, Tab to Next Field, or Index Document ). Previously when it was configured for 'No Action' the Indexing Layout would close when the Main Enter Key was pressed. Now this does not happen and nothing happens when the Main Enter Key is pressed. The other two options are also now respected in all browsers.

column6: Autofill Keyword Sets can now successfully be expanded in Web Client Batch Indexing when a document type contains hidden keywords with default values assigned
column7: Previously if a document type contained hidden keywords with default values assigned an autofill keyword set could not successfully be expanded by a user who did not have access to the hidden keywords. This has been corrected and Autofill Keyword Sets will successfully expand regardless of if the user has access to the hidden keywords or not.

column6: IDOl now displays image documents in Combined Viewer.
Updated the Integration for Jack Henry to the latest .NET dll references
column7: Updated the Integration for Jack Henry to the latest .NET dll references

column6: Support added for ADO.NET connection strings
All UnityForms, even with usergroup security configured, are visible to a service account
Relationship attributes will validate as expected when calculated attributes are present on a class
In the Web Client, right-side Notes panel is no longer visible when viewing a PdfPop URL
Inbound requests are rejected in the case of a duplicate SubmissionSet.UniqueID
Adding additional Primary Keyword Instance to an already expanded Keyset with multiple matching values now appends new secondary values and does not overwrite the initially expanded keyset
Relationship attributes are properly validated when modified from an editable embedded filter.
Corrected an issue that prevented the E-Forms printing dialog from displaying.
Deleting the last page of a document no longer causes an exception during ICAP and IDC indexing
column7: Deleting the last page of a document no longer causes an exception during Intelligent Capture for AP and Interactive Data Capture indexing

column6: Advanced Capture no longer throws an Out of Memory error when configuring against an Oracle database
Improvement to database upgrade logic
column7: The upgrade logic was improved to ensure that maxnums are always set correctly in the database, specifically for ihehl7fieldnum.

column6: Web Clients now successfully expand AutoFills within the Re-Index dialog
ArchiveLink Logging Utilities improved to handle logging/purging against Oracle Unicode FIPS compliant database systems
Addressed an issue with currency keywords in Custom Queries accessed from OnBase Mobile.
column7: The fix ensures that the user is able to view keyword panel as expected in OnBase Mobile for Custom Queries that contain currency keywords within the configured retrieval keywords.

column6: Addressed a loading issue of large PDFs in OnBase Mobile.
column7: Optimized the number of server requests when a user scrolls through the pages of large PDFs. This improves the page download performance of large documents viewed in Document Search or Workflow in the OnBase Mobile app.

column6: Addressed an issue with unsaved changes of a unity form opened in OnBase Mobile
column7: When a Unity form is already open in OnBase Mobile, and the user tries to open another form through a deeplink, a prompt is displayed for the user to save or discard changes made on the Unity form that is already open.

column6: Resolved a condition where ICU may segfault in low memory environments.
Addressed an upload issue when a Document Type contains a MIKG keyword set.
column7: Addressed an issue in OnBase Mobile to prevent upload failure when the documents are associated with Multi-Instance Keyword Groups (MIKGs).

column6: Addressed a login issue for OnBase Mobile users when the database is not licensed for the REST API Integration Toolkit
column7: Updated the authentication flow and removed the dependency with Document Management API. This ensures that the users can login to OnBase Mobile without requiring the REST API Integration Toolkit license.

column6: Corrected the functionality of "Ignore Change Prompt On Load" Unity Form setting in OnBase Mobile
column7: This correction ensures that users do not see the "Are you sure you want to continue without submitting this form? Any unsaved work will be lost" prompt, and can still close the Unity Form, provided the "Ignore Change Prompt On Load" settings is enabled, and one custom action is present that changes any field on the load, so that manual changes are not required. If the user changes the form manually and wants to navigate away from it, the user should still see the prompt.

column6: Corrected the functionality of "Ignore Change Prompt On Load" Unity Form setting in OnBase Mobile

column6: OnBase Client should recognize Document Type Bar Codes and the Keyword Type Bar Codes properly
column7: OnBase Client can differentiate between Document Type Bar Codes and the Keyword Type Bar Codes. So, both Unity and OnBase clients should behave the same way and not consider the Document Type Bar Codes in the Keyword Type.

column6: When F3 split operations create auxiliary (child) documents in OnBase, the original document’s date is now automatically copied from the parent to all newly created child documents.
column7:
When F3 split operations create auxiliary (child) documents in OnBase, the original document’s date is now automatically copied from the parent to all newly created child documents.

This ensures:

Consistent itemdate values between parent and auxiliary documents

Accurate document dating for downstream processes, reporting, and compliance

Behavior is applied unconditionally for created child document

column6: Fix for HL7 Backend Processor Failing to Run
column7: Resolved an issue where HL7 Backend Processor jobs configured to run via the Unity Scheduler service would unexpectedly fail.
